Congrats on the losses! Sounds like you are doing great! With the leptin reset, you will have absolutely no trouble stabilizing...it made all the difference for me, until I got to an artificially low weight that is. As long as you don't go too far, your body will stabilize just fine! Good luck!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-21T17:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-39993>

Boo Hiss!

Only down .2 Tomorrow is day 40 on the hcg program. I have skipped a day for several weeks - but I don't know if it makes any sense to keep going.

Right now I'm at 208.4.

Tomorrow will be the day I set as my goal day. . .it's what I decided up front what would be my "last" day. . and the day I could quit.

I always like to reevaluate - but at this point, I do feel it was a success on LOSING weight. Anxious about stabilizing.

Can I really drop 8 lbds in the next week? Am I wasting my time and should just move on? It's a little dissapointing as I was hoping to get comfortably in a 14 and need some new clothes. Right now I have some very nice brooks brothers suits and stuff in the current size, so it's not the end of the world.

It just feels like failure to not get to that 200 mark. It's been such a symbolic issue for me since that 6th grade experience - and I was really starting to think I could do it.

It's not like the diet is HARD, I don't really have cravings - hunger is controlled. I'm just bored out of my Fing mind.

Soul said something yesterday that hit it on the head - I'm doing some AN behavior. . .the last week I've been cooking beautiful and amazing meals for my family, and baking for clients and going above and beyond feeding other people. I can't even take a taste - so it's like some wierd mind game with myself.

Feels like a crossroads. Can't decide.

Going to garden again today - thanks to some great advice I am planting 6 asparagus plants and a few more tomatoes and two more stevia plants!

> Only down .2 Tomorrow is day 40 on the hcg program. I have skipped a day for several weeks - but I don't know if it makes any sense to keep going.
>
>
>
> Right now I'm at 208.4.
>
>
>
> Tomorrow will be the day I set as my goal day. . .it's what I decided up front what would be my "last" day. . and the day I could quit.
>
>
>
> I always like to reevaluate - but at this point, I do feel it was a success on LOSING weight. Anxious about stabilizing.
>
>
>
> Can I really drop 8 lbds in the next week? Am I wasting my time and should just move on? It's a little dissapointing as I was hoping to get comfortably in a 14 and need some new clothes. Right now I have some very nice brooks brothers suits and stuff in the current size, so it's not the end of the world.
>
>
>
> It just feels like failure to not get to that 200 mark. It's been such a symbolic issue for me since that 6th grade experience - and I was really starting to think I could do it.
>
>
>
> It's not like the diet is HARD, I don't really have cravings - hunger is controlled. I'm just bored out of my Fing mind.
>
>
>
> Soul said something yesterday that hit it on the head - I'm doing some AN behavior. . .the last week I've been cooking beautiful and amazing meals for my family, and baking for clients and going above and beyond feeding other people. I can't even take a taste - so it's like some wierd mind game with myself.
>
>
>
> Feels like a crossroads. Can't decide.
>
>
>
> Going to garden again today - thanks to some great advice I am planting 6 asparagus plants and a few more tomatoes and two more stevia plants!

I have no knowledge or experience with hcg so can't speak to that. It does sound to me like you are obsessing about the number on the scale and I can relate all too well to that. My day is good or bad based on the number I see first thing in the AM. I just went an entire week without getting on the scale and it was surprisingly empowering once I got past the few couple of days being worried about what might happen. Be careful of getting too focused on the scale and not on your end goal - health for yourself and the ability to have fun with your family.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-21T21:04:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40051>

Take the success and run with it. Leave the rest.

> Soul said something yesterday that hit it on the head - I'm doing some AN behavior. . .the last week I've been cooking beautiful and amazing meals for my family, and baking for clients and going above and beyond feeding other people. I can't even take a taste - so it's like some wierd mind game with myself.

I know nothing about Hcg and that's why I keep my mouth shut most of the time. But for me EDs + Hcg = trouble

If you are doing things like these then your hunger is not "gone". It's behavior people do who are deprived of food. It's a coping mechanism of your brain to deal with hunger/starvation. Cut your (non) losses and the seafood is waiting. And drop off some of those delicious foods you made  B/c I will actually \*eat\* them

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-21T23:34:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40084>

Oh dear, I broke. I suppose it very well may be that I'm at the end of this journey. My husband made lemon bars with a nut crust. There was some awesome local honey at the farmers market - and he loves to bake. I decided just to "taste" the lemon custard then spit it out.

One entire piece of lemon bar later (actually not that bad, nuts, coconut oil, lemon juice, eggs and 1/4 cup honey for baking dish) I left the room.

So, now what? I think I will weigh in the a.m., and if there is a drop then I shall consider that kismet I think and call it LIW. If a gain? Dunno.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-21T23:44:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40087>

> **Cavemam;40271 wrote:** I decided just to "taste" the lemon custard then spit it out.

Just so you know - that is an eating disordered behavior.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40113>

OK, let's pile on some more guilt.

Cavemam I think you know what the problem is. You've gone too long on the VLCD with hcg. time to ditch it and go back to LR. {{hugs}}

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-22T01:48:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40114>

So, I'm not disagreeing with you ladies - thanks for the observations.

I also am NOT going to go on a 2 hr walk tonight to "work it off" as was my initial reaction. . . I see the ED in that one too. Plus it's 100 degrees at 5 p.m.

I moved forward and had about 150 cals of protein for dinner (bison) and I'm moving on. I want to see the good advice from the HCG masters about late round cheating and if there is a benefit for doing a few days after cheat or if I can just stop and call today LIW.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-22T01:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40119>

Ok that entirely was not my intention. Sorry if you took it like that Cavemam. I'm the last one to guilt someone for ED behaviors. I just wanted to make sure you know which path your are starting to go down. I'm glad you called off that walk tonight. I called off my bike ride, too. Crazy weather out there.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-22T19:38:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40241>

HCG UPDATE!

I was up .02 lbds this a.m., and my total losses for the last week are minimal - so I think I'm calling it quits.

My GROSS losses are 32 lbds in 41 days (36 injections)

My NET losses are 27lbds

Hitting the Leptin RX with a BAB and some HWC in the coffee in the a.m.

Thanks for all the support!

I suggest you have your husband re-set your scale so it reads 9 pounds light. Then step on and see you've broken below 200 pounds. The joy you then feel will be enough to carry you through and re-set your mind to the healthiest choices for you, not ones focussed on a number. (I'm not actually kidding... if he did this and you didn't know, just think how you'd feel. You'd feel 'free' and successful and super-confident that you can do this. It's all already true, you're just hitching your proverbial star to a number. Scales are for fish... eat em! Raw!)

Thanks for this! It's a great way for me to try and re-frame it in my mind. I'm getting there. I think yesterday I mourned the idea and was sad about it all - but today is back to happy about what great losses I've had and a re-group on trying to maintain it.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-23T22:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40453>

had a few steps back the last couple of days. Moving on. Had a lovely bfast with the family this a.m. outside that had seafood and liverwurst. Lunch crab cakes. Dinner has salmon on the menu.

Got some lobster tails - never made that.

Ordered 2 fish cookery books and have vague dreams of working through them ala that movie about julia childs.

Stopped the hcg diet one day too quickly. Today weight was same as yesterday even with a terrible step back. Back to my epi-paleo. Let's hope tomorrow won't be a steak day!

Here is me counting all my good fortunes. I'm about to don the swimsuit and head out to the oasis to sunbathe, spot CT, and work all at the same time. How awesome is it that I can do that! Each day I get up I try to be thankful that I don't have to go to an office and I can have a relaxed morning easing into the day with my son (and some days hubby).

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-23T22:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40456>

These are the crabcakes I made for lunch.

Tub of crabmeat they sell in the cold section.

mustard

indian spices

a tablespoon or so of almond flour

2 eggs

Apple cidar vinegar

salt/pepper

Mixed up, sauted in CO. Crispy/Crunchy/Awesome!

I ate it with a salsa made with:

jar of green chili salsa

avocado

a couple of strawberries

You've never had lobster before?! Or you've just never cooked it? It's super easy, and so, so yummy. I used to work part-time at a fish market (of all places!), and they used to make lobster salad that was TO DIE FOR. Some days, if I got lucky and timed it right, I'd get there to start my shift before the lady mixed up the lobster salad and I could knick a little container of just straight lobster meat from the bowl for my dinner. Heaven!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-23T22:56:52+0300

Never made it before - LOVE to eat it, but because of prices at the swanky places I frequent - I hardly ever order. I spent a wayward year in Boston and ate weight in seafood. I effing LOVE lobster. I've been bugging my husband all summer long about taking a day trip up to Maine...the whole motive behind which is to eat cheap lobster, and possibly bring some home as well.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-23T23:09:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40467>

I've always had a sense of how important environment is, and thus how some houses have a "good" feel and some a "bad" feel for certain people. The more we learn here, the more that is being fleshed out as something less magical than a woo-woo concept - and that indeed environment can effect physical changes in us.

Thus, how proximity to us certain negative things are can effect us. The shootings here got me to thinking. . .because I think I've had a truely freakish amount of close proximity to disaster. Not that I effectively closer to this shooting anymore than you guys were - but it started that thought process. It feels close to me in my heart. Don't know why. Watched/Listened to the Fallon stuff (the guy related to lizzy borden who went into studying killers and found out he had serial killer brain).

Before there was Columbine - there was the first "school" shooting at Westside in Jonesboro Arkansas. I was finishing one of my degrees then (music education). At that school. Wasn't there that day. Killer kids had been in band that I taught.

When I was younger, giant tornado took out a walmart in my town. Friend were there and hurt. I was supposed to be there at that time but had lied to my mom and the people I was supposed to be with and hid out in the woods reading instead.

In college - big tornado hit the town, lots of damage and people hurt - again, close but not directly effected.

My professional life - worked in New Orleans at a theater there. . traveled there all the time from Houston. Tons of friends and such. Katrina. Was almost going to be there - but changed a meeting at the last minute. At that point I think I had just gotten preggers with my son, and didn't know I was (I was unaware I was a mom until months into pregnancy).

And there are MORE - many more, some where I WAS directly involved in said tragedy.

It's creeping me out.

### janagram — 2012-07-23T23:31:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40474>

maybe it's related to the way you think....? I saw somebody run a red light in front of the fire station at 30th and Arapahoe once. Head on collision at like 60miles an hour or so with another car. They didn't just crash into each other, they crashed and bounced and flew all over the place. They did have the location right and there was a police car somewhere behind me at the red light I was waiting at. Officer checked out the worse (unrecognizable) car and wouldn't even attempt to open the door... he just glanced into the window. It was a terrible scene with terrible silence at one of the most busy intersections of Boulder. I guess these things happen.... I saw another terrible accident on I-25 once with no emergency crew around. Can't get the bloody face sticking out of the windshield of the mini van out of my mind probably for the rest of my life.

You should check out Lynn McTaggart and The Field. Totally not dumb luck or coincidence. The body knows things way before the mind. If animals can do (flee before a natural disaster) ppl can too. You just pay attention and follow ur inner guidance. Honestly this probably happens a lot more than you or anyone realizes. If you avert the tragedy, then you don't know how close you came!

But I know what you mean. Feels creepy. But by the grace of God I guess...

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-24T04:36:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-40571>

The best way to pan sear things to get a nice crust and keep cleanup to a minimum is to cook on the grill! No flying fats in the kitchen and no worries about smoke!

Must have a good pan. Scan pan is the way to go. Non stickish, indestructible. Super easy to clean. Iron skillet works great - but cleanup and pan care ads significant time to the process.

Cavemam, how I understood his comments where more like 'you may not ever have optimal.' Remember, he settles for nothing less than an A+. He said the same thing about those of us with Hashis, especially if it was past a certain point. I relate to the 'oh \*\*\*\*' feeling (I ended up with cancer, after all, so I'm not exactly starting with a near-optimal platform), but I also heard (after the panic-induced whooshing in my ears stopped) a lot of optimism. We follow epi-paleo and circadian rhythms of all kinds and we trend our labs. Maybe you get to an A, not an A+. But my guess is your A is going to feel pretty damn good. (I also think the specifics of how much doom he casts (and the way out of it) can only be given in an individual consult; he has to talk in averages. And you, my dear, are WAY above the average.)

I wake up in wonder that I'm eating all this fish...who is this person? You never know what I'll do next, but its not likely to be a MHS!!

So why do you need his support? If you eat ice cream and continue to think it's fine even with your labs showing differently, then where's the point?

1\. I hate doctors that sugar coat everything. It doesn't help me a thing. It undermines the little bit of trust I might actually have in some of them. Give me the truth so I can go deal with it. And I DO NOT tolerate any doctor "scaring" me into any behavior. That's not their job. That's manipulative. And it doesn't work most of the time. You know how many times I was told that I was going to die if I continued doing what I was doing by doctors hoping to scare me into "health"? Well, guess what, I continued doing what I was doing anyways b/c it wasn't my choice in the first place and the only thing that the scaring did was get me into full on cortisol through the roof mode and flight/fight/freeze response. Not a good place to make a change.....

Sorry, pushed a button here. Dr K doesn't scare people into change. He tells the truth. There is a difference. You can then go and do with it what you want. Your choice.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-30T03:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-41889>

A quick hello! And thanks to those who ask after me. . . someone cares!

I've been el swamped with work. This is a very good thing! Yay me. I think part of what happened was my newer more confident attitude on how I look right now. I'm at my lowest weight ever, and all my fancy and cool clothes fit amazing. I think it gave me just the right air of confidence I needed to win a big fish this last week.

I look forward to slogging through the posts and catching up with you. After stopping the HCG, my weight stayed the same for 5 days - then I had a 3 lbd increase. Did a steak day, and the next day. . . .exact same weight. No loss. Did a high protien day. . .no loss. . . so now I'm chugging along on day 3 of the newer higher weight and making a point to get the BAB right and not snack.

I hope I can stay at this weight. If it goes up again, I don't know what I'll do! Any ideas?

Finally got my dhea powder in the mail - I need to make the magic cream for stretched skin. . .anybody make it and have any luck? JK mentioned it many times but he hasn't been forthcoming on how to whip up this magical concoction.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-30T04:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-41892>

Tried boating for the first time this a.m. Fun. Rented a kayak at boulder res. Plan on doing that much more.

Also, had some old friends over for grilling on Sat Night. I couldn't contain my opinions and before I knew it had revealed raw fish eating AND CT. . .I clearly had also sprouted 10 heads from the stunned looks I got. Their "omg she's gone nuts lets get out of here" bells were going off.

More of you move to Colorado. I'll give you a discount on real estate services!

### Inger — 2012-07-30T07:57:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-41910>

Cavemam, nice to hear from you! I tell everyone about it now. I was on a cruise a few days ago and I told the men that wanted to talk to me I have raw fish head in my cabin.. haha.. I told them what I eat too and about the smoothie.. I wanted to scare them off but NO. One even asked me to be his wife.. thought I will be a wonderful mother for his children.. OMG (he was divorced). Another guy who came to sit by me was maybe 20 YO and could not believe I was 35.. Sure it was fun but also annoying as I wanted to sit alone and enjoy the live music, not catching men.. uh.

I am not afraid to tell. My brother and his wife in Sweden both wanst to live optimized now, and even tried the mackerelhead-smoothie. It was so fun. She did it all when I was there, also eating shrimps with head and all. I hope they continue when I am gone. I often notice, when I am on someone's side they have no problem to eat the right way but when alone they fall into their old pattern. Breaks my heart as I cannot be everywhere whith everyone always. I need my time alone, alot. In the nature. To be perfectly happy. After all me needs to heal too! But one day I will have much more power, I know! I cannot wait for next spring.. doing lots of CT all winter long and darkness and seafoods and algae. So exiting.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-30T16:10:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-41930>

ok pisser! Up 2.5 lbds today. Going to try steak day again, although it was a flop last time. So now I'm at 213.2. . . That's plus 5.2 for ending the 1st week off the HCG.

Wish I knew how to stop the gain train. Informationoverload is on board. Haven't had any carbs to speak of, or really even too much to eat. I had plenty of good proteins and fats yesterday and was out and active. Minded my circadian cycles and all that.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-30T18:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-41947>

> **Cavemam;42171 wrote:** ok pisser! Up 2.5 lbds today. Going to try steak day again, although it was a flop last time. So now I'm at 213.2. . . That's plus 5.2 for ending the 1st week off the HCG.
>

>
> Wish I knew how to stop the gain train. Informationoverload is on board. Haven't had any carbs to speak of, or really even too much to eat. I had plenty of good proteins and fats yesterday and was out and active. Minded my circadian cycles and all that.

Ignore this if it doesn't fit

Thinking back to your last few days of Hcg, and the ED behaviors that were popping up while using Hcg.... do you think it is possible it might not have done what it was intended to do? I'm thinking... if your body didn't respond the way it's supposed to, and you went into starvation mode as suggested by the Ed behaviors - the mind can play a LOT of tricks on experiencing hunger - then this would look like the rebound from a crash diet.

In that case, steak days, fasting and fat days would be the last thing you want to do.

Sticking hardcore to leptin rx, eating lots and lots of food, accepting the gain as part of healing, and getting your seafood and frosties in would be the thing to do. I know this is entirely opposite of what Hcg suggests. So please ignore if no fit

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-31T00:43:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-41984>

Thanks for the observations - I'm torn on this, if it didn't involve the HCG I would be sure you were right. I am cogitating. I'm going to do a "steak day" today, and if it does NOT make the gain go down then I simply have to regroup.

I know it's all just a number on the scale - but the weight I'm at now is a great zone for me. . . I feel confident and I have some awesome clothes and I just seem to attract good business in the way I don't when I'm feeling I look bad.

It's funny, because I realize many people would be completely despondent if they got to my weight now, and other would be thrilled - self reflection is so relative. I'm trying to stay sane about it all - I feel like I'm not out of control with behaviors. . . but I do want to try everything I can do NOT gain. Logic and biology tells me that at my weight and circumstance, there is no "need" to get larger to get healthier.

At least that's what I'm thinking at this moment - I'll see how I deal with what tomorrow brings.

My "steak" day today has been Coffee throughout the day with HWC and going to do a BApiece of salmon for dinner.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-31T00:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-41987>

> **Anna K.;42173 wrote:** Cavemam, have you tried fasting 1 day a week? may be only eating some Fat (butter or CO) for dinner if you are hungry?
>
> Also, do you lift weights?

Anna - I've been debating the weight training. I've been avoiding over doing exercise, and sticking to my walking and being active - but perhaps it's time. My HS-CRP is .9, in a good range so perhaps I'm being overly cautious.

I've not been doing hitt for same reasons - I think I need to incorporate these things and see how it goes.

I've been doing leptin RX for some time now (5+months?) and fasting isn't a problem, I'm firmly not a sugar burner. I am torn on this one too until I get the LS going. . .as I get how important the BAB is.

SO MANY THINGS TO THINK ABOUT.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-31T16:23:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42043>

Got a grip and decided to apply logic. I read around the googles to see what others experiences were and see what I could glean from that. A common thread in the post HCG world is to "work in certain foods slowly". I did not do that. And in thinking back, perhaps I had too many nuts. I also have a recollection of nibbling a few sweet potato chunks while preparing for others. Also had goat cheese/tomato/pesto... perhaps the cheese and maters did some voo-doo?

Anywhoo, I realized perhaps I am more sensitive to certain foods than I thought? At any rate, going more "clean" certainly won't be a negative to my health.

So salmon day did work sort-of. My weight went back down 2 lbs. . so still a net up - but directionally accurate. I hate the idea of having to "watch what I eat", but here we go. If I don't at least try it, I won't know if it was BS or if I could have gone stable if I had done that.

So I'm cutting all the foods that could be culprits.

Cheese of all kinds, no more goat or parm thinking "it's not so bad".

Nuts

Nightshades (I'm a leaky leaky after all)

No fruits - not even a little raspberry strait from the bush

I'm going to eat the quality proteins ala Leptin RX. I am going to use HWC in coffee and mixing in some foods. I'm going to try and eat more veggies - it's long days and mid summer after all! Not starchy veggies.

I have been eating my 99% chocolate - and this morning opted for just cocoa powder. I can't decide if I should cut it out. . I guess there ARE 14 grams of carbs in half a bar. I know the answer - I should cut it so my experiment is clean.

Last quandry is the Coconut Kevita - I love adding splashes of that into sparkly water, DELISH. Although only 2grams of carbs per bottle. . .could it be causing something? I don't know how.

I want to go 5 days without a gain, then start working back in foods one at a time to see what up.

To me, this type of manipulation feels very ED - I hate hate having to use my OCD powers for food obsession. It's wrong, but there is zero health reason that I can find, that putting on a few pounds when one weighs 211.2 lbds is a good thing.

that's all I have to offer.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-31T19:13:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42075>

Cleaning up your diet sounds like a good idea... not sure it will give you an answer whether or not HCG works. Might just help in and of itself.

> To me, this type of manipulation feels very ED - I hate hate having to use my OCD powers for food obsession. It's wrong, but there is zero health reason that I can find, that putting on a few pounds when one weighs 211.2 lbds is a good thing.

As and outside perspective from an insider (lol, is that possible) you're walking a slippery slope. Whether or not weight gain can possibly be a "good" thing at your size - maybe this needs to be rephrased. Dr K gained weight on the leptin reset at first. Did he need to gain weight or was it "good"? Probably not. Was is necessary and part of the process? It appears it was. All leads back to the question whether or not your body went into starvation mode b/c if it did.... then yes, the weight gain is to be expected and controlling it will increase the problem. I've been through this process so many times.... and I am sure you, too.... it's just not worth it making it worse by trying to prevent it from happening. No matter what size you are. If your brain has gotten a famine message, it will turn on survival mechanisms, no matter if you're 100lbs or 200lbs or 300lbs.

And if I may say.... yes, there's something not quite right with you.... you had gastric bypass and while I don't know much about the effects of it I'm thinking.... you have a shorter digestive tract? We already know you're having trouble absorbing iron. Maybe you're having trouble absorbing more nutrients than you think? Maybe your body is making up for that by increasing food intake? I know the resection is supposed to decrease caloric absorption.... but you can't trick the body like that. In my mind, if you're LR then your body will ask for more food than it will use - no matter how short your bowels are. I'd think it might even get worse b/c of reduced nutrient absorption and that could possibly increase hunger... once the vagus nerve has rewired.

My first point of attack would be cleaning up your diet - as you're already doing

I had to part with the store-bought Kevita. I used to love it and now all I can taste is the ridiculous (in my eyes) amounts of stevia they put in there. I don't think it's a problem if you add it to you water (maybe outside meals it is) b/c the amount is so low - but wow, I tried a bottle last week and ended up dumping it b/c I couldn't stand the sweet taste anymore. Back to my homemade creations

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-31T22:40:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42113>

if you're LS then calories don't matter therefore a few too many shouldn't matter....and not put weight on her b/c she doesn't need to

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-31T23:17:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42131>

Sorry you're bouncing around, Cavemam. I agree that the best course of action is to clean up your diet -- get rid of the common allergens like eggs, nuts, dairy. Then, focus on that BAB and eating a smaller dinner.

To add to the conversation, she can't be in starvation mode. HCG doesn't cause that, especially if she didn't force herself to get to an artificially low weight, which I don't think she did. When on HCG, even if you're not eating a lot, your body is being fueled by calories from your body fat. And, if she didn't experience hunger on the round, I doubt that's the issue.

Sure, it's a sign of ED to focus on the scale and watch the weight, but in order to make the losses stick, cavemam has to be diligent. Steak days work, but if it gets to be where you're doing one every couple of days, then take a look at areas of inflammation and work to reduce those issues.

And, I agree that for many of us, our hunger signals are out of whack, so listening to your body and what it wants is sometimes not the best option. I think until you've gotten past that critical 3 week post hcg mark, you should just play it safe and track things on livestrong or another online calculator. Just my two cents...

Good luck!

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-01T00:02:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42138>

> **Anna K.;42368 wrote: sure, in ideal world, but what if because of her surgery, or whatever else, the hunger mechanism is permanently impaired to some degree and it's not working perfectly, **so conscious calorie restricted is needed? Again, not suggesting starvation, just eat less and see if you are hungry.
>
> **
>
> Again, if something is not working... LR for 5 months....may be it's time to try something else...
>
> **
>
> As for calories, they do matter, to some degree, it's never all or nothing game.

you obviously have never tried that b/c it's not possible to trick the body like that.... you will feel starved at even restricting a couple hundred calories for extended periods of time

as for 5 months on Lrx, I've been on it for 8 months+ now and I have no intentions of stopping - Dr K has explained over and over that some cases take longer to reset.... ED's take longer to reset!

What you are suggesting for calories is not part of Dr K's program. His program focuses on re-joining the messaging between hunger signals and caloric need.... so there will not be a caloric surplus and if there is, it will be taken care of by the body's many ways of doing so. But those means need to function for that to happen. They only work if LS. Cavemam has many indications of not being LS yet.

If there is a case that is permanently broken, there is many better ways to help that person than calorie restriction. Which sucks. For life quality.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-01T03:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42181>

Ok, so the first thing I want to do is thank all you guys for taking the time to comment. I really can't think of a group who's opinions I ever listen to - except for people on this forum. It's so appreciated that you will actually GIVE opinion and try to be constructive helpful with it.

Thanks!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-01T04:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42182>

Starvation yes or no?

I think no. The physical cues and responses on HCG were extremely different than when I've been on VLCD's before. Usually by week 2 or three I could gnaw off my childs arm with hunger. That hunger was missing on the HCG.

So after considering everything, I think I had not gone into starvation mode for most of the diet. I think I was doing the ED behaviors because the weight loss had stopped - which I ended up taking to mean it was time to stop as towards the end I was not seeing the fast results and I was feeling like it was something I could do to change that.

It clearly was not - I probably could have stuck it out a little longer, but I've gotten real in tune with observing that gut feeling on what is right, and it was telling me stop it now.

Ok, on to post HCG.

I hope laying this out here helps someone else - otherwise it's kind of creepy and narcississy.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-01T04:04:12+0300

THIS. I'm with ya on this, thus doing the HCG for weight loss. I was seeing and feeling lots o benefits of leptin RX, and I know that probably the healthiest course of action for me would have been to continue on ala Kruise - but I looked at my bigger picture and decided the biggest net positive would be to drop some poundage.

Of course, I may disagree with myself if I can't keep the weight off! I reserve the right to be wrong - it's a motto, if you are not failing, you are NOT moving forward. I totally believe that.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-01T04:17:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42192>

> **PaleoCowgirl;42383 wrote:** Sorry you're bouncing around, Cavemam. I agree that the best course of action is to clean up your diet -- get rid of the common allergens like eggs, nuts, dairy. Then, focus on that BAB and eating a smaller dinner.

When on HCG, even if you're not eating a lot, your body is being fueled by calories from your body fat. And, if she didn't experience hunger on the round, I doubt that's the issue.
>
>
>
> Sure, it's a sign of ED to focus on the scale and watch the weight, but in order to make the losses stick, cavemam has to be diligent. Steak days work, but if it gets to be where you're doing one every couple of days, then take a look at areas of inflammation and work to reduce those issues.
>

I think until you've gotten past that critical 3 week post hcg mark, you should just play it safe and track things on livestrong or another online calculator. Just my two cents...
>
>
>
> Good luck!

Thanks! I do think I've not been as strict as some people need to be to maintain, and honestly - I don't know why I didn't think that would apply to me. If I've not at least TRIED it as laid out - then I can't really say it didn't work for me. . .so here is me trying to actually be accountable in a logical and reasonable way.

Which reminds me. . . We are kinda at opposite ends of the spectrum, and it hit me - I think although SOME of the behaviors on ED are the same, there are some MAJOR polar opposites between AN/BP and Super Obesity by overeating. . .

One being, the gainers (vs the losers) do kind of an opposite thing, we DO NOT like to see the scale. . we justify that 1-2 lbd a day gain on all kinds of things TOM, a nut, a this, a that. . .and avoid weighing and acknowledging there is anything changing. Funny, that us gainers actually only get bigger. . there never seems to be a 5 lbds TOM LOSS at the end. . it's always going UP. And the disordered behavior is in the ignoring it instead of dealing. For me, it was a big thing to commit to weighing every day and dealing with it head on. For me I think the ED behavior would be to quit weighing and pull out the bigger clothes.

Gainers have such justifications as, the clothes that you are washing in too hot a water - so they shrink. So you buy the next size up. . .and on. Something gainers do is have a MULTITUDE of sets of clothes in different sizes. If we were in the right mental place, we would actually observe and acknowledge a 10 lbd, 20 lbd, 30 lbd gain. . .At some point it's simply NOT a single bite of this or that and it will go away.

The super fat may have a very difficult time LOSING after getting fat and can maintain on lower calories - but make no mistake, for the most part people who get to be over 300 lbds get that way through disordered OVER eating. Now, that isn't to say the impulse wasn't a character fault and it's a brain disorder - but the eating part has to be there.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-01T04:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42196>

Ok, I'm very heartened and inspired by all this conversation on my thread. . and I realized that I am better, I'm not ignoring the weight gain, I'm addressing it. I accept that for me, THIS is the positive behavior - and ignoring it and not addressing would be the Cavemam version of ED. I don't think I would have had this revelation without you guys. Thanks.

After my two gain days, I really did want to just rely on "oh well, HCG didn't work, guess I shouldn't throw out the bigger clothes". I could feel the pull and safety of ignoring it. I am going to try and move on. I am going to weigh every day. Scary. Uncomfortable - but I embrace that.

So I want to look back and see if I can ID patterns in eating and gain/loss. I don't think I've ever done that. Sure, I've done diets and followed strictly - but it's never in combo with weighing myself. I've always had a scare of the scale.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-01T04:56:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42204>

B: Leftover steak, cauli mash that had HWC and kerrygold in it, a sardine and a couple of smoked oysters. 2 coffee's with about 2 tablespoons of HWC and a couple of tablespoons of MCT oil, teaspoon of cocoa powder and some spices.

L: Steak, same cauli mash, coffee with HWC, MCT oil and cocoa powder

Afternoon Snack: 2 slices of an apple, two bites of oatmeal with honey, a few bites of steak and a bite of a salad that had raspberry vinagrette.

Dinner: grilled pork chop, broccoli with kerrygold, salad with 5 pecans, 2 strawberries, tsp of goat feta, and oil/balsamic.

Clearly afternoon snack was NOT on plan. Nor was the dinner salad. I had been sitting on the bed working for 3 hrs as my website/email had been breached and I had to go into tech-save mode. Got the website up, email going and passwords all fixed. I caught it before any damage. Whew. Anywhoooooo - came downstairs to check on the babysitter and son and ended up having all those bites.

The cool thing was I decided, hey it's about 4, I should go on my walk! Not only did I walk for an hour in 98 degree weather - I spontaneously burst into a jog now and then. Really, It couldn't be helped - normally I listen to podcasts, but I thought I would put on some old workout music and well - I'm a girl from the south, dirty naughty southern cowboys get me every time. Kid Rock rocked my world and then I was a jogging.

I did take 200 mgs of the DHA today - wonder if it's related. And my B12 shot.

Another curious thing was my HR barely started moving up - and I barely broke a sweat. The only thing that stopped me from continuing to jog was muscle soreness - I didn't want to have a crazy up moment and hurt myself again!

Caveman, I swell up from HWC. It makes me feel fat and gives me cellulitis.

Not good for me at all... No dairy for me (If I have it I always regret..)

I just visisted my brother for a week and had HWC every day. I could very well notice a gain even if I do not weight. Now after a few days dairyfree I have shrunken back to normal already, eating huge amouts of fish and coconutoil and olive oil and elk meat and avocado and MHS also a few almonds, and lots of wild fresh picked raspberries..

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-01T17:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42263>

Weight 212.2, Up from yesterday, Up 4 lbds total from LIW.

B: Inger coffee, egg yolk, CO, MCT, cocoa, spices, touch of stevia

Cod sauted in ghee and CO and 5 large shrimps. Think it was about 60 grams of protein, lime squirts on top. No veggies.

I could make a salad with the lettuce from my own garden, but it just seems like more trouble than it's worth considering I don't want it. Now, if I could put some olives, feta and strawberries on it then we would be a go

ok, noticed I'm snotty and leaky today after breakfast. Need to blow my nose, I've noticed this sometimes after eating and I've always thought. . oops, HWC or Tomato or something else naughty.

But today? Cod, Shrimp, CO, Ghee, Egg yolk? What could it be?

EGG?! I just realized I was avoiding eggs and then I went and got excited about Inger-coffee. Could I be sensitive to eggs? I've had them my whole life. Ponder Ponder. Tomorrow no egg.

Update: about an hour after finishing eating. Congestion all gone. Curiouser and Curiouser.

Clearly Dr K answered that one. Don't you love it?!

Re: cocoa crack, I've started making mine without Stevia. I'm just getting my taste buds used to no sugar taste at all and it seems to be working fine.

One minor thing that was huge for me when I had my consult with Dr K (and may be helpful to you as you try and negotiate the am-I, am-I-not LS thing) is that he told me the fact I was having ANY cravings meant I was still LR. I've had to up my fat content and be religious about getting enough food at BAB to stop my mind from wandering to thoughts of things like Kevita-type drinks or bites of chocolate or even sugar-free chewing gum.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42492>

MOTHER F-ING MOTHER %\*$&\*#(@

Up another 2, 214.2, 6 lbds over LIW.

I didn't have the HWC yesterday, I did have the one egg yolk - but good gravy. Nothing else I can think of. . . Stevia?! Cocoa Powder?! TOO much CO?

Today I'm going to try and correct. Breakfast/Lunch are going to be:

Tea w a little CO oil in it

Coffee w Kerry Gold

It's supposed to be our "dinner out" tonight this month. I know hubby and son were looking forward to going out to eat with me. . we've got the "hang out in Boulder" thing going on this afternoon with dinner at Shine planned. It chaps me to pay $30 for a 5 oz portion of Salmon and not eat any of the other stuff. It also does not seem right to NOT eat with the Family. We've been planning this night for awile.

Perhaps a switch to Ted's Montana Grill and I can get a big buffalo ribeye. Again, when I'm just eating the slab o meat, restauranting is just annoying.

ok, whatelse yesterday? I was active. . ran around looking at houses, took a 45 min walk, played with T, time between dinner and sleep, lots of good sleep.

F-ING MOTHER F%&$#\*(@ Anybody got a hack?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-02T16:49:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42495>

Getting my zen on. It's nice and sunny out.

Here are my blessings:

1\. It's cleaning day. I run around like mad for an hour to get the house ready - but then I leave the house for all afternoon to hang with the family and come back to a very clean house. This excites me. I grew up in a hoarder type house so whenever my life isn't like that I get excited. My world is such that I get to have a job I love that provides these niceties.

2\. Family. Great Husband and Son.

3\. I have the brains, time and motivation to try and figure this health thing out.

4\. I don't have anything debilitating "wrong" with me. I feel good (albeit pissed as \*\*\*\* today)

5\. I'm putting it out to the world right here - I'm going to start my book today. I didn't want to until I felt I got further along this weight journey, but what the hell. I can tell the first half of my crazy life. I'm not inspirational trust me. It's more of an August Burroughs/David Sedaris kind of thing.

Utterly unscientific here, but...maybe you're overthinking it? Maybe stressing out about gaining is making you gain? Maybe exactly what you need is a "\*\*\*\* the diet, let's just go have fun" meal with your family to unwind and let go of this for a while? I'm not saying like, go to T.G.I. McMurley's and stuff your gob with fried mozzarella sticks, processed cheesecake and beer...but just go to the nice place you want to go, order what you WANT to order, and just enjoy your meal and your family's company. When your mental quality of life is suffering because you're stressing about your diet, doesn't that kind of cancel out any good you might be doing yourself with the diet anyway? Again...not saying use it as an excuse to go hog-wild, but just as an excuse to RELAX.

### Melinda — 2012-08-02T18:48:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42520>

Do you read "Its the Woo?" [http://itsthewooo.blogspot.com](http://http://itsthewooo.blogspot.com) She has an interesting post today with lots of good comments that might apply to your situation. My interpretation of what she is saying and with what you are describing is that you need to be zero carb until you have no cravings or temptations. By zero I mean just meat/fish/fat and low carb veggies. No taste of sweet, even the thought of sweet can cause me to trigger some insulin, not so much anymore in me but I still don't hang around the kids eating fruits. I tell them it might trigger me. I wish you best luck and insights on your journey.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-03T16:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42663>

Down 1.8, steak day did SOMETHING. Weight 212.4, 4.2 lbds up from LIW

I'm going to cut ALLLLLLLLL dairy (even butta, because Inger said. She can eat wormy raw fish so she must know

Eggs

Trying to decide about nightshades - crowdsource this one yaaaaal.

So we cancelled fun family night last night so I could do steak day, and are going to go out tonight instead. Frankly we are old and hate going out on Friday nights. ### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-03T20:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42726>

Doing the IF day is not really a problem for me. I used to do them now and then, but when I started the Leptin RX I quit because I still have leptin issues.

You know, my consulting neurosurgeon keeps pointing out the lack of wisdom in doing the same thing over and over and wanting different results. I feel I'm kind of at a crossroads with that and the Leptin reset. . .perhaps there are some confounding factors that means it's never going to work for me (bypass, lifetime of super obesity) and maybe my best route is some hybrid.

I've always sort of dissmissed the "food sensitivity" as woo-woo and does not apply to me - but I have not tried a true elimination and work back in and methodically analyze what happens with my body as a result of certain foods. So I am giving that a go with dairy and eggs this week.

The thing that keeps sticking in my head is that I actually feel very good and have good energy. I'm not sick or tired or can't sleep or gassy or any of that. I'm simply fat!

Nobody has addressed the question of what happens to all the fat cells over years (10-20 years). . I wonder if they just stick around, are they dying and re-creating themselves? Are they all still there unless you've killed them somehow? I've found lots of answers but no real information on how it works or reason to believe any particular theory. I keep hoping Jack will address this in more details.

I still have some testing to do (Dexa, full hormone panel) so until I've exhausted THAT option, too - I still have stones to un-turn I suppose.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-03T20:18:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42728>

Bfast : Shrimp, lettuce, oil, vinegar, 4 raspberries, liverwurst, bacon, CO in tea.

Got very hungry early and decided on an early lunch.

Lunch: leftovers from bfast and a coffee with cocoa powder, spices, CO and MCT oil., 2 strawberries and 10 macadamia nuts.

Needed a snack - I've decided to stop forcing myself to not eat, perhaps I'm just not absorbing enough food or able to eat enough at BAB to sustain?

Had 4-5 bites of beef, handful of macadamia nuts, 1 sq 99% chocolate.

Dinner: 4 -5 oz piece of salmon and coconut cream kale (had cashews), had small plate apps a piece of handmade pepperoni, bite of pickled salmon, large oyster and a few bites of hubbys pork.

Snack: was very hungry about 1.5-2 hrs after dinner, so had about 4 squares of dark chocolate and a handful of mac nuts. Now I can't go to sleep early. Not like I would - our AC is out! We are setting up camping in the basement as it's cooler there.

A couple

### jib — 2012-08-03T20:46:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42736>

I seem to remember Doc talking about deep CT over a longer time (freezing like Zeltiq only cheaper) actually killing the fat cells and then being reabsorbed and taken out of our system. Where as dieting alone only shrinks them and they can fill back up again. (This was my interpretation of the explanation anyway.)

Have you ever thought about just doing the AI Rx protocol? Sounds like elimination to me.. Something else to ponder..

Yeah, being a yo-yo'er most of my life hasn't done me any favors either...I'm right there with ya, and feeling your pain. I need to try something new too!!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-04T17:44:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42838>

Up, 213.6, 5.4+ up since LIW

No dairy or eggs yesterday. Had plenty of seafood. 2nd day with no BM.

Everything inside, me is saying my weight gains are not related to food intolerances. I want to hear more about these, please everyone share. . is it just neolithic thoughts getting in my way? Before the HCG I ate HWC, eggs, nightshades etc without gains. . .why would it suddenly be such an irritant that I gain 2-3 lbds overnight?

I noticed I was dragging a bit yesterday. I still did stuff, I just didn't have my vim and vigor. . .no way was I about to break into a run. I went and played mini-golf, and walked around boulder I generally try not to "sit" alot. We got some stepping stones, and normally I would be fine lifting and moving those around - but I just didn't feel it, and let husband move them all. Of course it's been real hot - thank god for cold baths!

I could be failing today (which is always a step toward the good right?) but I'm chillaxing and eating foods that give me energy and pep and fuel my life. Going to go do LHT workout at gym. I can do that in about 20 mins while son plays ipad. Think I will also try and hike today. Active but not chronic crazyness.

I think I'll try to just edit one post in the journal for a day so I can look back at what I ate, what I did, and how I think. Future Cavemam - read this and learn!

Coffee with cocoa and HWC

Stuffed bell peppers - ground beef, tomatoes, olives, onion, goat feta

100mg 7-keto DHEA

25mg DHEA

Iron

3 hrs after BAB, no hunger. No congestion after BAB.

Random thought, this BAB had some veggies (bell beppers, tomatoes, onions) could that be influencing the hunger/cravings? I tend to get in the habit of just eating fat/protein as veggies seem like a lot of trouble and I rarely "want" them. I like them when eating out and someone went through A LOT of trouble to make tasty veggies. Of course, I had a salad for Bfast yesterday and that did nothing.

Went and did a 'body by science' style LHT workout. Headed out on a walk/hike with son. Energy fine and up today, feel like getting things done. Probably going to mow and garden some too in between emails/clients.

Lunch 5 hrs after BAB, just starting to get hungry.

about 5-6 oz steak and 1/2 cup or so of probiotic slaw all sprinkled liberally with balsamic.

A bit of leftover cocoacrack. Maybe 2 oz?

1/2 bottle of coconut kevita mixed in with tea, drank liberally while cooking and eating lunch

1/2 Cup o coffee with cocoa powder, spices, HWC, MCToil.

Do I eat alot? Too little? My body has NFI.

5 hrs between lunch and dinner, was starting to get hungry but no cravings or anything like that.

Dinner

1/2 Cup o coffee with cocoa powder, spices, HWC, MCToil. (made a whole cup for lunch and only drank half so drinking the 2nd half while cooking din)

6-8 oz (should I measure?) dover sole sauted in macadamia nut oil, topped with chopped macadamia nuts and lots of lime juice.

Grilled asparagus coated in macadamia nut oil.

1 square 99% dark chocolate

1/2 bottle of kevita

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-04T18:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42846>

> **jib;43015 wrote:** I seem to remember Doc talking about deep CT over a longer time (freezing like Zeltiq only cheaper) actually killing the fat cells and then being reabsorbed and taken out of our system. Where as dieting alone only shrinks them and they can fill back up again. (This was my interpretation of the explanation anyway.)
>
>
>
> Have you ever thought about just doing the AI Rx protocol? Sounds like elimination to me.. Something else to ponder..
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Yeah, being a yo-yo'er most of my life hasn't done me any favors either...I'm right there with ya, and feeling your pain. I need to try something new too!!

Yea, so he pretty much said exactly that - that some form of Cold or surgery was the only way to rid them. . but I'm wondering what that actually means in reality on a person. So if I had 100 extra pounds of fat at age 6, does that mean they are still there? If so those of us who are diet masters (always lose a lot of weight, always gain it back) could be sitting on 1000+ lbds of fat cells empty and screaming.

I am cogitating on the AI stuff - it makes me feel horrid, and I don't seem to have any symptoms of AI stuff. Unless of course FAT is an AI disease. . I also have a confounder that I have had a gastric bypass.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-04T18:43:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-42852>

> **Anna K.;43126 wrote:** Cavemam, I'm just confused a bit.
>
>
>
> On one hand you are saying that you are a fat burner and you can do intermittent fasting with no problem and you did a stake day with only eating some fat till dinner.
>
>
>
> On the other hand, you said that after eating BAB you were very hungry, after eating salmon dinner you were hungry 1.5 hour later. That doesn't sound like a fat burner to me.
>
>
>
> These are such different experience of hunger for you that I'm confused how can this be? May be it's emotional eating? Did you have a stressful day that was causing hunger? Or are you eating because you are a foodie and you just like the taste of food? I can relate to that.
>
>
>
> I hope these observations are helpful, I'm not trying to offend you, I'm just interested in the mechanism of hunger and how it works or doesn't.

You and me both. Good observation - I had not made that connection. It's like eating, no matter what it is starts some kind of process that is self perpetuating. Also, I wonder if my body was trying to make up for the fast day before?

Foodie is me, but I always assumed that was a side effect of being hungry all the time. I wonder if there is some dopamine/seratonin pathway issue?

All your thoughts are welcome. . . .I want to work through this somehow. I did leptin RX and some major CT for a long time - and I was able to change my eating for the good, it did not change my penchant for becoming bigger. I just was a gainer while eating good food.

"fixing hormones" seems like a BS magic bullet to me too - like when I finally get all that done, I'm going to suddenly start dropping weight? Or at least quit gaining? Experience tells me shenanigans.

Of course, on days I go up I'm not exactly the optimist. Making myself weigh and deal each day will surely get easier. This is only about the 3rd week of doing that - got to build some new neural pathways in regard to that I suppose.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-05T16:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43047>

214.8, up 6.6 from LIW. That is a 1.2 lbd gain from yesterday. Scale only seems to go up. Only thing moved it is fasting. I suppose another fasting day. Sadly, fasting/steak days never bring it down below enough so it's always creeping up.

Lifetime of IFing every 3rd day? Seems stupid and crazy. Did I just eat too much yesterday? I have no idea.

It's hard to say is my "lack of energy" related to steak day or because I'm upset? Meeting friends for kayaking this a.m., now my bathing suit is tight and I'm sure I won't fit in the fing kayak. After that going to be very active showing houses and walking around.

I was going to try and look for some clothes for vacation in my new smaller size - but it just seems futile and stupid. Pity party is me.

Ingested today:

green tea with a heaping spoon of CO

coffee with spices a 1/2 tbs of mct oil, 1.5 tbs hwc

More coffee same same as above

a little more coffee with about a tbs of CO about an hour before dinner (while CTing)

Dinner: about 10 oz ribeye and a small apple.

Interesting enough I got a little congested right after eating dinner. . probably unrelated - just a steak and an apple? It went away in about 20 mins.

No hunger during day - NP fasting. When I started to eat it kind of made me sick to my tummy a bit. . actually didn't want to keep eating much. Made sure I ate about 10 oz of the 16oz steak. Decided to do an apple in case it has some magical ingredient?

Took Blood Glucose!!! Went after dinner to get a little kit.

1 hr after eating steak and apple, BG 95

> **Anna K.;43343 wrote:** why does that sound stupid and crazy? Think about it...
>
>
>
> First of all, it's very healthy for you because of autophagy. There is a lot of research on this subject. IF is very primal. I'm doing it once a week, or every 10 days and I don't need to lose weight. I just like the way it makes me feel, it's liberating and it speeds up fat adaptation. I'm planning to do this lifelong, I like it.
>
>
>
> Second, the bottom line is you need to create caloric deficit one way or the other, so that your body uses your own fat for fuel. you can either somehow "fix" your hunger though out the day so that you don't overeat and snack, or you can try doing IF periodically. It seems to me that IF is easier for you, it's working, so why not do this?
>
>
>
> Third, may be doing IF will better your relationship with food and after a while you wouldn't have to do IF anymore. Why does it have to be lifelong? You haven't been doing it long enough and already upset that it will be life long. The fact that you are so opposed to IF seems to point to the fact that you need it because you are a bit obsessed with food. Sorry to sound so harsh, but in my limited experience with my family members, people who need IF the most and have difficulties losing weight, are the ones who oppose it the most and almost cry about it. Not because they are hungry while doing it, no, it's easy to do, but because the idea of not eating makes them sad. It's an eating disorder of sorts. May be what you need to "fix" is not your hormones, but your "relationship" with food.
>
>
>
> anyway, food for thought...

I think my opposition is part annoyance at not being a participant in mealtimes with my family. The other part is my struggle with what is best for my health vs managing my weight. Can gaining weight when I weight this much be the healthy option? That sure doesn't SEEM right.

So, I'm pondering the IFing, Since I'm in the 3 week HCG window - I've been doing the steak day version. . and I've been having fat during the day (3 table spoons or so). You seem to be up on the IF benefits, will doing fat during the day defeat all the benefits of fast? If my body is processing those, then I'm not reaping all the rewards of the fast, right?

Are you doing a total fast? How many hours? I'm totally down with the benefits of fasting - but I've not been doing them since starting this leptin stuff because I think Jack is correct in how things work in our bodies. Biggest concern: Is fasting, with something broken in me (as evidenced by the weight) a longer term net damage? I used to run 5K's and take boot camps and eat 1200 calories a day at 220 lbds and that clearly was a net negative over time, although it kept my weight at 220 lbds for awhile. I can't see the forest, there are too many trees in the way. I appreciate your thoughts!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-06T01:05:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43133>

> **Anna K.;43396 wrote:** Eating fat during the IF day doesn't defeat the purpose of IF, protein or carbs will though.
>
>
>
> The way I do IF - I eat a big late breakfast, coffee w/Butter for lunch and nothing for dinner. If I'm very hungry, I may eat a table spoon of butter with or without coffee around the dinner time. Next time I eat normal meal is the next day breakfast. So it ends up being about 24 hour fast. Sometimes I also work out in the morning before breakfast. I used to be very afraid of working out before breakfast because of dr. K, but I tried it after reading Barry's blog here:
>
> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?305-Fasted-State-Training-Adaptations](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?305-Fasted-State-Training-Adaptations)
>
> and it works great for me. So I don't think there is anything wrong with fasted state workouts for most people. It speeds up fat-burning adaptation and is great for leaning out.
>
>
>
> So you see, you can have breakfast with the family and just skip lunch (you could be working anyway) and skip dinner doing something productive or something fun for yourself and hang out with the family after. If you do it once a week it's not that bad.
>
>
>
> Also ,some days are easier to fast naturally, so I try to go with the body feel for it. I try not to be too rigid about it, but I plan for it. If I had a really big meal the night before, I plan to fast the next day because I know it will be easy. If it doesn't work out this day for some reason, i'll try for the next day.
>
>
>
> I don't think you can compare fasting with 5K runs and low calorie bootcamps. I think IF is good for you, unlike chronic starvation. I hate running, but I love sprinting. I know that dr K. said not to IF until you are LS, but how do you really know if you are? may be you are LS, but LR only on the liver level? may be you are almost there? there is no objective way to measure it. you've been on the reset for almost 5 months, it must have made you somewhat LS, it can't be completely not working. So may be IF will work for you now and may even make you more LS.
>
>
>
> Intermittent fasting was also shown to decrease neuropeptide-Y, a hormone that stimulates hunger. IF improve insuline sensitivity. Those are the 2 benefits I've noticed.
>
>
>
> The only way for you to know if it works for you is to try it for a few months, to IF once a week, for example, or once every 5 days. And closely monitor you weight, your eating habits, your energy levels, your food intake the next day, your hunger, etc.
>
>
>
> Best,
>
> Anna.

Thanks Anna,

So, I just added in LHT yesterday - and I will add in HIIT this week. . .I do have muscle soreness from LHT - so I'm thinking let that abate then do a HIIT workout. For me, that will probably be sprinting. So somewhere in the ball park of LHT 2X a week with a HIIT every 10 days or so I think.

Also, as today I'm fasting - I have been observing and It's not difficult for me to do. So that will be 2X this week that I IF'd for about 24 hrs with some fat thrown in. Both times have been skipping L/D and drinking coffee or tea with fat at those mealtimes. I've been timing the IF days with the gains on the scale. Like today I was out and about all day, so it made it a good day to IF as I wasn't going to have meals with fam anyway.

I'll have steak tonight. Reading right now on Blood Glucose and tracking that. And sitting in a cold tub. Our A/C is out and it's almost 100 degrees today!

### LinD — 2012-08-06T04:27:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43155>

Cavemam, how many days was your hCG round? Grammy (Colleen) advocates a longer round over a shorter round for stabilization purposes.

Do I remember that IF is suggested, but only once your LS? I could be wrong....

I'm sorry P3 is not going smooth. Due to gains, I had to start over from the beginning and slowly add in new things one item every three days... I hated that, but otherwise it was a guessing game.

Today is first day of TOM, and I am currently almost 5# over LIW, but it started to be a problem in January---about 8 or 9 weeks into P3 and life got crazy/stressful..... M-I-L died in Jan, DH had back surgery in Feb (after months of awful pain), started house renovations in April, put house in market, sold it, and now temporarily moved--until new house is finished Sept/Oct. Oh, and a weird mouth thing the DDS wanted to do a root canal, but large doses of Vitamin C keeps the prob away. Steak days didn't help me that much, either, so stopped trying those.

I am usually about 4# over LIW and try to accept that. After all, I'm still smaller than one year ago.

Best wishes to you.

In real world application, the days I've done "steak days" have been fairly easy to do. Yesterday my energy was good. The one before that it was a little slower. No emotional ups/down or any other signs that I am NOT buring fat for fuel

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-06T17:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43211>

End of 2nd week of P3 of HCG Round.

211.8, Down 3 lbd from yesterday, 3.6 lbds up from LIW (tmi but I need to know when looking back had a MBM before weighing).

Was verrrrry tempted to IF and steak day it again today. I'm sure tomorrow will go up from the BM alone.

Trying something new today. Having my protein upon waking, but not a BAB. I've been thinking a lot about if I'm storing more of the larger meals than utilizing them, due to bypass. Jack if your reading - is this even a possibility? I really need to figure out how all that works. Can't find much info on the googles.

Bfast: Coffee, HWC, Cocoa powder, spices, mct oil, 3oz or so of leftover sausage.

Hungry at about 3 hrs after starting bfast. BG 80. Thinking of food.

Snack: three hrs after starting sab, green tea w/ CO oil. (after claiming I have no bypass issues - this great amount of CO (4 tbs about) gave me a fat dump! I think I ended up losing all my lunch thus causing hunger between lunch/dinner. hehe my hope is since I cleaned my pipes that will help with lbds off for tomorrow. I know I know, no bitching on how insane and wrong that is. Don't hate me because I want to be pretty!

Lunch: Sole, shrimp, tomatoes, onion, garlic, bone broth, fresh herbs all in a stew. 1p, so CO worked for hunger - plus friend stopped by and lunch delayed. One hour after lunch BG 98

Snack: Hungry again about 2 hrs after lunch. After spending a.m. learning about digestion after bypass decided to have coffee with bullet proof protein mix. Starting to think snacking may be something I need.

Dinner: 5 meatballs (onion, garlic, anchovies, coconut flakes, seasonings and gelatin), about 1/2 cup of spaghetti squash and about 1/2 cup of tomato sauce (homemade). Should not have had the coffee w/protein - it ended up making me delay dinner cooking because I wasn't hungry. Probably should have just started cooking and having dinner closer to 5 than 6. So now bedtime no earlier than 10. BUT, a/c is working now so I'm expecting a nice cool sleep! Forgot, also had 1 strawberry 2 raspberries and a black berry.

Fasting BG, 77

30 mins after drinking coffee and eating sausage, 88

1 hr after 84

3 hr after 80 - feeling hungry, 3 hrs after starting sab.

1 hr after lunch 98

Noticed the sniffles after eating sausage! Same congestion as last night at dinner. Only thing in common perhaps is spices? I used a jeanie-awesome spice rub on my steak, but sausages were pre-made (the good stuff with actual ingredients ftw).

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-06T20:05:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43247>

In case you are a bypass person and want some info. . I came across this paper that has some good nuggets.

[http://www.lapspecialists.com/gw\_publication-\_physiology\_after\_gastric\_bypass.pdf](http://http://www.lapspecialists.com/gw_publication-_physiology_after_gastric_bypass.pdf)

Actually, this is a great explainer for anybody with an interest in what happens to food once it goes inside the body.

Summary:

The part of the stomach that normally processes protein is bypassed.

Due to the severing of vagal nerve the "stuff" that is normally secreted to help with protein also cuts way back. With me, Jack says vagal nerve has grown back - so one can hope that at least the secretions are upregulated to normal levels. All of this means much of the protein molecules remain intact and too large to be processed even later on in the small intestines. Protein deficiency is something to watch out for.

I don't have any symptoms of protein deficiency, so my thinking is perhaps I need to make sure to eat MORE protein? I need to figure out if I need to eat smaller portions more often. Perhaps I need to let go of the leptin reset stuff and think about my special needs in regard to best nutrition? Perhaps although 3 meals no snacks is best for most - it might be counter productive to me?

Similar to protein, the carb absorption is reduced as well, for the same reason. The initial breakdown of food happens in the part of the intestines (duodenal) that was bypassed. So perhaps I need to have a little bit of carbs to prevent the protein I do eat from being turned in to glucose, as protein absorption and utilizing those proteins should be towards the top of my eating hierarchy?

Guess what - same with fats. The portion bypassed is key to fat absorption. Fat doesn't form the spheres/groups needed and can end up in the colon undigested and pooped out (steatorrhea).

REALITY CHECK: This all sounds awful, but over I don't have - and didn't have, many of the side effect symptoms. No dumping from carbs and fat, no protein deficiency and no oily poo.

There also seems to be a correlation between all the nutrient issues and how "skinny" someone gets. In that regard, I'm in good stead - the operation didn't exactly work wonderful for me. AND, looks like that could end up being a very good thing for my version of optimal.

Clearly, maximizing nutrition and absorption is something I should be doing. Perhaps that explains run-away hunger even when I'm eating well? My body isn't absorbing it like most. It does make me wonder if trying to eat a couple of big meals a day is optimal for me.

Will I have more nutrient absorption eating several smaller meals through the day?

Perhaps I need a staggered program of grazing/fasting. I wonder would my ideal day look like this:

Continue to mind circadian stuff. No eating after dark and 4 hrs before bed. But upon waking, eat protein and fat in the a.m., add in a few carbs later in the day. Try to eat as many nutrient dense food - but in small quantities until it's stop time for eating. Every week or so, do a 24 hr fast as long as energy is up and feel good.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-07T02:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43320>

Cavemam, this is great info, great food for thought. I bet it's a different effect between the different wls surgeries though. For instance I had a DS duodenal switch so fats are more of a problem for me to absorb. But I do think I have some protein issues. But someone that had a RnY would be slightly different.

I am down 150 pounds from my highest weight so I guess it 'worked' for me, but I am by no means a normal weight. That is the frustrating part for me. So it still left me fat but also deficient.  I'm kinda bummed.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-07T02:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43324>

I'm about the same on the weight loss. Lost a lot, but still "fat". I would read the paper, its great seeing how things normally work, and then after bypass - there are some observations about DS vs RNY in there, particularly pertaining to the fat thing.

I've been struggling with only 3 meals, but doing it for a long time on the reset - I will try and talk with Jack about this at my next consult. What I don't understand is: Will the same amount of nutrients be absorbed if eaten at only 2-3 meals per day, or will we absorb our food better with grazing/small meals. I completely get how it's important NOT to snack, and to give our bodies the rest between meals - but at some point it's rock and hard place. I'm not trying to be difficult, I will do what I think is right - but I wonder if trying to only eat 3 meals is hurting absorption with WLS folks. I have had consults with him, and he didn't say anything about altering the RX for the bypass so I could be over thinking this completely.
