### PrimalPam — 2012-11-04T18:40:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62028>

> **Cavemam;63061 wrote:** I would love if more and more people would weigh in on their level of pleasure from old happy foods.
>
>
>
>
>
> Those of you that have binge eating issues (interestingly, I don't think I do), what I read isn't that the foods are so pleasurable, that it's a host of other things going on - but I would love to hear from you on this. Bingers comment?

Hi, my name is PrimalPam, and I'm a binger.

I had never tied this together, but after reading Dr. K's blogs about concussions, the timing of my binge-starting became clear: I had a really bad concussion when I was 13, unconscious for 3 days and to this day no memory of the accident or the day prior (I had gotten braces put on my teeth and was on my way home; I have no recollection of being in the orthodontists' chair). As a kid, I was normal weight/thin/active, ate tons of processed foods (it was the 70's; Rice Krispies were a staple). I don't recall ever bingeing prior, but all throughout my teens and up to now (I'm 50), I've binged.

I now firmly believe the start of my bingeing had at least something to do with my brain injury...but that's a whole 'nother topic.

I've been lowcarb, then paleo, now epi-paleo, for a total of about 3 years. And I still have binge episodes. I still suffer from the food addiction. For me, it's sweet, cake-y things, ice creams, etc. Tons of sugar. I can remember as a young teen, couldn't yet drive to get binge-food, and nothing binge-y in the house, I'd sneak spoonfuls of sugar. Maybe 1/4-1/2 cup at a time.

The only reason I never became super-obese was vanity: Binge-starve, repeat.

I thought...oh, I'll go lowcarb, and the cravings will go away! OK, I'll add fat, lots of healthy CO, and they'll go away...ok, now eating seafood....you get the point, the cravings, and the thoughts that go along with those cravings, still exist.

If I drink just 1/2 glass of red wine, the cravings come back. Or eat a small piece of birthday cake, and I'm driving around town, looking for ding-dongs.

I know, after all I've read here, that this just means I'm still really broken.

I hate seafood. Have hated it all my life. Canned tuna was as close as I get to the sea. I now eat it at least 5 times a week, and I'm sorry to say, I still don't love it. Heck, just last night, I had dinner with friends at a Tex-Mex restaurant, and enviously eyed my ever-thin friends' enchilada plates and tostadas while I ate my shrimp and avocado salad.

I recall Dr. K. saying (I'll paraphrase, 'cause I'd never find the actual post), when replying to a poster who said the thought of eating something made them ill (maybe raw seafood?): "I don't always eat everything because I like it. I eat it because I know what it does for me". Again, paraphrasing. I think of that quote every time I sit down to order, or make myself a meal. And yes, I have to think of it a lot. Last night, as everyone was drinking their margaritas and eating their chips, I just had to drink my mineral water and wait for my salad, because I KNOW if I took even one bite of one chip, it'd be over.

What Dr. K. wrote about how the foods don't even show up on his FMRI fascinates me, and I certainly hope to get to that point.

Do I wish Ding Dongs had DHA and Omega-3's? Abso-freaking-lutely.

We are such a now-now-now society, and I want to be thin and healthy NOW. I do this as much for vanity as I do for my health, and I believe in this so strongly, as I've definitely seen changes. And I KNOW, intellectually, that this didn't happen overnight. This started when I was given birth by a smoking mother who soy-formula-fed me. It's been 50 years...I need to give it a few more months.

### Lyndra — 2012-11-04T19:26:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62034>

> **ealachan;63686 wrote:** I do a killer mac and cheese with cauliflower instead of pasta. It does still have dairy in it though, so I guess it's not really paleo. It's wicked tasty, though!

Oooh, One Girl Cooks recipe ????

### janagram — 2012-11-04T19:30:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62037>

That sounds sooo good! Better google one girl cooks!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-04T19:44:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62042>

omg so much good stuff, and I'm running around working today trying to sneak in forum reads. Hmmmm, 500K house sale or respond on forum. . . .sophies choice!

### Inger — 2012-11-04T21:22:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62070>

I think I am... imaging a lot. I wonder if that has anything to do with it making it easy.

I imagine "unconsciously" that the raw liver, the fishhead smoothie.. the seaweeds, oysters.. all the foods I know are superfoods, I imagine them strongly connected with what I will one day be. I know what I want to achieve. Where I am going. It is weird it has maybe not always really a clear "shape" what I am imagineing, but it is very strong and it is, evolving. I know it is there...! The foods are very strong tied together with what I want to achieve. This makes them amazing to me... I am not sure fishead smoothie will ever taste good.. lol.. but raw hearts, liver, oystesr, fish etc. They really taste good to me, especially when I make some nice recipe out of them. But most also just plain. No need to make anything fancy.

Now.. this does not happened over night.

It have taken many years. So patience is needed. I am also not making too much pressure on myself, I take my time. I still drink HWC at times in my coffe at work. It does me NOT good. But I never buy it myself. I am also not lying to myself telling me HWC is good for me. I know my body swells from it and my skin gets not so pretty. I am giving me time to wean off it. I am not beating me up if I do something not too perfect. But I rarely do, except of the HWC. But I am feeling so good, that might be why I am not 100% strict.. if I would not feel so good I might be even stricter. I am going to leave the raw eggyolks too now, for the winter. And the occasional HWC for sure! I like challenges. I like to see what changes, when I change stuff. It is exiting to me. There is always something back to enjoy for sure!  I have a healthy appetite and enjoy very simple food too very much.

### Inger — 2012-11-04T21:36:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62073>

I just come home from work. My collegues are quite intrested in my ways of living, they ask a lot and today one guy tried my raw, plain reindeer meat I had with me for lunch..lol. He wanted to try one of the 4 live oysters I had with me too! He said he really wants to start to live like me because he wants to be as healthy as me too! I have told them about my hormone-healing and they always ask.. Inger, how are your hormones..? LOL Now they want to get better libido too..lol..and they ask me all the time what they can do.. why I use sunglasses after work and such and I tell them always. They think I have a amazing energy and that I am weird..lol but in a good way as a coworker said to me yesterday. I have much fun at work. IDK but my mood is so good almost always! I wonder why but I think it is all the pieces together... And when one has lots of energy and feels so light it makes fun to work for sure!

### Inger — 2012-11-04T21:44:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62074>

Cookies and croissants and such will always taste good to me for sure. Do I care? No not one bit. I know where those things brought be. I will never go back. It is not what I want.

I am going with the motto;

**I know about the bad, but I look at the good.**

Like in the video from the old woman that survived Auschwitch. She said that was her motto too. I loved that because that is how I think too. I love how beautiful it makes life...

I am not spendig one second thinking about the croissants. Not one second. I ignore what I want not.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-04T22:02:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62075>

The brain injury happens first........then things go south. That injury can be genetic or epigenetic but generally most are epigenetic in humans. Once you wrap your head around this then you can come up with a plan to go after rebuilding what got broke. It is a slow steady climb back but it can be done. Along the way you will notice many things that you did not realize when you were headed into your rut or place. Those beacons help you realize something about the descent and hopefully teach you a lot in your ascent back......so you never have to go back. Those who cant see those things usually have pretty massive neural network damage and will just need more persistence and diligence to get there. Everyone's mileage varies for this reason and some others......like where your epigenetic switches are set now.

### PrimalPam — 2012-11-04T23:49:06+0200

### PrimalPam — 2012-11-04T23:58:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62094>

> **PrimalPam;63805 wrote:** just last night, I had dinner with friends at a Tex-Mex restaurant, and enviously eyed my ever-thin friends' enchilada plates and tostadas while I ate my shrimp and avocado salad.

OK I feel so stupid quoting myself...LOL...

The thing is, all my friends are thin. I'm the fattest of everyone I know. They all drink, eat, whatever they want, while I constantly am looking for answers.

BUT....last night, a big topic of discussion was doctors. And health. And I kept completely QUIET, because they were all talking, kind of at the same time (a bunch of women, ya know?). Each one had sleeping pill issues...is this one right, they've had side effects from that one. Each one had anti-depressant and anti-anxiety med issues...again, side effects, etc. And they were talking about doctors who could get you in immediately to "prescribe something when you have a cold or flu", and they were all storing these doctors' numbers in their phones.

I just sat there, poking at my shrimp, thinking...well, I still crave Ding Dongs, but I haven't so much as had a headache in 5 years. Literally. The only "medical" type items in my house are band-aids and thermometers. These days, I sleep through the night, so a sleeping pill is beyond my comprehension. As are anti-anxiety, anti-dep. meds, because I feel calmer, more peaceful. And then it hit me....FINISH the damn shrimp.....

### PaulaRichards — 2012-11-05T01:51:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62106>

I have to tell you Caveman I've been trying not to "smell from the pumpkin" as you put it. The treats are everywhere and since I have binge issues it would be a slipery slope for moi. There would be no n=1 with a small bag of chip and a little chocolate. Nope. There would be a significant dent made in that bag of treats. During my last "slip-up" 3-4 weeks ago I did find out that milk chocolate now tastes disgusting to me! I seriously thought there was something wrong with the bar, it was my old favorite and I couldn't eat it. Thanks to all the 90% dark stuff I've been eating I guess I rewired that taste bud! I wish I could somehow do that with ice cream-my true binge addiction!

### janagram — 2012-11-05T02:10:36+0200

paulalynn, I have the same drop-in-my-tracks addiction to ice cream. the thing is, I can make the most amazing one in my vitamix!! It may not be epipaleo but its at least primal! It's frozen pineapple chunks with coconut milk and HWC plus stevia!!!!! You pick the amounts......

And...Ealachan, I'm trying your idea tonite...primal mac n cheese! (cauliflower)!!!

Probably I'll go through color phases of stages of my life where I felt a lack. When I was a kid, I couldn't fit into kid clothes, and didn't have the brightly colored dresses and tights and whatnot the other girls did. I was stuck with boys "husky" from sears. OH THE HUMANITY.

I figure now is my bright color stage to fill in the gap from kindergarten. When my son is in High School I'll be an almost 60 yr old woman dressed like madonna. GET INTO THE GROOVE BABY!

I can already hear the "mom, park down the street" coming.

Toughskins. Lol. I had to wear those b/c my dad got a discount b/c he worked for the corporate side of Sears & I had big muscular thighs! Good thing for the discount cause those toughskins didn't last more tan 2-3 Washings w/how I wore them.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-05T23:53:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62267>

> **DarleenMB;63682 wrote:** So how was the bread? I've got the recipe just haven't made it yet.

I liked it better than the almond flour breads for "bready" purposes. It was fairly bland, but that could easily be remedied. I thought adding a little parm cheese and roasted garlic would be nice - or a bunch of fresh herbs etc.

It sliced beautifully, and presented so well.

Doesn't fall apart like the other nut breads. . .and mac nuts should be better for you than almonds at those quantities for the most part.

It was a high-dish-scenario, cleaning the food processor etc was a big pain. I would make it for special things like I did, but it doesn't really add anything awesome to the meal. I might do it again to use for a picnic or breakfast sandwiches to take on a trip. . .the special thing. It's too expensive and high maintenance for everyday use imho.

I think it would be a PERFECT transition thing to have when people are switching over to paleo. You know when you go through the phase of having substitutes for old favorites. I think most of us outgrow that after a while, but this could make a great transition or company/holiday product.

it didn't toast like toast. But was superduper awesome hot with butter. Good way to up fats.

The last thing, it's a highly process food - and I think those mess with you somehow, so probably not the best choice for every day.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T16:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62511>

Well I did it, a mostly paleo party with no apologies or warnings to guests. Nary a chip in sight. Not one pastry. There was some white wine for my "losing weight the CW friend" but dear lord I'm ashamed, I served it with a little secret happyness knowing it wasn't helping her cause. ::SHAME::. Clearly I have some work to do on the enlightened-wise-person front.

Nobody IRL has a bead on my politics, but they certainly knew I was one of the few attendees that were firmly in the 'NOTOBAMA'camp. So when the writing was on the wall, there was genuine merryment and taunting of me. Which I love - so I felt it was a nice evening because people felt comfortable enough to poke the hostess with the mostess.

The setting was very gratifying too. We did do a big fire and sit around out in the cold listening to CNN and talking all kinds of actual issues + the new real housewives season.

so jealous.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T17:15:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62534>

> **ealachan;64370 wrote:** \*gasp\*
>
>
>
> so jealous.

And somehow I call my self a minimalist all the while amassing a Martha Stewart like kitchen and all the tools and appliances JUST IN CASE one day I have 50 friends and they wall want to be entertained by me on a regular basis.

Cognitive Dissonance runs deep here. I'm a major introvert who doesn't really like crowds. I don't actually "entertain" for the most part. I certainly don't have friends over often. ABSOLUTELY hate parties with people other than my closest friends. Mostly I just envision my powder blue lady's of the 50's dress and a coverup knitted by jail-martha while I sip martini's and pass around apps.

We all need a vision to strive for!

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T17:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62538>

I am firmly in the Alton Brown camp when it comes to kitchen stuff - if it's a uni-tasker, I don't want it. I need to be able to use an item for at least two different things that I do REGULARLY, or I reject it. Unless it's insanely cute and/or totally crazy...in which case, I cherish it and show it to everyone who sets foot in my kitchen.

50's style cocktail parties fascinate me. I'd love to host one, but our apartment is pretty small and my dog is bat\*\*\*\* crazy around people he doesn't know, so my Martha-esque dream will likely never come true.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T17:43:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62548>

>
>
>
> 50's style cocktail parties fascinate me. I'd love to host one, but our apartment is pretty small and my dog is bat\*\*\*\* crazy around people he doesn't know, so my Martha-esque dream will likely never come true.

I don't know why but my happy button gets pushed at that idealistic 50's housewife crap. For the longest time I had a retro pink kitchenaid coffee grinder. Beautiful work of art IMHO. I covet appliances in the candy kitchen aid colors of the 50s. Big farmhouse sink, pie safe for all the pies I don't bake. . .that kind of thing.

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T17:53:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62555>

> **Cavemam;64386 wrote:** I don't know why but my happy button gets pushed at that idealistic 50's housewife crap. For the longest time I had a retro pink kitchenaid coffee grinder. Beautiful work of art IMHO. I covet appliances in the candy kitchen aid colors of the 50s. Big farmhouse sink, pie safe for all the pies I don't bake. . .that kind of thing.

I don't get into the decorative asthetic so much, but for some reason the idea of cooking a bunch of goodies and then getting totally over-dressed and having a bunch of over-dressed friends over to sip ridiculous cocktails and eat tasty food just really appeals to me. I have a secret inner girly-girl that very seldom sees the light of day, apparently.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T20:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62629>

3 testosterone tests, all same lab, range for all three was 7-46.

1\. 8/23, had been taking DHEA 50 mgs, and 7-KETO 100mgs for about two weeks.

Total Testosterone 152. Stopped regular DHEA that day.

2\. 9/7, still taking 7-KETO 100mgs for a little over a month at this point.

Total Testosterone 315. Stopped 7Keto that day.

3\. 11/1, DHEA supps free.

Total Testosterone 28

I also re-checked Vit-D after a bio-hack. That went from

8/23, 21 Vit D, 25-OH, Total.

11/1, 77 Vit D, 25-OH, Total.

### ealachan — 2012-11-07T20:23:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62631>

So, in light of your vitamin D experiment, I'm curious - were you taking the DHEA and 7-Keto orally, or some other way?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T20:55:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62639>

> **ealachan;64470 wrote:** So, in light of your vitamin D experiment, I'm curious - were you taking the DHEA and 7-Keto orally, or some other way?

Reasonable question! Oral on the Dhea stuffs. the more "fat soluble" something is, the less it works on me. I'm guessing IF the dhea was the culprit\* it doesn't need as much fat to absorb.

Also, lab errors have been suggested - but what sent me to the test in the first place was the symptoms (hair falling out) and those do seem to have slacked off. In fact, I actually did something social last night - which was part of what made me think the Testosterone had fallen and decide to get it tested again. I don't have placebo gene's, so I'm 98% that isn't in play with me.

\* The other working theory on what it could be other than DHEA is a pituitary tumor. I'm hoping to turn that possibility into some insurance covered IGF-1 testing that I can use for testing the effectiveness of my intermittent fasting.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T22:47:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62670>

My triage just got easier. Vit-D fixed. Iron improved (still want injections but what can ya do). Vit B12 fixed.

So now my order of things to work on. . .

P/E ratio

50 lbds

That's it. Really. Although I've not had IGF-1 tests, I forsee being able to IF as a lifestyle change if I can jigger the best way for me.

I ate raw salmon for lunch yesterday, shrimps for lunch today, and making seafood chowder for dinner. Still not liking it much, but I'm powering on.

Cavemam, I appreciate so much what you are sharing. I appreciate it so very much! I love Nr1;s.

Thank you! You got me to think about what amazing things our vaginas are..lol I love to be a woman...! I guess that is why one feels so good after unprotected sex too. IDK but it is somehow magic... almost. Must be someting in the men's sperm that is so good for us. Too sad to have no partner. But you have. You are one blessed woman Cavemam..!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T23:22:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62681>

> **Jack Kruse;63795 wrote:** I like your ideas Cavemam but I am not sure you are really disagreeing......i think your post describes the in details of personal context. or N-1. Each brain is wired differently.

\*\*\*\*please note, all references to Inger in the following post are not about the wonderful girl Inger. They are about the idealized notion of Inger that we each have based on our worldview. My idealized Inger is a woodland nymph running around naked in the woods eating shrooms and berries and catching fish in her mouth after stunning them with her mermaid fin. I'm sorry if I make the real Inger uncomfortable, but it's really Jack's doing as he has used Inger as a public example more than once.  \*\*\*\*\*

We are not in disagreement at all. I am countering because I think the words say N-1, but the actions (and now and then posts on this forum) say something different. And so many people hang on your every word as gospels from a deity - I like to help people understand not just follow.

Since my POV and experience is different than the MAJORITY - I feel it's important to explore warts and all, even if I end up wrong. Perhaps it's even more important and meaningful to those who run into very very large hills when trying to be Jack or Inger. Those that see success have success as their guide and inspiration - the rest of us need some company in our misery every now and then.

For some, one way to move onward and upward is to step down a few other roads to find our path to the destination. Yet the forum can lean towards projecting the idea that there is one big well lit path and if you are not improving from it - you are not doing it hard enough. To me that's identical to spending MORE time on the treadmill because the 2 hrs a day you were not doing wasn't working.

I like the idea of everyone having flashlights and shining them all over the place, rather than following one well lit path. Admittedly I'm an instigator like that.

I do realize that my personal example and path is so far removed from the others, due to mechanical issues - my N-1 is damn irrelevant to anybody here. However, my point in sharing is to illustrate and testify that sometimes N-1 success isn't to sit and feel forever broken because you don't have the holy spirit when you have a raw fish head, but instead to encourage to keep look for your blissful raw fish head or realize you are not going to have bliss, and dealing with that and moving on. I still don't understand what is harmful in acknowledging when something sucks. I didn't say wallow, simply acknowledge.

I hope my thinking of counter examples, and alternate theory helps someone who is getting nowhere doing exactly what they think they should be yet facing failure. I didn't learn the value in failure until very late in life, so I want to serve by helping others fail a lot.

I do mean to counter balance "just do it!" enthusiasm. That reminds me so much of the lifelong athlete encouraging me to finish that 10th kilometer. It reminds me of it, because it's the same. People can be so happy and enthusiastic and want to help, particularly when they have an answer, it's easy to forget it's not very motivating to others when it's not their answer and the subjects experience is dismissed. It's also easy to deliver the message with the implied meaning of you are in error and your experience is irrelevant.

I know there are many more than not who are uplifted by the messages and the way they are presented by the great examples we have here - but to project that is the ONLY way is the opposite of N-1, and gives a cognitive dissonance. The specifics of any one persons N-1 are simply too complex to fit into a singular RX made my someone else.

Since you have a journal section, and it implies that one can testify to their own thoughts, feelings and N-1, I feel the need to offer another view when I see a drift on this site, in the platitude direction. JUST EAT MORE FISH! JUST TAKE COLDER BATHS! JUST DO SOMETHING HARDER AND MORE! Implying just ignore all evidence to the contrary and all of your N-1 signs. Thus this too long manifesto (thanks for reading this far if you did!).

I found out long ago, at dentist appointment gone bad, that you simply must own it when you are different. (red head mutation effects certain anesthetics).

So although I think when you boil it down, I'm repeating exactly what you are. N-1 for the win.

Cavemam this is by far one of the most amazing things I've read. Wow! I think you just wrote Jacks blog on paying attn to yourself and what your body's doing b/c it is the only thing that matters. Everyone's n=1 is important but the only one of value to you is yours! Awesome just freaking awesome

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-07T23:47:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-62697>

> **PrimalPam;63805 wrote:** Hi, my name is PrimalPam, and I'm a binger.
>
>
>
>
> Welcome Pam!
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> The only reason I never became super-obese was vanity: Binge-starve, repeat.

Thanks for sharing! I love hearing your story. However, I would like to plant a little seed for those who are super obese.

What if, it's not really about vanity, and vanity and superior self control had little to do with why you didn't become super-obese. This is a mindset that so hard for people to wrap their thoughts around. Frankly, until I really got it - I was stuck in a self-hate kind of space because I thought I wasn't vain enough to stop the fat train. Now although I don't look it, I realize I'm pathologically vain and that has no bearing on my weight.

Binge disordered eating is simply not the same as the overeating that comes with ones body putting on fat way into the super-obese category.

One personality marker of someone with binging as well as anorexia type disordered eating is that the concept that you are having a binge episode because of loss of will power or loss of discipline, thus the moments between binges are due to you being awesome and having discipline and control when you are not binging. Notice all the "high strung" bingers and adrenal fatigue and other co-problems that come with those control issues. Hunger signals are tied to all kinds of DIFFERENT things than with someone who is super obese.

For someone who is super obese, their body is simply in "MUST STORE FAT" mode. The reasons for this are not well understood and seem to be QUITE variable. They eat because their brains are putting all energy towards storing fat to stay alive. Someone who is super-obese, their body and brain will die if they don't store the fat. The body and brain that needs to store fat or die will eat anything and everything it can. There are not "triggers", there are not certain foods that cause a downward spiral. . they are being directed to keep eating period to fuel the need to stay alive.

### Inger — 2012-11-07T23:59:23+0200

Look Cavemam. I am built like this. I do not care one bit what you do to acieve success, the only thing that matters to me is, you do...! I so enjoy that people are happy and not suffering, I do not care one bit how they come to their Nirvana...lol

OMG I think I had too much wine by now.

IDK.. I opened this bottle of Malbec because everything was getting too much. I do not know what to do with my ex moving in here.. I am toally lost. But hey, I am not yet in despair. I know the answer will come to me soon. I just need to be patient and I will know what to do.

Huh. Patience. Listen. Not my strength so far.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-09T16:52:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-63125>

207.2. No variation in body weight nor body fat %. With my history, one could consider that no gains ARE a success however.

Probably the first time in my life I've been eating ad libitum without major gains.

Stable for 2 months now.

Health markers seem good. Need to test inflammation and some of the basics again, it's been 6 months - but I don't think I've got issues in those departments.

The pattern I'm in now - I would say these are averages:

IF 14 hrs a day

Break Fast with Stab Proof Coffee (not quite bullet proof as I use raw butter/cream)

Eat fat/protein at around hour 16-18.

Fat tea a couple of hrs later

Stab Proof Coffee or a latte with raw milk or yogurt/nuts/apple a couple of hrs later

Dinner 3 hrs later with a protein and try to get some greens and veggies in, even if just a few bites. Also a few bites of ferments.

All drinks contain 99% chocolate and most meals and I nibble it frequently.

Fish, have totally backed off on that. Now probably 5X a week.

Protein - along with my fish backing off, I'm finding my protein consumption has fallen as well. I'm doing lots of fat. The only food I desire is pork/bacon.

Exercise - I've stopped. None, nada. Perhaps that's why protein desire is backing off?

99% dark chocolate - I'm eating close to 600 kcal a day of this. When I look on paper, seems like I might be better off cutting that back and adding in more protein - but I'm not so sure as all my labs seem to be on the improving train, along with the fact that my weight is stable, my mind seems acute, and I'm in a pretty good mood. I snack on apples now and then when fixing for my son.

For me, the goal is to be able to just eat and be healthy without spending hours thinking, weighing, counting calories or having to use one ounce of "try/discipline/focus" etc. I want it to be natural as I think THAT is key to cortisol and inflammation controlling moods.

SO on one hand, I'm making progress there - on the other hand I would like to try to eat more of the healing foods ala Terry Wahls. I never seem to WANT to eat veggies etc. There is also the changing season factor - perhaps this is simply a change of season thing (although our days are in the 60-70's and sunny right now).

I know I would like to cram in MOAR SHELLFISH.

Is forcing it using discipline and will power a step forward or a step back? At this point IDK so I'm continuing on this road until I see a fork I want to take.

For me, no difference in how I feel when I eat mostly seafood or minimal seafood. Wonder what this means. Thinking issues or biology flashlight?

Also, i think there is a set amount of absorption of fats that I can handle at any given time - so for me a smaller and steady supply of them . ### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-11T01:06:13+0200

210.2 Yikes.

So, yesterdays 2 restaurant meals really show on the scale today! 3lbs overnight. I'm not surprised as I did have both lunch and dinner at restaurants with questionable sauces etc. The good news is it was all fish and veggies. Well, except for the 5 bites of warm oooey gooey chocolate cake with ice cream.

Also, it was almost 8 when we got seated and ate. Soooooo today is a fat fast/steak day. It's an easy yet hellish day for it. . .

I'm handling 2 extra boys for a sleepover AND we got twin kittens this afternoon and I had a crazy inspection for a buyer this a.m. It's one of those GO GO GO days. My husband just came home and I locked myself in my office to "work". This is me working, again.

My scale better be in the 207's tomorrow! It just better.

Also we had a little winter hit, it's snowy out and in the 30's and not sunny so it's a good day for fasting!

### Lyndra — 2012-11-11T04:19:52+0200

### Inger — 2012-11-11T09:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-63556>

I work as a waitress.. in 3 different restaurants at times. One big businessbuilding, Ericsson, one cozy traditional Czech restaurant and one old wonderful Finnish museum where are weddings and such organized but also a la carte with lokal foods.

Now.. I can tell you I never go out in restaurants to eat, if it is not a \*\*\*\*\* star place, because I see what happends in the kitchen and what the products are made of.. The places I work in are expencive too, and nice places. But guess what. It is so much fake products. No real cream at all, it is fake cream from vegetable oils. Do you really think the vanilla cream on your cake is from cream? No.. it is fake, with a long list of weird ingredients, I have checked it all. The "butter" what you get with the home made bread is fake butter... fake all along.

I can tell you these products go not well with my body. I once got a really bad root infection in my molar years ago after eating out.. what I thought was healthy foods, guacamole, snails in butter.. Now I know better. It was all fake for sure.

Only the most expensive restaurants still have genuine products. At least here in Finland. But I bet it is no different in USA.

In Germany it was different. There you got always real unsalted butter with your bread. There is less fake products anyway. The use traditionally lard and such still. But here most people think they will fall dead when eating those...lol So low fat it is. Or at least vegetable fats.

Thankfully there is a movement in the other direction too so if you really search you will find good products too!  Like the raw organic goats milk I can get in Helsinki city. And Lambshearts and stuff. I just got a bag full of Elk organs from the supermarket, because I asked for it! Boah.. it was so big I barely got it to fit on my bike..huh. It felt kind of funny biking home with this heavy plastic bag full of bloody elch organs.. the bag was that kind of plastic one can see trough.

They sell elk meat every fall during huntig season so I wondered if I could get the organs too.. But you need to ask, because no one else asks or want them. That is what they told me at least. I was the first one asking for these in his life so far.

The older lady at the counter that helped me pack it told her mom always made lungs soup and stuff from organs.

So if eating out I would ask them what the food is made of.

If I eat out I always do that. And then I change the meny after what I think is healthy. I promise a good waitress loves to serve and she loves to fulfill your every wish (haha..the cooks might hate you coming with all those extra wishes but hey.. thats cooks. The guest are more important.. just give the cook a kiss on his cheek or a gentle hug and he will love you again.. ..).

So just ask and you will recieve.

It felt kind of funny biking home with this heavy plastic bag full of bloody elch organs.. the bag was that kind of plastic one can see trough.
>

I promise a good waitress loves to serve and she loves to fulfill your every wish (haha..the cooks might hate you coming with all those extra wishes but hey.. thats cooks. The guest are more important.. just give the cook a kiss on his cheek or a gentle hug and he will love you again.. ..).
>
> So just ask and you will recieve.

Inger - I knew you were a kindred spirit! my intuition kicking in again ....yeah! I have wanted to ask for so long what you do

My husband and I had a small restaurant /cafe for 35 years. we made everything from scratch daily - breads, soups etc. etc. - we were definitely in the minority - in fact the only one. in most restaurants food costs are the bottom line so whatever is cheapest is used \[and prices are still high\]. The catch is - not all customers appreciate the quality and aren't willing to pay for it. We had an extremely loyal following of wonderful customers but a small restaurant struggles with high food costs when trying to do things this way. For instance - it is much more expensive to make wonderful breads from scratch using real butter and great olive oils and and real cream etc. not to mention how labour intensive it is.

We had amazing customers who appreciated this and others who would not pay $1.00 more. for most people, unfortunately, price is the bottom line. Could you imagine what kind of a team we could have made????

This is Abe Lincoln, and he is the motherlode of cats!!! He let 3 screaming children man handle him and play with him and he just kept coming back for more. ### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-11T23:19:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-63682>

Friends of my son's mom just had twins and in the hospital, so I had a house full of boys this weekend. 4,6,7 yrs old. I don't know why, but I thought it would be a good idea to shop for kittens with brood in tow. I was going to take them to the park, but it was sleeting out and that seemed like a bad idea.

Saw this pair, brought them into the playroom with three boys. Kitties were awesome, Abe Lincoln is like the coveted kitty dog who isn't afraid of people and lets children play. He let these boys play and pick him up and just kept coming back for more. George Washington was slightly more subdued, and when we got him home he's the sweet quiet one that likes sitting on shoulders. He was on my shoulder all morning when I was working on the computer.

I know how wrong it is to post many paragraphs about my kittehs, but someone showed the least bit of interest so here I go! Just be thankful I didn't just have a baby.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-14T18:36:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64279>

I'm doing a little "life planning by intuition".

As reported, do NOT feel like being active physically. It's like there is a production need going on right now, for instance, I cleaned and tackled the basement full of crap this week and plowed through a ton of paperwork that I hate. Sitting in the cold tub, or taking a walk right now seems super UN productive and I do not want. So I decided that if my physical inertia was down, but my mental inertia seemed fine, I can make this work for me.

Self given deadline on desk sitting work.

I agreed to a feature (advertorial) on 11/30 in the Boulder Daily Camera. This should coinside with me appearing on Rock Center doing real estating. So, in order to support this I need to create a landing page/website that targets those newspaper readers and tv watchers. I have 2 weeks to do a website build and point/move current sites and backend all that techy crap and marketing positioning. My positioning normally is geared towards super-techy people. Newspaper readers are a whole new ball of wax. And I have some very active clients right now.

Just made myself super busy with a hard non-optional deadline. The newspaper is gonna print and the tv show is going to air no matter the state of my websites.

I need to focus. So this is my last day here until I get my marketing/techy work done. Don't know how long it will take - if history is any indicator, it will take till minutes prior to deadline.

My a.m.'s are so mentally acute when I do the stab proof coffee until mid-day. I'm going to keep going with that through this high output time.

Weight 207. I'm leaky with a cold. I sort of wish I had not done this.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-17T17:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64946>

206, my diet seems to be migrating to high percentage of fat. Trying not to overeat protein. My stabby coffee a.m. seems to be working for me. I'm still on my productive (but not anxious) streak.

I bet you know what it's like to feel you can't make logical/reasonable choices when it comes to your own body image and eating things. I do, and I have a question for you.

I'm having a story (more advertorial) in a real estate mag locally. I'm positioning it as me informing the public what the process is, and what it's like to buy a house with me as your agent. I have the opportunity for several photos. . . and I think this concept/photo is kinda awesome -but then I panicked because "omg what if everyone is disgusted by seeing a fat girl eat a cupcake". Would you look at the copy and photo and tell me what it makes you feel? From the perspective of "you're a future home buyer" and was thumbing through real estate magazine.

Story Gist, you can have your cake and eat it too when Cavemam is your agent - she's makes it fun and will take care of you just as if you were her own family.

Photo to illustrate the point:

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/66949=574-48.jpg)

or

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/66949=575-01.jpg)

Thanks for responding, I'm a little lost on figuring stuff out for ME. I can help other people all day long, but have perception dysmorphia for moi.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-17T17:38:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64948>

You look very happy in the top photo. This should be a fun and exciting time for the home buyer. Looks like you are having fun and enjoying your cake too so it does say what you are trying to. You also don't look like a fat girl eating cake. No hint of that what-so-ever in my view.

The bottom pick says I am eating cake and I shouldn't be.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-17T17:49:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64950>

Jack totally needs to come to CO for a photo session.

### Lyndra — 2012-11-17T18:15:12+0200

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-17T18:20:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64958>

### Souldanzer — 2012-11-17T18:31:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64959>

I like the first one, too. I would have reservations about the crowd you're appealing to with this though as I live in Boulder, like Lyndra pointed out. Fitness & health crowd. Even if a person is not, they'd like to be. Most people here identify with the opposite of cake eating - super healthy, paleo, vegan, vegetarian, raw food people. Unless you're targeting students. Which I'm sure you're not.

Personally, I would feel more attracted to a realtor who is representing more the outdoorsy, alternative, natural side. But then I'm a hippy girl (with potential ).

### KiwiLauren — 2012-11-17T20:09:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64975>

Hi Soul!!!! So glad to 'see' you! xx

Mam, I love the first photo. Honestly, you look happy and the thought of you looking fat never crossed my mind. Somehow the outdoor, sunny setting also seems to capture the Colorado thing. I bet all those super healthy folks secretly wish for cupcakes anyway

### diane — 2012-11-17T20:11:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-64976>

From reading what you wrote, it sounds like your brand is about making a sometime overwhelming process fun, and at the same time, they will get the results that they want. The first photo definitely shows your personality, and to me is a great representation of your brand.

Sure, some people won't like the cupcake, some will. If you wanted to be really upfront about who you are, you could put a side note like (cupcake is gluten-free, dairy-free, sugar-free but not taste free!).

Just think of the people that you want to work with - and if you want to work with people who want someone who cares, and appreciate a sense of humour - then go for it.

There's a real estate agent in toronto who's last name is Lamb. And when he started out, he made billboards with pictures of a lamb with his head on it. Lots of people laughed and made fun of him - but he was willing to put himself out there to stand out. Now he's one of the most successful (and of course well known) real estate agents in Toronto. Think he also had his own reality TV show.

So put out there what you want - and don't worry if there's some people who won't like it!

The cupcake picture is cute, but I like this picture better. I understand the "have your cake and eat it too" tagline, but...I don't know. I just think this picture is very appealing while still looking sort of professional, which, when it comes right down to it, is what I'd want someone who was helping me buy a house to be.

Also, FWIW, your comment about "would the public be disgusted by seeing a fat girl eating a cupcake" really spoke to me. First of all because you don't look even remotely fat in any recent picture I've ever seen of you, and second of all because that was what kept me from going to culinary school after high school. I was completely convinced that no one would take me seriously, because oh hey, fat girl constantly around food, what a shocker. It's hard to explain, but...yeah. I get it.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T17:37:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-65356>

207.6, not suprised at the raise - ate out for dinner last night. God choices that had seafood, seaweed, lots of spices and veggies - but probably just as much sugar and msg. Can't win at everything now can ya.

Friend just had twins, this is a friend that became a friend because we were both paleo a year ago. Fast forward - she isn't paleo anymore and mcD's plays heavy in their rotation. This is what happens when we don't have an actual villiage to help raise our children.

SO, the walk through "holiday cooking aisles" at the stores is driving me nuts. I miss all the fun (and easier!) holiday/family cooking stuff. So I'm going to make her some packaged/processed casseroles in cute aluminum pans with bows and sweet labels. They are going to eat crap anyways - I'm at peace with this.

I still feed her boys our stuff when they eat here - but I want to do something for them, and this is something that will be a gift TO THEM. To me it would be "why are you trying to kill us", but we can't all be the same now can we.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T17:56:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-65365>

> **Anna K.;67378 wrote:** Just curious, why not make a primal casserole? I'm sure they will enjoy it too.
>
>
>
> Also, why did they go off paleo?

The main reason is they won't enjoy that as much. I think it's rude and judgemental to force my choices on someone else, and this is something I want to be a blessing for them. If it's a blessing is all in the perception . . .the fact that I made something THEY LIKE seems better.

Also, For less money and time I can make them three CW meals that they will really appreciate and enjoy receiving MUCH more than the one paleo casserole that comes with judgement.

I did give a passing thought to the "would you give heroine to an addict"., but frankly I see this is different.

They went off it as they didn't see the reward in relation to the effort and cost. They are naturally thin people, so there is nothing obvious like weight to deal with. And, if you have kids - it's not an easy thing to make your kids be the ones that don't fit in, Mom is a very social extrovert and that kind of "deprivation" was too harmful in her opinion.

Note that all the moms with multiple kids here are fairly introverted and wouldn't put much value to "being normal" that a real extroverted person does.

It's very very tough to prepare all these perfect meals and provide this life for your family. I think meals and pleasure from them are a part of the mental makeup of homo's, and expecting everyone to dismiss that aspect of life is being deliberately obtuse and blind to reality.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T18:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-65375>

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T21:03:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-65400>

Not really hungry per se, but feeling a need to eat some carbs so I ate a little brown rice with salt and butter since I had that handy (from evil casserole production). It didn't feel like a craving-craving, but something my body needed. I wonder if the easy energy is wanted to support fever and infection fighting. I have both. Like my body going through the trouble of making glucose in the liver is an extra step and the body CAN do it easier and save resources for other things.

The nose pain goes down into my teeth. It gives me the wierd sensation of really wanting to floss hard.

My energy is suspiciously high considering. I've got a lot going on today (showing, two kid friends for dinner and playdate, cooking dinner for my family, working on writing copy for PR thingy. I'm not in that space of "omg I wish I could just go to bed", more like. . .ok, lets put on the headphones and just do this stuff and wait out the pain/discomfort.

The only thing really whacked on my profile is my low iron. I stopped the supplimenting and heavy liver eating for the last couple of months so I go below the bottom of range and thus can get the iron infusion.

I wonder if the low Iron is contributor to virus/infections.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-19T21:36:21+0200

It's a snippy kind of day. I don't know about you but I have been trying to multi=task. hahahahahaha. And at my age. pfbbbt.

the result is I come across as bitchy, distant, snippy, snarky ... we need more emoticons.!!!!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-19T22:07:44+0200

>
> As for fighting infections, sugar is actually counter productive because it competes with vitamin C for cellular absorption.
>

You are probably right, I think there is so much that goes into play when one is sick (emotional, psychological) that it's hard to seperate fact from fiction.

I keep coming back to the livers production of glucose tho. . .clearly we are set up that we can process starches/carbs/fruit whatever.. . so why would we have this ability unless there was a place for it.

Is it simply a backup plan in case primary food source unavail? That doesn't quite sound right to me. Unsure.

# Thread: Iron-Sulfur World theory
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-sulfur-world-theory.1193/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-04-29T19:34:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-sulfur-world-theory.1193/post-19858>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19808020](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19808020)

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iron%E2%80%93sulfur\_world\_theory](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iron%E2%80%93sulfur_world_theory)

### chocolate — 2012-04-29T19:38:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-sulfur-world-theory.1193/post-19860>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC50631/pdf/pnas01098-0096.pdf](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC50631/pdf/pnas01098-0096.pdf)

Cellular regulation of the iron-responsive element binding protein:

Disassembly of the cubane iron-sulfur cluster results in

high-affinity RNA binding

ABSTRACT The translation of ferritin mRNA and degradation

of transferrin receptor mRNA are regulated by the

interaction of an RNA-binding protein, the iron-responsive

element binding protein (ERE-BP), with RNA stem-oop structures

known as iron-responsive elements (IREs) contained

within these transcripts. IRE-BP produced in iron-replete cells

has aconitase (EC 4.2.1.3) activity. The protein shows extensive

sequence homology with mitochondrial aconitase, and sequences

of peptides prepared from cytosolic aconitase are

identical with peptides of IRE-BP. As an active aconitase,

IRE-BP is expected to have an Fe-S duster, in analogy to other

aconitases. This Fe-S cluster has been implicated as the region

of the protein that senses intracellular iron levels and accordingly

modifies the ability of the IRE-BP to interact with IREs.

Expression of the IRE-BP in cultured cells has revealed that the

IRE-BP functions either as an active aconitase, when the cells

are iron-replete, or as an active RNA-binding protein, when the

cells are iron-depleted. We compare properties of purified

authentic cytosolic aconitase from beef liver with those of

IRE-BP from tissue culture cells and establish that characteristics

of the physiologically relevant form of the protein from

iron-depleted cells resemble those of cytosolic aconitase apoprotein.

**We demonstrate that loss of the labile fourth iron atom

of the Fe-S cluster results in loss of aconitase activity, but that

more extensive cluster alteration is required before the IRE-BP

acquires the capacity to bind RNA with the affinity seen in vivo.

These results are consistent with a model in which the cubane

Fe-S cluster is disassembled when intracellular iron is depleted**

Maybe chelating isn't such a great idea....it sure feels great....grrrrrr

### chocolate — 2012-04-29T19:45:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-sulfur-world-theory.1193/post-19863>

Or maybe chelating is the best idea.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11032887](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11032887)

Reversible inactivation of superoxide-sensitive aconitase in Abeta1-42-treated neuronal cell lines.

Longo VD, Viola KL, Klein WL, Finch CE.

Source

Andrus Gerontology Center and Department of Biological Sciences, University of Southern California, Los Angeles, California 90089-0191, USA. [vlongo@usc.edu](mailto:vlongo@usc.edu)

Abstract

The activity of the superoxide-sensitive enzyme aconitase was monitored to evaluate the generation of superoxide in neuronal cell lines treated with beta-amyloid (Abeta) peptide 1-42. Treatment of differentiated and undifferentiated rat PC12 and human neuroblastoma SK-N-SH cells with soluble Abeta1-42 (Abeta-derived diffusible ligands) or fibrillar Abeta1-42 caused a 35% reversible inactivation of aconitase, which preceded loss of viability and was correlated with altered cellular function. **Aconitase was reactivated upon incubation of cellular extracts with iron and sulfur, suggesting that Abeta causes the release of iron from 4Fe-4S clusters. Abeta neurotoxicity was partially blocked by the iron chelator deferoxamine. These data suggest that increased superoxide generation and the release of iron from 4Fe-4S clusters are early events in Abeta1-42 neurotoxicity.**

### Jim — 2012-04-29T19:53:16+0300

# Thread: Study finds â€˜Western dietâ€™ detrimental to fetal hippocampal tissue transplants
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/study-finds-%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%CB%9Cwestern-diet%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2-detrimental-to-fetal-hippocampal-tissue-transplants.1194/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-04-29T20:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/study-finds-%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%CB%9Cwestern-diet%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2-detrimental-to-fetal-hippocampal-tissue-transplants.1194/post-19881>

[http://www.psypost.org/2012/04/study-finds-western-diet-detrimental-to-fetal-hippocampal-tissue-transplants-11337](http://http://www.psypost.org/2012/04/study-finds-western-diet-detrimental-to-fetal-hippocampal-tissue-transplants-11337)

Hippocampus by Life Science DatabasesResearchers interested in determining the direct effects of a high saturated fat and high cholesterol (HFHC) diet on implanted fetal hippocampal tissues have found that in middle-aged laboratory rats the HFHC diet elevated microglial activation and reduced neuronal development.

While the resulting damage was due to an inflammatory response in the central nervous system, they found that the effects of the HFHC diet were alleviated by the interleukin (IL)-1 receptor antagonist IL-1Ra, leading them to conclude that IL-Ra has potential use in neurological disorders involving neuroinflammation.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-04-29T22:50:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jersey-swim-meet-up.1195/post-19927>

Anyone interested in meeting in Avon for an ocean dip? I did this today, in the AM, water temp was 51, splashed around for 25 minutes. I had some company in the form of fisherman on the jetty, and horrified walkers on the beach and boardwalk, but would have been nice to have some company IN the water! It was magnificent.

The first jetty after the Belmar bridge (Shark River Inlet). The first beach entrance in Avon when coming over the bridge, last entrance if you are coming down from Bradley.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-8-y-o-cts.1196/post-19938>

So my 8 y/o son really loves to CT with me. He is kind of cold adapted, always running around without his jacket. I used to always tell him to put his jacket on, now I just marvel at his cold adapted ways.

I let him het into the tub with me for 1 minute at the start and we ad 1 minute each day. he is up to 10 minutes. he really loves it. its kind of a challenge for him. we hang out in the tub together. Its kind of like bonding with him.

# Thread: Scar Tissue Turned Into Heart Muscle Without Using Stem Cells
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scar-tissue-turned-into-heart-muscle-without-using-stem-cells.1197/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-04-30T01:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/scar-tissue-turned-into-heart-muscle-without-using-stem-cells.1197/post-19949>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/04/120426174110.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/04/120426174110.htm)

ScienceDaily (Apr. 26, 2012) â€” Scientists at Duke University Medical Center have shown the ability to turn scar tissue that forms after a heart attack into heart muscle cells using a new process that eliminates the need for stem cell transplant.

The study, published online April 26 in the journal Circulation Research, used molecules called microRNAs to trigger the cardiac tissue conversion in a lab dish and, for the first time, in a living mouse, demonstrating the potential of a simpler process for tissue regeneration.

If additional studies confirm the approach in human cells, it could lead to a new way for treating many of the 23 million people worldwide who suffer heart failure, which is often caused by scar tissue that develops after a heart attack. The approach could also have benefit beyond heart disease.

# Thread: Serum ferritin levels are associated with vascular damage in patients with nonalcohol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serum-ferritin-levels-are-associated-with-vascular-damage-in-patients-with-nonalcohol.1198/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-04-30T01:52:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serum-ferritin-levels-are-associated-with-vascular-damage-in-patients-with-nonalcohol.1198/post-19957>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20392616](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20392616)

fatty liver disease

Increased ferritin and body iron stores are frequently observed in nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), associated with heightened susceptibility to vascular damage. Conflicting data have been reported on the role of iron in atherosclerosis, with recent data suggesting that excess iron induces vascular damage by increasing levels of the hormone hepcidin, which would determine iron trapping into macrophages, oxidative stress, and promotion of transformation into foam cells. Aim of this study was to investigate the relationship between iron status and cardiovascular damage in NAFLD.

METHODS AND RESULTS:

Vascular damage was evaluated by common carotid arteries intima-media thickness (CC-IMT) measurement and plaque detection by ecocolor-doppler ultrasonography in 506 patients with clinical and ultrasonographic diagnosis of NAFLD, hemochromatosis gene (HFE) mutations by restriction analysis in 342 patients. Serum hepcidin-25 was measured by time-of-flight mass spectrometry in 143 patients. At multivariate analysis CC-IMT was associated with systolic blood pressure, glucose, LDL cholesterol, abdominal circumference, age, and ferritin (p=0.048). Carotid plaques were independently associated with age, ferritin, glucose, and hypertension. **Ferritin reflected iron stores and metabolic syndrome components, but not inflammation or liver damage.** Hyperferritinemia was associated with increased vascular damage only in patients with HFE genotypes associated with hepcidin upregulation by iron stores (p

# Thread: serious ice cream - pure energy.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-04-30T03:07:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/post-19975>

Made the bullet proof execs ice cream for my son and a couple of his friends. HOLY CRIPES! This stuff tasted good.

I used 3 tbs of maple syrup instead of xylitol (still not gonna do highly processed stuff). I also added cinnamon and used my homemade vanilla.

It was awesome pure energy food. It's basically eggs, super-fats and a little syrup. I put 3 boys out about 1p, and they ran constantly till about 4p. Mine had a few buttery chunks left in, I should have blended a little more.

here is the recipe

[http://www.bulletproofexec.com/get-some-ice-cream/](http://http://www.bulletproofexec.com/get-some-ice-cream/)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-30T03:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/post-19978>

It's the creamiest, most gooey ice cream I have ever made. I've mad a lot of different ones: french custard, goat, eggless and coconut milk. I experiiment a lot with flavors like cardamom-tangerine, mexican chocolate, etc. all staying within post reset guidelines with just a little stevia for sweetening, if needed. I love bulletproof! Now I am on a hunt for a recipe that has the results of the bulletproof without the eggs.

### IceBarbie — 2012-04-30T03:38:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/post-19979>

I've made that a few times and last time added chopped pecans so it tasted like Butter Pecan! YUM! I also used Stevia instead of Xylitol. Hubby liked it too. But I still didn't Get Some....lol!

### BeingVenus — 2012-04-30T04:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/post-19982>

What IS the deal with xylitol? I won't touch it because I don't want to eat sweets but I just cannot decide if it's Ok or bad. Generally the few times I've ever made my son something sweet I've used coconut sugar but if xylitol is Ok, I would prefer that. Anyone know??? This recipe looks great btw. The few times I've broke out my ice cream maker I've used heavy cream and eggs but this recipe looks awesome. Thanks for posting.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-04-30T04:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/post-19993>

Dr Kruse is not cool with xylitol. I just read what he said to someone in the comments on the MSG, Brain, Gut blog. He said:

Go re read my post on Peripheral Neuropathy. Realize that xylitol is a polyol (FODMAP). And they say it is safe because the people making xylitol made sure that was the case in studies they supported and pushed. I don’t advocate for any dietary polyol. I like only raw Stevia with no fillers like Maltodextrin. Usually the liquid is the only one that fits that bill.

### janagram — 2012-04-30T19:50:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/post-20129>

while that sounds good.....I made the best ice cream the other day:

put about 3/4 c. still frozen raspberries in a blender container (mine's a stick blender)

add a bit of vanilla

add heavy cream to cover or almost cover berries

add stevia (your on yr own with amount)

blend............awesome!!!! (its ready immediately!)

### TribalSpice — 2012-08-29T00:42:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serious-ice-cream-pure-energy.1199/post-47939>

I have a thermomix, so make ice cream a fair bit for the kids in summer. My favourite is really simple: a handful of almonds, 1/2 cup coconut cream speed 10 for 1:30 then add 3 frozen bananas and blend for another 20-30 seconds. Serve and enjoy. No added processed sugars at all.

# Thread: CT and metallic detox
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-04-30T06:56:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/post-20019>

Has anyone else noticed the following since starting CT?

1\. Since going primal/Paleo 11 months ago, I do not have BO. I don't use a deodorant anymore (just a salt crystal thing), but since starting CT, I've noticed I often smell off a metallic BO, especially in my armpits.

2\. I've had a cough for 4 weeks now, its finally almost gone, but it kept bringing up phlegm that tasted very metallic (not like blood, just purely metallic).

I'm pretty sure I'm not imagining things, anyone else seem to be leaking heavy metals thanks to CT and detox?

### freesia — 2012-04-30T07:26:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/post-20024>

I have heard of this when people start detox supplements (active B vitamins), and I had it personally when I did hyperbaric oxygen treatment. I haven't had any metallic taste from CT yet but maybe I have that to look forward too

### Glamorama — 2012-04-30T07:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/post-20025>

> **freesia;20097 wrote:** I have heard of this when people start detox supplements (active B vitamins), and I had it personally when I did hyperbaric oxygen treatment. I haven't had any metallic taste from CT yet but maybe I have that to look forward too

I should add that the metaliic thing didn't kick in until Week 4, so after the initial, more obvious detox symthomps like pimples, itchy skin and excess estrogen. I've now endured it from Week 4 till week 8, and the cough is only just starting to disappear. I took sublingual B12 during this entire period too... Hmmm.

### janagram — 2012-04-30T07:32:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/post-20026>

maybe eating cilantro on everything would be good...it helps to detox metal..

### Glamorama — 2012-04-30T11:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/post-20031>

### MonteD — 2012-04-30T16:45:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/post-20062>

### PaleoMom — 2012-05-01T00:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-metallic-detox.1201/post-20212>

I'm currently chelating heavy metals out. I have often smelled like metal in my armpits, even tasted it in my mouth. Drink WAY more water when this happens, it helps.

You can damage yourself by haphazardly trying to get rid of metals. Cilantro does help release stored metals, but it doesn't help then actually leave your body, so you could be releasing them from a relatively safe place to just replant them in your brain. I have been through many types of heavy metal programs that were mostly very risky and made things worse. I'm now using [http://www.detoxmetals.com/](http://http://www.detoxmetals.com/) I know it looks a bit scam-like, but I swear it is amazing. My paleo, naturopathic dentist told me about it. I truly believe it is the only effective AND safe way to get rid of metals.

For now just drink way more water and try taking some chlorella. It will help get out what you've released. Kombucha is great too.

# Thread: CT-induced hunger... when will it pass?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### youthfuliz — 2012-04-30T15:38:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20047>

Hello Dr. Kruse and my fellow CTers... I'm almost four months into leptin reset, and about two months into CT - have done 21 cold bath/shower, full submersion up to my neck, most sessions about 45+ minutes, the coldest I have gone is 56F.

Before CT, thanks to leptin reset, I hardly felt hungry. I was eating BAB and dinner, and didn't feel hungry all day long. The only time when I felt hungry was a few days before TOM. Lately I have been stepping up my CT to daily cold bath... and the hunger has been constant. I eat lunch now. And that's not enough. I eat a big dinner. I crave fat, and can't get enough of it. Twice this last week I stuffed myself at dinner that made me feel bad - my stomach was complaining and it was emotionally frustrating. I still eat keto paleo, since I don't crave carbs any more. But I eat too much that my stomach feels bad.

Now I think it's natural that my body tries to grab more food when it senses the winter is coming.  Every year when the temperature falls in late autumn I experience this strong desire to eat. It usually continues for a week.

With CT, if I keep lowering the temperature, will hunger go away as I get more adapted? Or do I have to eat like a hungry lioness as soon as I soak myself in the cold water? I really want to gain back the serenity I acquired with keto paleo and leptin reset.

Please enlighten me with your experiences...

### youthfuliz — 2012-04-30T18:31:53+0300

> **KiwiLauren;17001 wrote: About a month into CT my hunger went through the roof. Dr K said it was likely estrogen detox and to up the protein. It lasted about a week then receded. Not saying its not the CO in your case, but CT does seem an unpredictable variable.

It's better to eat more protein at BAB than feeling hungry during the day anyways.

PS: Doctor's answer in the Ask Jack section: "CT destroys hunger so if you're hungry something else is driving it and a lab check would be in order."

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-30T19:40:55+0300

The doc has mentioned it, both on MDA and here, that hunger while CTing, is probably due to a hormone imbalance....for me is was estrogen dominance.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-30T20:10:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20134>

I am still hungry too on and off. Was really hungry when I started CT and it lasted a loooong time. Interestingly, it is almost TOM and I am hungrier the last few days. This was mentioned above. ### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-30T20:26:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20139>

It took me 62 days of CT before I noticed a sizeable decrease in my hunger. (yes I am logging everything)

since everyone has a different pace, on day 62 I was doing:

40 minute immersions in the evening 4x per week (53 F)

10 minute cold showers in the morning (53 F)

60 minutes icepacks on chest and gut each night

### AKMan — 2012-04-30T20:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20145>

Here's something I've noticed.

While CT'ing, there is NO HUNGER. Even if I was hungry beforehand, it goes away. When I warm back up, the hunger returns. Maybe this is what Jack means when he says CT kills hunger.

For me, I knew it was going to happen, so I made sure I have nutrient dense/calorie poor food on hand, think bags of frozen veggies, leaf lettuce, raw brocolli. No dips or sauces.

Once I warm up, if hunger strikes, I eat that.

### vwaggs — 2012-04-30T21:47:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20165>

When I first started CTing I wasn't hungry at all even during the day. I was losing fat fast and hardly eating! Then about 3 weeks in I starting have detox symptoms (rash, nasal stuff, cough). After the detox (3 weeks or so) I started to get very hungry. It is unbearable now. I even skipped the last few days of CT. Now "the girls" are super sore (not TOM) and so I must be having estrogen issues. It is the only thing I can think of. I am craving carbs again too! UGH! I thought I was past all of this! Going to CT for sure tonight!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-30T22:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20176>

### Dali Dula — 2012-05-01T00:40:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20217>

I have been doing Ct for 60 + days. For the past 30 days 15 min @48 deg full immersion. For just the past few days I am not hungry. I usually wake up ready for my BAB, but today I was just not hungry. Had a little nibble of dry sausage to let my body know it had food and then didn't eat until 4:30 and not because I was particularly hungry, but because i am very active and feel I should eat. I don't remember being unusually hungry since I started CT and now I'm just not hungry. It's a bit unsettling because I do love to eat.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-01T02:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20242>

### PaleoMom — 2012-05-01T02:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-20244>

I was ravenous at first. It wasn't until my baths went to 50 on average (44-55) that my appetite plummeted.

If it helps you not feel bad about it though, I think it's worth noting that for me I haven't lost a pound, in fact I have gained a bit, but I think that was muscle. My measurements have only gone down 1 cm at the waist in 6 weeks. My point was, that even though I've lost my appetite and am eating at a deficit I don't seem any better off fat loss wise than when my appetite was super high.

### kathylu — 2012-05-03T19:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-21042>

So glad to hear it goes away! I get ravenous after CT, even if I have eaten just beforehand.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-03T19:47:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-21052>

> **kathylu;21117 wrote:** So glad to hear it goes away! I get ravenous after CT, even if I have eaten just beforehand.

Actually my hunger has become manageable. I eat 30% more protein and fat at breakfast and make sure I eat a nice dinner too. I still feel more hungry than pre-CT, but it doesn't bother me any more. Especially because my skin is getting more silky smooth each day.

### donkjellberg — 2012-05-03T19:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-21054>

CT has been curbing my appetite. I do get an elusive, insatiable hunger when sleep deprived. When I work out I do work up an appetite but that goes away with a good meal. The sleep-hunger only goes away with restored sleep. I am fortunate to have no hormone issues mucking things up.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-11T01:48:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-induced-hunger-when-will-it-pass.1202/post-23049>

I'd like to update:

Hunger is under control... Well I had a satisfying breakfast, 50g protein + fat + mushrooms, then around 4pm I felt a hint of hunger - the gentle quiet type, reminding you it may be time to recharge. I went to the grocery store since I'm running out of meat. Picked up beef ribs and made a very delicious stew. I was to eat half, and leave half for tomorrow's breakfast. Well, I couldn't finish my dinner, actually I stopped eating after I ate half of what I intended to eat! I felt FULL!  Such a nice feeling.
