### Glamazon — 2012-10-05T05:28:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-56415>

I just wanted to add that I recently received a new pair of blue blockers. I wanted to have one set to keep upstairs and one to keep down stairs.

As soon as I put them on at sunset I can feel my eyes begin to relax. If I take them off the strain of the regular light cause pain to my eyes as they start to "tighten" up.

Within a half hour I become drowsy.

If I put them on during the day it makes me drowsy. If I take them off my eyes will start to hurt. I put them back on and leave them on for a couple of hours and then I can take them off without my eyes beginning to hurt. This is during the day time. I need some further hormone testing because of this.

This just my own testing results.

They are $8.25 at Amazon.

[http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-Eyewear-SCT-Orange-Anti-Fog/dp/B000USRG90/ref=sr\_1\_17?ie=UTF8&qid=1349403768&sr=8-17&keywords=uvex+safety+goggles](http://http://www.amazon.com/Uvex-S1933X-Eyewear-SCT-Orange-Anti-Fog/dp/B000USRG90/ref=sr_1_17?ie=UTF8&qid=1349403768&sr=8-17&keywords=uvex+safety+goggles)

### diane — 2012-10-07T02:47:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-56654>

I went to a local optical shop, as I really want to get glasses that I can wear during the day. I have been wearing my Oakley sunglasses while sitting at my desk, but I spend a lot of the day in meetings. I do notice a difference the days I can wear my glasses for significant amounts of time. I used to think that it was the air in the office that made me feel like crap during the day (and felt so much better once I left), but now really think it's the light and sitting in front of a computer that does it.

I picked out a pair of glasses, and have asked for blue blocking lenses (no prescription as I wear contacts most of the time). They want to put 25% yellow lenses in as they are trying to tell me that any more is detrimental (which I know better). But even at 50% - is this what I'm looking for? I need glasses that I can wear to work - so want to make sure I'm getting the right ones.

### LinD — 2012-10-08T19:05:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-56906>

### diane — 2012-10-08T19:47:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-56910>

Yes - you got it!

I would love to get out during the day. I walk the dogs in the morning, but my day is usually so jam packed with meetings, I don't see daylight from the time arrive to when I leave. Which shortly will be dark when I leave.

I definitely notice a difference with my sunglasses on at my desk. I think the blue blockers might make me look crazier lol. But I'm not sure I'm asking for the right lenses - or that 25% yellow is going to do anything at all.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-18T16:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-58919>

I just received some prescription glasses from Walmart with yellow lenses. The plastic lenses cost $49. They are yellow, not amber or orange. They really seemed to help my eyes while at work, yesterday afternoon. The lady at the counter had difficulty ordering them because she had never sold that lens, so the optician had to help her. There are three shades of yellow, and I chose the darkest. They are still very light, however. The optician said they were shooter's lenses. They also had the choice of a darker, more expensive lens, but I wanted something lighter.

They seriously don't pass the CD Test, but still I like the effect that they have. And they aren't very noticable, until I'm up close to the other person. Today after logging into my computer at work, I immediately remembered them and put them on, to my eye's relief.

I wish I had gotten larger lenses, so less light comes in from the top and bottom, but having the yellow lens right where you are focusing is quite nice. Previously I tried some clip-on Blublocker lenses, but they are so dark I have difficulty seeing some of the smaller print on my computer.

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-19T08:24:21+0300

I see, well let me tell you that I use the blue blockers to block computer light, even in the day time. My eyes are just so tired but once I put the blue blockers, they work. I dont use them when I go outside since you want your eyes see the sun so your clock cycle doesn't get whacked. It seems that artificial light, mainly from computers just messes you up more I guess. It seems to me to get exposure from natural light and block artificial light as much as possible weather that be in the day or night.

Nonchalant, I know about those glasses, for some folks it does work. Others like dont. I needed the full blue blockers.

### Endless — 2012-10-24T04:44:11+0300

### Entelechy — 2012-11-18T01:59:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-65045>

I've received a few PM's regarding Zircadium..

Zircadium technology was developed by Bob Casper, MD, a professor of medicine at the University of Toronto and an optometrist, Jonathan Spilkin, OD. The technology was commercialized into eyewear products at the beginning of 2009. They used dichroic filters rather than tinting the glasses to achieve a sharp spectral cut off. Therefore, the glasses looked "normal" (albeit expensive)

[www.zircadium.com](http://http://www.zircadium.com) no longer seems to be active.

Not sure what happened with them, but am following up. If anyone else has inside knowledge, could you share?

Thanks.

### persistence — 2014-03-17T03:40:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-115886>

I tried my first Uvex orange blublockers recently and wow they work.  But what I was not prepared for is that they have an added benefit of enhancing the readability in low light conditions. They alter contrast in some way that makes it much easier to pick up print on a tablet or printed page. So that's a win-win for reading at night.

Does Uvex - or any vendor - make a version of these that fit over regular reading glasses? I would like to recommend this to others who I think would benefit from it.

### Josh — 2014-03-17T04:48:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-115892>

The 425's do look like the best choice for before-bed use.

### Josh — 2014-03-17T07:00:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-115908>

I ordered a pair of 500's to try on the computer in the day.

### persistence — 2014-03-17T07:50:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-115915>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=115908)
>
> I ordered a pair of 500's to try on the computer in the day.

Do all five models block all blue light? I wonder if there is a negative side effect to circadian rhythm to block blue light from the computer during the peak light hours of the day?

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-17T12:21:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-115924>

> [persistence said:](/goto/post?id=115915)
>
> Do all five models block all blue light? I wonder if there is a negative side effect to circadian rhythm to block blue light from the computer during the peak light hours of the day?

Better to be out in the sunlight during the day. Of course, since most of us can't be ... do what you can.

I suddenly noticed the other day when travelling on the train how white many people's hands are. Now I'm very fair-skinned like most Northern Europeans, but my hands and face are fairly ruddy at the least and pretty much all year. The conclusion I draw is that many people rarely go outside _at all_, if their hands look so pale. (There's an interesting parallel with sitting: many of us now spend most of our waking lives in chairs, at home, at work, travelling between the two, and so on ...)

The Caveman Doctor says he has had special blue-light bulbs fitted in the room at the hospital where he works in the day, since it's in the basement and there's no natural light. IIRC, he said it has been helping his sleep. This seems logical, since I understand that blue-light in the day should act to flush out melatonin when it's not needed, and help re-establish the proper rhythm. But I guess you wouldn't be wearing blue-blockers in the day or using f.lux then anyway.

### Julie5 — 2014-03-18T07:24:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-116131>

Question about the Uvex goggles. I have to wear them over my glasses, so there is a gap between the edge of the goggles and my face. Light filters in from around the goggles, but not directly on my eyes. Is that indirect light harmful? Maybe I need ski goggles? Hope not!

### DrAudrey — 2015-10-31T02:13:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-180820>

Here is a helpful link. [http://www.coopersafety.com/lensguide.aspx](http://www.coopersafety.com/lensguide.aspx)
Uvex Orange appears to be the best choice for evening TV viewing. >98% blue light blocked.
Uvex Espresso may be too dark for TV or movies and is definitely too dark for driving.

### nancydion — 2017-08-09T10:58:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-your-yellow-tinted-glasses-sufficient-a-simple-test-to-find-out.1257/post-221259>

I work on a computer for about 8 hours a day, so I purchased my [prescription eyeglasses](https://www.binocle-factory.com/en/eyeglasses.html) from Binocle Factory with [Blue light protection](https://www.binocle-factory.com/en/blue-light-glasses.html) option.

# Thread: restoring gut mucosa
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restoring-gut-mucosa.1262/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-03T17:11:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/restoring-gut-mucosa.1262/post-20985>

[http://jn.nutrition.org/content/136/6/1558.full](http://http://jn.nutrition.org/content/136/6/1558.full)

Specific Amino Acids Increase Mucin Synthesis and Microbiota in Dextran Sulfate Sodiumâ€“Treated Rats

During the anabolic response associated with inflammation, mucin synthesis and colonic protection may be compromised by the limited availability of specific amino acids. We therefore determined the effect of dietary amino acid supplementation on the microbiota, mucin status, and mucosal damage in dextran sulfate sodium (DSS)-treated rats. From 8 d before to 28 d after colitis induction, male Sprague-Dawley rats (10 mo old, n = 8/group) were fed a control diet supplemented or not with 2 different doses of an amino acid cocktail containing l-threonine, l-serine, l-proline, and l-cysteine. All diets were isonitrogenous (adjusted with l-alanine). The higher dose of amino acids increased the number of Muc2-containing goblet cells in the surface epithelium of the ulcerated area, stimulated mucin production in the colon, and restored the mucin amino acid composition and mucosal content to healthy, control values. **The colonic mucin synthesis rate was specifically stimulated by 95%, whereas the protein turnover was unchanged. All bacterial populations, markedly altered by the DSS treatment, were promoted. In conclusion, in inflammatory situations, an increase in threonine, serine, proline, and cysteine dietary supply can promote mucin synthesis, reequilibrate the gut microbiota, and thus favor colonic protection and mucosal healing.**

YOu miss your estradiol if you don't have enough of it.

# Thread: Boston Harbor Dip
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-harbor-dip.1264/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### N~Doro — 2012-05-03T18:19:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-harbor-dip.1264/post-21011>

Water temp: 48-50, air temp 53 and very windy off the water.

So, last weekend I had a birthday. Other than being treated like the bees knees for two days, I mentioned to DH that I was hoping to take a little dip in Boston Harbor at Castle Island a couple miles from where we live. He was amused but didnâ€™t say much as we headed off for a lovely hike in the wayburbs. Accidently happened upon a climbing rope and two sets of rings in the woods where someone has set up a parkour course on one of the trails. The higher set was just out of my reach, so with just a wee leap I was doing my first unassisted pull-up in my 51 years (been working on this for months with a stretchy band). Almost got three good ones. Way cool.

Drove back to Boston just as the sun was going down and without a word DH drove to the beach. Thought he was calling my bluff. I wasnâ€™t really prepared so had to go in the water in my t-shirt & skivvies instead of a bathing suit. I walked right in up to my waist. At that point it started to drop off faster though and I freaked a bit since it was now dark out. Stinging legs, shivery from the wind but managed to stay in for a few minutes. Probably less than ten, but DH kept yelling out to me â€œI thought you were just going to walk in and back out â€“ how much longer - Iâ€™m freezingâ€￾. He did manage to take a blurry picture as he stood on shore. It was pretty great to be in the ocean at night with downtown Boston as the backdrop. Not a bad birthday all around.

Canâ€™t wait to do it again.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/21086=176-castle_island4_29.jpg)

### MJ* — 2012-05-03T18:52:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-harbor-dip.1264/post-21026>

> **N~Doro;21086 wrote:** Water temp: 48-50, air temp 53 and very windy off the water.
>
>
>
> So, last weekend I had a birthday. Other than being treated like the bees knees for two days, I mentioned to DH that I was hoping to take a little dip in Boston Harbor at Castle Island a couple miles from where we live. He was amused but didn’t say much as we headed off for a lovely hike in the wayburbs. Accidently happened upon a climbing rope and two sets of rings in the woods where someone has set up a parkour course on one of the trails. The higher set was just out of my reach, so with just a wee leap I was doing my first unassisted pull-up in my 51 years (been working on this for months with a stretchy band). Almost got three good ones. Way cool.
>
>
>
> Drove back to Boston just as the sun was going down and without a word DH drove to the beach. Thought he was calling my bluff. I wasn’t really prepared so had to go in the water in my t-shirt & skivvies instead of a bathing suit. I walked right in up to my waist. At that point it started to drop off faster though and I freaked a bit since it was now dark out. Stinging legs, shivery from the wind but managed to stay in for a few minutes. Probably less than ten, but DH kept yelling out to me “I thought you were just going to walk in and back out – how much longer - I’m freezing”. He did manage to take a blurry picture as he stood on shore. It was pretty great to be in the ocean at night with downtown Boston as the backdrop. Not a bad birthday all around.
>
> Can’t wait to do it again.
>
>
>
>
> [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/21086=176-castle_island4_29.jpg)

Happy Birthday!!! And Awesome job!!

I like that you did it at night!!!! Why did not you go in all the way?

### N~Doro — 2012-05-03T22:48:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/boston-harbor-dip.1264/post-21110>

> **Jack Kruse;21173 wrote:** I like that you did it at night!!!! Why did not you go in all the way?

Meant to of course, but I actually haven't been swimming there even during the day. It started to drop off quickly and the shore was looking pretty far off as I was a shiverin' away - all the while knowing that DH could barely see me and would _not_ be coming in after me. Just got spooked. The water was about 6 degrees colder than I have attempted with a bath, but probably would have been easier if i had gone up to my neck because of the wind. It was pretty cool to walk onto a beach in Boston and drop trau without anyone around.

Anyway, enough excuses. Next time should be a breeze.

Going to CT this weekend and DH promised to take me to a childhood fishing/swimming hole.

# Thread: So California Optimal Ocean Dip
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 264
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-03T19:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21045>

Who is up for a dip in the beautiful Pacific?

I thought we may get the most amount of people involved if we met in San Clemente. I don't know the area at all except for a great place to get burgers! If anyone has suggestions on the beach please make them. I'm pretty flexible on times to meet. I suppose it should be a weekend....Sat or Sun? Which is better?

If anyone is super adventurous on a short notice and wants to do it this weekend, I'm in! Mothers Day weekend is probably not good for most. Or would anyone want to do a weekday? Let's post some preferences and see what we can come up with where we can get the most amount of people.

Who's in?

### neaderthal — 2012-05-04T06:47:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21269>

The Cove @ Dana's Point sounds great! It should be about 63 degrees per: [http://www.nodc.noaa.gov/dsdt/cwtg/spac.html](http://http://www.nodc.noaa.gov/dsdt/cwtg/spac.html)

Without the surf to negotiate, we should be able to enjoy the nice cool water for a long while. A sunset or sunrise to top it off would be even more special.

BTW, May 6, which is this coming Sun, is the next full moon; so, for those of you that enjoy sunset at the beach or evening hikes there's nothing like watching the sunset as the full moon rises. Take the opportunity to enjoy it if you can!

### cuivie@me.com — 2012-05-05T00:16:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21442>

Greetings, all! Long time lurker, first time poster, CT addict.  I live in Pasadena and would love to attend and contribute to a SoCal Pacific CT plunge.

I was at Black's Beach down near San Diego a coupe of weeks ago and went into the water. Overcast skies, foggy, air temp in the lower 60s, gusty winds....and I was the only one in the water, everyone else was bundled up in coats on shore, haha. Funnily enough, the ocean didn't feel all that cold at all.

Would cast my vote for Dana Point, it is indeed quite beautiful and would be a nice central location between San Diego and LA, (though it would be a bigger trip for anybody up in Santa Barbara or farther north).

### neaderthal — 2012-05-05T01:39:47+0300

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-06T00:45:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21698>

CT in the Pacific Ocean....done! Neaderthal and I went to Carlsbad State Beach this morning. It was during high tide and needless to say we got pummeled! It was fun and water temp had to be in the mid 60s. Not that cold for either of us. But I do have to say it has been a while since I found sand in some of those long lost cracks!

I lost a water shoe in the surf and my phone someplace else! Thank you Officer Caldwell for finding it!!!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-06T00:53:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21699>

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/21775=188-CarlsbadCT.jpg)

We both wished for colder water but it was a nice "washing machine" like drenching!

### ColdBren — 2012-05-06T01:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21703>

That is so awesome!! Good job, I hope we get these beach sessions going on a regular basis, hope to meet all the Krusers!

### neaderthal — 2012-05-06T03:02:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21713>

Pummeled is definitely an understatement! We got knocked down face first, then feet first countless times with full body sand exfoliation. Patty Cakes forgot to mention we not only lost a shoe and a phone, but almost lost our pants several times! LOL! Looking forward to the calmness of the cove @ Dana Point. In the interim, we will be enjoying many more beach bloopers, CTg in the Pacific off the coast in San Diego!

### Birdy — 2012-05-07T01:58:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21850>

We went to Nicholas cyn early this morning. It was fun being at the beach, but I didn't feel I got that much cold on. Waves were 3-4 feet and choppy, and I got pummeled (and filled with sand) almost right away. I am not confident I am ready for head-dunks, and that was what was required to get farther out. I don't feel I am a really strong swimmer anymore so this will take some time. The water didn't really feel that cold, though I did get cold hands and feet after around 10 minutes. Everyone on the beach was bundled up, and all the surfers had full suits on.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-07T03:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-21860>

The best part is you got out there! I wonder if your water was much colder than ours down here. I bet it was close to the same temp. Sounds just like the conditions we had. There were too many surfers for us to get out further and I didn't want to get hit in the head with a surfboard yesterday. Did you have water socks/shoes on? The weird thing for me was my feet were fine until I took off the water socks to rinse them out. Within 5 minutes my toes started to get cold and turn white. My hands were fine although didn't feel too cold they had a little trouble working proficiently!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-23762>

As to censorship I'm with Coldbren on this one. He's parsing out Factor X to a select bunch who help support the website effort, so as to save a big reveal for when the book comes out.

Think of it like "spoilers". The TV and movie critics tour the set and promise to keep it to themselves until after the episode or movie airs, because the authors request. It will come out.... In time.... and I'm content not to spill the beans until Jack is good and ready to share with the world via his book... After all he has done so far...for free... I'm not going to give him any crap if he wants the rest of the world to be "spoiler free" for a little while longer.

Anyone considering coming to Dana pt know of a good place for a nice dinner ahead of time, suggest away.....

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-20T07:22:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-25656>

Tapatalk ate my post darnit! Trying again....

Just heading home from the beach, so it was: Krusing and hubby, JohhnyB and wife (non-CT), and joeBranca.

Parking is great.. Beach is small so easy to spot each other. Lots of shelters and bbqs including one with a full on outdoor fireplace up against the bluffs. Protected cove, no waves. Lots of folks standup paddle boarding and kayaking (rentals nearby).

Water supposedly 64 per the NOAA buoy past the jetty, but, felt like 68-70 to me.... Shallow water here gets warmed by the sun. Next time will bring my thermometer! I stayed in a full hour chatting with everyone, got great info on local cow source and eggs too, thanks guys!!

Always great to meet others and hear about their journey to optimal. I look forward to doing this again soon!!

Sorry Jack, no sunset pic... It got real overcast so it just got dark without a light show, besides, for this beach the sun will set behind the bluff . But we do have some photos to post if I can figure out how!

JohnnyB has some photos of the 'amenities' of the beach too for future meetings.

Would be fun to spend the afternoon at the beach, maybe kayak around the cove, enjoy the beach, throw some cow on the BBQ and then jump in for some CT. Can't wait til the water gets colder!!!

It was fun standing there chatting afterwards as passersby walked by all bundled up

Thanks everyone for a great time!

### neaderthal — 2012-05-26T22:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-27698>

I'm a little confused by everyone's use of the term "grass-fed." Southern CA doesn't have the grasslands & pastureland to support 100% grass-fed and 100% grass finished cattle; therefore, the cow's diet is usually supplemented with produce, which is not actually part of the cow's natural diet, from the agricultural crops grown here. That's why the term "green-fed" is often used vs. grass-fed. For example, Sage Mtn, uses the "green-fed" term to describe the diet used for their cattle. Also from the 5 Bar Beef website: "They are raised on natural grasses and browse. Their supplemental feeding consist of crop residue (tomatoes, carrots, brewer's waste and parsley) and/or non-irrigated hay (no alfalfa)- no hard grain is fed at all."

I love the idea of supporting locally raised, & obviously, 5 Bar Beef is far superior & preferable to commercially raised cattle--if just for the fact that the cattle are pastured their entire lives & not in feed-lots, but I believe our nearest source for 100% grass-fed & finished cattle (where their diets do not have to be supplemented) would be cows pastured in central CA.

I only bring this up as it may make a difference to some of you if you are comparing costs and what's important to you, i.e. locally raised vs. a diet consisting of 100% grasses.

### neaderthal — 2012-05-29T18:05:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-28180>

> **JoeBranca;28246 wrote:** neanderthal, do you have any leads on locally raised goat? I love goat beyond words. I sometimes get cut up goat meat frozen from Zion market (Korean grocery store) for a pretty good price; great for stews and curries. It comes from New Zealand which I presume means more like a pastured / meadowed setting but who's to say for sure? Sourcing something local would be great.

Joe, you may want to check with Curtis Womach [http://womachranch.com/](http://http://womachranch.com/)

I believe the last time I went to the Hillcrest Farmer's Market in San Diego he told me that he is now selling goat. There's a link on his website to a nice article Candice Woo wrote about Curtis' farm operation.

This is another good article about locally raised meat: [http://www.ediblecommunities.com/sandiego/winter-2009/meat-gets-local.htm](http://http://www.ediblecommunities.com/sandiego/winter-2009/meat-gets-local.htm)

It's from 2009, but much of the info is still relevant.

I've purchased whole chickens from Da-Le, which are very good. I'd love to purchase a chicken or turkey from Curtis Womach, but I rarely get down to the Hillcrest Farmer's market, & the last time I rode with my vegetarian friend; so, that sort of minimized my conversational opportunities with the meat vendors. Not to mention bringing meat home in her car really wasn't an option. LOL!

If anyone eats pork, Da-Le offers pork as well as grassfed beef & poultry. They are not certified organic, but personally, what's important to me is not the actual certification itself but how the animal is raised and details about their diets. I'd really like to try Da-Le's pork sometime. They offer tours of their Lake Elsinore farm, but require a group of 5, I think.

I like getting pastured chicken b/c I can just slow cook the whole chicken in the crockpot until the bones dissolve & get the benefit of the minerals from the bones. I never do this with a commercially raised chicken though. In fact, last time I purchased a whole Foster Farms chicken, it reeked of formaldehyde. It was very strong, & quite a familiar smell to me since I've spent many hours in gross anatomy labs. I was like, "What the heck? This chicken smells like formaldehyde." Even after I cooked it, it reeked. Formaldehyde is a disinfectant used in poultry operations; so, that last experience made me even MORE skeptical about purchasing chicken in the store.

Whole pastured chicken has gotten more expensive b/c the USDA is gouging the small farmers on the costs of inspections, etc. It's extremely political; so, I try to keep that in mind when I'm making my purchases. AND I make sure and use the whole bird; so, I get the biggest 'bang for my buck' (i.e. save the carcass for homemade bone soup, etc). Actually, I also supplement my dog's diet with bone broths and dissolved carcasses, which seems to really help her digestive system.

Anyways, another option for meat is to find an "old fashion" butcher that processes wild game meats. I've been able to get wild boar chops (great for breakfast, if you eat pork, yum!) and elk steak, etc. They will usually also have ground up meat & organs available as pet food and raw knuckle bones for dogs for REALLY CHEAP. These are not grass-fed, of course, I can barely afford grass-fed for myself, but a nice supplement to dry kibble, in my opinion.

Just wanted to share some ideas for everyone....I've spent a lot of time researching available options over the last several years and becoming familiar with the local produce and meat available even though I'm somewhat restricted in what I can personally afford to purchase. I really like the idea of supporting local farms by purchasing shares of produce or meat (if one can afford to do so). I think we are REALLY fortunate in So CA to have local organically grown produce available for such great prices comparative to elsewhere in the country.

This post is making me HUNGRY. I think I'm going to the Escondido farmer's market today!

### JoeBranca — 2012-05-30T00:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-28245>

various reports from a good beach weekend here in So Cal. Water a bit warmer but spent some time at Dog Beach in Huntington (beach sprints with dogs is a hoot), and also Newport Pier. I like swimming out a bit further but at the pier there was riptide pointed out to me by a lifeguard. Point taken.

At Newport I return from flopping around in the waves, a salty gruffy grey bearded old beach hobo at the pier, in a raspy voice: "That water cold out there, bro??" me, without skipping a beat: "NOT COLD ENOUGH!!!"

Swam out to a rock formation off pirate's cove in Corona Del Mar. The base of it is loaded with mussells! So tempting, but I'm sure there's a city ordinance against harvesting them, not to mention the iffy situation with water pollutants inside the Newport Harbor (who knows who dumps whatever wherever off those sailboats). I'm resigned to fantasizing about picking fresh raw mussells and taking them home to drown them in ghee garlic and basil. Oh well.

### johnnyb — 2012-06-02T21:17:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-29089>

> **neaderthal;27796 wrote:** Also from the 5 Bar Beef website: "They are raised on natural grasses and browse. Their supplemental feeding consist of crop residue (tomatoes, carrots, brewer's waste and parsley) and/or non-irrigated hay (no alfalfa)- no hard grain is fed at all."

He's the story as I understand it. The beneficial o6/o3 ratio comes from the rumen bacteria when cows eat a grass diet. When cows feed on a grain diet the pH, dominant bacteria, and o6:03 ratio all change for the worse. The supplemental feed he uses is grass from the county. The crop browse that are ordinary plants which don't alter the pH, bacteria, or o6/o3 from that of grass. The brewers waste is grain, but it's what I as a homebrewer refer to as "spent grain" where the starch has been converted to sugar an mostly rinsed from the grain by the brewer. What's left is low in sugar/starch, mostly fiber feed which has sugar to fiber ration close to green grass. So, it doesn't mess with beneficial pH, bacteria, o6/o3 ratio. I hope that's pretty much correct. Either way I'll be enjoying the local most grass fed beef for probably then next year. He mentioned that most of the "grass-fed" beef available from the store is still fed with 20% grain since the government regulations allow that.

### neaderthal — 2012-06-03T00:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-29125>

> **johnnyb;29189 wrote:** He's the story as I understand it. The beneficial o6/o3 ratio comes from the rumen bacteria when cows eat a grass diet. When cows feed on a grain diet the pH, dominant bacteria, and o6:03 ratio all change for the worse. The supplemental feed he uses is grass from the county. The crop browse that are ordinary plants which don't alter the pH, bacteria, or o6/o3 from that of grass. The brewers waste is grain, but it's what I as a homebrewer refer to as "spent grain" where the starch has been converted to sugar an mostly rinsed from the grain by the brewer. What's left is low in sugar/starch, mostly fiber feed which has sugar to fiber ration close to green grass. So, it doesn't mess with beneficial pH, bacteria, o6/o3 ratio. I hope that's pretty much correct. Either way I'll be enjoying the local most grass fed beef for probably then next year. He mentioned that most of the "grass-fed" beef available from the store is still fed with 20% grain since the government regulations allow that.

Johnny, thanks for all the info! I love being able to have this conversation with everyone on this thread about these dietary "details" as most of the people I know personally would have no interest as well as no knowledge about the subject of our questions and concerns. I always think when I look around at the very negative & prevalent results of the SAD & lifestyle, "If you want to look differently, you have to act differently." And those thoughts are a little reaffirming as I sport my vibrams, walking around town instead of driving, and open-up a can of sardines for a snack instead of stopping for lunch at a local eatery. However, I must admit that some of my recent diversions back to aspects of the standard american lifestyle that I have always thoroughly enjoyed have me moving towards looking like a SAD follower once again. One of those favorite diversions of mine is great craft beer; so, I have to ask you, since you are a homebrewer, what have you found works for you for including great craft beer in your paleo type diet? After reading your last post, I recalled my roommate brought home a growler of Port Brewing Wipeout IPA last night (at my suggestion, of course ); so, I decided to pour myself about 8 ounces . I like to enjoy a little & I think that's o.k. once in awhile, but I reallly LOVE LOVE the entire brewery scene - the business from an entrepreneurial standpoint, the product etc. I mean REALLY great craft beer is ABSOLUTELY delicious, and it's great to see how some brewers and their businesses have become so increasingly successful over the past decade.

I can go for months and months without, but when I start imbibing, I realize how much I've missed what I really enjoy. I couldn't care less about bread, tortilla chips, popcorn, or common domestic beer for that matter, but a good craft beer?

I hope I'm not making anyone else THIRSTY! Right now with all this beef & homebrewed beer talk, I'm dreaming of a roast from one of those great 5 BarBeef cows, whose image has been dancing in my head ever since I pulled up their website, to accompany this IPA. LOL!

### neaderthal — 2012-06-03T00:18:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-29127>

> **Patty Cakes;29000 wrote:** Neaderthal, sorry to hear you are still hanging on to that ugly sinus infection. Everything goes into my chest when I get sick and it is just miserable.
>
>
>
> Krusing yours sounds awful too!
>
>
>
> I hope you two feel better soon!

Patty, thanks! I'm hanging in there. The good thing is I've been able to use my time at home to spend some much needed attention on financial affairs. The odd thing is I have no desire to get my daily dose of vitamin D, or to CT, or to eat the usual amount of protein that I always crave. It's like I did a 180 overnight. I'm very uncomfortable whenever I go outside in the sun, & the thought of a cold shower, repulses me. I haven't eaten an egg in 2 weeks. It's quite bizarre, especially, considering I usually live to be outside on a great sunny day.

### JoeBranca — 2012-06-03T03:03:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-29150>

I'm no expert but johnnyb's beers are top notch in my experience. I still think fondly of some red ale I had some time back. If I were to go on a suboptimal bender, that would be the way to do it ; )

### johnnyb — 2012-06-03T04:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-29160>

> **neaderthal;29225 wrote:** One of those favorite diversions of mine is great craft beer; so, I have to ask you, since you are a homebrewer, what have you found works for you for including great craft beer in your paleo type diet?

When I started paleo dieting I followed Primal/MDA guidelines doing 80/20 that is to say it fit into the 20% which amounted to acceptable cheating. I steadily lost weight that way. No beer during Leptin Rx. Now its an occasional thing again. Moving forward I'll probably avoid beer in winter and enjoy some in summer. I get over it by savoring the few I still drink.

One hard lesson I have been hit with is that my body is no longer very good at processing alchohol presumably due to how little I drink and maybe ketogenic dieting too. I participate in 1 or 2 beer festivals per year and 2 or 3 parties that involve lots of beer. I now have a 3 driink maximum. It's not so much that I get really drunk but that I get sick the next day even if I never got drunk.

I miss brewing even more then drinking. I have not brewed since last summer. Oh, I have a burbon barrel aged barley wine I'll bring you a bottle of at some future get together if you would like.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-14T04:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-31051>

Right now Von's has Wild Copper River Salmon on sale, I think $10.99/#. We bought some the other day and it was really good. Copper River is only in season for a short time and I know a couple of the Vons here in SD have sold out a few times. My Vons in the middle of suburbia doesn't know that this is prized stuff!! The fish monger at Vons said they get it in every other day. Normally I don't buy my fish there but this really is pretty good and the price is great for CR Salmon.

Anyone looking for great fish in San Diego go to El Pescador in the village of La Jolla. The best and freshest around. Oh and you'll pay for it too but the flavor and quality is worth every penny!

### neaderthal — 2012-06-20T20:05:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-32457>

> **Birdy;31591 wrote:** Now what? [http://www.vcreporter.com/cms/story/detail/a\_radioactive\_nightmare/9886/](http://http://www.vcreporter.com/cms/story/detail/a_radioactive_nightmare/9886/)

I haven't been active on this site lately; so, I'm behind on the posts. However, I came across this article this morning and found it quite interesting:

[http://phys.org/news202456660.html](http://http://phys.org/news202456660.html)

Personally, I appreciated that the Ventura County Reporter even published the article re: Fukishima to which Birdy provided the link. IMO, there's always been too much propaganda in support of the use of nuclear technology; yet, a concerted effort to provide little information to the public in the wake and aftermath of catastrophes caused by its use. There are alternatives, and I believe if the public had accurate and full awareness of all the implications surrounding the use of nuclear technology, many of us _would_ choose to live lifestyles that minimize energy consumption; thereby, making the pursuit of alternative technologies a viable economic option, just as we choose alternative approaches to mainstream medicine. Knowledge is power; however, that is the point--that we remain powerless.

We wield a technology for which we have no effective solutions in the event of a disaster and as the article to which I provided the link shows, a lack of knowledge of physical principles.

Personally, I do wish I lived in a former era...

### Riversedge — 2012-09-13T10:14:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-51398>

JohnnyB say it isn't so? Im happy now I had the surgery it feels better than the other one now. dislocated that one 26 years ago and did nothing for it. Research your surgeon and if they want an MRI get the dye imaging, the insurance will not approve it with a regular MRI.

### johnnyb — 2012-09-14T05:28:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-california-optimal-ocean-dip.1265/post-51606>

I have had two X-rays and two MRIs about a year and and a half apart? Diagnosis is calcific tendinitis. The labrum has a small tear but the rotator cuff looks fine. It's not really getting better. It's mostly fine until I do activity that resembles exercise. For instance swimming leaves the shoulder stiff but not usually painful. The neck muscles that compensate for the shoulder get very stiff and and a lightning rod for headaches. I'm at the point I think I should be doing HIT. My plan is to do some real exercise, use lots of ice and see how my shoulder responds. If that goes poorly then I think I should get the calcium removed. You and success so I hope to learn from you in case surgery ends up being my course of action.

I am afraid I overdid it yesterday. I had trouble getting to sleep and work up tired. It took me forever to warm up. I think next time I go, I won't try to stay in the water as long, just my feet. Watch for me on the Zuma cam lol. Try cams 44,45,46

# Thread: SkywayKate's Journey to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 83
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### skywaykate — 2012-05-03T23:10:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-21116>

Sooooo...I'm here. I'm doing the LR, officially started yesterday.

I'm going to record it all here as I go through this gauntlet, it is a matter of life-or-death. But I am going to do it!!!

After the history, hopefully I can get some laughing going on. Need it to make life bearable.

In case anyone is interested, a little background...

The happy things: 33 y.o. SAHM of extremely active 2.5 y.o. twin girls. Live in downtown St. Paul, MN (for now, we will likely be moving this year). Have a husband very committed to our marriage, bc otherwise the effects of my health on our life would have driven us to divorce by now. He's an awesome father. I do a small amount of freelance small business marketing consulting, graphic & web design.

The not-so-happy: Struggled with extreme fatigue on and off for the time I've known my husband, 10 years. Had my first bought of what might have been chronic fatigue soon after we met (only had catastrophic insurance), gained 50# in 5-6 months because I was eating to stay awake and finish school and work. Dead on the couch by 7pm, not like me AT ALL. Have continued to gain weight over the years, might take a little off here and there but gain it back. Have had times when I had to take 5+ hour naps on the weekends, in addition to 12+ hours of sleep to be functional during the week. Expected that once my girls started sleeping through the night, I would have more energy, but it has only gotten worse and worse. I tried following the Wheat Belly diet for 7 weeks, but didn't feel any better, and knew I could get the lift I needed from carbs to get through the day. Bottomed out around Christmas time, had about 4 hours of functional energy in a day, had to fight to stay awake the next day if I used more than that allotted amount. That means time spent showering, cooking, wrangling my girls to get them dressed, trying to get out of the house, etc.

I got off my 2 high-dose "happy pills" and had my Armour dose tripled in the first part of this year. A very positive salivatory gluten antibody test in mid-April gave credence to my husband that I need to be reforming my eating, not just to loose weight/burn (or CT, as it is) fat off. He finally started to support my dietary changes, instead of no support back in February when I lasted 6 days on the LR. I know I have to do this for myself, but even marginal support goes a really long way when you have this little energy.

Struggle on and off with major depression since the age of 11, but therapy has trained me to cope, along with a lot of work on my own.

Did the candida diet for 8 months in 2001 to clear up painful tonsils and other things, like seasonal allergies.

The unhappy: Besides being super overweight (I hate the word obese, just as a word, but I'm morbidly so), lacking energy even compared to a year or two ago, my BP is high-ish, though I've been able to skirt meds. My family history is horrible...my dad's cardiologist is Dr. William Davis, of Wheat Belly fame, bc my dad was thisclose to having a heart attack at 54, despite generally eating "healthy" and biking 700 miles that summer up to that point (August). His brother, my uncle, died at age 49 with 99% blockage in his corated (sp?) artery (though his life style was anything but healthy), my grandma of a clot-induced stroke at 62, my grandpa of mismanaged T2D at 70, which was 10 years later than anyone expected him to live. A little better on my maternal side, but still enough issues.

Progesterone is low, Testosterone is high but still w/in "normal". Not taking any supplements at the moment but ordering them tonight. Had taken a slew for about 2 months at the beginning of 2009 based on the book by Dr. Stephen Sinatra, Reverse Heart Disease Now, that's how I had enough energy to get pregnant, lol, but most were unknown for safety during pregnancy/breastfeeding so I stopped. Won't do that in the future if I get pregnant again!

Twins are way more tiring in general than I ever expected, and I've done tons and tons of childcare--it was even easier when I had my friend's three in addition to mine!--but I keep trying to figure out what is wrong, thankfully God lead me to Dr. Meyer while researching. Doctors don't seem to believe me, most blame it on my weight, and now more recently, my lack of conditioning. No real help offered. Some testing done, nothing out of the ordinary but no where near optimal. Saliva cortisol test was low, DHEA high, progest low. Low Vit D (25.something).

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

OK, need to go get busy for now, will come back later to add more, including stats and food and stuff.

If you've read this far, wow, thank you!

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### nonchalant — 2012-05-04T00:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-21134>

Hang in there, Skyway! I used to sleep the weekends away, and most weekdays too, for that matter.

Now I can't remember when I took a nap last, probably a year ago. My BP went down to normal, and I got off the presciption med, before I lost the first pound. It isn't necessarily the weight, it may be what you were eating.

Glad you're with us.

\-Sally

### skywaykate — 2012-05-04T03:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-21180>

Uh, was reading thru what I wrote, not sure where Dr. Meyer came from, because I meant Kruse!!

Taking a two min break before pushing myself again to keep going and get stuff done at hone before my parents visit tomorrow for the weekend (they stay with us). Donno what possessed me, but I had a coffee w dinner last night and ended up wide awake til 2. I was anticipating doing tons of laundry, for side tracked by a "discussion" w hubby, ended up starting at 11:45, got 5 done last night. Got 5.5 hours of sleep. Normal is about 8. Did another 7 loads today. We had a huge backlog and the machines in the building are on the smaller side. One set on each floor, so I can do a bunch at a time but still requires a lot of walking!!

The rest of the info (weight, measurements, etc) will have to wait at this point.

\-Kate

### skywaykate — 2012-05-07T01:52:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-21849>

Uh, beware if you're opening a new jar of coconut aminos stored at room temperature. It explodes like a fizzy, dizzy soda...in my case on me, the burner and other parts of the stove. Oh, and some on my meat, too, which is where it was intended. Making a top round steak for dinner instead of hubby's fall back of PB&j on wheat bread for the girls & likely a lunch meat turkey sandwich for himself (I would at least have leftover chicken burgers).

The girls have already had cereal snacks in the snack baggies offered at church, a bunch of popcorn and then graham crackers. Need to get a nuts.com order in so I can make them coconut & almond flour options, STAT!!

It says on the label to store the aminos in the fridge after opening to reduce fermentation fuzz, but I recommend before, too!

Hooray for resisting the urge to munch! I had to admit, it totally felt like magic to me the first few times I was able to just walk right by my beloved Dunkin' Donuts without even a second glance! Not having to fight yourself over whether or not to eat junk is awesome. Keep it up!

### skywaykate — 2012-05-09T16:55:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-22538>

BT (basal temp before getting out of bed): 97.33...much lower than I was expecting

Plan to eat is ELAD (Eat Like a Dinosaur cookbook by Paleo Parents) Eggs in a Nest for breakfast, chicken sausage and salad for lunch, dinner will be shrimp scampi w cauliflower rice.

Had to go to my 4th and 5th store for more Kerrygold butter last night. I had gotten some in the last 1-2 weeks at one of the places I normally shop, but they don't carry it any more, wherever that was. Super annoyed, but at least I know I have to go to Whole Foods, which is just an annoyance, not the end of the world. Picked up 3 of their 4 1/2# in stock, didn't want to take them all in case someone like me was out looking and getting desperate. (And store #4 was according to Kerrygold's website that they should have stocked it, but they hadn't ever heard of it, boo.)

Expecting my bulletproof coffee in the mail today, hopefully! That was part of the reason for tracking down the butter.

Warning, disturbing story about child abuse ahead. Skip to the next paragraph if you want the warm fuzzy part...

Yesterday, a friend on FB posted a link to a news story about a father who broke his 3 mo triplet daughters legs on purpose, and described how they would become so hoarse from crying they would lose their voice and they would turn white from screaming so hard. I cried three times thinking about those girls, my heart aches for them.

Their story, and others like it, are why I need to get healthy, and get my energy back. It has been strong on my heart since I was about middle school aged to do foster care and adoption. This is another reason to to what I'm doing and only adds to my resolve. I need to do it for myself, my own daughters, and for those kids that need good foster and adoptive parents.

Here's to a great day!!

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-09T23:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-22661>

omg, what a heart breaking story!!  How is that possible??

### skywaykate — 2012-05-10T19:33:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-22918>

Today is LR day 10.

Yahooo! Down 8.5" in the last week-- started measuring last Thursday--and that's with a very unusual night out last night (even on dates w hubs we're home by 10:30) where I had 2 pints hard cider and French fries at 11:00pm. Will post the deets later but needed to share.

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### skywaykate — 2012-05-12T19:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-23438>

Thursday evening I ran a cool bath, not very cold for me but cool for most people, and my newly little meat eating Zoe hopped in with me. She's also a fish. She saw me in the tub and wanted in, I put her hand in to help her realize it was pretty cool, but she just gradually got more and more of her body in until she was dunking her head. Cracked me up, made it a lot more fun. My hips, knee on my right leg, and legs in general were sore for reasons I can't figure out (except not enough sleep), hence the cool bath in the first place. I wasn't going to officially start any CTing for a bit, but I'm so estrogen dominant, I might need to do that. Need to do more reading on the blog and elsewhere about estrogen dominance so I understand what it causes more.

As I stated before, I lost 8.5" in my first week of measuring (Thursday-Thursday), days 2-9 of the LR. That breaks down...

Calf: 20" (-.5")

Thigh: 30" (-.5")

Hips: 59.5" (-.5")

Clothing waist: 54" **(-3.5" woohoo!!)**

Upper waist (my Urkle waist): 45" (-1.75")

Band (bra band): 44" (-.75")

Arm: 20.75" (-.5")

Wednesday night I went out with my cousin who was here visiting us from Monday-Thursday. I didn't plan well for food on Thursday and ended up chowing 2 Larabars at almost lunchtime. I also ate mashed potatoes at Ikea, along with some chicken, both of which I went with because they were gluten free (as well as a salad, but the dressing probably wasn't gf). Thursday I started feeling flemy and my tonsils were swelling, which happens when I have too much yeast and/or sugar in my system, and it's still going pretty strong today. I had a small coffee frappuccino from Starbucks late afternoon Thursday. Full of sugar and milk. Had two more Larabars on Friday, one around lunch and one after dinner last night (\*\*\*\*\*\*\*no, I didn't! aha! found the one I thought I must have eaten mid-day this morning so I am amending this entry with this statement). Between the Larabars and the Paleo Parents Pumpkin Pucks from ELAD, I've also had a lot of nuts. So I'm backing up again and making sure to stick with the basics now today so I hopefully can shake this, because I went to sleep last night with a headache too. Yesterday I also ate 4 times, because I was hungry, but I hadn't had enough fats, and had too much sugar instead, relatively speaking here, this is a fraction of the sugar I was having even before starting the reset.

I'm in the second half of my cycle now but I need to re-figure where I am, lost track this week, because then I can pin this also to my cycle. I know that I've always gotten very hungry in the second half of my cycle. Productive-ness with the cousin while she visited but also used brain power that went elsewhere before, like remembering where I was in my cycle.

Going to jot my food down here including amounts, it's been too crazy to keep up with this week but I've been eating like I'm writing it down, lol. All meat is grass fed unless otherwise stated, eggs are organic

Breakfast today was late because I got to sleep in/take a morning nap...

2 5" breakfast sausages

1 HB egg

1.75 "rat on a stick" with mustard added after reheating (the second one had a few bites missing from it) -mini ground meat "loaves" baked on a skewer, shaped to look like rat-ish, from the ELAD book, inspired by Shrek cooking rats for Fiona in the movie. My newly carnivorous Zoe loved these when we had them for dinner. They were about 3.5 oz precooked weight, 1/2 beef 1/2 pork

8 oz sweetened coconut milk

1 pkt SB Via Caramel Coffee (24g sugar)...been waiting a week for my bulletproof coffee to come, need to contact them!!

1 serving "manna junk" as I call it - ~2T coconut manna, 1t+ coconut oil, 1t raw cacao powder, 1/4t maple syrup, microwaved for 25 sec to melt, mix, eat with spoon...this has been an awesome way to make sure I'm satiated after breakfast or dinner.

.5 oz 85% G&B chocolate...need to research this 99% chocolate mentioned, but for a little hit, this is good.

Am thinking HB eggs for lunch/mid-day snack, going to try to not eat as much today in general, and I know we'll have meatloaf today, just a matter of when, maybe an early dinner. Need to hit the co-op for more breakfast meats, and will get more meats, eggs, veg, and...oh yeah, pregnancy prevention comes from the co-op too, lol, that's the other thing on my list. I like the brand they stock best.

My brain is having trouble remembering if I started on Tuesday or Wednesday last week. It wasn't that long ago, but I had it in my head that when I started measuring it was that I started the reset on Tuesday, but now I can't recall. Need to go look back to see if this is Day 11 or Day 12. Not that it matters exactly, just want to know.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-12T21:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-23464>

Ask and ye shall receive...the FedEx package that was first attempted to be delivered on Thursday was my bulletproof coffee, wahoo! Looking forward to trying it tomorrow.

They said it would come USPS so I wasn't in a hurry for the FedEx package, but if I would have known it was this, I would have tried harder!

### skywaykate — 2012-05-12T22:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-23486>

### skywaykate — 2012-05-13T03:20:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-23561>

Dinner: had about 10 oz precook weight ground beef, 1/2 c mushrooms & a few tablespoons onion in a meatloaf, with about 1/3rd cup tomato sauce on top, made from diced tomatoes, paste and a few seasonings like red Thai chili paste, mustard & a bit of coconut aminos. 1/2 c green beans (can't wait for the real thing, frozen kinda suck), 4 oz Izze Blackberry in more sparkling water (10g sugar), 1 oz 85% G&B chocolate bar.

Pissed off my system bc I still have swollen tonsils, snot and another developing headache. Hope to get some probiotics tonight at the co-op. Need to really get on finishing my research and actually order my full compliment of supplements, as well as my blue blockers and temp gun for CTing.

Ugh, it's a few min later and still feeling worse, getting achier. Still have laundry to fold before going to bed, and the co-op run so I can have my BAB with more than just eggs. Maybe a dip in a cool tub will help, need to put kiddie to bed pretty soon (40ish min) and give hubby a break in the meantime, so need to fig out how I'm going to do this.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-13T07:13:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-23611>

Not feeling quite as bad but still not great. Got probiotics, went middle of the road in the amount per capsule to not spend a ton. Also going to try some Bragg's ACV on an empty stomach a few times tomorrow, additionally.

Should be going to sleep but have 4 loads of laundry to fold on my bed before I can. At least got stocked up for the week on my meats and veg. Need to check into purchasing a bunch sometime soon. Should at least be folding by candlelight but too tired to get them down out of their bin, would require a stepstool, etc.

Going to "compensate" by continuing to listen to Kruse's podcast w JoAnne from his podcast page. Love it.

Want to eat, the urge is getting stronger but resisting. Hubby came in to bedroom just now and told me to put the laundry in baskets and hinted that he might do it since he needs to step it up tomorrow since it's Mother's Day, lol.

G'night!

PS had 3 oz or fewer of the Izze after dinner, wasn't feeling it so I just dumped the rest.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-13T21:00:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-23725>

Kate you are making remarkable progress! Love the pics, btw.

Making this changeover can be very difficult but as long as you keep heading in the right direction you WILL get there. I'm starting to notice positive changes in myself and I've been _trying_ to stay on track since last October with the LR and low-carb since a year ago last January. It's getting easier day by day. Stay focused. Course corrections are always gonna happen.

I just did a quick check on the Larabars. They are loaded with sugar. Granted it's fructose but that's still an awful lot. Fructose, in case you didn't know it, is converted by the liver into triglycerides. Too much is definitely not a good thing. /lecture mode

Hang in there kiddo! You're doing great!

### skywaykate — 2012-05-14T04:54:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-23805>

Happy Mother's Day to all the moms out there. And if you want to be a mom but aren't, or have a mom that has been a disappointment, may you find peace.

Had a really good day. Hubs made me bacon, 5 strips, and I had 2 HB eggs for breakfast, along with 2 c Bulletproof coffee & 3T KG butter, 1T maple syrup. Syrup didn't help, so will be omitting in the future. Great church service, then got totally lucky and got this picture of the girls on the lawn after the service, which I promptly ordered an 8x10 of for my mom in Milwaukee as her mother's day present, to try and make up for the fact that I got her card in the mail last night at 10:30 pm, oops. Awe, don't know how to load it here, will add it on my phone later.

Lunch was 3 oz Kerrygold Dubliner cheese (my favorite! or one of them anyway!), a HB egg a paleo PB&J ("graham" cookie bread made of coconut flour, almond flour and a bit of tapioca, oh my was it way too good! recipe from ELAD, surprise, surprise!), and I can't remember what else, grrr...will think on it and add. I remembered when I sat down to write but now I can't.

Had a really nice, 2.5 hour nap. Made me pretty worthless for the rest of the day, but I did make turkey taco meat, ate about 8oz precook weight, with salsa and lettuce and an avocado. Had a few little bites of tortilla chips, there were only little chips left at the end of the bag. Had another "paleo PB&J" (although I know the PB and J aren't very paleo...), done with those for a bit, too addicting and too good, but I needed that!

### skywaykate — 2012-05-16T04:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-24496>

How young is too young to CT??? Had TWO co-CTers with me tonight in the tub, guess I have to close the door when I want to do that if they're still awake....and they had already had a bath tonight!! One was so determined she took off her pants, her diapers (we double at night, helps us all sleep longer) and most of her top. Decided at that point to let the other one in too, who had collapsed in a crying heap when I said she couldn't come in.

Mind you, this was still quite mild, it was a getting-used to it one for me, still a tiny bit of warm water in there, not just straight cold, and it doesn't come out all that cold...cool-ish cold, but not really cold. Ended up in for over 35 min, was shivery by the end after scootching down to get my back in. I can't get my belly in, even if I had one of those overflow drain covers, I'm in a lame, shallow apartment tub. So I poured water over myself for the second half of that. The girls enjoyed bath, one stayed in with me past when I was in.

The good news, I'm down 12 lbs!! I stopped at the doctor's clinic office on my way home from bible study this morning, and I'm down to 297, wahoo!!!!!! That's just 14 days in, today is Day 14. And the amazing thing is that I'm mid-to-late in my cycle, so that doesn't include a loss from the build up to shark week, water retention that comes and goes like clockwork every month--meaning, something that could inflate that number.

Wasn't such a great day on the food front. Breakfast wasn't until 2.5 hours after waking because I need to prep more on bible study mornings. I have a hard enough time getting up that early as it is, already arrive about 2 minutes late every time as it is. Had about 6 slices canadian bacon, which only had about 6 g protein ea, I think if I remember correctly, plus 3 eggs. I could tell I needed more but we had to leave to get my hubby to work. I had started to cook 2 more breakfast sausages but they were going to take too long.

Went to Target and got both Godiva dark chocolate truffle squares and dark chocolate covered espresso beans. Had 3 squares plus 1/4c of the beans, and a hard boiled egg I realized I had with me. This was about a hour after I finished breakfast.

Lunch was two more squares of truffle chocolate, 1/4c feta, can of tuna (is that 5 oz?), 1 c lettuce w "crisp apple maple" dressing by Salad Girl, a local favorite, and 3.5 breakfast sausages, and about 3 oz Izze Blackberry.

My girls napped well today so I also got a 2 hour nap. Been worn out for a couple of days, was out in the sun this morning at the playground too so an early bedtime tonight plus the nap will hopefully get me back on track. I'm still doing way better than even a few weeks ago, not to mention 1-2 months ago.

Dinner was about 8 oz shrimp, a cup-ish mushrooms and some zucchini latkes (ELAD recipe, basically zucchini and egg), all cooked in butter. Made some coconut junk for dessert. Can't believe I'm through my whole jar of coconut manna now, and now I can eat it straight if I wanted. Couldn't stand it plain at first, coconut flavor was too strong, lol!

Am thinking I need to add a little sugar to my coffee and then work on backing that off, because I really enjoy the coffee, it's a ritual I love, I think that that added to today's not great choices, but I can't stand it with only the butter at this point. I can choke it down, but it's such a ritual for me I need to reconsider how to add it back in, but also work on cutting the sugar down. Also, shark week IS coming, so I'm starting to seriously PMS, which means cravings.

I'm also physiologically hungry again tonight, I've eaten enough today, I think my body is just trying to get me to hold on to my fat by saying, "Eat, eat!!" Hopefully I can fall asleep quickly and escape it's pleas!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-16T04:56:44+0300

How young was it to be born in a cave 2.5 million years ago into a world with no light, heat of processed food?........We have the same metabolic machinery and engine. Go for it and don't let your modern thoughts subjugate you any further.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-17T04:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-24788>

Dr Kruse, of course you're right...they will self-regulate and get out when it's too cold, or not get in if it's too cold in the first place. I should know better than this as an interested parent in attachment parenting, unschooling/homeschooling, yadda yadda yadda. I've heard of optimal kids, I should start an optimal toddlers section, lol!

Tense afternoon/evening...one daughter napped 10-15 min in the car after our morning adventures and then pressed her luck much of the afternoon and evening. Hubs then knocked a library book in the toilet I had on the shelf above it. Thankfully I could order a replacement new on Amazon for just over $4 instead of whatever the library will charge.

BAB: 1.5 breakfast sausages, 5 eggs w a few shrooms, onions and about 2-3oz breakfast sausage (the ground kind, not the sausage shape), 16 oz BP coffee w 3 T butter, 6 oz coconut milk and 2.25T of maple syrup...made it much, much better...and the coffee is prob the only way I survived wo a nap after all of our activity this am.

Had a fruit strip about an hour before lunch because I felt somewhat obligated to eat it, we were at a friend's house and they offered it to my girls who weren't interested.

Lunch: 1 c salad, 5 oz salmon from pouch, salad girl dressing; 1 oz G&B chocolate; 2 oz Dubliner cheese, 1 before and one after, thought one would be ok but really enjoyed the second a little after lunch; 4 Swedish fish, 2 as an appetizer and 2 as dessert, none of which my daughters saw me eat, lol

Dinner: 1/3 apple, .75 oz chocolate, 10 oz precooked hamburger in patties with a bit of coconut flour (1 egg amongst 10 burgers, I had 4), about 2 c brussel sprouts tossed with evoo and balsamic, roasted in the oven; coconut junk about 45 min later after I got more manna from the co-op

Had a morning of swimming at a friend's house and going to the playground near there, after a Target run. Was a lot of fun. Am ordering floatation swim suits for the girls in the next day or two because they're too young to really go further in swim lessons (lack of an attention span) to really learn how to swim, can't afford private lessons, and then they can go in the water like they want to, with me nearby but without requiring me to carry them as they go. Our neighbors when I was growing up had them for their grandkids and we thought they were genius. My girls have little to no fear of the water, either, for better and for worse. I wanted to get them training that is specifically for babies and toddlers to learn to float and rescue themselves but it is both very expensive ($600-$700 per child) and long (6-8 weeks) and would require me to stay with my parents in Milwaukee for that long because the closest instructor is there. I inquired about being an instructor, and it would cost me $10-15k. Um, yeah, let me just reach into my back pocket for that...

Also considering either a family Y membership for the summer or a 3 month membership to the city pools (which are a really great aquatic center with tons my girls love, and an indoor place much the same). Pros for the Y are included childcare if I want to work out (although the break would be great, I'm not super keen on "working out" even later this summer--except maybe easy laps, or walking on the track--because I am still working on getting my energy back...but a break would be great!) and pros for the aquatic center is that they love, love, love it.

\--edited to add coffee and cheese to food eaten, forgot on the first go around.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-17T05:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-24798>

Also forgot to add, was really hungry between the tub and bed last night, but thought of it as my body eating my fat instead!

One daughter woke at 2 am calling for me while she was still mostly asleep, but came into our bed after I went to comfort her. I was wired at that point, and unable to do the things I normally do to get myself back to sleep in the middle of the night, like eat or cruise the internet. Not sure how long I was awake but it felt like an hour. That was a bummer to me.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-17T19:37:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-24946>

Day 16 of LR

Down 4" in the past week on 7 points measured. I feel more "bloated" in general today compared with even yesterday, which I think is due to my cycle (usually gain about 6# and then loose it as soon as my period is done).

Now I'm "hungry" during the day. This is frustrating, but since this is just my first cycle while on the LR, I should have expected it...hunger is my #1 PMS/cycle-dependent symptom, followed by crampy muscles (not just lower back/stomach cramps, but all over my body muscle cramps), eventually closer to the time, crabbiness/shorter fuse. I'm on day 26 of my cycle, and prior to 2012 my cycles were quite regular at 30 days. I started on progesterone at the beginning of January, and my cycles were 23 and 20 days. Stopped that and then they've been 35 days. Oy. Hope they are closer to 30 again now so that I'm almost done with this crap.

The wind has really picked up outside, which isn't so cool, makes me nervous about my apartment, since half of it is cantilevered out 12 stories up. Yes, I know we picked to live here but I wasn't planning on (or thought about) crazy winds making me nervous!
