### skywaykate — 2012-05-19T19:13:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-25530>

I thought living in a place where the windows don't open would be ok because we're 12 floors up and the AC will keep us cool. NOT TRUE. Anything over 72 inside here starts sucking the life out of me and we're up to 74. I wish I could open things up at night. The hubs took the girls to the park so I need to take advantage and do a CT tub dip. I think I need to really get going on the CTing because these last few days have been crazy as far as my hormones, my lacking progest and dominating estrogen, I'm on day 28 of my cycle. Hopefully Aunt Flo will come soon, but the last three times have been 35 days, urg.

So Thursday night was an essential oils party at my girlfriend's house. I ate about a dozen and a half tortilla/tortilla like chips and some hummus, a few strawberries, a little Texas caviar. Overall, I did really well, I ate these at one time, didn't graze. Had 1 crystal light appletini, water the rest of the time. Stayed up too late, came home about 11:30 but was wired. Did this really cool bio feedback thing that pinpointed things there was no way of the essential oils seller knowing (my needing/needing to offer forgiveness, the need for creative inspiration, anti-inflamitory "tylenol" need was the top result). Considering their progesterone because I need something, but need to do some more research. Going to get some natural bug repellant and some anti-everything (sickness, fungal, bacteria, etc.) stuff. But of course it's pretty expensive, so I can't plunk down for everything of the minimum I want, need to figure it out.

Yesterday was a crappy day as far as my eating goes...BAB w a Godiva chocolate truffle sq and some coconut manna straight out of the jar, an individual packet of Pamela's gf brownie mix (full of "refined" flours other than wheat) mid-morning, regular lunch (tuna and salad) and dinner (pork chop and bunch of broccoli, a serving of coconut junk), then had 2 more servings of coconut junk, about 3 c GMO popcorn (made at home w CO, a little butter, and too much salt, hubs always over salts, drives me nuts!) and the last Godiva truffle sq, and this was about 8:30 last night. Oh yeah, AND 2 HB eggs. I think I just couldn't handle the perception of hunger/I was hungry because I had the eggs also. And dinner had been earlier, at 5:30, where as the rest of the week I had been eating at 6 or 7. Back on track today, for the most part.

BAB this AM was later since I tried to sleep in but hubs kept letting the girls yell their requests (and they're 2, so this is repeated requests ad nauseum while you pull up whatever TV they're requesting or getting juice in a sippy cup) and repeatedly slam doors for fun. Seriously. I came out pissed because I just wanted 15 more min of sleep and he said, but it's been longer than that (had been about 45 min), and I'm like yes, but I haven't yet gotten my 15 min of sleep! 3 eggs, 4 pieces of bacon-ham as I'm thinking of it, a cross between bacon and ham and I want to call it country bacon but that's not right...went and looked: cottage style bacon. And these pieces are much bigger than bacon, so it was more like 6-8 pieces of bacon, with a ham like quality to it. Also had about 8 chocolate covered espresso beans, 1/2t manna, and am working on the last of a coffee that was 16 oz coffee w just shy of 1 T CO, 1 c unsweetened coconut milk, and 2 1/3T maple syrup. Good news is both kiddos ate sausage and eggs, which is huge triumph, will make getting them healthy options in the morning easier. They were still hungry enough to request cheerios...need to get a remotely healthier alternative, at least a non-wheat source if not a paleo option. Was going to make everyday cookies out of the ELAD cookbook but it's too hot in here to turn on the over. Grrrrrr!!!!!!!

Yea! Just checked the forecast, only 63 for the high tomorrow!

Not sure what I'm going to do for lunch as I'm out of salad and the only packets/cans of seafood I have left are for tonight's dinner of salmon burgers and roasted grape tomatoes and any other veg I can find left in the fridge, I think we have a zucchini. Also going to do sweet potato fries, mostly for the girls, if I can get them to eat them.

Off to CTish!

### skywaykate — 2012-05-19T20:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-25544>

Had 2 visitors again at the end of my CTing, they got back from the park. It was good for them too because they were so warm from being outside. I sat in a tub of the coldest water that comes out of the spout for about 45 min, then took a cold shower that got colder as I went (mixed a small amount of warm in at the beginning so I could shampoo my hair more easily), including 2 min of pure cold at the end on my upper body, neck, head and face. The problem with my tub is that it's so shallow, relatively speaking, even if I had the last 2" of the tub I could fill (glad I can fill it that full and don't need a cover), my belly isn't covered. I got my back and legs in, and the lower part of my belly, and the last 5 min of the tub part I was pouring cold water on my upper body--but it's my belly that needs it, so I'd better figure out what I'm going to do to about icing it. Also need to read all the CT posts, too, and want to re-read the LR ones so I can tighten up my protocol. And then get on ordering my supplements, lol!

Thankfully I can do this while the girls are going down for their nap. Thinking lunch will be HB eggs and don't know what else I can scrounge. Was hoping to wait longer, maybe skip lunch, but the hunger is starting to get to me, especially because I've really been wanting some of my daughter's Annie's Cheddar Bunnies, but I can't have them even if I wanted because of the gluten. Will need to make something grain-like tomorrow to help me manage. Need to figure out veggie-based dehydrator crackers.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-21T07:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-25905>

What's that computer program that blocks the blue light?? I need to get that, now that I'm working on my websites again, including tonight for about 4 hours.

Did another round of CTing last night for about 35 min at about 10:15; I've determined it takes me 15 min to fill my tub, though I'm in it the whole time. I used a candle and dimmed my phone to it's lowest setting to read more CT posts, I'm up to #4. It was too hot to not go to bed without doing it, plus I had to wait for my girls to get into a deeper sleep so I could move them out of my bed, hubs put them down in there last night.

Dinner last night was salmon burgers, roasted sweet potato "fries" (they didn't crisp up because I wasn't patient enough for them to/pan was too crowded), and roasted tomatoes, zucchini, red onion and garlic. The first was cooked in CO as was the latter with some evoo, the middle in evoo. Had 5.5oz salmon (made w co flour and almond flour), 3T ranch dressing (I shouldn't have been lazy and should have added onion and a little feta to the burgers, would have helped me to use less ranch, 1 c. sweet potato, 1.5 c other veg. Other items included coconut junk and 4 swedish fish, 2 before and 2 after, though I was going to have 5 and stopped at the 2nd after dinner one because I need to stop myself from doing this, period. Also had 2 string cheese about 2 hours later because I wanted one, and the other were halves my daughter didn't eat while we were out getting groceries.

Girls let us sleep in until 7:30 am--it was cloudy and they went to bed late--and it was wonderful! Lately it's been about 6, because the light is earlier and earlier these days. BAB was 5 sausages, 3 eggs, about 8 chocolate covered espresso beans, 8 oz BP coffee w 1 T CO and 8 oz coconut milk. AND about 1/2 c cinnamon rice chex. Cause they were tasty; new cereal for the girls. One daughter figured out the 1/2 that have the cinnamon sugar on them--1/2 are plain--ate all those out of her bowl, then hopped over to her sister's chair and ate those out of her sister's temporarily abandoned bowl.

Then comes the extra stupid part of the day. We were gifted a gift card to a frozen yogurt place for our volunteer work in the nursery at church. So after we went to late church, toured the possible place we are going to move to as a church, we went the very short distance to the frozen yogurt place. I had about 10 oz of random crap, plus some toppings, including dried out chocolate chips (didn't know them when I tried), PB cup pieces, and even a few "cheesecake bites"...and proceeded to eat it despite it not being good. It wasn't bad, but not good. Why, oh, why did I do that??? Because I longed for the taste of something similar from my childhood, because I wanted it to taste good, because I wanted to be satisfied by this "treat." THANK THE LORD it WASN'T good, because I'm not going to be wanting that again.

Lunch was a leftover egg from breakfast, another HB egg, 2.5 salmon patties (a mere 2 oz of salmon in that, they were about the size of smooshed meatballs), the rest of the tomato, etc veg, and a serving of coconut junk.

Dinner was the Baja Salad from Wendy's. Too tired to cook. Would have been an ok option until I dumped the little dorrito pieces on top of the salad and was then thinking, why in the world did I ruin this salad (well, it tasted great, it was just a poor choice, especially considering my lunch poor choice)...trying not to have all-or-nothing thinking about food because that's how I get in trouble with my previous history of compulsive overeating. Ate about 1/3 of the cup of chili that came with my salad. The salad did have shredded cheese on it too. Also had about 1/3 of a small caramel shake. Also had about 10 french fries. Again, not good choices, but this time, I really enjoyed them and was ok with those choices, for now.

Need to get to bed!!!!!!! Going to SIL's tomorrow to cook up some meals for her hubs to have in the freezer after she comes home from the hospital. Need to bring my own food with me, but have good options since stocking up last night.

Finally has cooled off in here, I'm shivering a little from sitting at the computer in a tee and skirt, yea!!

### skywaykate — 2012-05-21T19:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-26014>

Getting my 10-15 min of direct sun now, glad I can do it in my bed.

Wrote the above about 7:30 this am, it's a bit after 11 now. But need to get my nephew to the bus stop for school so I will write more later.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-22T22:13:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-26465>

Frustrated. My emotions are all over the place, my patience is thin (not good when your job is taking care of your own twin toddlers...seems easier to have patience for other people's kids), I'm crazy hungry for not good choices, and cranky. I really, really hope Aunt Flo comes soon and this all goes back to being a minimal issue, but I'm afraid it won't be because I'm making these changes and it's hard to wait it out when you feel so intense about everything. And I'm tired too.

The good news is that yesterday I made 9 meals, 8 went into the freezer for my BIL to bake because SIL is in surgery right now to remove her brain tumor. The 9th was supposed to be for us, a roast, but even with 2.5 hours on high in the slow cooker and another hour++ sitting in its own juices on the way home, it wasn't done. But what's good about this is that I had the energy to do this in the first place AND am still functional today. I know this should be a reward in and off itself, and is what's keeping me from totally going off the rails today, but it's still not easy. As Jack said today on facebook, life just gets harder and harder, we just need to accept it (or something along those lines), but this is HARD. Sometimes, just acknowledging that it's hard helps. And makes it more bearable.

My food choices yesterday weren't great either, so I'm sure that's affecting today, it's just not a totally direct link to say so, so I'm saying it's Aunt Flo's pending arrival. BAB was 2 strawberries, the usual 3 (HB this time so I could eat on the go) eggs, cottage bacon, and BP coffee 8 oz w coconut milk 6 oz and CO, and some palm sugar. I'm out of maple syrup so that's subbing for it right now. Lunch was tuna and salad with Salad Girl dressing. Oh, yeah, and before I ate that, I had 2 c of moose tracks frozen yogurt. Now that is some damn good frozen yogurt, the way it should be. Totally loved it. Had a few bites of one daughter's drumstick ice cream cone (well, I avoided the cone because of the gluten, but ate a few bites of the ice cream) in the middle of the afternoon. Dinner ended up being Chipotle at 8:15 pm because of the roast issue (got home really late, then discovered it and had to go get dinner still)...the bowl wouldn't be so bad by itself, but I stuffed my face with chips, had probably 2/3rds of the bag myself. The bowl was easy on the rice and black beans, carnitas, cheese, sour cream, mild salsa and a serving a guac, delicious guac!

Going to go take a little nap, and then go to a playdate at a good friend's instead of cleaning. ;-) Those will at least temporarily help my mood. Dinner is ready since it was done last night after we finished cooking it, though I'm going to steam some green beans to go with it, also have mushrooms and carrots in the slow cooker.

### skywaykate — 2012-05-24T00:12:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-26840>

Happy to report I weighed myself yesterday morning and I was the same as the week prior, but I know that's Aunt Flo bloating (regularly gain 5-6# of water weight), there are definite improvements in my stomach area...TMI, but not as much "twin skin" fat hanging down, and little places where I'm smaller. Yea!!

Still no Aunt Flo. But I think I've turned a corner somewhere because I couldn't finish my usual 5 sausages and 3 eggs, only got 4 in. Also 1 T cinnamon chex and 2 strawberries. Lunch was two servings of coconut junk and about 4 oz of salmon burgers from the other night, with

### chocolate — 2012-05-24T00:25:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-26844>

Just a suggestion... even though doc says no ice with epsoms... get a skinny towel and wear a ratty shirt and dip the towel in epsoms salt water and make a scarf. You'll get magnesium as your skin warms the towel and might take some of you pissy heat away. If your husband is crabby, you might do that to him. Feign compassion (if necessary) in the name of self preservation. My husband would let me compassion myself to death, so I try to minimize. Ya gotta do what ya gotta do. No judging. And have an onion simmering, if you don't have dinner going. I read somewhere that it smells promising and it works. Unfortunately, that solitary onion has become the foundation for several meals. You can make onion fried anything. Fodmaps is now my enemy. And close all the doors. one mess at a time. I've heard of hiding dirty dishes in the oven. It worked for a little while for my teen aged friend.

Chocolate, I love you. These are all awesome! I did a towel soaked in epsoms around my neck and touched it to my face during 2/3rds of my CT. I'm new to the salts, so not sure I used enough or whatever. But for sure at least the humor in what you wrote and the CTing made me feel tons better.

I will write more tomorrow, I need to ride this wave to bed!

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### skywaykate — 2012-05-25T05:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-27261>

I don't know if others have this experience w CTing, but last night I was in an amazingly good mood after my 55 min session, and today I was again, this time after a 40 min session, and not just because one of my 2.5 yo daughters was in with me almost the entire time and having a blast while doing it, even repeatedly dunking her face and head in eventuality.

The good mood is enough to motivate me to do it as often as I can. Went to visit SIL in hospital tonight, she's out of ICU now at least. Going to try to sleep but if I can't, you know I'll be hitting the tub!!!

sent from my phone while wrangling at least 1 toddler...expect typos, smile when you see them...the toddlers or the typos

### skywaykate — 2012-05-26T17:46:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-27641>

Did another 35-40 min tub CT w spout cold only (no ice yet, so it's not as cold as I hope, lol) right after my BAB of 3+ eggs and 4 sausages, a 6 oz container of honey greek yogurt (20 g carbs, mostly sugar) and .5 oz mint chocolate (G&B "dark" but I'm sure it's probably only like 70%)...along with 12 oz coffee, 2T maple syrup, 1 T CO, and 6 oz CM. Same daughter found out that I was in the tub and joined me for 5-10 min. I had the happy endorphins well kicked in by that time plus her general happiness meant she was dancing in the tub, lots of hugging, general fun.

Have the runs, which is sucky. One daughter has had them since Monday but thankfully is otherwise ok. Parents are coming for another visit today...was hoping to go from the last visit at the beginning of the month until we go there in early-mid-June but my mom has only gone more than a month once since my daughters were born between visits. So, time to panic and go clean-clean-clean!!!

Quick notes for later:

\-Thurs evening had ground turkey tacos on tostada shells, had 2 of them.

\-Had chipotle for lunch yesterday, but limited myself to 8 chips total, 2 at lunch and 6 while prepping lunch. Didn't have anything outside of meals yesterday, even had my coffee at lunch, because I want to tighten that up, but had 1.5 oz of the mint chocolate bar at about 9:15 on my way to see my SIL in the hospital. It was still 3 hours before I went to sleep.

\-Lost 1.25"...some measurements I wasn't confident of being in the same place so I just left them as the same--a small move one way or the other would be an increase or a (significant) decrease. Lost 1" off my clothing waist and .25" off my upper waist.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-26T22:58:06+0300

How's your SIL?

### skywaykate — 2012-05-28T19:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-28056>

> **indigogirl;27812 wrote:** Hi skywaykate...I love your screen name! It sounds so Native American/spiritual to me. Could be the name of a great heroine for a children's picture book!

>
>
>
> Edit: just finished reading from your first post! You've come a long way baby!
>
> How's your SIL?

My SIL is home now, she went home yesterday, so that's really good. Thank you for asking! Thank you also for pointing out my progress because one of the reasons I sat down to type right now was because I was feeling frustrated and needed to think through some things and get some things out from ruminating in my head, where they torture me.

And I love your idea of my name, sounds much more grand than I was thinking...in the Twin Cities, in both downtowns, there are skyways, which is what they call the 2nd floor connectors between buildings, they keep you "indoors" and are climate controlled. I think Toronto has some also? Instead of tunnels, they went up. I've lived and/or worked on them for most of my time living in the Twin Cities. But I love the Native American/spiritual idea too.

I still have the runs, and so does my one daughter, she's had it a week now, and just now developed a bit of a fever. So that doesn't help either of us feel better. But at least it hasn't bothered her as much as it has me. I don't want to use imodium because I think my body wants to get it out, right?

I am feeling frustrated because I'm feeling like I'm stalling. I'm still beholden to the Sugar Fairy, and give in to her still too often. Yesterday was a (small) caramel shake, which I didn't quite finish, but I kept feeling a compulsion to drink it on our way home from the pool via Wendy's. This morning, it was a few sips of hub's Mt. Dew. I'm also frustrated because I'm not loosing weight quickly. I know that's not the point of this and I have to look at how much I'm gaining in energy and benefits I can't see (like reducing inflamation), as well as resetting my Leptin, which I know was off, and how long it took me to get to this weight, so it's going to take a long time to get rid of this weight. I guess I got my hopes up when I lost 12# in the first two weeks.

How do I afford supplements to help me when we're barely going to be able to afford groceries til the next paycheck in two weeks? I'm working on getting some of my retirement account funds to help with this, but it'll take a while.

I guess I want to cry. I think I would feel better if I did. Not sure how that fits into todays plans, lol.

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-29T04:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-28113>

I hope you'll feel better soon, and your daughter too!

Be kind to yourself, it's ok if your not perfect all the time. Those cravings will go away. At first, when sugar was calling my name, I would grab a fruit (now, I know better, I grab some fat!). I also know that if I only have a tiny bit of sugar, I'll want more, so I just don't touch it. Give yourself some time and it will become easier. And don't look at how much you weight!! It took you many years to get there, don't put more pressure on yourself. It will take time, but it will come off. You need to fix yourself inside before your can see any significant change on your body. You need to fix every single cell of yours, so you can function like your supposed to, feel good again and then burn the extra fat. Right now, every time you eat sugar, even if it's only a little here and there, it's stored into fat because your body doesnt know better.

If you need to, up the protein in your BAB. It helped me much too. I was having 85g of protein. After a few weeks, I went down to 75 and I'm still there. I don't eat much at lunch, and have a bigger dinner. But the key point is the BAB. And I'm on a budget too: canned sardines, makerel and salmon are pretty cheap. I know, it's canned, but for now I think it's better than regular meat....And eggs too, if you're not on the AI protocol.

Hang in there, and have a good cry, you'll feel bettter after  Sending you a hug!

Katy

### indigogirl — 2012-05-29T06:31:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-28140>

Ditto what Abetterme said!

Hope you're feeling better.

For me eating anything with sugar or carbs, but especially sweets makes me crave more. It takes a few days to get it out of my system to the point where it's not calling me. I drink seltzer that I put flavored stevia in when I want something sweet. Eng toffee flavored stevia is my favorite. I buy it at Whole Foods and it's liquid. I also sip iced green tea all day long with a little stevia to sweeten it.

Try carrying coconut manna with you and having a spoonful if you're hungry or craving something sweet.

Don't sweat the supplements at this point! You're eating well...on a tight budget one needs to prioritize...

I asked Dr Kruse why some of us feel so good after CTing and he said it reduces inflammation and helps with depression (see The Ask Jack thread a couple of days ago).

### skywaykate — 2012-05-31T16:15:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-28562>

First month results: 15# lost and 14.25" gone (including 1.5" off my hips and over 6"!! from my waist). I measured my calf, thigh, hips, clothing waist, upper waist, band (just under bust), and arm. Last week I added my neck and I lost an additional .25" on that. But more than all of this, although I'm super happy about this, is the increased energy. I'm no longer a zombie the vast majority of the time. I'm a generally functioning human being! I hadn't gotten any help from doctors, real, true help that had made an actual difference in my life like this. And I've been far from perfect...but I'm perfection in training, lol.

Thank you, Dr. Kruse for saving my life and my family. It has been a very long time since I've been this excited about the future.Thank you also to my friends on this forum, your help has been invaluable!

Need to go get ready for my day but will write more soon.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-28581>

I measured my calf, thigh, hips, clothing waist, upper waist, band (just under bust), and arm. Last week I added my neck and I lost an additional .25" on that. But more than all of this, although I'm super happy about this, is the increased energy. I'm no longer a zombie the vast majority of the time. I'm a generally functioning human being! I hadn't gotten any help from doctors, real, true help that had made an actual difference in my life like this. And I've been far from perfect...but I'm perfection in training, lol.

YAY!

I know you were talking about having a lot of sugar craving issues, still - are you getting enough fat, especially with your BAB? I resisted the bullet-proof coffee thing for quite a while because it just sounded weird to me, but once I started added 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and a tablespoon or so of butter to my coffee in the mornings, my sugar cravings died right off. Nowadays, if I'm going to eat something higher-carb, I try to eat a large amount of fat with it, as that keeps my blood sugar from going right through the roof.

I'm so glad you're feeling better!

### skywaykate — 2012-06-04T03:43:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-29304>

Already the 3rd of June and the last I posted was 4 days ago. I'm struggling to find myself and getting overwhelmed with life. I don't want this to just be a journal of the highs and lows, but everyday life, but I was avoiding writing about it the last few days and now it's weighing on me. I ate kinda cruddy, mostly some potato chips, dairy (cheese for munching, and dip for the chips) and a bowl of gluten-free brownies (a few days ago at this point), munching on too many strawberries while waiting for dinner two nights in a row...not horrible, but too much margin creeping into life. Eating 4-5 times a day because of weird schedules and fear of lacking. Even though I'm not going to starve, I'm totally worried because we're broke until Friday and I can't just go out and buy more breakfast sausage or coconut manna. So even though it's probably going only be a single day, at the most two, that I don't have everything that I need, I'm freaking out. It's a bad symptom of the underlying anxiety I feel, and makes it hard to be rational about food, about life.

I am pretty sure that marginal eating is leading me to have less energy, a backslide toward where I was a month ago, before starting the LR. That scares me. It's been hard for hubs to deal because I haven't kept up on cleaning around the house, and he's obsessively focusing (it seems to me) on his freelance writing and that it's annoying to me, especially when I wish he was looking for a different job so that we could move to Milwaukee, or at least afford all of our bills. We need to move one way or another to get to that point. I'm sharing too much personal stuff but this really is my journal right now--because I see the pastured meats I'm buying as one place he's going to say, see, we need to cut down that expense. But honestly, I see it as a health expense, not a food expense. This is going to get me to the point that I can be a fully functioning member of society, of my family. I was on my way but got a bit derailed. Semi-derailed, not all the way, mostly not actually. But no CTing, either. Need to do that tonight. And I was thinking about, if either or both hubs and I are freelancing and we don't have insurance through a company and need it on our own, I will be too expensive to insure at my current weight--or anything near it--and health. Scary.

I haven't felt like I'm being drained by all three of my family members at once until today. And letting them down. And letting myself down.

I have to figure out how to get my BAB earlier, it's ending up on average, 1.5 hours after I wake up, often followed by coffee (with CO, a little sugar of some kind, and coconut milk, so it's not calorie free). Breakfast was 3 of my usual sausages, 2 hamburgers (about 6 oz total, precook weight I guesstimate), and 3 HB eggs, along w a smallish, relatively speaking, coffee. Today I had some coconut junk about 2 hours after finishing my coffee, with the hopes I could skip lunch. Ended up eating some salad, 2 HB eggs and .5 oz 85% chocolate about 2 hours after that. Dinner was a bunch of meatloaf (beef, carrots, onions, almond flour, an egg) and peas. Had 4-5 chocolate chips, 60%. I'm out of my bar chocolate and although the chips don't taste all that great (which is a HUGE triumph, I could inhale a bag in 1-2 days), the bit of dessert makes me feel not deprived, mentally.

Keep trying to hit reset but keep falling into potholes. I am going to try again tonight.

### chocolate — 2012-06-05T03:13:48+0300

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-05T07:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-29502>

Another idea is up your seafood.... Cod is great... Lots of good frozen stuff at the big box stores. Jack has said here somewhere that seafood is the base of his food pyramid and is even more optimal than any Grassfed land animal. So if you are eating seafood 3-5x per week you eat less Grassfed meat.

Do what you can, where you can and make sure your BAB has enough protein. As soon as I did that it was only a few days before it was easy to go with only 2 meals a day....

I understand the sugar and junk food cravings. I'll share what's helped me most (besides the BAB)... Is reading what sugar and grains DO TO YOUR body to keep it inflamed, you fat, and your liver u happy. For me, KNOWING the consequences of putti g junk in me has been very effective at not letting any in. To accomplish that, I read this blog voraciously... And read and re-read the reasoning on why grains dairy and legumes (not to mention sugar) are NOT MY FRIENDS!! The more I read and internalize it, the easier it becomes to just say no.

The other tip I have is I 'obsess' over what delicious things I 'can' eat and try to fix a few new things each week and if we love it, it goes into our 'rotation'. Lately I tried crispy roasted brussels spouts with lemons ... This way you never feel deprived as you are always making new and exciting g things to eat!

Hope some of this is helpful and good luck to you in your journey...!! Start shopping for cheap seafood on sale and stock up that freezer!!

### skywaykate — 2012-06-06T18:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-29738>

> **chocolate;29560 wrote:** Is your air conditoner individual or integrated as part of the building? You could mickey mouse/ senor rat a fan in the ducting to pull a little extra.... or you could add a few fans anyway.

We can technically control the temperature, to a point. But the vents are up above 8' (exposed pipes, 14 foot ceilings in much of our place, so we can't really reach the vents. I did buy a fan when the AC hadn't been turned on, but I need to think to use it, at least at night if it's too hot. We do have a ceiling fan in our bedroom which I love. The rest of the apartment is one big open room, and with toddlers running around mean I'm limited in what I can do.

### skywaykate — 2012-06-06T19:02:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-29741>

> **indigogirl;29558 wrote:** So true, Ealachan!
>
>
>
> If you can't afford the pastured meat and things like organic eggs right now it's still better eating Paleo than not. Grass fed beef is about 2X the price of regular. Do what you can.
>
> You've got a lot going on. My kids are grown...I really feel for you doing this with young twins!
>
> Keep taking little steps you'll get there!

Thank for the encouragement, Ealachan and indigogirl!! I am picking back up. I finally feel a little better today but I think I just need some extra sleep. Been at my SIL's helping with her kids this week because her hubs had to go back to work. So I have to get going in the morning, bring stuff for lunch to make for me and for the kids, plus dinner to prep/cook while I'm there to be ready when we get home. It's about an hour each way, plus extra to drop my hubs off at work and picking him up in the evening. I'm glad my girls get to hang out with their cousins, who are 3 yo twins (boy and girl) and 6 yo boy.

I had an inadvertent expirement yesterday. In the afternoon I had a bad headache and was just wiped. I left earlier than normal and had my girls sleep in the car for their nap. On the ride home and then after dinner, I chowed on cinnamon and chocolate rice chex. I couldn't figure out wherre the compulsion came from, it was kind of a disaster, but later in the evening, I realized I had no added fat yesterday...HB eggs and sausage for breakfast, but neither had any added fat, and I didn't get around to making my coffee (which I usually put CO in) because the night before had been so rough I had tried to get a little extra sleep I ended up with 30 min to get ready. I had some leftover ground beef burgers, maybe 5-6 oz plus some salad. Although on the way out to my SIL's I snacked on some manna, I normally melt CO in it, but I had been just eating it straight out of the jar in the car. I had a little junk in the afternoon of my coconut junk (so with the CO), and dinner was a porkchop, salad and cabbage slaw (good flavor...lime, mint and cilantro!), cooked in some EVOO, but not much. So I had had significantly less fat and that's why I wasn't satiated and ate the cereal. Fixed that, back on track today, already had my coffee and used CO with my breakfast ground sausage and eggs.

>
>
> Do what you can, where you can and make sure your BAB has enough protein. As soon as I did that it was only a few days before it was easy to go with only 2 meals a day....
>
>
>
> I understand the sugar and junk food cravings. I'll share what's helped me most (besides the BAB)... Is reading what sugar and grains DO TO YOUR body to keep it inflamed, you fat, and your liver u happy. For me, KNOWING the consequences of putti g junk in me has been very effective at not letting any in. To accomplish that, I read this blog voraciously... And read and re-read the reasoning on why grains dairy and legumes (not to mention sugar) are NOT MY FRIENDS!! The more I read and internalize it, the easier it becomes to just say no.
>
>
>
> The other tip I have is I 'obsess' over what delicious things I 'can' eat and try to fix a few new things each week and if we love it, it goes into our 'rotation'. Lately I tried crispy roasted brussels spouts with lemons ... This way you never feel deprived as you are always making new and exciting g things to eat!
>
>
>
>
>
> Hope some of this is helpful and good luck to you in your journey...!! Start shopping for cheap seafood on sale and stock up that freezer!!

Thank for the suggestions, Krusing!! I do need to stock up on seafood and figure out how to cook it well...I think I end up distracted by my life and don't want it undercooked so it ends up a little tough. I need to come up with that list of things I can have to obsess about. My BAB is usually good, and with some CO in my coffee I am usually good. But I need to figure out how much protein I'm having, I haven't done the math with some of the stuff I eat. I do have tuna at lunch, that's cheap and easy.

I need to remember what grains and sugar do to me, thank you. I have a sense but need to keep that in the front of my brain. Thank you, again, for your storpping by and your ideas, I appreciate it very, very much!!

### skywaykate — 2012-06-09T14:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-30256>

Stepping up my reset as of today. Was pretty loosey-goosey this week, ate basically Paleo but I didn't follow the no snacking rule much at all. I also had a boatload of ice cream last night, which is giving me stomach crampiness this morning. But I knew going in it's going to pretty much be the last ice cream I have for a long time, I need to tighten things up in general, and move toward the AIP. I've always had a suspicion of leaky gut and although I've never confirmed it in many of the ways people talk about, I do have gluten intolerance in my saliva test (negative on the blood test) and my hope is that by clearing things up for a while, I'll heal it so I can go back to eating that stuff at least occasionally (not grains, but eggs, tomatoes, etc.).

The hardest is going to be the eggs because I eat copious amounts of eggs, and have for many years. Tomatoes will be the second because who doesn't love tomatoes in and on things, or tomato sauce on your meat?? Yeah, just have to find alternatives and continue wrapping my brain around this.

Another thing is that I think my tiredness is related to my insulin spikes. When I eat things that spike my insulin, I get tired, a little right away, a lot over the course of the next day or two. This is just a hypothesis I'm working on.

I also am going to work hard on getting my meal at least started within 30 min of waking, I did 40 today, which is a 50% improvement from my usual 90 or so min. The sun isn't over the building next to ours so I can't sit in it yet. There's a thread that has really helped me to understand things on a deeper-into-my-brain level, "Obesity is a disease of inflammation and hormonal disruption of hormone signaling." So that's a major reason why I'm digging into the reset deeper.

We are going to my parents for the next 9 days, leaving in a bit. We're not going to leave as early as we had wanted because hubs has to go back into work to tie up some loose ends and I've got to finish cleaning and packing. We were both too tired last night to do so.

Breakfast was 6+ oz ground pork breakfast sausage plus 3+ eggs. I made a pan of 10+ oz and 6 eggs and had 2/3rds of it, left the rest for hubs. The bad thing about eating before the kids get up is that they're not eating off my plate and less likely to get good protein as a result. Need to figure that out, also, though I suppose most days I'm not up before them, they are my alarm clock.

Off to make some coffee and keep cracking on this getting ready to leave business.

### skywaykate — 2012-06-23T01:08:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-33057>

I need help. I need advice. I am scared, of myself. I contemplated my own SOS thread last night but I was just trying to resist the suicidal thoughts and the self-hatred and the desire to binge on SOMETHING (preferably ice cream, since gf cookies aren't as good as oreo cookies...but \*\*\*\* it if I'm going to binge, who cares if it's gf??? or even just cheap cheese from a regular grocery store). Although a lot of it has subsided, I feel it all still brewing right below the surface and I'm scared. I am going out with girlfriends for dinner and feel strong urges to drink heavily, at least for a bit until I force myself to sober up to get back home. And spend money with little regard. It feels so free to spend money without regard, until you're shackled later by lack of it.

I'm not actually suicidal, I won't act on it. I still can hold on to knowing I don't want to do that to my girls. They really have saved me, because they keep me from seriously considering it, and I know I don't want to pass my food issues on to them, so I went through an eating disorder program (well, part of it, I couldn't get to the extra stuff outside of therapy and the dietician because I didn't have child care) for compulsive overeating. I think it was the fear of God that kept me from purging in my life, but I had a high ratio of girlfriends, from different parts of my life, that struggled with eating disorders.

But the thoughts and self-hatred--something I haven't felt in a very long time to this degree and honestly thought I was moving away from all together--still rage. I feel so unworthy of breath, I can't believe how lucky I am to have a wonderful daughter in my arms while I type this (split the computer screen, she gets to watch Curious George, lol, and she was the one who climbed into my arms, I didn't suggest it). I know I've let my husband down so much in my exhaustion and I weigh so \*\*\*\*ing much that I can't be remotely attractive. I'm still 120# more than when I met him. He's occupied with his day job and his attempts to be a writer anyway, he can't be bothered. I asked him for a hug last night just to try and get things a little more bearable. Two nights ago, all I fantasized about was being thin. I thought it might be different because I didn't think, if was thin the rest of my life would fall into place like I did for so many, many years growing up. It would just be magical to be free of my body and it's major problems. For so long when I was young, it was about wanting to be loved, a guy. Somehow I got a great guy and was married at 23. It was 2 weeks after I had finally learned to love myself that I met him.

I didn't even feel worthy enough to pray. I read some devotions and a few passages to find some relief, which I did. I know it's by the grace of God that I'm still walking the earth (though I can't fathom why) because otherwise I would have ended my life at age 11, during my first major depression. I know it's hard to undo 22 years of struggle in a few weeks, but I've been through a ton of therapy, and before yesterday, that really was helpful. Not yesterday. Didn't matter a bit, except that I knew I had to make choices to get out of it instead of continuing to wallow in the deepest depths, scaring myself.

I didn't meant to be away from here for 2 weeks, at least from my journal, I kinda kept up with other people and the blog posts. I did well on vacation, overall, and had incredible energy...the highlight was on 5.5 hours of sleep after a night of going out (and a day in the sun at a kiddie amusement park), I was functional all day and not dying for a nap, but took one for an hour so that I could be up late at another party. But I had 2.5 mai tais in an hour and a half before that short night of sleep (and I sure can't hold my alcohol like I used to!), and then went out for a full mexican meal at 1 am. Earlier in the day, I had nuts even though I have been trying to give them up and move towards AIP. I ate a boatload and a half of popcorn and 8 marshmallows at the party Saturday, I was loosing control. I had a small Starbucks and a medium later in the day on both Friday and Saturday. On Saturday, I chose the Starbucks over custard ice cream because I wanted to resist. (It probably would have been a better choice because at least it had fat in it, but I was emotional after seeing my grandma treat my aunt crappy and then turn around treat me like you would expect someone to act during my visit, but yet it not be good enough, that I didn't bring the girls with me to visit, that I could only really stay an hour, etc.) I had a small custard ice cream on my way home from vacation, and then consumed another 3/4 of a quart when we got home, around 9 pm. I had some non-wheat grains this week, sort of lasts before I give them up for a long while. Rice crackers, some GF pancakes from a mix, etc.. Loosey goosey on snacking. I was really tightening things back up yesterday but then I ended up going for a 1+ mile walk with a daughter (well, on the way there we went as a family but one didn't want to stay so hubs took her home), with a stop in between for an overrun with kids park. Normally not a problem, but these are somewhat neglected kids who ask a lot out of any adult that shows interest. And I ended up walking home with her on my shoulders so we could get home before the sun rose again, she was taking her good sweet time walking and stopping and lollygagging. But I think the kick in the balls was that I went in these new but dumpy looking shorts, and there were CO stains on my shirt and I had my tennis shoes on. I was looking forward to just watching my girls play on the rocks in a closer park, but we paraded through a very full park, in front of everyone because that was the shortest route and my hubs was leading well ahead of me, people out to watch a band. My raging insecurity came totally flooding back. My legs have been aching for a while. I finally got a 35 min CT in the tub last night. (I did 2 great sessions in 55 and 60 degree water in my girls pool on vacation, at least.)

Oh yeah, and a check from my 401K wasn't there on Monday when we got home as expected, for some reason the mail sent it back but no one can tell me the problem. So I have to wait longer. I am using some to get me some supplements and some on my business, and the rest is being stashed so we can move this year. And I'm out of my armour because I was taking 3+3 per day but the doc (the super lame 2nd endo I saw, not the original doc who upped my dose in Dec) wrote it for 3+2, and the pharmacy I have to use is totally lame and had to fight with them and it takes 24-72 hours to get an insurance exception. I found a few I had stashed in an unused purse and had 3 yesterday and 4 today.

And I really felt like I was on a good path. I was working on cleaning things up in my diet, I'm meeting my girlfriends to, among other things, talk through my next steps on my business plan that I'm moving forward on. But one invited her coworker and her coworker's friend along, and I am scared because I feel so broken and insecure and don't want to be around people where I have to put up a front and pretend to be ok. I didn't write out my plan like I promised I would before meeting tonight, one girlfriend has volunteered to help keep me accountable. I got overwhelmed by cleaning our apartment this week. I am back again after a decent day yesterday.

I am planning on posting my labs (from November thru April, what I have) to get feedback but I need to shower and get the dishes done before hubs comes home, especially if I am going to get anything down out of my head onto paper before I meet with my girlfriends and try and be reasonable about deciding in advance about how much I'm going to drink tonight and what I'm going to eat.

Ugh.

### chocolate — 2012-06-23T03:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/skywaykates-journey-to-optimal.1271/post-33087>

Skyway, when I was raising kids, my husband would have been more helpful if I knocked him out with a frying pan. We survived and even flourished, somehow. My kids were close together in age as well. This will work very much to your advantage soon. The meds stuff is so upsetting. I had a nerve pill... not prozac, but something after months of no sleep form an auto accident... i can't remember.... and went off it. I was heading to my car in the desert... (rough first year)... lots of snakes and wild stories about how deadly this particular one was... I screamed so loud when I stepped over a snake in my bare legs and sandals that my neighbor came running with a gun...literally. That medicine will mess with you. If you brainstorm your business plan.... then have your friends brainstorm with you.... you can organize it at home... you just need ideas and thoughts... you know how to fill in the blanks... We've all had open book tests.

Just because you have something broken somewhere inside doesn't make you a bad person. It doesn't make you unworthy of love and respect (from yourself and from others), and it certainly doesn't make you unworthy of your God's love.

Hang in there, girl. Fight the good fight, and when you feel like you're not up for fighting, don't be afraid to reach out and let someone know that you're tired and hurting and need support.

# Thread: Hack My Mud Run Please :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-mud-run-please.1272/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Evo Mama — 2012-05-03T23:11:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-mud-run-please.1272/post-21118>

Hi all! ...

Thanks!

### Barry — 2012-05-04T12:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-mud-run-please.1272/post-21278>

> **Evo Mama;21193 wrote:** Hi all!
>
>
>
> I'm doing a 5k mud run, including 11 obstacles, this weekend (called Dirty Girl).
>
>
>
> I've been eating Keto-Paleo and doing the Leptin Rx and CT protocols since March 6. All carbs come from non-starchy vegetables. Just this week, I started adding berries (and one upripe banana) in the form of a morning smoothie. I did this earlier in the week and felt good ... no excess hunger or blood sugar issues. I had another one this morning. My smoothies consist of 1 unpripe banana, a handful of berries (raspberries & wild blueberries), coconut milk (minus the water), 1 tbsp. almond butter, high vitamin butter oil, MCT oil and an egg yolk (mixed in after blending).
>
>
>
> Anyway, because I've been doing the Leptin Rx, I haven't really been "training" for this run (since the Leptin Rx requires no exercise). I actually interested to see how my body will respond without much training. That said, I've done a few small runs (a mile or two) over the past two weeks, taking a cold bath after, and I haven't noticed any DOMS to speak of. I've also been testing and have noticed my muscle strength improve since starting CT.
>
>
>
> My question is ... what the heck should I be eating day of race?
>
>
>
> What I should eat the morning of the run (12:30 race start)? I'm wondering if I should do the smoothie to make sure my muscles are full of glycogen, and then just have ample amounts of fat through the morning to actually fuel the run, or if I should just have my usual VLC BABs (i.e. grass fed meatballs, eggs/bacon with liver, salmon, etc.).
>
>
>
> Also, should I try to be in ketosis for the run? I've never done that before, but I think I'm generally in ketosis most of the time these days (due to being keto-Paleo for the CT protocol).
>
>
>
> If you were in my running shoes, what would you eat to PB your mud run?
>
>
>
> Thanks!

Evo Mama,

I'll try and help you here. I will say straight off the bat that your nutrition is not really going to be determinant of your performance. Simply, if you haven't trained for it than you cannot expect to run a PB. In order to properly race a 5km, you would need to do a combination of endurance, threshold, tempo, interval and neuromuscular exercise. I hope I'm not stating the obvious here, but doing 1 or 2 miles jog is not going to really going to enhance performance and promote all the various training adaptations need .

In terms of "race day" nutrition, as I mentioned, its not going to make a huge impact. In order for an athlete to properly keto adapt, you are looking at a 24-36month time period. If you want to race at the upper end of your V02 max (i.e. run almost flat out) using fats, your fat adaptation needs to be seriously upregulated by long term ketogenesis, CT and my one (fasted state training). So in your case, given you are recently keto adapting, you may not be able to race using fats as your only fuel, again, it will depend on your pace and speed.

Realised I haven't given you much advice so far but just trying to help you understand ! So racing at 12.30, 5K... in terms of fuel, you will naturally have glycogen in the muscle from preceeding days carb intake, you will have IMTG in the muscle and of course, adipose tissue fats. The energy requirement for a 5km race is not going to be much more than a few hundred calories. Therefore, in terms of "fueling", you will have plently of fuel from the way you currently eat, no additional fuel is required. In terms of your breakfast options, your smoothie or BAB will work fine. Again, for this distance and for the level of fitness/experience you are at, nutrition on the day is not going to dictate your performance. Really, its about what you feel good on and what foods don't give you any GI distress when eaten before a run. Your smoothie sounds good, and maybe the bit of glucose/fructose from the banana and certainly the MCT, will give your liver and muscle some direct sources of ATP.

Ultimately though, just go out and run and enjoy yourself ! Honestly, thats the most important thing to focus on, the type of meat or smoothie you have a couple of hours before the race is not going to make much of a difference. I don't have any breakfast before my 100mile races so I reckon you'll do the 5Km well without any problems ;-)

best of luck

Barry

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-04T12:15:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-mud-run-please.1272/post-21280>

Before the race I would eat large meal of protein and fat 12 hours out.......right before the race I would have large cup of hot broth with two large scoops of CO melted in it. After the race I would use the broth trick again or use green tea with CO melted in it......I would also probably eat a pound of bacon post race.

### Barry — 2012-05-04T15:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-mud-run-please.1272/post-21290>

If you want an added kick right before the race and don't mind a coffee flavoured broth, throw in a double espresso ;-)

### Evo Mama — 2012-05-04T15:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-mud-run-please.1272/post-21293>

Thanks, gentlemen. I really appreciate all of the information and advice.

Barry - I probably should have mentioned that I'm not really out for a PB (esp. since it's my first mud run, so whatever time I come in at will be a PB, lol), and my main goal is to have fun. That said, fun for me is still kicking a little ass, 'cause I'm competitive in nature.

Definitely going to try the broth with CO/MCT pre- and post-race. Sounds like a solid plan. I'll plan on making some green tea macha for post-race, as well.

I think the plan is to eat sashimi post race, but I'm HIGHLY tempted by the bacon recommendation.  I'm also hoping to find a cold body of water to jump into post race to help cool the inflammation. The race is in the Poconos, for those familiar with that area.

Thanks again. I'll post an update after the run!

### Evo Mama — 2012-05-06T01:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-mud-run-please.1272/post-21701>

I had an amazing run. It was a blast to get dirty and test myself. Your advice was spot on. Can't wait to get home and jump in some icy water, though.

# Thread: CRP and birth control
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.1273/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BeingVenus — 2012-05-03T23:52:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crp-and-birth-control.1273/post-21130>

So after a year and two blood tests showing very high CRP (11 in the one a few months after my c-section and 7 a few months ago) I read recently that birth control and IUD's can cause elevated CRP (pregnancy does too.) I recently found out that my IUD was protruding (I was expelling it so they had to put a new one in OUCH!!) so now I'm not quite trusting what my inflammation read out was. Just wanted to point this out to any of the ladies here using these methods. It is making me question whether using the copper iud is a good choice, though. I wonder if chronic minor inflammation of the uterine walls is a good idea now (though, weighing that against the cortisol elevation from an unwanted pregnancy, hahahaha.) I was just trying to take a non-hormone route. I take a little extra zinc picolinate and eat plenty of red meat and offal to keep my balance in check. Anyway, as always, context is everything.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-04T04:58:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-youtube-video-challenge.1274/post-21227>

I think you can't lead with "sit in ice water, the fatter the longer!". Ease em in slowly. I think many of the low carbers are just as dogmatic as CW, but they really are in need of some pedagogy on the seasonal nature of our biology, including how our cells change with season. . explain how in summer our bodies are prepping for the cold that never comes. These are people who think they are science literate and reasonable so give them some education.

I think if these are people taking a cruise to see Jimmy Moore, they also are not in perfect health and probably somewhere deep down realize there is a NEXT step. Don't tell them they are wrong, explain how your theories build on Low Carb. . moving to "more seafood" instead of "cutting X and stop doing X" . . and I think the sheer logic and "ah ha" of seasonal eating will resonate. I don't like bagging on other people - but Jimmy does not seem to be a picture of health. . and I bet there are many people there in the same boat if they have been living the same way. It should be easy to make the suggestion to the audience about THE NEXT STEP ON THE ROAD TO IMPROVING HEALTH. Give them an invite to try something to improve on where they are now, not rag on them for doing things wrong. You must give people a way to digest this info without beating them about the head with how wrong they have been doing it. Human nature must save face.

Also, "Kills Cellulite" is a pretty good hook.

Then you can go on to explain, IF you are suffering from neolithic diseases - then you can help yourself reverse and improve by utilizing a therapeutic winter to reverse the damage they are suffering from living in summer all the time.

You must send us some pics and interesting tidbits.

Also, I think you should END the talk in the cold water. This is a cruise ship, there are ice makers out the WAZOO. . .ice won't be a problem. Then offer a little primer on how to start with the face dunks. Or perhaps they can roll out an ice machine and you pop out of the ice. . . some twisted version of a stripper popping out of a cake!

YES. You have one person right here who knew what to do for four years, but couldn't make it happen because of cravings. This is common, especially among LC'ers who are doing it for weight loss - there seem to be a higher percentage in that crowd who have cravings.

The LR SHUT DOWN the voices undermining my success. Shut it down!! What I wouldn't have done to have heard this five years ago!

# Thread: Found an awesome doctor!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-an-awesome-doctor.1275/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-05-04T03:43:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-an-awesome-doctor.1275/post-21194>

I am on cloud 9 right now . . . and hoping this isn't too good to be true! I went to a new doc today (a chiropractor actually) and found out he eats a paleo diet himself! He specializes in chiropractic neurology and was well versed in hormonal/thyroid issues. I was totally stunned when he said my diet sounded perfect. Every other doctor I've seen (and there have been many) have always tried to push the "healthy whole grains" down my throat and tell me to limit my fat, eat lean meats, etc, etc. This guy was awesome!

He was impressed with the testing I already had done like the VAP and said very few practitioners are even aware of that test. Of course that gave me the opportunity to talk a bit about Dr. Kruse and he seemed very interested in learning more. I'm going to email him a link to the CT info.

Just had to share - maybe there are a few good ones out there, so don't give up hope yet! Although I have to say I've had my hopes up in the past with some others that have fooled me and turned out to be complete idiots, so I'm trying to stay cautiously optimistic this time. He did order a bunch of other tests (ANA, CMP, CBC, RA Factor, Lyme, full thyroid/iron panels, DHEA, pregnenolone, etc, etc) - some I've had done in the past, but some are new and I'm so excited to see what happens with all this.

He did determine just by looking at my last tests that there is definitely a "brain function" issue going on, likely a pituitary problem moreso than an actual thyroid issue. That jives with everything I've been reading lately, so it was nice to get the confirmation and feel like we are on the same page. FINALLY!!

### nonchalant — 2012-05-04T04:14:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-an-awesome-doctor.1275/post-21209>

Wonderful, BJK!! That is fabulous news. Yes, folks, there are some good doctors out there! Don't give up hope!

I know when I found my new doctor, I was so excited/happy that my blood pressure went way up! LOL! It was normal the second visit, though.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-04T05:46:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-an-awesome-doctor.1275/post-21255>

Awesome news! So glad to hear good people are out there and that you found one!

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-21T17:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-an-awesome-doctor.1275/post-39997>

BJK77, isn't it crazy that you're actually excited about your doctor? I mean, shouldn't going to the doctor just be a normal thing, like they help us with our health issues, diagnose, and treat? But because there's so much garbage being fed to so many of them, and they eat it up and spit it back out to us, that we actually have to go and seek a doctor who has read through what we have? I mean, isn't this odd?

I know exactly how you feel! I finally found a doctor (OB/GYN) on PaleoPhysiciansNetwork.com who GETS it!! She eats Paleo, reads a ton of the same stuff, does BioHRT (compounding), etc. I was on Cloud 9 when I left her office. We are now doing my Genova nutritional testing, as she wants to tweak every single bit of my nutrition first. It's like....what, no meds? No "5 second PAP and you're outta here?"

It's become such a sad state in the medical world.

But, for anyone still looking for a good doctor, try paleophysiciansnetwork.com.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-16T15:33:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/found-an-awesome-doctor.1275/post-67652>

This is great news. It is so important to have an outstanding Doc. I have one too in Germany. He is professor and runs an organization called International Society of Applied Preventative Medicine. There are about 5000 doctor members and they operate worldwide. He was actually the one who got me started and soon after I found this website. He is very interested in Dr.Kruses work. He did the right tests at the first consultation and also works with bioidentical hormones. I am so glad I found him (well actually he found me as he was interested in my product) and he knew after the lab tests came back what was the problem. Now I received my first check-up results and I am thrilled to see that my results are so much better and in some cases normal again. Like alpha-amylase, lipase, Hb A1c etc. Inflammation marker is way down. So now I am waiting for my hormone panel.

http://www.i-gap.org/wissenschaftlicher\_beirat.php

There are also some addresses from the States (where you see the american flags). If you are looking for doctors, they might be of help, as they are on the board of directors of IGAP and they should have the addresses of doctors in the USA.

# Thread: Intermittent fasting and fitness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-fitness.1276/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danco3636 — 2012-05-04T05:34:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-fitness.1276/post-21248>

Any one here doing intermittent fasting along with lifting and perhaps some sprints/ conditioning? If so what are your experiences with carb cycling such as in Leangains, Martin B. method? I know Dr. Jack has talked about it in one of his blogs.

I am lean now 5-6% body fat and have done the LG IF.... Trying to see how carbs may play into this. As of right now I have been doing the Leptin RX and started CT.... been doing CT for 3 weeks now. Looking to optimize my approach along with lifting, getting strong, fast and optimizing my health and athletics.

Been at Keto now for 4 weeks and thinking of adding back in the fasting, (16/8 up to 20/4) skipping breakfast and adding in carbs post workout..... Perhaps not as much carbs but using whey, protein source/ BCAA's and some fruit/ Berries and perhaps a sweat-potato post workout. Winter months I will take it more into all VLC, keto and use protein, coconut oil, fish oil and BCAA's, more for post workouts. Summer allowing more seasonal fruits/ paleo carb sources to add in carbs.

Would still see if I can get down to around 4-5% body fat and maintain it.....

Just looking for others thoughts that have been working with this and what may be optimal.

So:

Off/ rest days, VLC days (4 days a week)

Workout/ Lifting days moderate carb up and protein days (3 days a week). This day fat will be lower as in a re-feed day. Mark Sisson discusses this on his blog to help reset leptin and shed fat. Kind of a reset day. Alternate summer more carbs with winter 2-3 months keto.

Hoping to start a thread to discuss optimizing performance using fasting, working out, diet around workouts, CT and circadian rhythm..... All the while staying with the "Ancient Pathway" and getting optimum gene expression.

### Barry — 2012-05-04T12:14:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-fitness.1276/post-21279>

Danco

thread already started !! some guys here doing lift/HIIT exercise like yourself too

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories)

### ASHO — 2012-05-04T07:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21272>

What is the appliance Jack is using to cook his steak?

[](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/21347=177-Jack's Meat.jpg>)

### janagram — 2012-05-04T07:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21274>

looks like a cast iron grillpan.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-04T12:23:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21282>

Le Crueset cast iron enameled pan......got it for Xmas and I want the whole set now.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-04T16:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21303>

> **Jack Kruse;21357 wrote:** Le Crueset cast iron enameled pan......got it for Xmas and I want the whole set now.

I recognize that! grew up using one.. seriously considering swapping out all my caphelon (old school kind - not non-stick) for le crueset. I bought a dutch oven back in nov 2010 - dh bitched about it b/c of the cost - now its pretty much his go to pot. he loves it and every time he uses it he tells me its the best purchase I've ever made!

I do have a cast iron griddle by le crueset - but the problem is we don't have a true hood vent... so its a PITA to cook w/it b/c of the smoke points I'm waiting til we do our addition and bump out the kitchen... I'm going to be putting in a real hood vent, as well as a double wall oven... then I can truly fire up that bad boy

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-04T17:54:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21337>

Anther aaaaaaaawwwweeeeesome fry pan (tho not cheaper!) is scan pans. Look em up. Get the professional so it's easy to go from stove to oven.

I wonder if Jack has to do cleanup., I will fire up the grill outside any day (specially in winter!) rather than have to clean the grill pan.

We now have 4 cook pieces that never make it from the "stove-sink-dry" cycle to be put away, because that is all we use:

1\. 8 inch fry scan pan (eggs, small quantity saute)

2\. 12 in fry scan pan (meats and what that need evaporation room for searing)

3\. 13 in saute w/lid san pan (anything cooked in sauce) -also good for fast meat thaw. Cooks illustrated did some testing, it's ok to thaw meats in warm water if you are going to use them within a couple of hrs.

4\. 13 qt le crueset - NOTHING does a braise, chili, soup or slow cooked item better than this.

I started giving scan-pans to clients as housewarming gifts - and I've gotten so many great comments and new business from past clients that say "OMG I TOLD SO AND SO TO USE YOU SO YOU WILL GIVE HER THOSE PANS".

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-04T20:08:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21377>

Totally agree Cavemam! I love my 9 & 12" ScanPans! I now need to invest in that saute pan it sounds like. The cleanup is amazing and it they are my go to pans for almost everything....as long as it fits!

Also second HATE cleaning up the grill pan we used to have. Got rid of it last year. I do want a Le Creueset dutch oven though even if I will hate cleaning it up. BF does most of the cleaning anyway!!!

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-05-05T03:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21473>

you know everyone raves about their Le Cru cookware BUT I was in W&S today to drool vice buy (3kids in college problem). The lids to the dutch ovens/roasters don't have nubs or rings in the lids. And those are key to getting the liquids that condense on the lid to drop back into the pot. I got a Kirkland of all things as a Christmas gift and it gets tons of use. I have a Lodge skillet - 16" - watch out burglars! And my new range came with a Lodge Chef's griddle which has nice high sides to keep the bacon splatter somewhat confined.

Cast iron... optimal when used to cook, LHT, and ultimately after enough LHT as a home defense system...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-05T03:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21489>

> **Cavemam;21412 wrote:** Anther aaaaaaaawwwweeeeesome fry pan (tho not cheaper!) is scan pans. Look em up. Get the professional so it's easy to go from stove to oven.
>
>
>
> I wonder if Jack has to do cleanup., I will fire up the grill outside any day (specially in winter!) rather than have to clean the grill pan.
>
>
>
> We now have 4 cook pieces that never make it from the "stove-sink-dry" cycle to be put away, because that is all we use:
>
>
>
> 1\. 8 inch fry scan pan (eggs, small quantity saute)
>
>
>
> 2\. 12 in fry scan pan (meats and what that need evaporation room for searing)
>
>
>
> 3\. 13 in saute w/lid san pan (anything cooked in sauce) -also good for fast meat thaw. Cooks illustrated did some testing, it's ok to thaw meats in warm water if you are going to use them within a couple of hrs.
>
>
>
> 4\. 13 qt le crueset - NOTHING does a braise, chili, soup or slow cooked item better than this.
>
>
>
> I started giving scan-pans to clients as housewarming gifts - and I've gotten so many great comments and new business from past clients that say "OMG I TOLD SO AND SO TO USE YOU SO YOU WILL GIVE HER THOSE PANS".

I cook wife cleans.....she cooks she cleans.......i dont clean. I dont do laundry, and I dont make the beds......but I bring the bacon.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-05T21:24:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21658>

> **Jack Kruse;21565 wrote:** I cook wife cleans.....she cooks she cleans.......i dont clean. I dont do laundry, and I dont make the beds......but I bring the bacon.

That better be darn good bacon!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-05T21:52:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-21665>

Some of the best relationships I've witnessed work just like this. No issue with it if both parties agree...

I grew up in a "traditional" household and it is lovely to see my mother enjoy caring for my dad this way. And my dad makes sure mom is well taken care of by bringing home good bacon.

After the kids left the nest my mom was bored and got a job anyways, lol. And I'm not sure my dad even knows how to use a can opener

### ginastarke — 2012-05-08T23:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-22364>

Cast iron... optimal when used to cook, LHT, and ultimately after enough LHT as a home defense system...  LOl ever seen the Disney movie "Tangled"? There's a great running "pan" joke.

I used to have scary neighbors. I remember standing behind my door with a phone to the police in one hand and Nana's cast iron skillet in the other!

BTW how did you ever get the lodge pan seasoned? I heard the surface on them is rough.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-09T00:25:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-22384>

They're talking enamel pans ($$$). I use all non-enameled cast iron (way cheap). I season by coating in coconut oil and baking at like 350 forever or something (can't remember; it's been a while), but mostly by using them frequently with lots of bacon grease. For the first month with a new pan, after they're clean, we rub some (good) oil in the pan, heat it for a few seconds, then wipe gently with a paper towel and turn upside down on the stove racks to dry. Every day. Poof, they're seasoned.

I always use fat when I cook, and they just get more and more seasoned. My eggs slide around the pan. So much better than poisonous nonstick expensive crap that loses its nonstickiness in a year. (BTDT) (I learned about nonstick poison when a friend asked me to watch her bird because I was the only person she knew who never used nonstick, which, apparently, kills birds by the fumes)

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-09T01:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-22405>

> **Jack Kruse;21565 wrote:** I cook wife cleans.....she cooks she cleans.......i dont clean. I dont do laundry, and I dont make the beds......but I bring the bacon.

+1 I DO NOT CLEAN! I cook, and will do yard work, to include using power tools... I do laundry (it seems I spend most of laundry time on washing diapers) - However, I do not fold it.

### LinD — 2012-05-09T01:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-22418>

> **Jack Kruse;21357 wrote:** Le Crueset cast iron enameled pan......got it for Xmas and I want the whole set now.

La Kruse-t !

Sprint Samsung Epic

### tes3@mac.com — 2012-05-15T04:56:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-24112>

"You can have my Le Creuset grill pan when you pry my cold dead fingers off it."

Seriously, only way to cook a steak indoors. And, I might add, I never turn my vent hood on when using it (I have the real deal, too--it will move serious air!) as I'm cooking grassfed beef. Grassfed needs low and slow temps, so my gas burner is on med-low to med. No smoke. Steaks get nice sear marks, but the insides stay juicy, red/pink and tender. My Le Creuset skillets will work in a pinch, but I prefer the grill pan.

I agree with a previous poster on the dutch oven. I opted for a Lodge enameled one from Target. Much cheaper than Le Creuset and does a great job. As it is getting pot roast and stew, no real difference.

I found that no matter how well seasoned, I couldn't cook grassfed beef in iron skillets without adding fat. I prefer not to add fat for cooking steaks and burgers, so that's what got me to use Le Creuset. I have some sweet old iron skillets from my hillbilly granny, but without a river of bacon grease, things seem to stick. Bummer. Any suggestions?

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-15T05:20:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-appliance-jack-is-using-to-cook-his-steak.1277/post-24117>

Strip and re-season. That's what we do with garage sale iron finds.

But steaks & burgers should be cooked outside!
