### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-08T17:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kicked-off-cruise.1341/post-22217>

Someone just made a threat to a Paleo fx leader via FB that the Letter from "Carnival is a fake".......Here is the funny thing.......I have the email from Carnival in my personal account and 350 people on the cruise got that letter last night at dinner from the Captain. I wonder why someone would make a claim like this when it is obvious how serious this situation has become. This is a federal matter now and I think it is far out of my hands. The news media actually read the email in my house last night from the Carnival spokes person and I also received an apology from the head of security at Carnival yesterday. I think Carnival has handled this situation as best they can. An agent has been collecting data off many blogs and many websites that have motive and cause. If you cooperate you'll likely be dealt with differently than if they find you.

### Birdy — 2012-05-08T17:35:21+0300

### Lyndra — 2012-05-08T18:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kicked-off-cruise.1341/post-22245>

Unless whoever is behind this is quite sophisticated, it should be easy to track their electronic footprints. Did they really think this was a harmless prank or were they trying to damage Dr. K and his reputation? In this day and age, I can't imagine anyone pulling a stunt like that without being fully determined to cause harm. What if Carnival had decided to evacuate the entire ship? Imagine the panic that would ensue, worse yet if they had already sailed and had to calm passengers who were thinking there was a bioterrorist on board. I don't expect everyone to like what Dr. K has to say but can't they just stop with polite disagreement? Or just go away and ignore him. This is just such an outrageous incident on so many levels.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-08T19:26:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kicked-off-cruise.1341/post-22282>

> **Cavemam;22339 wrote:** This does not smack of sophistication. Seems like the fake/mock tweet account may be a separate issue from the clearly "going off the rails" individual that did the real damage - the phone call. Can't wait to see how this unfolds. If you drop your avatar, I think it will change immediately on your posts, but don't quote me on that!

I think whoever posted the tweet, whether in jest or not, did huge damage. It's like being at the airport and having a conversation about terrorism. Even if you're not making a threat and you're just commenting on some other situation, the authorities will take you aside and investigate. You just can't do that. I think whoever placed the phone call was right on; they had no way of knowing whether it was a joke or a potentially real threat (why they didn't call 911 instead of Carnival makes me suspicious). If you saw a tweet where someone was "joking" about bringing weapons to a school, you'd call it in right away. Same thing here IMO. You can't afford to not take something like this seriously.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-08T19:32:05+0300

### David — 2012-05-08T19:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kicked-off-cruise.1341/post-22285>

> **Cavemam;22360 wrote:** Keep us posted on what is going on with everything! Retype the letter !

May 7, 2012

Greetings from the bridge,

I want to take this opportunity to personally welcome you on board. I also want to share some information regarding Dr. John Kruse, a guest who was supposed to sail as a part of your group.

Yesterday afternoon, we were notified of a Twitter message, under the handle of Dr. Kruse, alleging he intended to cause harm to the ship. Galveston police, the FBI, the Department of Homeland Security and the U.S. Coast Guard were informed immediately and, shortly thereafter, authorithies boarded the vessel to investigate the matter. Dr. Kruse was questioned, and since the investigation was ongoing, he was asked to disembark the vessel just prior sailing.

The FBI has been able to confirm today that Dr. Kruse was in no way responsible for threatening Twitter message.

I know Dr. Kruse was to speak with your group this week and I sincerely apologize he will be unable to join you as previously scheduled. Since the safety and well-being of my guests and crew is my number one priority, every security threat is taken seriously and fully investigated. It is for this reason that I felt it was in the best interest of all guests to err on the side of caution and not allow him to sail as planned.

Thank you for your understanding. Please enjoy your time on board.

Sincerely,

Captain Giovanni Cutugno

### Jim — 2012-05-08T20:12:09+0300

I would venture to guess that these individuals aren't even necessarily aware of this subconscious motivation within themselves.

Whenever a person has such distaste for another as to be moved to do even the slightest of harm, that is the precise moment one must look inward. In that delicate instance, a person can find the self awareness needed to let go of anger and hate, and discover a path to the betterment one's own existence.

### chocolate — 2012-05-09T04:14:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kicked-off-cruise.1341/post-22454>

Anyone that comments on this as trivial makes the whole Paleo movement look stupid. It reminds me of the saying that silence equals permission. Paleo needs to raise hell. Carnival had a tough call and nothing is perfect. I worked for the airlines and discretion is a tough call. It could be that they reserved the behavior of denying boarding so in the future, if they truly felt threatened, they couldn't be sued for discrimination. It gets very kinky trying to cover everyone's rear. You should see the wheelchair hoops ....Christmas time you can throw a paying passenger off a plane if a wheelchair passenger wants the chair on board versus, cargo. It is considered part of their body.

Not militant and outrageously demanding.... or it was ten years ago. Never mind trying to rebook the wheelchair passenger for a later flight. A fat buxom bleach blonde complained they were chosen by male coworkers to be searched by me, during the window before TSA/ post 9/11, saying my coworker picked on her because she was attractive. I was called in to be asked if I had to search any attractive women on a certain flight. Nicely reported that, "no, I just searched a fat, sweaty (she was, not just an opinion) woman, the kind the guys go to baggage claim post flight and laugh at when thongs pop up from their jeans as they retrieve their luggage." The fact is, if my co-worker had the choice of who to search , I would have kicked his ass for making me lay hands on the her. Once, a TSA told me to search the next young lady, I thought he meant the next young lady. I didn't search the middle aged lady next in line and she got insulted. This is why discretion loses all of its merit. Then we had the option of refusing to search the opposite sex if no one trained was available. There was no way I would let someone miss their flight because I knew that I could behave professionally, it was also their option to be searched, and men don't overreact to uncomfortable situations. There was actually a shortage of metal detectors and we had to use our bare hands. I would never expect a guy searcher to trust a female in that situation. Sucks, but I understand the separation. You just have to have blanket policies to CYA. Dr Kruse IMO was removed so a real perceived threat wouldn't sue in the future. It goes to show you how piss poor the leadership is that doesn't speak out on this sort of thing if it does turn out to be Paleo establishment backlash. There is nothing not "Paleo" about this blog, its just circadian and evolutionary. Isn't that Paleo?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-09T16:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kicked-off-cruise.1341/post-22531>

> For some reason there seems to be more personal vitriol directed at Jack rather than professional disagreement.

That's the part that messes with me. It points out that what is happening really doesn't have much to do with what he's saying/writing or not. This is much more of a psychological/mind issue. I'm thinking it might have to do with people not wanting their paleo world rocked.

It's a known phenomenon that when you have a certain concept in your mind - the longer you've believed it the harder it is to give it up. People will ignore information to the opposite so they can stick to their known belief. It is unbelievable how long this can go on... look at some of the elderly who will keep doing things that are entirely outdated despite overwhelming information that it's useless.

The next stage would be cognitive dissonance - the moment part of you realizes you're wrong ("directionally incorrect" in this case... ) you start to feel crazy in your mind. Your established belief system goes into chaos b/c of the new information. That can cause severe anger/rage in my experience. It really can make you nuts.

While change appears difficult for most people, it is actually a concept the mind can learn to welcome. We have both evolutionary concepts in our brains - we like the known b/c it spells security, but we also have curiosity which humans have used extensively in the past to advance to the point we are now. Yes, there is risk involved with that... but also huge reward.

Curiosity is something that is neat to see in children... as they haven't been taught yet to be afraid of change. I love to watch my dog's curiosity

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-09T16:59:25+0300

You are right; this feels like a silly elementary school bully squad. I have seen only once the main 4 or 5 against Jack critique his ideas. Every other time they speak, it's about his style, or a misunderstanding of what he said because they don't get his style/lack of it (hard to blame them for the latter, IF they eventually realize it's actually a misunderstanding instead of continuing to beat a very dead straw man). I'm trying to understand their motivation, and all I get is a) they still truly believe he's an idiot because of how he wrote at the beginning or b) they were so thrilled at "discovering" they "could" eat carbs that their inner addicts are waging this war for them.

Discourse. Reason. Is it so hard to stick to the facts?

# Thread: Help me identify my problem please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-identify-my-problem-please.1343/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2012-05-08T11:52:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-identify-my-problem-please.1343/post-22164>

I am a 61yo female with leaky gut and I am insulin resistant, I am obese and badly need to lose weight but am incapable of weighloss at present. I have been eating VLC for approx 4 years (Atkins). The first year I lost 10kg the next year I plateaued, the following 2 years I slowly put back all the weight I lost. I came across your site and now I am following, a VLC Paleo to the best of my ability (I live in South Africa, where you cannot find organic but rather a dubious "free range", half the lab tests mentioned here are "un available" like rev T3, and when you mention leaky gut to our doctors, they look at you as if you came from another planet!!.....Endocrinologists still work on 20 year old research.....

MY HDL is very low and my LDL very high my triglicerides are in the low range

My vit D3=86

So I am coming to you for help please.

I take the following meds:

Bio identical HRT (femigel & utragestan)

Euthyrox 0.05mg (Levothyroxine)

Micardis 40mg (Telmisartan) hypertension

I take the following supplements:

Probiotic

Omega 3 EPA 860mg DHA 560mg

Metagenics-Glutagenic (Lglutamine 3500mg, licorice root 500mg, aloe leaf extrct 50mg)

NAC 500 mg

D3 5000mg

CoQ10 150mg

VitK2 (MK4 155iu & MK7 45iu)

Vit C 1000mg

At night

Magnesium Glycinate 1026mg

Twice a week 500mg pottassium (I will up these)

I have just started the leptin RX

My weight will not budge and I still have these awful leg cramps and very little energy.

What am I still doing wrong?

### shilohman — 2012-05-08T15:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-identify-my-problem-please.1343/post-22174>

Do you eat three meals a day with no snacking between meals? Do 4-5 hours pass before you go to bed at night? Do you get 8 hours of sleep every night?

### bugsy1950@gmail.com — 2012-05-08T16:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-identify-my-problem-please.1343/post-22189>

Yes with the exeption of having to take melatonin to help me fall asleep, I do get 8 hrs but sometimes it is interupted because I wake at approx 3 am and struggle to go back to sleep. This then disrups my breakfast time as I get up later.

### shilohman — 2012-05-08T17:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-identify-my-problem-please.1343/post-22215>

### hazyjane — 2012-05-08T18:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-identify-my-problem-please.1343/post-22256>

> **Bugsy;22243 wrote:**
>
> I have just started the leptin RX
>
>
>
> My weight will not budge and I still have these awful leg cramps and very little energy.
>
> What am I still doing wrong?

Many women don't lose weight at first as their metabolism can take awhile to heal. Dr. K says that women will notice changes in mood and sleep before they notice weight loss. Maybe have your cal/mag ratio tested, if possible. Have you tried cold thermogenesis yet? If not, it would be a good thing to begin implementing.

You might be able to get a US doc to work with you long distance: [http://thyroidbook.com/practitioner-locator.html](http://http://thyroidbook.com/practitioner-locator.html)

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-08T19:59:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-identify-my-problem-please.1343/post-22291>

> **Bugsy;22268 wrote:** Yes with the exeption of having to take melatonin to help me fall asleep, I do get 8 hrs but sometimes it is interupted because I wake at approx 3 am and struggle to go back to sleep. This then disrups my breakfast time as I get up later.

Melatonin is not good for women as it interferes with hormones during sleep. I think it's in CT-9?

Waking at that time can be an indicator of adrenal fatigue. Also, do you have recent hormone labs (including pregnenolone and DHEA) since you're already taking hormones? Supplementing DHEA helped my sleep.

# Thread: CT and bug bites?!?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TerrierMom — 2012-05-08T17:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22216>

For some reason bug bites are really nasty for me. My usual course is to get spray Benadryl on them right away. It will take about 24 hrs for me to even notice a bite... day 2 is like "do I have a bite or just dry skin?". Then I see the welt. Days 3-5 are excruciating itchiness and the welt is like a hard little lump. I know if I scratch, it's gonna grow to the size of a quarter (or a silver dollar pancake!) ... So I am good at leaving them alone. But man oh man it's ridiculously itchy....especially in the morning when my cortisol wakes me up it's like they're on fire!

Anyway if I spray Benadryl on them it helps a LOT but not sure what that does to my system. Isnt it kindof a steroid? Or just an antihistamine? (bad?) Clearly my immune system is unhappy. Just wondering if CT will resolve the bites faster? Or just make me numb... I was dumb enough to sit outside after sunset with no shoes while in Austin and now I have 8 bites.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-08T17:43:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22227>

Cortisol should help the itchiness? It's an anti-inflammatory, iirc.

I'd spot ice. Benadryl = baaaaad ...... even my CW allergist hates it. Maybe try menthol on it if you need a spot treatment beyond the ice.

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-08T18:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22239>

Commencing the spot ice. And hoping CT will eventually cure the extreme bug bite reactions I have!!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-09T00:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22395>

Btw... How do antihistamines mess with one? I know steroids can wreak havoc but haven't come across what's wrong with Benadryl.... Not that I am surprised to find out yet another CW remedy has worse implications then I knew about....!

### PaleoMom — 2012-05-09T05:17:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22472>

I've been getting tons of bites daily with almost no itching at all! I have to blame CT for this. I see them on me, but almost none of them itch and even the ones that have I have only scratched about once and then forget about them because they stop itching.

While you're waiting for CT to work, putting Swedish Bitters ( a liquid sold in health food stores ) on them takes the itch out right away. Haven't had to use it this year though!

### nonchalant — 2012-05-09T05:29:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22475>

You could try white vinegar on the bites. It relieves itching, at least for a while.

A wasp stung me a month or so ago. I was amazed at how little it hurt, and it didn't swell like I expected. I just put an ice cube against it for a while, and forgot about it.

### JC2K — 2012-05-09T11:22:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22506>

Benedryl = antihistimine. I have a cousin that always carries it around for his son (bad food allergies) for the bad reactions that aren't severe enough for the epi-pen. I don't know that Benedryl's bad for you per se. In his case, he'll use it when he sees something starting (tightening of the throat etc).

In the case of the bug bites. They causing an allergic reaction, causing your cells to release histimine to fight it. Histimine's are a natural part of your body's immune system. They help your white blood cells get through the capillaries to get to the invaders to destroy them. The problem is when your body goes overboard on creating the histimine.

Benedryl helps block the creation of more histimine (although it doesn't remove what's existing). Epi-pens (Epinephrine/Adrenaline) will clear histimine from the body. Your body (when it's working right) can do that as well (pumping the adrenaline, giving you panic attacks, etc). If you're already in bad shape due to excess histimine (severe food allergy for example), you need that Epi-Pen to clear it, the Benedryl's not going to save you.

So, now, can CT stop/slow histimine. I suspect it will, depending on the location. If it's a skin bite, ice away. If it's a food allergy, take drugs. If it's an internal bug bite, don't eat bugs.

It sounds like it would be a good N=1 test. You get definite reactions from bug bites, so a non-reaction after CT a few times would be pretty definitive for you (you'll want to do it more than once). Try it out and let us know how it works out.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-05-09T17:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-22574>

Glad I ran across this thread. I'm right there with Krusing to Cali in my reaction to bug bites. Mosquitoes and chigger bites are the worst and I still have scars on my ankles from 2 seasons ago. It's horrible. People who don't know me well and have this misfortune to catch me in a pair of capris are often shocked that the whelps on my legs are simple bug bites. I thought I was the only one with this issue. Benadryl helps, but only for a little while. I've not tried icing them, and haven't taken any CT soaks since the bugs really started nibbling. I'll have to test this on myself too. Thanks for asking this question and starting this discussion.

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-11T16:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-23155>

Thanks all! Clabbergirl, I too have the bug bite scars you do, even if I do not scratch. I bet CT will fade them, though!! (I hope!)

I soaked my feet in 43 degree water (bites on feet and ankles) and let me tell you, they did not itch after that for a long long time. Before that the coldest water I've been in is 64. They itched the next day but didn't wake me up at night itching like they did pre-CT! Within 24 hrs they stopped itching altogether, but this was day 5 post-bite which is usually when the I tense itching naturally tapers for me,so not sure if CT helped overall or just temporarily.

Next time I get bites it will be ct as soon as I notice them...! And will report back. Thanks for all the suggestions because this is a big issue for me when I get bites!!

### SeaHorse — 2012-05-13T16:42:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-23650>

I'm another one that is very reactive to bug bites....there is a lot of interesting information about histamine and histadalia if you google. I didn't realize that some people have way more histamine action in their bodies than others...related to more mast cells. I'm looking into how food can be a hidden source of histamine. It's a bit tricky because some of the things that are really good for us to eat...ie seafood, shellfish, spinach, fermented foods can be huge sources of histamine. I'm trying to figure this out...eat well, heal a leaky gut and not get eaten by the black flies! All left-overs start to produce histamine....so really fresh food is vital. I still don't know if this is something that can be turned around, but I'm hoping CT will be a big part of the solution....Also I'm a big fan of bug shirts and hats that have the repellant in the material so there is less stuff directly on your skin.

It's going to be black fly season here in a few days...yikes!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-13T18:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-bug-bites.1344/post-23688>

Interesting note. The one bite I got on my hand (no ice or CT applied, out of sheer laziness) ..... Still itches! (8 days post bite! Not crazy itchy but enough to scratch.)

The ones on my feet and ankles (subjected to spot ice and 40-43 degree water...? Not itchy.

# Thread: CT and Leptin RX Weight Gain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-leptin-rx-weight-gain.1351/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 32

### tweety — 2012-05-08T22:01:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-leptin-rx-weight-gain.1351/post-22334>

I know Dr. K said weight gain can occur when hormones are out of balance. Heres my history:

Started Leptin Rx last summer, lost weight, was feeling good, but did not have signs of LS. Lost track, tried to do Post Leptin RX for a few months. Went nowhere. I have also been trying to treat my hormones and adrenal fatigue. Continued gaining weight, so decided to recommit to Leptin Rx and doing CT.

I have been Leptin Rx protocol for three weeks. CTing since end of Feb, early March. I have now gained 9 pounds since Nov. Two and a half pounds since the Leptin Rx. I am eating less than I was prior to LRx, and have completely removed dairy. Why am I still gaining?

I continue to have fatigue despite my supplements, and I have slow but constant detox from the CT. I have had no positive improvements from CT or Leptin Rx, which makes it very hard to stay on the straight and narrow. At this point though I am afraid of stopping because I don't know where it leaves me!

My thyroid is low, but I am told it is due to hormones and adrenal fatigue. So I am treating (or attempting to treat) those, but in the meantime I am getting worse. At what point does one consider treating thyroid?

Is it possible that CT is screwing around with my hormones to the point of causing weight gain where I had lost before? Anyone with insight feel free to jump in here!

keep on w/the LR and the CT. it will work itself out... just remember you don't want to test while your detoxing...

I know your story well! I just didn't want my estrogen dump and detox to include weight gain! I have low estrogen, but relative E dominance. But I did order a liver detox supp to see if that helps me. It did occur to me that in the two months of CT I have been doing I have not had increased need to urinate, so I am wondering if I am not clearing these detox symptoms. I just want to purge my body of this crap right now!

I started the progesterone cream at the same time as the weight gain began, so I am thinking I am on too low a dose to do anything but anger my estrogen.

body comp in both sexes is all about Testosterone and IGF1 levels.....not estrogen

> **August;24188 wrote:** I would like to understand better how this works for women . . especially re: testosterone & PCOS . . . it seems like women with PCOS struggle with body comp issues (I both struggle with excess body fat and difficulty gaining muscle) . . in theory it seems like at least the muscle part of that equation would not be such a struggle (sadly not the case for me at least) . . in actuality it seems like the excess fat part is actually less of a struggle when testosterone/androgens are better under control . . .

with pcos, the big problem with body comp and the like, is the insulin factor....insulin mucks everything up - i can see it blocking any good side effects of all the testosterone in your body. insulin is what makes people store fat in a sense....think of diabetics who get fat patches where they inject insulin (so they have to move around their injection sites).

### tweety — 2012-05-17T20:08:35+0300

>
>
>
>
>
> (copying this response \[from this thread\] over again . . because I thought it was just really interesting!!!!

No worries here. I know I am not the only person experiencing this. While I would love to have a magic answer about how to undo the weight gain, I will settle for hearing other people's experiences! I am hoping to hear from someone who did gain weight, and how they eventually came around to losing it. I know some have, am sure there are more.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-05-25T02:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-leptin-rx-weight-gain.1351/post-27219>

I've gained nearly 10 lbs over the last few months. I've not really changed anything except adding CT and tweaking some supplements. I feel great except for the ocassional slow digestion/follow through and some bloating but the biggest hinderance is the pesky pounds. I've been racking my brain trying to think what I'm doing differently and the only thing I can come up with is BP coffee. I would drink coffee a few times a week with just HWC and stevia w/ my BAB but I started doing BP coffee and was having it almost every day. I'm wondering if all of the added fat is the issue or it is the increase in coffee. I seem to feel better with the added fat since it can help along the BAB in the morning but not sure what the culprit is. Then again, it is probable that CT inducde detox and hormonal chaos is to blame for perhaps part or all of it. Who knows. Sometimes I'm sick of thinking of these things after hitting the same brick wall repeatedly.

So today is day one of my experiment: Operation Sans Coffee.

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-05-25T02:55:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-leptin-rx-weight-gain.1351/post-27222>

try dropping the stevia

### tweety — 2012-05-25T02:56:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-leptin-rx-weight-gain.1351/post-27223>

tell me about it. I have steadily been gaining 1.5lbs a month, which doesn't seem like much. But, given that its been six months of this... I didn't notice or worry until a few months ago. Now I weigh more than I have since I have known my husband. And yes I changed my diet. But I reintrodiced the Leptin RX and can tell you I am eating less than I was previously and still gaining. Its all hormones. I say this because I weighed myself on saturday and was down a pound and a half. I felt good, continued on. Got on the scale today and was three pounds up! I did nothing between these days that would warrant that, so it has to be hormones.

If you're gaining I would look at CT- its changing your hormones around for sure.

### hazyjane — 2012-05-25T20:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-leptin-rx-weight-gain.1351/post-27400>

Re: progesterone cream- I've heard of women gaining weight from progesterone cream. It can be converted to estrogen and it can also build up in the fatty tissues.

I did a little search on it and I found a message board with loads of women reporting weight gain from progesterone cream and BHRT:

[http://www.progesteronetherapy.com/progesterone-weight-gain.html#axzz1vu2o3NEH](http://http://www.progesteronetherapy.com/progesterone-weight-gain.html#axzz1vu2o3NEH)

My best friend was on the cream during her pregnancy and she gained a ridiculous amount of weight considering her diet (seriously- her main food craving was raw spinach!!) I saw what she was eating and it wasn't the kind of food that should lead to that kind of weight gain.

I found this quote that I thought was interesting (and I wonder if it applies to me, since progesterone cream has never been helpful, but very low dose (5 mgs) oral natural progesterone liquid (Apex Energetics) sublingually def. had a nice diuretic affect on me).

"There is another possible reason for weight gain with progesterone. According to Dr. Uzzi Reiss, a GYN in Beverly Hills, there are a small percentage of women who can tolerate only a minimum amount of progesterone. For these women, a normal dose is like an overdose. And one of the side effects is an increased appetite and weight gain (2).

For women who have a low tolerance, Dr. Reiss recommends using a low-dose 3% cream, applied vaginally and to the breasts. He said that this minimum amount is usually enough to give the benefits that would require a much higher dosage in other women."

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-13T20:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exatest.1353/post-23718>

[http://www.exatest.com](http://http://www.exatest.com)

Date: Fri, 11 May 2012 17:31:23 -0700

From: [icd@exatest.com](mailto:icd@exatest.com)

To:

Subject: INTRACELLULAR EXA TEST Re: Price of Exatest?

We don't provide the cost on the web site because we accept assignment. That is we accept what the insurance allows and it depends on your insurance contract.

Below is our standard informaton:

Thank you for contacting IntraCellular Diagnostics; we appreciate your interest in EXA Test

Below you will find

General information, cost, insurance issues, and a clinical description that may provide answers to questions patients generally ask:

Upon receipt of an order the specimen collection kit is sent priority mail to the healthcare provider. The test results are faxed and mailed to the provider one to two days from the moment we receive the specimen at the Intracellular Diagnostics laboratory.

EXA Test provides results for six elements: Magnesium, Calcium, Phosphorus, Potassium, Sodium, and Chloride.

We will review the specimen collection procedure with the healthcare provider and we also provide support to the physician for interpretation and protocol: 541-245-3212

Patients need a clean mouth for the test and must avoid all dairy, soy or any sticky foods, sodium, chewable tablets, Tums, chewable vitamins and minerals etc. before the test. It is best to fast overnight. When food is necessary, PT can eat water soluble light foods. Contact IntraCellular if you have any questions on this or any other matter: 541-245-3212

We do not distribute the specimen collection kit to patients. It is important that patients consult with their physician and plan any treatment with his or her advice.

We do make an exception if the patient lives in a rural area or has difficulty getting to a physician's office; but we require a prescription. It can be faxed to: 541-245-3215.

The kit will be sent wherever the healthcare provider requests.

For providers not familiar with the EXA test, we have scientific information, peer reviewed research and publications including quality of life studies.

ICD is a CLIA licensed laboratory and is HIPPA compliant.

Billing information and payment options

We are a Medicare contracted laboratory and we bill everyone below the Medicare allowed fees. Allowed fee is the maximum fee the insurance company will pay. We accept assignment, which means that we accept what the insurance company allows.

Medicare always pays 80% of the EXA Test if the patient's contract is in order and has not expired. Secondary insurance pays the balance. If the patient does not have secondary insurance we accept the Medicare payment. We do not accept any prepayments from Medicare patients.

Private insurance pays according to patient contract, please see if you have out of network benefits or deductibles; a prepayment of $250.00 and a copy of your insurance information is required(see below.) Any overpayments that may occur will be refunded to you after the insurance pays.

Below is additional billing information that may or may not apply to you. And, don't miss the letter to your doctor at the bottom of this email:

We do not accept Medicaid as primary insurance though we accept it as secondary insurance.

r />
If the patient's contract does not provide for out of network service, or the carrier is an HMO, the patient needs to obtain a pre-authorization for CPT Code 88348. The insurance company will also need an ICD9 Code or the physician's reason for the test. You may call our office for assistance.

For a Pre-authorization, the insurance company needs to know that Intracellular Diagnostics is an Independent Clinical Laboratory and the only Specialty Reference Laboratory that processes this specific test

Patients not certain that their insurance will pay for the EXA test may call our office 541-245-3212, Patient Services, for additional information.

The healthcare provider may send the specimen to Intracellular Diagnostics with a prepayment of $250.00 (with the PT's check or credit card) and the patient's insurance information so that we may process the EXA Test, and bill the insurance and try to get payment on the patient's behalf.

If the claim is denied or the patient is not covered, is out of network or not insured, IntraCellular accepts the prepayment of $250.00 as settlement. If there is an overpayment a refund will be sent to the patient.

.

Some insurance companies consider IntraCellular Diagnostics out of network and may send the check to the patient. The patient needs to send that check to IntraCellular Diagnostics. We bill everyone $570.00 which is below the Medicare allowed fee. Private insurance may or may not pay the full amount allowed. But if the insurance pays the claim IntraCellular will refund the patient any overpayments.

Other literature is on the Internet at [http://www.exatest.com](http://http://www.exatest.com) and published research is under Research and Reference [http://WWW.exatest.Com/Research.htm](http://http://WWW.exatest.Com/Research.htm)

Below is a clinical description and indications for the physician..

Sincerely,

Patient Services

IntraCellular Diagnostics, Inc.

945 Town Centre Dr. Ste. A

Medford, Oregon 97504

[www.exatest.com](http://http://www.exatest.com)

541-245-3212

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-13T20:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exatest.1353/post-23719>

For Physicians:

RE: EXA TEST, Energy Dispersive X-Ray Analysis using Analytical Scanning Electron Microscopy; Testing for intracellular tissue status of Magnesium, Calcium, Potassium, Sodium, Chloride, and Phosphorus.

The EXA test is most helpful when used with patients who: experience atrial fibrillation, are taking blood pressure medications or any medication that impact mineral electrolytes, have implanted devices/ICDs, or have other CAD-(Coronary Artery Disease), for pregnant women to diagnose and prevent eclampsia, diabetics, those who have chronic pain, or have difficult to diagnose pathological complaints like chronic fatigue syndrome, those who routinely supplement with calcium, athletes who deplete electrolytes in sports, and for patient with conditions where mineral electrolyte balance is compromised.

The EXA method for intracellular tissue analysis was developed within a traditional scientific environment by a medical scientist and professor of medicine, Burton Silver, Ph.D. EXA Test has seen extensive research by major institutions. It is evidence based and publications are in peer reviewed journals. The EXA Test is a noninvasive tissue analysis that also addresses the concerns of integrative medicine practitioners.

Although EXA was originally developed for cardiology, the sublingual epithelial cells as used by IntraCellular Diagnostics correlate with cells that are prevalent throughout the body. Essential minerals and electrolytes are so crucial throughout the body that EXA can be used as a diagnostic assay in a variety of conditions.

Sublingual cells have a high cytoplasm to nucleus structure facilitating mineral analysis. Epithelial buccal cells are aerobic and contain full metabolic biochemical pathways. They offer a rapidly renewing, homogeneous and easily accessible population which reflects current total body intracellular mineral status.

The EXA Test is a direct measurement of mineral electrolyte content in epithelial cells. Once collected and fixed, there is no manipulation of the cells and the specimen a long life span for diagnostic purposes.

Throughout his career, IntraCellular Diagnostics's founder, Dr. Burton Silver, has worked closely with many cardiologists including those at Baylor, John Hopkins, Bethesda Army Hospital, NASA, Cedars Sinai, and the NIH.

The test was validated by clinical work and research with the institutions above mentioned and others. The double blind studies were published with Johns Hopkins in Circulation-the Journal of the American Heart Association. More studies are published in other peer review journals. One of our more recent quality of life studies with Cedars Sinai and UCLA was published in the American Journal of Cardiology and is on our web site [http://WWW.exatest.Com/PDF%20Files/42003Silver%20Shechter%20Quality%20of%20Life%20in%20CAD%20PT%20AJC42003.pdf](http://http://WWW.exatest.Com/PDF%20Files/42003Silver%20Shechter%20Quality%20of%20Life%20in%20CAD%20PT%20AJC42003.pdf)

We also have publications online from the Hartford Hospital in Connecticut that references quality of life for patients with ICD devices and antiarrhythmic medications, and one that references the differences between serum and EXA tissue magnesium:

[http://www.exatest.Com/PDF%20Files/AdMagHTNStudy\_AnnPharmacother2009;43;online.pdf](http://http://www.exatest.Com/PDF%20Files/AdMagHTNStudy_AnnPharmacother2009;43;online.pdf) .

The various publications in Circulation, The Journal of the American Heart Association, focused on correlation of the sublingual epithelial tissue with heart, musculoskeletal, and deep organ tissue. The EXA Test results do not correlate with blood but with tissue:

[http://www.exatest.com/PDF%20Files/1.%20Correlation%20of%20%20Tissue%20Magnesium%20with%20Cardiac%20Levels%20Using%20a%20Non-invasive%20Testing%20Method%20(full%20paper)..PDF](http://http://www.exatest.com/PDF%20Files/1.%20Correlation%20of%20%20Tissue%20Magnesium%20with%20Cardiac%20Levels%20Using%20a%20Non-invasive%20Testing%20Method%20\(full%20paper\)..PDF)

Another Johns Hopkins research showed the magnesium and electrolytes of sublingual cells correlate with soft and connective tissue in the body. Magnesium studies do not correlate with blood; only 1% of magnesium is in the blood; 99% of Mg (and Potassium) is in the soft tissue.

Problems with metabolism of these essential elements are common. And there are a variety of conditions that EXA results can assist in identifying; When a baseline is established with EXA a first easy step is taken to resolve a complex problem. With EXA it is easy to track a patient's progress because dynamic changes show in the tissue first.

EXA Test is an early detector of a variety of conditions related to magnesium deficiency, calcification of tissue and overall mineral electrolyte imbalance. EXA results can assist in an early diagnosis of Hyperparathyroidism, osteoporosis not yet detected through bone scan, medication related metabolic changes, and the impact of some medications that either suppress or retain some elements creating symptoms not identifiable through blood tests.

For some patients dietary intake can complicate metabolism of essential elements. An example of excess dietary calcium intake are patients who have ingested for years large amounts of calcium to prevent osteoporosis without a balanced electrolyte protocol to regulate or modulate the excess. This practice is common and yet can lead to destructive high levels of calcium in the tissues. Early detection can assist in the prevention of complex diseases.

Altogether, EXA is a very useful diagnostic assay that assists the physicians in following their patients closely. EXA encourages treatment and patient compliance. ICD's EXA can assist you and your patients with solutions to short as well as long term problems and in obtaining desirable results that contribute to quality of life.

ICD is CLIA licensed and HIPPA compliant. IntraCellular Diagnostics is contracted with Medicare. EXA is covered by Medicare and most insurance. We do not accept Medicaid as primary insurance. We are a "Specialty Reference Lab" so out of network coverage for private insurance is available with pre-authorization and depending on patient contract.

Feel free to contact ICD if you have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Physician Services

IntraCellular Diagnostics, Inc.

945 Town Centre Drive

Suite A

Medford, Oregon 97504

[www.exatest.com](http://http://www.exatest.com)

541-245-3212

.............

> Thanks for the input. I actually haven't supplemented with Calcium in months, ever since reading the osteoporosis blogs when starting out my Kruse Journey.
>
>
>
> From the Exatest Interpretation Guide:
>
>
>
> **HIGH CALCIUM
>
> Elevated tissue calcium may be a sign of mobilization of bone calcium into soft
>
> tissues signaling early signs of developing osteoporosis. High intracellular calcium
>
> interferes with ATP formation, muscle contraction, relaxation, enzyme activity, and
>
> neuromuscular transmission.
>
> Increased cellular calcium predisposes to spasm of peripheral arterioles leading to
>
> increased blood pressure. Calcium may also be a factor in plaque formation,
>
> angina, hypertension and athero-arteriosclerosis. Calcium channel blockers as well
>
> as magnesium affects movement of calcium into the soft tissues and heart muscle.
>
> Parathyroid hormone, which regulates calcium, is increased when magnesium is
>
> low. PTH is lowered when magnesium stores are high.** Hypomagnesia occurs
>
> commonly in hyperthyroidism.
>
> Hypercalcemia is seen in malignant neoplasms (with or without bone involvement),
>
> primary and tertiary hyperparathyroidism, sarcoidosis, vitamin D intoxication,
>
> Paget's disease of bone, thyrotoxicosis, acromegaly, and the diuretic phase of renal
>
> acute tubular necrosis.
>
> In summary: Elevated intracellular calcium might indicate loss of calcium from
>
> skeletal tissues and might possibly indicate that the adequate intake of daily
>
> calcium be maintained or increased, depending on the judgment and diagnosis of
>
> the physician.**

Those would be the parts that worry me.... I'm not taking calcium so the calcium is coming from somewhere else than my nutritional intake..... my bones? I'm trying to HEAL my bones!! Now, it could just be the other option, which is that I'm low on mg and PTH is increased due to that, increasing Calcium. How to find out?

### shilohman — 2012-06-24T17:43:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exatest.1353/post-33281>

> **Souldanzer;33351 wrote:** I've been supplementing mg like crazy and can't possibly go any higher. I realize that my body might be dumping it b/c of elevated BG but there's nothing I can do about the high BG either.... my brain won't tolerate a lower BG right now.
>

> How long does it take to replete mg from a severe deficiency? I think I didn't have one bit of mg left a few years ago. Then I was supplementing @ 400-800mg for a few years, now stepped it up to 800-1200mg plus oil for 8 months now. Maybe I just need more time to undo the deficiency?

How is your vitamin D level? Also, are you taking vitamin K2? Dr. K says a healthy vitamin D level causes calcium to be absorbed and that vitamin K2 is what causes it to be absorbed into the bone.

As you already stated, the reason you are having problems with your Mg level is that high BG causes Mg to be sequestered into the cells of the body. Can't remember if it was Dr. K or someone else, but I have read that to decrease BG you need a high protein diet with low glycemic carbs.

You are correct, it just takes time to undo the deficiency. Just keep on trucking and before you know it your BG will come down and your Mg levels will improve.

Isn't it aggravating that we just can't flip a switch and fix all of this stuff.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-04T21:52:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exatest.1353/post-35526>

[http://chriskresser.com/the-role-of-vitamin-d-deficiency-in-thyroid-disorders](http://http://chriskresser.com/the-role-of-vitamin-d-deficiency-in-thyroid-disorders)

> My current approach with these patients is to do a cautious trial of raising their serum levels to a range of 60-70 ng/mL. If their symptoms improve at this level, I will then switch them to a maintenance dose while watching for clinical signs of vitamin D toxicity. These include kidney stones (also a sign of vitamin K2 deficiency), low appetite, nausea, vomiting, thirst, excessive urination, weakness and nervousness. I will also monitor serum calcium levels, because **elevated calcium in the blood is a sign of vitamin D toxicity** and a significant risk factor for cardiovascular disease (especially in the presence of vitamin K2 deficiency). Calcium levels above 11-12 mg/dL (or 2.8-3 mmol/L) are indicative of vitamin D toxicity.

# Thread: Strange Symptom
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-symptom.1354/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rosekelly — 2012-05-09T00:42:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-symptom.1354/post-22390>

I recently started icing my neck. Reason: to tighten it up per some photos of a forum member who did the same, saw great benefit in one month. So I iced my neck for 2-3 days, and then I noticed the inner end of my right clavicle had bulged. It's hard, as in the bone grew. It's not obvious when you look at it, but it feels bigger than the other clavicle.

Weird, but also scary, since I currently have endometrial cancer and thought it looked like a metastatic spread into the bone or maybe lymph. So -- a visit to the ER, where I got myself thoroughly checked out with an X-ray, blood test, and CT scan, all of which showed nothing. The Stanford docs were stumped. Never seen that, but they don't think it's cancer-related.

All I can conclude is that it's ice-related. It's been there for five days now. Btw, I stopped icing right away on noticing it, and I'm reluctant to start again. Now that I'm not scared about it, I have to say I'm quite fascinated.

### JC2K — 2012-05-09T11:02:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-symptom.1354/post-22505>

Bizarre one. Did you have a layer of fat there that could have been covering it? I don't see the fat going away that fast (2-3 days) though. Other thought could be some sort of allergic reaction (hard = swollen vs hard = bone?)

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-09T18:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-symptom.1354/post-22590>

Bizarre is right. The Stanford docs said they'd never seen this. The thing is, it's only on one side, so if it were a fat layer melting away, you'd think it would happen the same on both sides. I don't have that much fat there anyway, so probably not. I'm curious as to whether it will stay like this or go back to normal. So far, it's staying, but it's only been six days.

I should add that there was some mild tenderness to the throat area when this began. That's now gone, but the lump remains.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Getting my runner husband to try Leptin RX
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-my-runner-husband-to-try-leptin-rx.1355/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BeingVenus — 2012-05-09T00:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-my-runner-husband-to-try-leptin-rx.1355/post-22391>

So, I finally convinced my husband to try the Leptin RX (he still refuses to try CT,) but he absolutely will not stop running. He's about 30-40 pounds overweight and runs 3-5 miles 5 times a week. I really want him to try this but I don't want him to feel horrible and then blame it on the diet (instead of on the cardio... which is what he should do,) He's been eating about 80/20 primal for a year. His back, teeth, and arthritis have all improved dramatically but he just can't seem to get the abdominal fat off. I keep trying to convince him it's the stress of his job + staying up too late on the computer + the chronic cardio but he thinks the running is the only thing keeping him sane. I tried letting him read some of the Leptin RX series but he says he doesn't really understand any of it and I'm having a hard time translating it for him. Some day I'll convince him but for now I don't think he's biting and I want him to try this sooner rather than later. Anyway, my question is, will anything bad happen if he tries the diet and timing portion of Leptin RX but doesn't quit the cardio? I'll try to encourage (or entice) him to go to bed earlier. Any other recommendations? Does anyone know of any links that would show him why doing cardio on a leptin plan would be detrimental that's in slightly plainer-speak? English is his second language

### Petra — 2012-05-10T09:22:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-my-runner-husband-to-try-leptin-rx.1355/post-22785>

Take it easy on him. Men have the tendency to follow, BUT they need to feel they lead the way!

# Thread: Vaccines and leaky gut
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BeingVenus — 2012-05-09T01:21:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-22408>

OK I know I'm going to ruffle some

Feathers bringing this up but what are you other mother's stance on this? I personally decided to delay since my son was born via csection and a ton of antibiotics and I was worried about possible leaky gut (which judging from his semi-frequent food induced rashes was well warranted.) Are your kids on schedule? Delayed/selective? Complete opt-out ? My current plan is to get my toddler on GAPS as soon as he's nursing less than 2x a day and then selective vax. I still think getting wild chicken pox is a good idea for instance. Then again I also second guess myself too and wonder if I've entered the pit of quackery and am doing the wrong thing. Anyway, I am extremely ambivalent. Please no vitriol, I'm just looking for personal experiences/decisions.

### donkjellberg — 2012-05-09T07:29:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-22493>

My son received all recommended vaccines before I really looked into it. Currently, he suffers from autoimmune problems. He is sensitive to dairy (even raw, grass-fed kefir), and pretty much all grains. His gut is definitely damaged. He use to have intermittent stomach aches that would put him on the ground in the fetal position as a toddler. We now home school him due the complications and headaches that came from public school. I now realize he is our responsibility alone. Most of his complications are controlled and he is much more creative these days.

Don't know if the vaccines contributed or not. I personally would have waited. Hindsight is 20/20. As you know, there are benefits and risks with vaccines. Don't decide in haste or worry about the ruffling others' feathers. Just follow your heart tempering the safety of your children.

### ColdBren — 2012-05-09T07:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-22496>

I too attribute some issues to giving my 5 year old 5 vaccines at once (required for kindergarten) she developed a vocal tic that night. I believe it was from the chicken pox virus but have no way of knowing. She is now 7 and still has the occasional tics. I wont be giving my 2 year old the chicken pox one, ever. She will probably get the rest but never more than 2 at a time......I so regret the way I did the first one

### IceBarbie — 2012-05-09T08:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-22502>

I started vaccinating my first son but he had such a bad reaction that I figured it wasn't a good idea to risk it again. 104 fever, high pitched screaming followed by deep sleep from which it was hard to wake him. This was 28+ years ago--before I had the internet to do research. So my 2nd son received no vaccines at all--even at birth. And my daughter has not received any vaccines either. Not a one. We use the Personal Conviction Waiver for Wisconsin. I continue to read all I find about vaccines and I have yet to find a truly good reason to use any vaccine. They are not tested on children (since they say that would be "unethical" to 1/2 the kids in the research group) and they contain the blood of diseased animals, cells from aborted fetuses (called "human duploid cells"), aluminum, mercury (yes, even though they say mercury was "phased out" it's still in flu shots and a number of others), peanut oil (which is thought to be the cause of all those severe peanut allergies in this country) etc. Yes, even the dreaded Tetanus shot is suspect in my book. It's rare to contract it and it's treatable even though they won't tell you that. I could go on all day about this...

One last thing: My kids are now 18, 25 & 28 and have been very healthy. In 28 years we've had 3 ear infections in our home and one of them was MINE. (They were all nursed until 2-3 years of age). They have high IQ's (son has a 150 IQ in Math & my youngest just scored a 33 out of 36 on her ACT's) and none ever had any learning issues or ADHD or ADD or other behavioral issues. I don't think it's a coincidence either. 1 in 55 boys now has an Autism diagnosis. And I never relied on the "herd mentality" that assumes I am willing to let everyone else's kids take the risk from shots to protect my own kids. Anytime we find out a kid had just been vaccinated, we avoid that kid like the plague. If vaccines were TRULY effective, vaccinated kids should be safe from unvaccinated kids anyway. But they aren't. So vaccinated kids are always getting the measles and whooping cough and it's not MY fault. The vaccines are crap and not without risk. Some young children are more vulnerable than others and no one knows who those poor kids are until the damage has been done. The chicken pox vaccine is nothing like the natural chicken pox kids used to get. Now 2 year olds are getting shingles after getting the chicken pox vaccine. My friend's baby contracted Whooping Cough from her just vaccinated 2 year old sister and almost died as an tiny infant before she was old enough to get vaccinated. On and on it goes. Okay...off my soap box now.....

### kathylu — 2012-05-09T15:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-22522>

Whoo boy! I am in the middle of this right now. I have a 5 year old who may be starting school this fall (still haven't decided, we may homeschool) and I have had him on a waaay delayed vaccine schedule. My personal policy is: no more than 1 vaccine per visit, I always request the one that has the least harmful substances in it (you have to research the ingredients for each one) and some I will not let him have at all. If we do put him in school, I will have to sign the religious exemption waiver for my state (Maryland). That doesn't feel honest to me, but it may be the best of a bad situation. Also, I should point out that I am not totally anti-vaccine, as I do believe they prevent disease. They also harm some children and I don't want mine to be one of them. Read "The Vaccine Book" by Robert W. Sears, MD. He gives a thorough and reasonable analysis of the issues and he has a delayed vaccine schedule in the book for you to use if you wish.

Also, my boy has never been ill (well, maybe 3 colds in 5 years), knock on wood. No allergies, ear infections, etc. None of the other things that seem to plague most households. Is that because I have been selective about vaccines? Who knows.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-09T15:46:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-22523>

We vaccinated both kids on a delayed schedule. Fortunately our pediatrician is old school and believes we pump our kids w/too many vaccines/antibiotics. instead of vaccinating w/ MMR at 12mth he does it at 18mths. we just finished both kids vaccine series this past march.

W/dd we waited on the chicken pox to see if we could contract it w/o having to have the shot - but there are so few cases in the area in NoVA that is damn hard contract it naturally. so we just went ahead and had her get the vaccine.

the kids get probitocs regularly, as well as bone broths...

I've worked w/biological agents - vaccines are very important - however, I do agree w/our Ped that we give far too many than we used to. I had a different set of vaccines than my kids had - they've had more - but I had tiburclum and small pox vaccines in 73 - when the were no longer standard practice (yet again old school ped) my kids now have almost as many vaccines as I've had - from when I was in the military.

Vaccines have their place and are necessary. parents and peds need to be aware of the entire family dynamic when vaccinating, especially when live viruses are used, as there can be complications associated w/those live vaccines and they can transmit the disease to un-vaccinated family members. However I do not think they are contributing to the ASD increase. I think epigenetics are playing a roll, as is diet of mother, and child.

However I do not think they are contributing to the ASD increase. I think epigenetics are playing a roll, as is diet of mother, and child.

Amen

Parenting 5 kids for the last 21 years to include one with a genetic disorder with ASD features, I have had my head wrapped all over vaccines. Autism's False Prophets is an excellent book to read to understand more about the "science" behind the studies that are often done on vaccines.

It has been dizzying to me how the vaccine schedule has changed over the years I have parented. And without a doubt I would NOT vaccinate for chicken pox. I would much rather spend the 5 days at home with a child than vaccinate.

I was late to the party on adopting a delayed schedule. Mostly I did it for #5 after #4 was diagnosed. She eventually got them all, but I took my sweet time doing it. And truthfully since none of them can be "undone", I just try not to worry anymore about it.

With respect to content of vaccines, beside the obvious ethical issues of using aborted fetal cells, I think the scientific studies that have moved away from mercury to this are the most promising. When looking at the whole body of stem cell research, there have been no successes and oh so many tumors caused by embryonic cells. The only successes have been from adult stem cells. And those have been truly amazing cures. I would hypothesize that it is because adult stem cells are more stable and if they are coming from one's own body, there is no reason for the body to reject them. What I wish for is for someone to see that and start directing vaccination in that direction. If there is way to make a vaccine "just for you" by using one's own stem cells, then perhaps it would be possible to vaccinate without the immune damage we suspect is happening.

What if we could test them for leaky gut then and use that to make informed decisions about how we nourish, vaccinate, etc.? ### IceBarbie — 2012-05-15T19:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-24285>

(NaturalNews) There is only one kind of immunity and that is natural immunity which is achieved by battling the infectious diseases itself. Vaccination is merely the artificial triggering of temporary responses to manmade pathogens. Vaccines are both harmful and dangerous and are leading to generations of humans with no natural defenses to disease.

Vaccines do not provide long-term immunity; only temporary at best. In vaccines, an antigen is injected into the body to produce a reaction and the immune system responds in the form of antibodies, but antibody presence does not confer immunity. People still catch the diseases that they are vaccinated against. Vaccines actually skip the normal immune responses to activate killer cells which can trigger an overproduction of cytokines in response to the toxic vaccine adjuvants and can damage tissues and organs and even stop the heart and block air pathways.

Vaccines should never be called immunizations because that is a misnomer. Immunity and vaccinations are two different subjects altogether. In fact, breast milk is so potent with immune energizing effects in the infant that researchers at the CDC recommend women withhold breastfeeding their children in order to boost the "effectiveness" of childhood vaccines. The paper claims that women should stop breastfeeding long enough for the man-made poison to work on artificial/temporary "immunity."

There is no such thing as a "side effect"

After-effects of vaccines are only followed for a very short time. Effects that are not seen for 30 years will not even be associated with the vaccine. Immunizations are contributing to the lowering of immunity along with the spread of auto-immune diseases such as arthritis and even AIDS throughout the world. Research clearly shows that aluminum mixed into vaccines carries a risk for autoimmunity, long-term brain inflammation, and subsequent neurological complications and may have profound and widespread adverse health complications. Many vaccines contain both aluminum and trace amounts of mercury. When you mix these 2 metals together it causes Extreme Synergistic Toxicity.

The facts:

As vaccinated disease rates go down (but not necessarily as a result of toxic vaccinations), the rate of chronic disease goes up in lock-step. Vaccines, as they are commonly given, destroy the natural immunity process and accelerate the auto-immune disease process.

Deaths from measles in 1900 were 13 per 100,000 people. In 1948: less than one. Measles vaccines introduced in 1963 but took full credit for what they never did - eliminate measles. Japanese health authorities realized that early inoculations were causing crib deaths so they postponed them until the 24th month and SIDS virtually disappeared along with whooping cough (pertussis) during the first two years of babies' lives. Instead of preventing whooping cough the DPT promotes it as well as SIDS. Reuters recently reports that according to the CDC, the number of pertussis cases is growing - in the fully vaccinated population!

The recent Bachmair vaccine study reveals that the allergy rate in vaccinated children is more than double the rate in unvaccinated ones. Vaccinated children are also nearly eight times more prone to develop asthma or chronic bronchitis than unvaccinated. Furthermore, vaccinated kiddos suffer from more neurodermatitis, herpes, otitis media, hay fever, hyperactivity, scoliosis, epilepsy and seizures, migraine headaches, thyroid disease, and SIDS than unvaccinated children. Vaccinated kids historically also have more measles than unvaccinated kids

Doctor after doctor will tell you that vaccinations have reduced the incidence of many infectious diseases but they have no real proof. In fact, all the epidemical evidence shows that disease rates rise after vaccines - in the vaccinated population. Should we trust them just on their word? Make an informed decision about you and your child's health.

I haven't yet vaccinated our son (he's just past 8 months) but I am under some pressure to at least discuss the options from my wife's side as she fears the social and governmental implications of not adhering to the schedule. I have done my best to read the relevant books, following this thread and Jack's epic commentary on vaccines on Facebook and accumulating a list of questions for the GP, so I can go down and explain which vaccines I don't see risk-benefit working out for and which I would consider but have concerns about the ingredients. I don't know how this conversation will go but I won't start him on the schedule until I feel we have had a conversation leading to satisfying answers. Currently he's super-healthy and happy child - very robust and I would hate to be the one 'doing' something to change that. I looked back at my own vaccination schedule and I received less than 10 times the vaccinations in the official Irish schedule!

Any advice on how such conversations have gone for you are welcome. We had similar discussions around child-birth where informing ourselves and standing our ground helped us avoid most common interventions so that he had an almost perfect birth. I'd like to apply the same principles here. The most grating thing is that people consider you conspiratorial because I refuse you to trust any authority point blank whereas I see this as the only sensible position given what history teaches us.

Preference is given to Karnivore and Klub members first. Support sends out an email with a link for you all to register and seal your seat for the live event. If that does not happen, more seats are then available for public purchase when they are allowed to buy.

Make sure [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com) does not go to your junk or spam folder. Make sure you see those emails, as we periodically give Karnivore and Klub members opportunities that others will never get.

### Jasmin — 2012-05-09T11:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-22508>

My doc orderd some blood tests but Im not sure if it shows if Im LR or LS - can you please help me? I asked for the rT3 and cortisoltest but he dident find it necessary.

Haemoglobin 7,7 (7,3-9,5)

Potassium 4,1 (3,5-4,6)

Natrium 140 (137-145)

Kreatinin 65 (50-90)

eGFR Kidney 88 (>=60)

HDL 2,3 (1-2,7)

LDL 10,7 (1,4-4,7)

Cholesterol 13,4 (3,3-6,9)

Triglycerid 1,0 (0,45-2,6)

Bloodpressure 120/70

Oestradiol 0,06

Prolaktin 132 (64-432)

Globulin 134 (26-110)

TSH 0,01 (0,3-4)

fT4 10,1 (10-26)

T3 0,74 (1,2-2,8)

Leukocytes 7,9 (7,3-9,5)

Platelets 204 (145-390)

Lymfocyttes 0.9 (1,3-3,5)

Monocyttes 0,4 (0,2-0,7)

ALAT 42 (10-45)

CCP -Ab(IgG) 4,4 (0.0-7.0)

CRP 0,5 (0.0-8)

DNA-Ab(IgG) 0,7 (0.0-10.0)

Is all of this of any use?

### Jasmin — 2012-05-18T10:17:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-25141>

### janagram — 2012-05-19T01:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-25345>

mmm, cravings are under control....

joints still ache....

### Jennelizabee — 2012-05-24T06:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-26963>

Jasmin, I'm new around here & this is my first post. I just had to respond to your Thyroid numbers. Your TSH is REALLY low like suppressed/hyper. Current normal TSH ranges are considered between .3-3.0 by the American Association of Clinical Endocrinology with a caveat that those of us with antibodies for Hashimotos often need a TSH 1.5 or lower. However your t4 & t3 are both quite low as in hypothyroid. What this means is that you are showing signs of being hyper & hypo at the same time. Are you on thyroid medication and if yes are you on just T4 or a t3/t4 combo?

### Jasmin — 2012-05-27T11:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-27813>

Jenneelizabee, first wellcome to this amazing forum  And thank you for your answer

I'm on thyroid medication, Erfa thyroid (combo). I have tried a lot of different medications, but they diden't work - I got Erfa in 2011. But I still fell like a zombi and gets stressed too easy. My energy is very, very low and I feel like my life is passing by without me being able to partizipate. I have been diagnosed with osteoporosis and renaud so I really need to get my life back  (I'm 37 years old) I started LR 24 of january but I'm probably one of those that have to keep working with the LR for a long time

Everything was getting better in march and so I tried to cut back on the thyroid medication but in the end of april I had to bring up the dose again because I was so exhausted - it was really bad. SLOWLY I feel the energy coming back but I really have to be carefull about what I am doing - my energy runns out so easy. The bloodtest is from end april and I am going to make a new test in the beginning of june. But since I started medications in 2009, my TSH went down and my T3 and T4 has allways stayed in the lower range. The highest my T3 and T4 went up was T3 1,21 and T4 14, so I have allways been in the low range although I took 3.5 grain Erfa.

How is it possible to treat both hyper and hypo at the same time?

### janagram — 2012-05-27T21:17:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-27902>

I'm post menopausal, yet I feel I am getting benefits from maca caps....

### Momma28 — 2012-05-27T23:36:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-27923>

> **janagram;25428 wrote:** mmm, cravings are under control....
>
> joints still ache....

Have you eliminated eggs and nightshades? I was still having a small amount of joint pain as well, until I discontinued these foods. The eggs are usually fed a ratio of grain, hence we still suffer inflammation from eating these. Try this and see what happens...

### janagram — 2012-05-29T00:28:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-28082>

# Thread: Thought I was LS but now I am back to the BAB.  Anyone else?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vwaggs — 2012-05-09T16:10:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22526>

I was doing great on the post Leptin Rx. I was becoming cold adapted, no cravings, and all the signs were there that I was LS. Then the toxin dump hit and I haven't been the same even a month later so now I am starting again with the Leptin Rx. Not fair I tell you! I had gained back most of what I lost during the dump and felt like crap. And the cravings and hunger kicked in and I found myself eating junk (even WHEAT) after months of no cheats and not even wanting to. I am so upset because I thought I had this craving thing cured. So I am back to the BAB and things leptin Rx. Anyone else have to go back after they thought everything was fixed? So not happy right now. Is this even normal?

### Melinda — 2012-05-09T16:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22532>

I'm not able to leave Leptin Rx. Not sure why since I can't afford testing right now. When I try to move away from the BAB, my day just doesn't go well: weird sweat, increased hunger and too alert in the evening. I'm not hungry for breakfast (never have been) so I'd rather "listen to my body" but it doesn't work for me now. I'm hoping that when I live with the cold next fall and winter that I'll get a great circadian reset and be "most" fixed!

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-05-09T19:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22613>

I had to go back to the BAB too. I started the Rx last October and I'm thinking I moved to the post leptin rx in January. When I started CT I had the cravings, hunger, and mega toxin dump too initially. No weight gain, but I also was continuing a ketogenic paleo diet. I went back to eating the BAB late March / early April and am continuing now. Despite the correlation with the CT, I'm confident that the cause of needing to go back to the leptin RX is my unresolved underlying health issues.

In my case I had lingering fatigue and brain fog and after one of Dr. K's posts over at MDA I had my MSH tested. It was on the low side (as was my ACTH and serum cortisol). It took some work to find a helpful Dr. and figure out what the root cause was. For me this is mycoplasma pneumonia and HHV6 reactivated infections. Also, I've lost over 60lbs so far, but I still have 50 to lose to get back down to my normal weight - so I'm still obese and so I still have all those fat cells working against me. Guess it's a double whammy.

So to wrap it up.... I think the key is a combo of history and symptoms with educated testing. Unlimited financial resources would be helpful too!!! Too bad mine are finite.

### kathylu — 2012-05-09T21:57:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22650>

Same story here. I had most of the LS signs and was losing weight slowly. Started CT in March thinking it would accelerate my gains and it was like a brick wall fell on me! Weight loss stopped, I get very sick from the toxin dump (muscle soreness like I was a marathoner, nasty headaches, nausea) and I became RAVENOUS! I went back to the BAB and eating lunch. Emailed Jack and he told me to get my hormones tested (3rd time he had said that to me), so I finally did. Everything except cortisol was low (my pregnenolone was 7. Seven!) I started BHRT on 4/3 and I am feeling much better. Hunger is better now and weight loss has re-started. So, maybe you need a biohack too...just sayin'.

Hang in there. There is a solution!

### melrito — 2012-05-09T22:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22659>

same issue for me, did the reset, had all the "wins", but as soon as i started ct, i was hungry and gained 4 pounds in a week. I've been doing Ct almost a month now. sugar cravings that i hadn't had for months are back, although i am not giving in and feel like I'm slowly making progress again with this - some days are better than others. I have gone on and off the BAB since starting CT and in general feel better with the BAB so do that most days. I am not experiencing the toxic dump like others, just this small weight gain and a bit of tummy bloating sometimes. I have no money for labs now - I'm sure my hormones are off - but am planning to do what I can afford next month.

### janagram — 2012-05-10T01:29:51+0300

### vwaggs — 2012-05-10T01:33:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22695>

I am so relieved I am not alone! I KNOW I need to do labs but keep putting it off due to money. I was going to wait until 6 months after starting ct to test. The BAB helped me so much today. Cravings gone. I will just keep going until I become more cold adapted (although I thought it would be here by now) I think I started mid February but I have only been doing it 3 times a week for 30 min and my water with ice only gets down to 58-68 degrees. So it could be a slower process for me but I am not sick and I am almost at goal weight so I didn't think I needed it every day. Plus I HATE it still so there is that! I hope I can embrace the cold soon! Very frustrating to not get results.

So it could be a slower process for me but I am not sick and I am almost at goal weight so I didn't think I needed it every day. Plus I HATE it still so there is that! I hope I can embrace the cold soon! Very frustrating to not get results.

CT is supposed to help you detoxify and balance your hormones too, amongst other things (thyroid, adrenals). So CT as much as you can. It took me 2 weeks of CTing to get the detoxifying going along with the ugly side affect of hunger. Then another 8 weeks of CTing to get to a point where I lost 3 of the 8 I gained from CT. However, all along I know I'm getting rid of excess estrogen and now my hunger has really subsided. I CT about 5-6 days a week in 55ish water. I wish I could get it colder but I need more ice right now.

### tweety — 2012-05-10T02:58:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22706>

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-10T03:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22710>

I've been CTing for 12-13 weeks now so you may be turning a corner soon. I'm going to keep at it as my thermostat is definitely coming on. I jump two degrees in temp after 30 min in the bath!

### Petra — 2012-05-10T09:21:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22784>

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-11T17:23:41+0300
