After reading this thread, I'm a little scared to even do CT!! I wasn't sure if I was going to do it anyway. I'm starting LR Sunday and just hoping that goes well for me. I obviously don't want to gain on it!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-11T17:29:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23176>

Do the CT - it's like spring cleaning a messy hoarder-like closet. The entire area in and surrounding look horrid and get worse before they get better. You have to take everything out, look at it, throw some away, organize, figure out how to build it back. Some people have a much messier disorganized closet with clothes from the last 20 years. Those are going to get much worse and take longer before they end up with a glossy new Container Store masterpiece!

Substitute closet for fat cells and there you go. If you are not healthy, consider your closet a mess and just enjoy the fact that even though you have a messy disaster waiting for you this weekend, you will work though it and end up with something mucho better.

Too bad we can't get tax write offs for the crap coming out of our bodies like we do our closets.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23500>

Maybe that's what my problem is. I've been on the LR since January 1st and holding steady weight wise, then when I started CT (mostly spot icing on my stomach) I started slowly gaining & my stomach has gotten bigger! Ugh. The hunger and the amount of time it's taking to see any results is frustrating and I'm finding myself cheating more often, I'm sure that doesn't help.

### Dali Dula — 2012-05-14T14:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23866>

I basically never stopped the BAB. It's the way I live now. Some mornings like right now I wake up not hungry at all even though my last meal was over twelve hours ago. If I don't eat a BAB I eat a small amount of protein and put some butter in my coffee just to let my body know it's ok. I also noticed ,although it's hard to tell, that everyone on this thread is female. Don't know if that means anything.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-14T16:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23879>

> **Dali Dula;23948 wrote:** I basically never stopped the BAB. It's the way I live now. Some mornings like right now I wake up not hungry at all even though my last meal was over twelve hours ago. If I don't eat a BAB I eat a small amount of protein and put some butter in my coffee just to let my body know it's ok. I also noticed ,although it's hard to tell, that everyone on this thread is female. Don't know if that means anything.

I've been strict on it since early March, and doing serious CT. Losses have been slow (but I am shrinking - big improvement over previous year of paleo) and I never ever ever want that damn BAB. I tried transitioning to the post RX, but the snacking and cravings came back so I realized I had a ways to go on the RX. I was a fat kid tho, so I am going on the theory there is a LOT to work through in these cells!

Also, if judging from anecdotal evidence of the ladies on this site - the fat cell apoptosis isn't an overnight thing. It's simply going to take awhile. Afterall, I imagine it would NOT be optimal to have a gazillion dead cells floating around trying to be processed out. Probably you lose at what you can effectively process. That's my working theory.

To add to Dali's comment, I've observed a pretty material difference in:

Men/Women

Former athletic folks/always fat folks

Old/young folks.

That my friend, is the way the cookie crumbles. Fortunately those of us that are old and fat are wise and can handle it!

### vlynnb — 2012-05-14T17:11:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23899>

Caveman,

Your closet analogy was just what I needed. Maybe have something to do with cleaning out my recently deceased mother's house this past month. The mess is still a mess but gradually getting better.

Because of mom's illness, death, and all that comes after I have put off CT though the LRX seems to be going well, losing about a pound a week (only about 12 pounds to go), increased perspiration, really no cravings from the beginning but had been on hhcg. Also haven't done any testing, just too busy trying to meet deadlines with all of the post death financial stuff. I did get to hate the BAB though and cut back a bit. Then to start reading more about CT problems people were having was really making me more reluctant. I needed the reassurance your closet analogy gives.

We close on the house sale on the 25th of this month so that is my target date for starting CT, though I'll start the face CT right now.

Thanks for all I learn from all of you.

### janagram — 2012-06-09T03:01:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-30224>

well, i'm having trouble sticking with the LR...I just wanna see some weight loss. I know yr not sposed to lose on LR, yr just priming yrself....but ...I do get cravings...sometimes I really want a pancake, sometimes its ice cream...argh!! CT? that would really ttake a while....well just the kinda mood I'm in today! (plus I just ate some choc. chips)

# Thread: Vit D, TSH, T4/T3 Free lab results - please review
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clabbergirl — 2012-05-09T17:27:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22565>

Here are the results of labs I had drawn a week ago. I'd love some input on how far from optimal they are and any suggestions on what I might try to move these numbers in the right direction.

Vitamin D, 25 OH total - 95 (30-100 ng/mL range)

Vitamin D, 25 OH, D2 - < 4

Vitamin D, 25 OH, D3 - 95

TSH - 3.98 (.40 - 4.50 range)

T4, Free - 1.0 (.8 - 1.8 ng/dL)

T3, Free - 2.7 (2.3 - 4.2 pg/mL)

I've been supplementing Vit D at least 5,000/day for over 2 years, and upped it to 10,000-15,000/day within the last 2 months. I'm currently taking Armour for low thyroid, so those numbers are with supplementation. Thanks.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-09T17:36:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22569>

I'm sooo jealous of your Vit D! That is great! However, your thyroid is not so great. Your FT3 (the bio-available hormone) should be at the top of the range and the FT4 should be mid-range to near the top. Not to mention that TSH is way too high. I would guess you still feel yucky, fatigued and maybe even lethargic. That's how I felt with similar numbers. Many docs these days use 3.0 as a top range for TSH. Look to me like you need to up your meds. How much are you taking?

Are you chewing or braking up the Armour before swallowing it? Armour has a filler (can't remember which one) that doesn't release well into the system, if not broken apart. Maybe try that before upping the meds.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-09T17:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22573>

> **Patty Cakes;22648 wrote:** I'm sooo jealous of your Vit D! That is great! However, your thyroid is not so great. Your FT3 (the bio-available hormone) should be at the top of the range and the FT4 should be mid-range to near the top. Not to mention that TSH is way too high. I would guess you still feel yucky, fatigued and maybe even lethargic. That's how I felt with similar numbers. Many docs these days use 3.0 as a top range for TSH. Look to me like you need to up your meds. How much are you taking?
>
> .

Concur w/this... your ranges are crazy! did you get a rT3 run as well??? if not next time you need to push for that, even if you have topay out pocket for it!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-05-09T17:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22580>

I did not get rT3 even though I asked for it. Frustrating. I had to beg for the Vit D test also. Ugh.

Patty Cakes and Shijin, I do feel lethargic and fatigued. I'm taking 120mg of thryoid so I guess I should up it. I had upped it several months back and felt nothing at all. This is after a year of slowing upping my amount and continuing to feel lousy and gain weight (or maintain) even at 300mg. I never felt 'wired and tired' either. I finally said forget it and just went back to what my doctor prescribed - 120mg.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-09T18:07:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22582>

Clabbergirl, I liked the old version of Armour but not the new version (post late 2009). I just wonder if you need to change the med first. I've read where some people have challenges with Armour. Your lab tests almost look like you are not on anything at all, certainly not 120 mg. I read somewhere that the average amount most folks take is around 150-300mg/day.

I also tried Naturethroid and it was OK but I still wasn't satisfied. Found Thyroid-S (from Thailand) and liked the results but didn't like the fillers. But I still used it for over two years. Then I found NP Thyroid by Acella. It's the generic version of Armour so very easy to get and taken sublingually under the tongue it just dissolves into nothingness almost immediately. I had a rT3 problem so I'm on T3 only for a few months but when I'm done I'm going back on the NP Thyroid (150 mg)!

# Thread: No TOM after HCG
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-09T20:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-22631>

Just wondering if other hcg users have had trouble with no TOM??

I did my first round of hcg in July 2011 and had one period after starting and nothing since. I also turned 40 in Aug 2011.

I went to my gyn and told them about the hcg but they don't seem to think it is related. They made me take a pregnancy test and I wasn't preggers (obviously). They ran labs and my estrogen is way low but normal FSH. So now the endo is sending me for a Pituitary MRI because he says that my FSH should be high in light of the low estrogen.

I saw one comment on the blogs about this happening to a 20 year old girl who lost a lot of weight and Dr K said not to worry it was most likely hypothalmic amenorrhea and it would correct itself. How do you restart the hypothalmus?

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T03:28:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-22713>

it's common for hcg to mess up your periods while you are on it but it's not common for it to cause any problems this far out. I've been seeing a rash of pituitary problems lately. Weird. have you been doing CT? It might help.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-10T03:52:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-22728>

HI Colleen! Have you hEard of other pituitary problems Out there? I feel like it was in the middle of some reset and now it's just stuck. Lol. I just want to control - alt - delete the whole HPA axis. I quit the CT because my Raynauds kept flaring and I just couldn't face being cold any more. But now that the air temp is warmer I need to get back in the cold tub!

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T04:00:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-22729>

I've run into 3 friends lately with pituitary problems. One had cysts on his. Have you tried Dr. Terry Wahl's diet for autoimmune disease? I bet it would help you. It got her out a wheel chair even with the worst kind of MS.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-10T04:21:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-22737>

I've watched the video on her and took notes but I'm still trying to get to fat burner status! Plus, I put on a bunch of weight when I went rogue on the reset  \[fell off reset into a barrel of nuts and coconut manna\]

Right now I'm back to doing keto diet, as in no veggies. Once I get over this low blood sugar business, I plan to move toward eating like that.

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T04:25:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-22738>

I really think it might help. And I bet you'd lose weight on it anyway.

### Claudia — 2012-05-18T03:48:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-25069>

Is the diet available for free or do you have to buy her book and DVDs?

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-18T06:36:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-tom-after-hcg.1370/post-25114>

If you watch the TED talk she did you can take notes. It's fairly simple. Well, I have my notes here:

Brain mitochondria diet

3 cups of green leaves- kale, parsley,\*

3 cups sulfur rich veggies- cabbage, broc, cauliflower, turnips, radishes, onion, asparagus

3 cups of bright color- beets carrots peppers red cabbage berries peaches oranges\*

Grass fed meat salmon herring\*

Organ meat once a week. Heart tongue sweetbreads

Seaweed once a week at least\*

# Thread: Please Help With My Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1373/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### indigogirl — 2012-05-09T22:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1373/post-22652>

Story of my life...I'm always tired...been a poor/light sleeper my whole life.

I am a 59 year old female, menopausal, hypothyroid for almost 20yrs (familial...mom and sister also hypo), weight perfect until my mid thirties when I started emotionally overeating...yo-yo'ed for the last 20 years...about 60-70 lbs overweight now. 5' 2", small boned. No uterus...still have my ovaries and cervix.

On 9/28/11 :

Vitamin B12. 617. range 254-1320

Cortisol AM. 14.30. range 3.70-19.40

My doc said these were both in the normal range.

ARE THEY????

Most recent labs from 1/18/12: for blood work

Estradiol. 17.0 (pg/ml). Range 50-150

Testosterone total. 18.53 (14.00-76.00 ng/dl). Range 40-80

Progesterone. 0.3. (0.0-0.2 ng/ml) H Range 1.0-10

T4 Free. 0.72 (0.76-1.46 ng/dL) L.

TSH. 2.610 (0.358-3.740 uIU/ mL)

T3 total. 1.43 (0.70-1.90 ng/mL)

T3 Free. 4.1 (2.0-4.4 pg/mL)

I take 90 mg of Armour.

I take bioedentical estriol,estradiol and progesterone.

I know my Testosterone sucks...won't take bioedentical testosterone...I get facial cysts...am taking 25 mg 7 keto DHEA when I remember.

The above labs were done with my GYN who is CW, but open to discussing things with me.

Today my internist told me I need a sleep study because of my constant exhaustion. I think it's a waste of time.

I told her I wanted a CRP. She said she couldnt really justify it. My GYN will do the CRP at my next visit (August) if I want to her to.

Will someone please hack these labs and tell me what you think??

THANK YOU!!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

I am a 59 year old female, menopausal, hypothyroid for almost 20yrs (familial...mom and sister also hypo), weight perfect until my mid thirties when I started emotionally overeating...yo-yo'ed for the last 20 years...about 60-70 lbs overweight now. 5' 2", small boned. ARE THEY????

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T03:27:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1374/post-22711>

Oh man, you are a mess, honey. Just about everything is in the toilet. 7 Keto DHEA isn't the best to take if you want to raise your testosterone. It won't do it. Regular DHEA will though. I take 50 mg and it keeps my testosterone on the high end of normal. You need more bioidentical estrogen and progesterone though. Your free T4 is in the toilet but your T3 is pretty decent. The progesterone would help your thyroid convert better. Your cortisol is pretty low for morning too. I'd get a 4 times a day one done and see if you're getting your peak at night instead of the morning. Do you wake up about 3 am? your B12 is low too. I like mine up at 1000 or a little higher.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-10T04:26:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1374/post-22739>

Colleen

Thank you for responding. I can't take the testosterone. I broke out from DHEA, too.. That's why I'm taking the 7 Keto DHEA evn tho it doesn't convert to testosterone. Every time I've taken it I get huge facial cysts. I just can't go there!

What do I do about my T4?

I can ask to have my cortisol tested.

The problem is my GYN, who is doing the testing thinks my ranges are "normal".

Does Dr. Dzugan (correct name? )work with people from all over?

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T04:32:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1374/post-22740>

Yes Dzugan works with people from all over, but you need a local doctor who is willing to consult with him and follow his direction.

How much dhea were you taking? You might have been taking too much. You really need to get that testosterone up there. It might be that the zits came from all your other hormones being so low in comparison too. They all need balanced and yours are NOT in the normal range. The doctor could add a bit of T4 to your armor to get the T4 levels up. But it may be that your iron is low or your cortisol is low and that accounts for the strange results there. How can she even say your ranges are normal? They are in the toilet!

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T04:34:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1374/post-22742>

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-10T04:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-up-for-hacking.1376/post-22747>

Here are my labs as of now. I have an appointment with a functional med doctor next week Wed. Saw the endo this week and he ordered a pituitary mri. What I am working on is - no TOM, leaky gut, Hashimotos, adrenal fatigue, and losing 10 pounds!

Vit D is 44 (range 30 - 100)

DHEA is 141 (range 102-1185).

Estradiol is < 15 (as in way low).

FSH 9.8 (Follicular phase 2.5-10.2; postmenopausal 23-116)

TSH 2.82 (.4-4.5)

Free T4 1.0 (.8-1.8)

Prolactin 7.3 (non-pregnant range 3-30; Postmenopausal 2-10),

Progesterone 10 mg (Follicular phase < or = 185; Luteal phase < or = 285; Postmenopausal < or = 45);

Testosterone 11 (2-45);

Testosterone Free 1.2 (0.1-6.4).

GYN has me on Estradiol. I've been taking progesterone cream on my own.

muchas gracias

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-10T16:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-up-for-hacking.1376/post-22816>

Do you know where you're at in your cycle via temps or CM? that might help.

your DHEA is low, your E is low (btw was this hsEstradiol or just the regular one?)

your P is low as well.

T is ok, I think you want in more in the middle ranges (but in general all your hormones are in the toilet!)

You're missing Pregnenolone, alderstrone - not sure if they were run - but if they were they some times are the last set of labs to come in.

Your also missing some thyroid test: Free T3, rT3, - hard to determine where you are at w/o a full set of labs.

initial guess - is you've probably got some pregnenolone steal going on, as a result of your adrenal fatigue - in addition to your Hashis...

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-11T00:40:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-up-for-hacking.1376/post-23034>

Agree w/ almost all that has been said here! Although I think you may need more Testosterone as I thought optimal was around 60. Don't quote me on that but I had that in my notes although not sure who said that so the source could be bad.

If I were you, I would go to a natural dessicated thyroid (NDT) for the Hashis unless you have a rT3 ratio that's too low. My ratio was low so in March I switched from NDT to T3 only to help clear the rT3. I plan on going back on the NDT if everything goes to plan. However, if you have low cortisol/adrenal function than T3 may be a good choice! I have done just about everything there is to do w/Hashis/Hypo to help it, except T4 only drugs (which I won't take). I have taken almost every NDT out there and now have my favorite brand of what works best for me.

Progesterone affects thyroid and low progesterone can lead to low thyroid hormones. So make sure your progesterone is optimal but still balanced with the estrogen. The pregnenolone will also affect progesterone. I'm not a sex hormone expert but all those things are interconnected. Also I beleive the doc said that any DHEA under 160 is deficient.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-31T20:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-up-for-hacking.1376/post-28667>

Agree about the thyroid. Definitely need testing of FT3 and rT3 to check that conversion. Also my guess is that you will have a low FT3 based on the symptoms you have reported and you will need to add in some meds. It took me three years and four docs and no one would write a script with confirmed Hashis antibodies and a TSH over 4, just like yours. I self-treated until I finally found a wonderful BHRT doc. Be proactive when you go see this endo. I personally have had the worst time with endos so hopefully your experience will be better. Also even though your basic iron looks good your ferritin is very low and your iron sat % should be around 35%. Definitely need that offal! Good luck!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-04T23:49:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-up-for-hacking.1376/post-29424>

Just had super fun appt w endo -NOT. Geez. He basically said that he doesn't know why I'm not making hormones and has no advice for me. Then he gave me a hard time about seeing the func med dr. Which I don't really know how he knew about that. I did bring my lab work with me but he didn't look at at it end the end of appt. I did see the med student first and he looked at them. Idk. The med student told me my cholesterol was a little high. I said I wasn't worried and didn't you make hormones from cholesterol? He said yes. I said I'd take the extra cholesterol for now. The endo first said the pituitary cyst was not big enough to be causing my problems but then he looked at the MRI again and sort of changed his mind. But I think it's an incidental finding. I told him I stopped eating gluten and he asked me why. I said gluten and Hashimotos could be related. He said they are. So why are you asking why I quit gluten? You should be telling me to quit! Plus he gave me a hard time about switching to natural thyroid. He said "you want pig?" "who knows what's in that." he'd rather have chemicals. Whatever. Then he asked what the func med doc recommended and when I said elimination diet he pretty much rolled his eyes. I guess he doesn't think food matters. Frustrating. He suggested I go on back to the gyn or go on a birth control pill if I want to cycle. Umm, that's not the freaking point! He sent me away for 6 months w lab work before. He's going to check TSH, free T 4, prolactin and morning cortisol. Seems like he should check the cortisol now but whatever. And repeat MRI in 9 months. I will keep up w func med doc and new PMD in Aug and idk maybe I should step up to a gun who specializes in hormones. Or maybe that's overkill? Endo def seemed annoyed that "someone else" was measuring my thyroid antibodies. Drs are so territorial! Then he wonders why I pay out of pocket - umm to be treated like a whole person! I told him I've gains 15#s. do you think he cares? No but it's a big freaking deal to me! Ugh. Sorry rant over.

# Thread: New to LR, well, formally at least. Some Qs.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### paisley777 — 2012-05-10T06:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-22766>

Hello all.

(It's a long post....)

I found my way to LR by way of HCG and Grammy1952.

LR protocol came as a bit of a surprise as it seems I have naturally fallen into many of the LR habits on my own.

History:

Always been overweight but never been higher than 160# (5'2). When I hit that number I decided to change things and found HCG in Fall 2010.

After R1 â€“ lost 20. Stabilized and never gained it back.

R2 â€“ lost 23 â€“ didn't stabilize for a few reasons. Went too fast adding variety, traveling and traveling food.

Now... Just finished R3 (VERY STRICT) and lost 10# (ran out of hcg). Was stabilizing fine for 11 days and then jumped 2#. (TOM may have been a factor). I got worried so I started LR officially 3 days ago. Now down 1# & eating 2 meals.

I had just read about LR maybe 5 days before all that, but decided to read more. Came as a surprise...

Before 2010 I was a night owl. Exhausted in the mornings. Very carb heavy food, processed everything. Cravings.

But, my R1 P3 in 2010 looked amazingly like LR and I have kept these habits. I loved eating a BAB of bacon, eggs, a little cheese (1-2oz), and heavy cream in coffee. Could often skip lunch. I still enjoy sweet things, but don't 'crave' it. Don't snack a lot. I have never liked working out in the morning and usually worked out after getting home ~4-6pm. (not exercising now though) Eat dinner by 7pm.

Now I wake up naturally 5:30 â€“ 6:30. Almost never need an alarm. Feel awake in the morning too. Really sleepy by ~9 - 9:30.

So....

I have another 10-15# to go. No HCG. I now live and work in South Korea, so access to tests and Drs (communication issues, cultural issues) are not available. Also, even if I could find a test source to ship here, my budget is very small.

???

From what I've read it seems I may be less LR than I was after I started naturally making some of these changes, but I don't think I'm all the way there yet. Does this make sense???

Since I don't have nearly all the LR symptoms and I may have some LS... can I start HIIT exercise? I really want to but I have been afraid to do anything that'll further mess up stabilization & now LR. If I do start to exercise, how will I know if it's too soon and I should stop?

Nice to meet you all.

Thanks!

Paisley

### colleencoble — 2012-05-10T22:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-22984>

Glad you made it, Paisley! I use my real name here but I'm Grammy1952 over on the hcg forum.  I have said all along that I believe hcg got me partially leptin sensitive. I always ate just the 2 meals allowed on hcg which I think helped. Plus the action of hcg on the hypothalamus. just read everything here and monitor your symptoms since you can't get any help over there. Dr. K has some posts about symptoms and when to start HIIT and what time of day to do it as well. Good luck!

### paisley777 — 2012-05-11T03:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-23064>

Thank you for the reply Collen.

I have appreciated your time and posts on the HCG forum.

One confusing point... the sweat thing. First - if you are not exercising, then how can you know if you sweat different when you exercise? lol

Also, when doing searches, I have seen MANY posts - some say the change is you will sweat more BUT others by those who have completed the reset say they used to sweat but hardly do at all anymore.

So, I still don't know what to make of that LS marker. :/

### colleencoble — 2012-05-11T15:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-lr-well-formally-at-least-some-qs.1377/post-23142>

I've only heard people who are LS remark that they sweat more. I never used to sweat at all but I do a little now. And you know me--I'm allergic to exercise. LOL So it's just a difference I've noticed that's very minor since I don't do much exercise either.

# Thread: Faulty Fat Sensor Implicated in Obesity and Liver Disease
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faulty-fat-sensor-implicated-in-obesity-and-liver-disease.1378/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-10T06:13:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faulty-fat-sensor-implicated-in-obesity-and-liver-disease.1378/post-22767>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/02/120219143222.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/02/120219143222.htm)

ScienceDaily (Feb. 19, 2012) â€” Defects in a protein that functions as a dietary fat sensor may be a cause of obesity and liver disease, according to a study published in the journal Nature, led by researchers at Imperial College London. The findings highlight a promising target for new drugs to treat obesity and metabolic disorders.

The protein GPR120 is found on the surface of cells in the gut, liver and fat tissue and allows cells to detect and respond to unsaturated fatty acids from the diet, especially the omega-3 fatty acids which are believed to have a beneficial impact on health. Scientists found that mice deficient in GPR120 were more prone to developing obesity and liver disease when fed a high-fat diet. They also found that people with a certain mutation in the gene encoding GPR120, which stops the protein from responding to omega-3 fatty acids, were significantly more likely to be obese.

In the gut, when unsaturated fatty acids from food bind to GPR120, this stimulates the release of hormones that suppress appetite and stimulate the pancreas to secrete insulin. When fat cells sense high levels of fat in the blood through GPR120, it stimulates them to divide to produce more fat cells to store all the fat, reducing the risk of fatty liver and furring of the arteries. This mechanism could be an important pathway for bringing about some of the healthy effects of omega-3s.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The epigenetic switch for t-cell mediated auto immunity is identified
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-epigenetic-switch-for-t-cell-mediated-auto-immunity-is-identified.1379/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-10T06:30:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-epigenetic-switch-for-t-cell-mediated-auto-immunity-is-identified.1379/post-22771>

Histone deacetylase 9 deficiency protects against effector T cell-mediated systemic autoimmunity.

Yan K, Cao Q, Reilly CM, Young NL, Garcia BA, Mishra N.

Source

Section on Rheumatology, Department of Internal Medicine, Wake Forest University School of Medicine, Winston-Salem, North Carolina 27157, UAS.

Abstract

Co-repressor histone deacetylase 9 (HDAC9) plays a key role in the development and differentiation of many types of cells, including regulatory T cells. However, the biological function of HDAC9 in T effector cells is unknown. Systemic autoimmune diseases like lupus, diabetes, and rheumatoid arthritis have dysfunctional effector T cells. To determine the role of HDAC9 in systemic autoimmunity, we created MRL/lpr mice with HDAC9 deficiency that have aberrant effector T cell function. HDAC9 deficiency led to decreased lympho-proliferation, inflammation, autoantibody production, and increased survival in MRL/lpr mice. HDAC9-deficient mice manifested Th2 polarization, decreased T effector follicular cells positive for inducible co-stimulator, and activated T cells in vivo compared with HDAC9-intact MRL/lpr mice. HDAC9 deficiency also resulted in increased GATA3 and roquin and decreased BCL6 gene expression. HDAC9 deficiency was associated with increased site-specific lysine histone acetylation at H3 (H3K9, H3K14, and H3K18) globally that was localized to IL-4, roquin, and peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor-γ promoters with increased gene expression, respectively. In kidney and spleen, HDAC9 deficiency decreased inflammation and cytokine and chemokine production due to peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor γ overexpression. These findings suggest that HDAC9 acts as an epigenetic switch in effector T cell-mediated systemic autoimmunity.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Bleeding ear?  Ct?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-10T17:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22827>

Ok, I've had tinnitus a bit lately - THOUGHT it was in conjunction with my new deeper CT efforts. This a.m. I q-tipped and there was blood in one of my ears.

Yikes! Bleeding from the head doesn't really seem good. Has anybody had anything like this? Tinnitus? Debating on giving it a day or going to the clinic. It's not painful, although I did have a headache this a.m., and off and on this last week (again, attributed to deeper CT and detox).

Thoughts from the very sweet and brilliant peanut gallery here?

omg, the thought of saying "well, i thought the headaches and tinnitus were from my cold baths and detox" is just not something I want to face today! Time to google. . .

### thyme — 2012-05-10T17:30:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22830>

I'm sorry about your ear!!!

I can't give you any useful advice on the clinic or no front, but I can be somewhat useless by getting onto my hard-won experience soapbox for a moment, which might at least distract you....

I came down with swimmer's ear a few years ago; the pain was far beyond any physical injury I'd ever had before, including migraines and broken limbs. I wasn't swimming (or CTing at the time.) The ER doc and the follow-up general doc \_both\_ looked at my ear infection and stated, as simple fact, "You use Q-tips."

The inner part of your ear is so very delicate that the cotton bud of a q-tip can scratch it, and if an infection then sets in...holy bleep, but I wondered how young children survive repeated ear infections with their mental facilities intact.

You could well have an outer ear infection/swimmer's ear--but blood there, I don't want to speculate.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-10T17:49:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22833>

I get tinnitus from CTing. Have been wondering about that. It usually gets better or goes away as I warm up.

I'd bin those q-tips. My ear got clogged from them as a child and the doc said to just point the shower head towards the ear when in the shower... I guess you do need a European shower head to accomplish this. I'm not sure even that is necessary...

Not much input on whether or not this needs to be seen by a doctor, sorry.

# Thread: Confused and Pondering Next Step
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rosekelly — 2012-05-10T20:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22930>

So I love CT. I was doing ice baths daily, gradually lowering the temp and immersing more of my body. I've also done three oceans swims in the last two weeks, temp 54. But, as I've mentioned here before, I have surgery scheduled later this month for endometrial cancer. I did think CT and abdominal icing would help me prepare for surgery, as well as healing my body in other ways that would help me deal with this and also prevent future cancer.

But every time I ice, my belly swells, and I get some cramping. Ice is not supposed to cause inflammation. But there you have it.

I don't know if I should ice or not. I'm baffled.

This type of cancer is estrogen-sensitive. I started taking DIM and Pro-gest natural cream last week. Less swelling, but I was also doing less icing. Now I'm icing again. Swelling and cramps again.

What is causing this swelling?

I don't want to ask Jack on the ask Jack thread, because it seems inappropriate to ask him questions directly related to my personal case of illness. However, I have no one else to ask. When I last saw the oncologist, I asked her what this fluid was all about, and she shrugged. "Just the disease," she said, as if it was immaterial. Well, it's not immaterial to me!

What I'm trying to figure out is if icing is safe in this situation, and post-surgery as well. Is it a separate issue from CT causing toxins to be released, including excess estrogen? Will it ever be safe for me to CT if I'm prone to cancer? Is weight loss even safe, since that releases toxins and estrogen too? Does this swelling even have anything to do with CT detoxing? Or is it something else entirely? You see my dilemma.

I'm not looking for professional medical guidance with this question, because I understand that's not the purpose of this forum. I'm exploring ideas, hoping to come up with a better understanding of what's happening in my body. I don't expect anyone out there to actually know the exact answer, but some help pondering the situation would help. Thanks!

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T20:21:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22938>

So you only get the swelling and cramping when you ice directly, but not when you do submersion CT?

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-10T20:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22941>

Ah, good question, thanks. I was getting it daily when I was doing submersion in ice baths, which I've stopped a week ago, around the same time I added DIM and Pro-Gest. Now I've begun spot icing, and I'm getting it again.

Interestingly, after my last ocean dunk five days ago, I didn't get it at all. I can't remember if I did after the earlier two. Hmm!

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T20:36:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22947>

Interesting. I have nothing useful to tell you, unfortunately...but I'm certainly curious as to what's going on.

>
> This type of cancer is estrogen-sensitive. I started taking DIM and Pro-gest natural cream last week. Less swelling, but I was also doing less icing. Now I'm icing again. Swelling and cramps again.

First I think you can ask Jack if CT could be releasing estrogen, which I believe I read somewhere it can...and if that is the case that could be what's doing it...ask Jack, this is too important for you not to ask him.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-10T20:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22958>

Yes on the estrogen dump and if you are not getting rid of it (elimination) it stays circulating in your system from what I've read. I agree with MJ, you need to ask Jack. If he can't answer you directly, he won't. I remember Quelson and Dr. K talking about dumping a ton of estrogen during CT but don't know about the swelling. I can tell you since CT'ing, my gut is working and I don't have the bloat/swelling like I did. I dumped a lot of estrogen and it was a bxxxx in the process.

Ask him for sure but do your own n=1 with ice vs. cold water. Maybe try the water at different temps too. I feel for you. Hope all goes well.

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-10T21:02:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22962>

Thanks, MJ and Vkiernan. I did ask Jack about estrogen dump during CT, specifically if I should be taking DIM and Pro-Gest. He said it wouldn't be a bad idea and to consult with my doc. That's when I started taking both of those. (It seems as though anyone diagnosed with an estrogen-sensitive cancer might do well to do the same, but again, I'm making my own decisions here based on what knowledge I have.)

Does estrogen dump cause swelling, and so quickly? See, I'm not sure if this is estrogen dump or not. Maybe that's a question I can ask Jack. I'll do that and see if he can answer.

### PaleoMom — 2012-05-10T21:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22965>

If you really didn't get it during your ocean dunks, that makes me think about Earthing. The ocean or any natural body of water is grounded to the earths energy. When we connect with this water or stand barefoot on the ground the electrical currents in our body become negatively charged which has a very strong anti-inflammatory effect.

Your belly swelling does seem contrary to what we'd all expect to hear. I hope Dr. K will offer some insight for you.

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-10T21:28:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22968>

I posted a question about the swelling on Ask Jack. Also, I googled endometrial cancer symptoms, and one is abdominal swelling. Don't know how I missed that. So I'm going to assume for a moment that this has nothing to do with estrogen and is only related to the cancer. So why does ice cause more of it? This is fascinating.

PaleoMom, I'm intrigued by the ocean affect. Since starting these swims, I'm over-the-head in love with the ocean and am sure it's good for me in many ways. I only wish I could go more often, and perhaps I will soon. I've always been an ardent barefooter, walk in my yard barefoot all summer, walk my dog barefoot (I'm the neighborhood hippie), but maybe I need bigger doses of earthing, and the ocean is the way to go.

And no, I really didn't get it after my last (third) ocean dunk. I remember feeling happy about that. The earlier two, it's hard to tell, because I was taking ice baths every day and also spot icing. But at the time of this last ocean dunk, I'd quit the other for a few days. It was a singular event. And no swelling.

This may be the key? Also, the ocean has so many important minerals. Another key? Okay, now I want to get over to the ocean again pronto!

### AKMan — 2012-05-10T22:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22991>

If I had cancer of any type I would:

a) Daily CT for 30-60 minutes as cold as I could get the water, (below 45 degress for sure)

b) Eat a fully ketogenic diet full of nutritious vegetables and loaded with fish

c) Fast for 48 hours once a week

That's just me.

Have you read Jacks blog about cancer? [http://jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/)

The comments section of that blog might be a good place to ask your question if you phrase it in general terms.

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-10T22:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-22994>

Okay, Dr. Jack answered:

"not too surprising.....someone with cancer has increase inflammation and toxins in lymph and fat. CT will liberate more of them and cause more surface changes. Improve the diet and look at the supplements and read this blog [http://jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with...cer-diagnosis/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with...cer-diagnosis/)

"Using CT detox diet and supplements should be considered by you and your oncologist. Discuss all options with them before implementation. I think the ocean swims are best too."

I have read his cancer post. I do supplement as advised. I'm fully keto and have been for several weeks, my diet consisting of only grass-fed meat, wild fish, coconut oil, cruciferous veggies, homemade sauerkraut, and occasional mushrooms. Green tea. Water.

Akman, so you would go with the ice baths even though they aggravate the swelling? I'm game, just unsure still.

Why fast? Or is that on Jack's cancer blog. I'll go re-read it now. Thanks!

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-11T00:06:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23030>

I read Jack's cancer blog again. I'm going to assume any estrogen-dump concern I have is small potatoes compared to the benefits of CT, and I'll hope that the supps I'm taking will blunt any estrogen dump anyway. So back to daily CT baths and icing with some ocean swims mixed in.

The swelling and cramps were rather uncomfortable, at times causing me to take pain meds. This is so weird. Ice causing swelling and pain! But I am going to assume this is the best course to take.

45 degrees, Akman? Yikes. That will take a humongous amount of ice. And iron will.

### AKMan — 2012-05-11T04:07:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23076>

45 degrees. Unless you can get your water colder! 32 degrees if possible. But, really, I would go for as cold as I could get.

As to the fasting, I think that fasting is a pretty well-studied factor in beating cancer. Read this: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2815756/](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2815756/)

It says, in part: "Previous human studies have shown that alternate day dietary restriction and short-term fasting (5 days) are well tolerated and safe \[10-12\]. In fact, children ranging from 6 months to 15 years of age were able to complete 14 to 40 hours of fasting in a clinical study carried out at the Children's hospital of Philadelphia \[13\]. Furthermore, alternate day calorie restriction caused clinical improvements and reduced markers of inflammation and oxidative stress in obese asthmatic patients \[12,14\]. Here, we report 10 cases of patients diagnosed with various types of cancer, who have voluntarily fasted prior to and following chemotherapy. The results presented here, which are based on self-assessed health outcomes (Table ​(Table1)1) and laboratory reports, suggest that fasting is safe and raise the possibility that it can reduce chemotherapy-associated side effects. However, only a randomized controlled clinical trial can establish its efficacy. "

And this: [http://www.worldhealth.net/news/every\_other\_day\_fasting\_may\_reduce\_cance/](http://http://www.worldhealth.net/news/every_other_day_fasting_may_reduce_cance/)

And, Mark Sisson at Mark's Daily Apple did a great series on fasting recently: [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/fasting-cancer/#axzz1uWFrh4VY](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/fasting-cancer/#axzz1uWFrh4VY)

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-11T04:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23085>

> **Rosekelly;23112 wrote:** I read Jack's cancer blog again. I'm going to assume any estrogen-dump concern I have is small potatoes compared to the benefits of CT, and I'll hope that the supps I'm taking will blunt any estrogen dump anyway. So back to daily CT baths and icing with some ocean swims mixed in.
>
>
>
> The swelling and cramps were rather uncomfortable, at times causing me to take pain meds. This is so weird. Ice causing swelling and pain! But I am going to assume this is the best course to take.
>
>
>
> 45 degrees, Akman? Yikes. That will take a humongous amount of ice. And iron will.

Rose, please keep us posted on your progress and what you are doing. I second the fasting. Start researching that.

Also, I have done 32 degree baths - Its harder to get the nerve to get in, but the joy is its actually easier!

### PaleoMom — 2012-05-11T07:46:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23111>

I agree with AKman's suggestions and would add homebrew kombucha to that mix. That would be important to me in your situation. I would also look into earthing more and stick with the ocean CT until you stop having the swelling from other CT. Do you live close to the ocean? If so I would really try to do that daily and for as long as your body can tolerate. Get lots of vitamin D from the sun too, so important to help your body fight this off.

Hugs to you. I think if you do the things suggested in this thread you'll do great and probably blow your docs away.

### Inger — 2012-05-11T09:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23119>

Rosekelly,

the sister of my brothers wife had ovarian cancer and fasted, 30 days on vegetable juices and herb-teas only (Breuss-fast, you can google it). She had no money to go to the doctor (she lives in Serbia, Europe) and my brother who lives in Schweden, sent her the juices/fasting-packages (you can buy the whole package for the Breuss-fast here) per post. She was really really weak and bleeded a LOT. She still fasted, and got really thin.. but she got a lot better and the bleeding stopped! After the fast she went to an acupuncturist, who gave her foot-massage. He pushed on a special point under her feet and it hurted quite a lot. After the treatment she got really bad pain in her stomach and went to the bathroom. There she realesed a big lump.. probably the ovarian tumor..? After that, she was 100% well, and it is a few years ago and she still ist healthy. I know this sounds crazy but it is true.

Also her father had prostate cancer and did the same long fast, got healed.

They eat a normal diet otherwise, not perfect so to say.. but still they remain healed. Amazing.

I do think fasting can help to heal cancer. Maybe the vegetable juices or herb teas helped too? I do not know..

Rosekelly,

try to eat some wild edibles/herbs/berries every day! They are really healing! If you do not know what kind are edible I can send you some pics of the ones I eat regularely. They grow everywhere! Wild food are absolutely the most healing ever! Try to eat mostly wild if you can..

And sun.. yes. Try to get sun ALL over your body! You need to be totally naked, cause certain parts that never get sun really are the places that needs it a lot, it helps the hormones, and it does the body so much good. You just have to find a place where no one can see you.. and stay in the sun as much as you can. I tan naked as much as possible, I am happy to have a yard where no neighbours can see me.

Inger

### kathylu — 2012-05-11T15:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23149>

Hi Roskelly,

I think CT can only help your situation, but it seems ocean is best if you can do it. You'll get lots of trace minerals and a lot of magnesium that way. I also think you could use a LOT more progesterone than you can get from Pro-Gest cream. Progesterone prevents endometrial hyperplasia, opposes the action of estrogen (very important if you are dumping estrogen from the CT) and is a natural diuretic. Read books by Uzzi Reiss, MD or Sergey Dzugan, MD or Thierry Hertoghe, MD. All have excellent explanations of estrogen/progesterone balancing and the reasons it works well. Then get a prescription from your doctor for bioidentical progesterone cream. I use 1 ml of 100mg/ml progesterone per day (no bloating since I started). Your other steroid hormones may need balancing as well, but I'm sure that has to wait until after your surgery. Are you having a hysterectomy/oophorechtomy? You will need to become an expert on the hormone replacement issue, because you cannot depend on your doctors knowing the latest information. Sad, but true.

Also, follow Jack's advice about cancer. Don't eat any sugar, ever (cancer LOVES sugar). IF may be useful too. Tons of green veggies.

Let us know how you are progressing...

### kathylu — 2012-05-11T15:47:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23151>

I'm sure each situation is different, but my sister-in-law had a complete hysterectomy in January due to endometrial cancer. Her doctor has her on BHRT (estriol/progesterone/testosterone/dhea/pregnenolone) post surgery, to deal with the hormonal fall-out. So, there ARE doctors who get it out there...

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-11T18:40:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23191>

Thank you all for your help. It's great to get all of your wisdom and experience. Now I have to sort it out and choose.

Ocean swims vs. deep CT with ice baths. I'm thinking I'll start driving over to the ocean every morning. Even though it's a 45 min drive, it's still less onerous than creating daily baths cold enough to qualify as deep CT. I'm waking early anyway. I can eat my BAB, drive over, swim for 30 min, and get back by 10. I make my own hours, so I can still fit my work schedule in. I start today, although later, because I have a mammogram this morning. Blowing off work entirely today. First things first.

Fasting? I can do one day a week. Not juice fasting though, due to sugars. My job, as I understand it, is to keep insulin as low as possible. I believe your SIL healed from it, Inger, but these things are personal, and I don't have a good feeling about juice fasting, so I don't think it will work for me. Is this foolish of me? I don't know! Nude sunbathing I can do though.

Kombucha is also out, for same reason, sugars. I was making it but decided to err on the side of caution, and stopped. I eat homemade saeurkraut every morning.

As for getting a prescription for bio-identical progesterone, I'm doubtful either of my docs will give me one, but I'll try today. My oncologist is the one who wants to remove my ovaries because "You don't need them at your age." !!! (I respectfully disagreed, and she has agreed to leave them unless she sees they are obviously diseased.) My other wonderful paleo doc who I saw for a consult says she isn't comfortable prescribing BHRT hormones to me without my oncologist's consent. She was talking post-op. Maybe pre-op, she might. Time for phone calls.

I think I covered everything as of now. You all are marvelous!

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-11T19:10:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23197>

Good luck Rose. Maybe lowcarb vegetable juice is a good idea. I would try fasting too and make sure you drink plenty of water. Enjoy the ocean!

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-11T19:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23198>

Called Paleo doc's office. Nurse told me the doc is generally against prescribing anything at all until the cancer itself is dealt with. Would never just prescribe progesterone alone anyway. Will have the doc call me to discuss further.

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-11T19:13:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23199>

I do have a juicer because I was once into juicing. So maybe I could juice cruciferous veggies only. Sounds icky. Broccoli juice? But I'll consider it.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-11T19:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23205>

> **Rosekelly;23281 wrote:** I do have a juicer because I was once into juicing. So maybe I could juice cruciferous veggies only. Sounds icky. Broccoli juice? But I'll consider it.

I think all leafy greens are lowcarb and high in nutrients? celery, cilantro, parsley? Just whatever you want and can eat, drink a glass of it!  From what I read, when we fast and lose weight, our body utilizes the bad/sick cells as energy - just like when winter comes the older leaves turn yellow and fall first - our body knows how to prioritize when it has to use some cells as energy to survive - the healthy and most important are saved while the ugly and broken are used. I don't know who "scientific" this idea is, but it makes very good sense to me. Besides, you may just starve the cancer cells to death!

### Birdy — 2012-05-11T19:52:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23210>

I am not an expert by any means, but if I was in your situation, I would try to get the estrogen dump before the surgery instead of after. Listen to Robb Wolf's May 8 podcast about pms. For me, the problem was estrogen clearance, and until I have that licked, I am not moving forward with any HRT since I don't have a knowledgeable doc who can work with me on this. I needed to make sure that my insulin sensitivity was rock solid before adding in any other type of hormone. I started CT gradually and didn't get the high estrogen dump issues, but I was also working to manage the insulin with a more pristine lowcarb diet. (Think Bernstein/Rosedale over Wolf). I wouldn't do vegetable juices, but would drink a kale/celery shake in smaller quantities. Now that I know more about CT, I am trying to use that to solve the imbalance issues instead of even considering HRT. But, off to read more about estrogen clearance...

### Birdy — 2012-05-11T19:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-23214>

### Rosekelly — 2012-05-15T18:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-24246>

Update: I'm no longer confused. Yay! I'm ocean swimming as much as I possibly can, which was twice this weekend. I'll be going the next three, maybe four days too. I want to get in five days a week. These cold ocean waters are intense! I love it.

I also did a 48-hour juice fast with cruciferous veggies. Felt great, and it was easy, no hunger. I'm planning to fast every 5 days for 48 hours.

Birdy, I'm eating homemade sauerkraut every day. And the question of when to dump toxins, before or after surgery, was something I couldn't figure out, but a friend of mine had a consult with Dr. K. on Saturday, she was gracious enough to ask that question on my behalf. He said, now, now, now. Just do it. Get rid of the crap. Jump in the ocean as often as possible. So now I'm comfortable with my path.

### Rosekelly — 2012-06-02T09:30:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-29026>

Update: I had my surgery Tuesday. It went perfectly. The doc thinks she got all the cancer, says it looks like a stage one with no infiltration,and will know more with the lab report. And I kept my ovaries!

My recovery is going easily. Not much pain at all. The anesthesia really knocked me loopy more than anything else, but I'm feeling normal again. The incisions are tiny, it was all done laparascopically. The whole thing was amazingly easy with the best possible outcome.

I'm glad I did all that CT in the ocean beforehand. I can't tell how it affected the cancer directly, but obviously it did no harm. I felt really, really healthy and vigorous going into the surgery, and now, four days later, I'm almost back to normal. I have to wait two weeks until I can submerge myself in a bath (or ocean!), the doc says, but I can spot ice, and I have been, even did it at the hospital post-surgery. They thought it was funny but didn't stop me.

I'm sold on ice forever. Onward to balancing out all my hormones, with ovaries intact! Thank you, Dr. Kruse, for all your info here, which alerted me to the importance of my ovaries. God, I love my ovaries. I never gave them a second thought my entire life, and now I want to buy them flowers and sing to them.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-06-02T16:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-29039>

Congratulations on the surgery going well! fingers crossed that there was no spreading and your labs are boring as heck!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-04T01:10:14+0300

+1 Rosekelly. I'm also post-cancer (just under 2 years ago, surgery cleared it) and am totally embracing my 'clean slate.' Inflammation is always part of my focus... so in addition to the keto/seasonal paleo with CT and light cycles, I take turmeric in large doses (boiled for 10 minutes to increase bioavailability) daily and do breathing/meditation too. I get out for at least one (preferably two) walks every day, too. Those ocean swims you're doing are perfect! Enjoy your entire life as much as you're enjoying your ovaries right now. ### Rosekelly — 2012-06-04T23:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-29419>

Thanks for chiming in with your history, KiwiLauren. Yay for clean slates! How do you ingest the turmeric? What is the large dose? I've been taking circumin as a supplement, but it would probably be cheaper to take turmeric every day in my food.

I walk daily too, and can't wait to return to the ocean soon. I meditate off and on, but for some reason keep forgetting to. I'm going to do go it right now.

(I didn't know turmeric should be boiled. Good tip.)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-05T00:03:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-29428>

> **Rosekelly;29519 wrote:** Thanks for chiming in with your history, KiwiLauren. Yay for clean slates! How do you ingest the turmeric? What is the large dose? I've been taking circumin as a supplement, but it would probably be cheaper to take turmeric every day in my food.
>
>
>
> I walk daily too, and can't wait to return to the ocean soon. I meditate off and on, but for some reason keep forgetting to. I'm going to do go it right now.
>
>
>
> (I didn't know turmeric should be boiled. Good tip.)

On one of the liver threads, CoolingWeb posted a published bit of research showing the increased efficacy of curcurmin when it has been boiled for 10 minutes. My supplement budget is really limited so I use turmeric and use this research to make it more effective. Basically, I take about a heaping tablespoon per day. The way I make it is pour about 5 T into a pan with water (after it's boiled, it ends up being about an inch of water in a glass per day). I then boil it for 10 minutes, then pour it into a glass jar and put it in the fridge. Each morning for the next 5 days I pour 1/5 of the jar into a glass, add pepper (which also helps increase it's availability/activity) and drink it down with my morning meal in a few big glugs. It's not tasty, but it's not bad and only takes a few seconds. I like that it's cheap and I get the curcurmin from food, not a supplement. You can also squeeze fresh ginger into it, as well as cinnamon.

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-05T06:14:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-29493>

Doh and CONGRATS on the surgical success as well!! (hit send too fast!!)

### Rosekelly — 2012-06-05T18:12:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confused-and-pondering-next-step.1384/post-29558>

> **KiwiLauren;29528 wrote:** On one of the liver threads, CoolingWeb posted a published bit of research showing the increased efficacy of curcurmin when it has been boiled for 10 minutes. My supplement budget is really limited so I use turmeric and use this research to make it more effective. Basically, I take about a heaping tablespoon per day. The way I make it is pour about 5 T into a pan with water (after it's boiled, it ends up being about an inch of water in a glass per day). I then boil it for 10 minutes, then pour it into a glass jar and put it in the fridge. Each morning for the next 5 days I pour 1/5 of the jar into a glass, add pepper (which also helps increase it's availability/activity) and drink it down with my morning meal in a few big glugs. It's not tasty, but it's not bad and only takes a few seconds. I like that it's cheap and I get the curcurmin from food, not a supplement. You can also squeeze fresh ginger into it, as well as cinnamon.

Thanks. I'm going to be doing this. Googling, I also found several versions of turmeric tea. This is my fave, which I haven't tried yet, but looks good! [http://meghantelpnerblog.com/2010/02/05/tea-time-with-turmeric/](http://http://meghantelpnerblog.com/2010/02/05/tea-time-with-turmeric/)

Since some of you have already had consults, I thought we could use this thread to post ideas on how to make the most of our time with Dr. Kruse. Please share any advice for what you think others should do to prepare, good questions to ask, what information to have at your fingertips etc.,

### indigogirl — 2012-05-10T21:46:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-22975>

MJ,

I had emailed the MM's asking how to figure out what labs I would need before having a consult with Jack.

They said they were working on a package where one could speak to Jack first to discuss health concerns so he could then prescribe labs, and after the labs follow up with us.

### MJ* — 2012-05-10T23:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23001>

That's awesome, if you get a response on that, can you post it here?

### MJ* — 2012-05-10T23:39:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23009>

> **August;23060 wrote:** Great topic for a thread, MJ!
>
>
>
> I always go the the doctor with a list of questions and concerns . . . if I am feeling trustful and cooperate, I provide a copy for the doctor . . . I think that would likely be a good trick for making the most of a phone consult type of appointment espeically.
>
>
>
> I guess more obvious . . . provide a copy of recent lab results at the same time that you send over you list of questions . . . .
>
>
>
> and I think in the case of a consult with Dr Kruse . . it would very likely be worth while to get special/specific labs run for the occasion . . . I hope that we can discuss generally what would be some good ones to have done . . . although that is something that has been discussed elsewhere . . so maybe not what this excellent (I can already tell) thread should be all about . . .
>
>
>
> I usually try to plan out what I'm going to say/what I will try to focus on (list or not) . . . but it seems like in my mind a conversation with Dr K might be a difficult thing to plan for . . . I would tend to expect the unexpected!
>
>
>
> Now I have a question . . . Do you "get" a recorded audio of the call afterwards . . . or it is acceptable to record the call yourself . . .I think it would be desirable to be able to listen to it many, many times afterwards!

I brought a list of all the tests and supplements to my doc to discuss, some unfortunately I don't think I'm going to be able to get like the saliva tests because NYS law prohibits it! I have no idea why...how dumb!

For Dr. Kruse I would like to maximize the time and have all the labs he most wants to see...so a list would be great! It would also be really nice if we can report back here what we might have done differently if anything...good question on the audio...

### indigogirl — 2012-05-10T23:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23023>

MJ, Here is my PM to the MM's about a consult and their response:

Originally Posted by indigogirl

Hi MM's,

I would like to make an appt. with Dr Kruse to go over labs...I can give you details of how I'm feeling and where I want to go to be optimal, but would first like to know what labs he would require and what kind of an education pkg I should buy...

This wouldn't be prescribing medicine over the Internet I assume, and he could request specific blood work and based on my labs prescribe supplements and actually give me advice?

Also, how long would I have to wait for an appointment with him?

Thanks!!

We are working on a package to purchase that would allow you to setup a time to go over your history to get a list of labs you may need. Currently, it is not yet available. It should be soon.

You would need to check the calendar for an open time and date. Thanks!

JMM

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-11T17:51:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-23183>

> **August;23251 wrote:** I think some doctors have a set lists of tests that they run . . and then special add on set depending on which conditions you have (or think you might have, if you are looking for a diagnosis) . . I think questionnaires might work the same way (or could) . . .
>
>
>
> MJ . . I'm glad that you mentioned supplements . . . I think in addition to providing labs . . it is important to provide a list of medications and supplements . . in order to provide a complete picture . . .
>
>
>
> That reminds me too . . I used to see a specialist (who was wonderful in many ways although very tradition western medicine in many ways too) . . . she would have me complete several questionnaires before each appointment . . . questions like what supplements and Prescriptions are you taking . . what's better . . what's not better (since you last appointment) . . and questions and concerns to address at the appointment . . . . and then there was another questionnaire that just seemed to be about well being/sleep quality/mood . . that sort of reminded me that it would likely be a good idea to be prepared to talk about sleep quality . . and issues like that . . . things that we might not think to prepare to talk about with other doctors . . . .

Sounds like a great doc August, where'd she go? I agree about supplements...I have lots of questions about those...

### Tizawee — 2012-05-24T14:57:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-27003>

> **MJ\*;23091 wrote: I brought a list of all the tests and supplements to my doc to discuss, some unfortunately I don't think I'm going to be able to get like the saliva tests because NYS law prohibits it! I have no idea why...how dumb!**

**

I just googled "saliva test New York State law." It's the mailing of the saliva that is outlawed, not the tests themselves. One of the sites ( www.hmenterprises.com/Hormonesaliva**.html ) is saying it's now legal. The others say it's not. Ostensibly, you could hand carry the test to your local lab.

Elin

### MJ* — 2012-05-24T16:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-27008>

>
>
>
> Elin

Thanks so much for looking into this...so I thought the same thing got on line, gave my card to get the test, thinking I would mail it from Conn or NJ, and I got a letter back a couple of days later saying they could not even mail me the test! I did though just find a place in NY that says they have gotten licensing to do the test here, it's about an hour away...I need to call them to find out the details and price and all, unfortunately there's no shopping around...

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-11-08T21:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-62971>

Bump....

Any other new suggestions or advice?

### caroline — 2012-11-08T21:52:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-the-most-of-your-consult-with-dr-kruse.1387/post-62980>

I am in Australia - 4 months ago I order a saliva test from ZRT. It was confiscated by customs and put in quarantine. Customs wanted it re- documented which ZRT did and then I was charged an extra $75.00 to get it out of quarantine.

I just ordered the same test again today - but beforehand I emailed Canary Club and asked if the test could be sent by US mail instead. They said - no problem and she would watch for my order to come thru. ZRT has great customer service - by the way.

So - we will see if that works better. I am told by other companies shipping to Aus - that shipping by US mail attracts less customs attention. I am keeping my fingers crossed! will update

# Thread: Blood Oxygen Saturation

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-05-11T03:51:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23071>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### nonchalant — 2012-05-11T06:01:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23099>

Souldanzer, I think it means they don't have anything to prescribe for it.

### Glamazon — 2012-05-11T08:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23112>

> **Souldanzer;23153 wrote:** When you go to the doctor, they often take your blood oxygen saturation level with one of those little finger clips.
>
>
>
> Well, mine has been going down for the last couple of years. Today it was jumping between 94/95 percent. I asked the nurse about it who said I shouldn't worry, that it's normal. Anything between 95 and 100 percent is considered normal.
>
> I pointed out that I was sort of below the lower limit of even that "normal". Was told to talk to the doc about it which I subsequently forgot as we had a lot of other stuff to talk about.
>
>
>
> What is a normal/optimal level of blood oxygen saturation? When ever they tell me not to worry, I DO!!

Ask the doctor to explain it to you. It all depends on context which could be multifactoral. If you notice a slow decline over the years ask your doc. Generally, recommendations to wear Oxygen doesn't come till you hit about 88% because that is when insurance will pay for it.

[http://www.mayoclinic.com/health/hypoxemia/MY00219](http://http://www.mayoclinic.com/health/hypoxemia/MY00219)

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-11T12:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23122>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-11T12:15:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23123>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Paradox — 2012-05-12T01:39:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23298>

Hi Souldanzer. I noticed you live in a dark cold cave in the Rockies. My question for you is at what elevation and how long have you lived there. The Rockies can be quite high and at higher elevations there is less oxygen, hence many people may have lower blood 02 saturations.

I speak from firsthand knowledge as I have seriously impaired lung function and cannot go to altitude without supplemental oxygen. I personally wouldnt worry about a 94-95 reading at altitude. What is your heart rate? Is it elevated? The heart will compensate if the body senses a lack of oxygen. If you are worried have a pulmonary function test.

Next time at your doctors ask if many people at your altitude have reduced levels of O2. Would be interesting to know.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-12T02:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23310>

Thanks paradox. I've been wondering about the altitude issue but I'm really not that high (5,500ft). I've been here for years now and my oxygen level has been going down while living here. My heart rate has gone up significantly in the past couple of years as well.

I will let my doc know next week that I'm worried about it.... I was supposed to have a stress echo a couple of weeks ago (b/c of my heart symptoms) but had to postpone since the cardiologist wasn't too thrilled about me hopping on the treadmill after telling him that my doc is worried about a brain bleed from my concussion. That has been cleared so maybe I can get it done soon.

I feel like a real mess tonight. Having a discouraged moment

# Thread: Massive Strength Increase Following CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/massive-strength-increase-following-ct.1393/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KA24 — 2012-05-11T06:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/massive-strength-increase-following-ct.1393/post-23101>

I've noticed lately that my capacity for strength (total weight) and muscle endurance (more reps) is greatly increased after CT.

Has anyone else noticed this? I was able to do 36 pushups (I can generally do 25-30 quickly before fatigue) and was doing some 40 lb kettlebell one-handed snatches with ease. This is the third or fourth time I've tested this afterward and noticed a difference. Not only do I feel stronger, but mentally I'm convinced as well, which has really allowed me to do some really good strength work for a few minutes after CT.

Do you think there might some possible gains by doing a more intense session immediately post-CT and take advantage of this? This "feeling" seems to wear off after about 30 minutes as I start to really hit the coldest point, but before that, it's pretty cool.

Thoughts?

# Thread: Head submerged during CT for long periods
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Swagger — 2012-05-11T07:27:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/post-23110>

As if sitting in ice water wasn't enough...

I have begun to use a snorkel in an effort to submerge my head for longer periods during CT baths. (I jumped into CT, skipping the face submerging.) At first the shock of the cold caused me to hyperventilate and , however this passes once you adapt to the cold. Once you adapt to using the snorkel, the sensation is wonderful. Has anyone else experimented with this? (I will tell you if you snort 55F water up your nose accidentally, it will clear out your sinuses with a quickness!)

### chocolate — 2012-05-11T08:47:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/post-23115>

My kid used to love scuba just for that reason. The cooling all over.

### Swagger — 2012-05-14T08:41:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/post-23855>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/post-24580>

Ct is about lowering skin temps to activate BAT, not lowering core body temp.... Submerging head you will lose a lot of body heat from your scalp.
