# Thread: Coconut Oil???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nzgal — 2012-05-11T16:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23166>

Hey guys!!!

I am soo confused!!!!!! There seems to be so many different kinds of coconut oil out there. Am I supposed to buy unrefined, organic??

Does it matter what brand I get???

Will the Walmart brand still work the same???

Thanks!!!!!

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-05-12T05:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23336>

Trader Joe's now sells "organic virgin coconut oil" in a 16oz jar for $6.99

Coconuts come from the Phillipines. Not as tasty as Nutiva's extra virgin, but serviceable imo.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-12T05:27:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23339>

> **nzgal;23248 wrote:** Hey guys!!!
>
>
>
> I am soo confused!!!!!! There seems to be so many different kinds of coconut oil out there. Am I supposed to buy unrefined, organic??
>
>
>
> Does it matter what brand I get???
>
>
>
> Will the Walmart brand still work the same???
>
>
>
>
>
> Thanks!!!!!

Definately get unrefined organic cold pressed. The lovely smell is awesome. You will start rubbing it on your skin! I tried the walmart once, not for me. No coconut smell - and i since learned of all e chemicals used to extract oil.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-12T18:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23415>

Slight tangent - what is coconut manna? I know about coconut oil & coconut cream but don't know what manna is?

### JC2K — 2012-05-14T23:14:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24002>

Tropical Traditions has a good explanation on virgin, etc at:

[http://www.tropicaltraditions.com/what\_is\_virgin\_coconut\_oil.htm](http://http://www.tropicaltraditions.com/what_is_virgin_coconut_oil.htm)

All the research studies I have seen on coconut oil use refined oil. I don't really recall seeing much in the way of studies on the virgin/unrefined stuff. But basic idea is that the unrefined stuff is a better source, so a better starting point. It does have a better taste too.

Coconut Manna = coconut oil with a lot of the coconut fiber blended in. Takes some mixing from time to time because it will separate. Gives it a thick mouth feel like peanut butter, so you can treat it like peanut butter on sandwiches and the like. I think a little goes a long ways though.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-15T00:22:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24031>

The refined usually doesn't have the coconut taste and aroma. I keep a jar of this around for when I don't want the taste but don't use it often. But for daily use and eating it by the spoonful I love the Nutiva.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-15T06:58:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24132>

Coconut manna=coconut butter. I've tried Artisinal and Nutiva and like the Nutiva better. JC2K gave an accurate description.

I also use both refined and unrefined in cooking...mostly unrefined, refined when I don't want my food to taste like coconut.

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-16T16:35:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24589>

On another thread, someone suggested adding some coconut oil to the manna (once its heated and stirred), which will keep it loose so you don't have to restir it all the time. (assuming you don't live Ina cold place that is). Have not tried this yet....

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T18:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24612>

Krusing....that's what I do and add some cinnamon. It doesn't stay loose unless it's summer for me. Yesterday our house got to 75 and it still was solid.

PS - For anyone traveling, I have carried CO with me for years when flying but this time since it was solid I decided to pack it in my carry on suitcase and not take it out for the TSA screening. Oooops I was told I have to take it out for them to see but it is fine to carry on. Save yourself an extra screening of the suitcase and the CO!!!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-17T01:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24730>

So I heard it from you!!! Funny thing I just flew to TX and had CO in my checked baggage...LAX was apparently unconcerned as my baggage was intact upon arrival. Next time I'll carry on! I could see it getting some scrutiny in this day and age.

# Thread: Sugar And carb cravings?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-05-11T18:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23193>

Just came off a disastrous hcg round where I was plagued with emotional eating and sugar/carb cravings. I am starting the LR tomorrow. Any advice on how to beat back these God awful cravings and deal with the emotional eating? Would love any help at all. I don't want to gain all my weight back from round one (round two was a wash!). But I almost feel like I am being driven to gain it back. It's awful. Thanks for your suggestions in advance!

### nonchalant — 2012-05-11T21:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23251>

You could try some 5-HTP for a few days. I know it helps for evening cravings, after you have already eaten dinner. It takes 2-3 hours to kick in, though. Helps with low seratonin levels. Long term, though, the LR will fix the cravings. Oh, and CT, for most people, kills cravings even quicker, if you don't have Omega 6 issues.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-11T23:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23255>

PROTEIN FAT PROTEIN FAT PROTEIN FAT.

Any questions?

Okay, so what I mean is look up the leptin reset and follow that. Make sure you get at least 50g protein every morning (and then another full serving at lunch and/or dinner), and don't hold back on the fat. Tell me if a miracle doesn't happen.

(And IIRC, don't ever take 5-HTP if you're on antidepressants, or have been in the last six months.)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T01:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23289>

MamaGrok hits a homerun........

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-12T04:43:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23329>

Reigning Ex-Queen of Cravings, at your service...

### Sem — 2012-05-12T20:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23446>

Thanks MamaGrok. I started the Leptin Reset again today. I had done it with great success before this last round of hcg but I don't think I gave it enough time. I'm going to really give it the full amount of time and not rush into another round. Best to try to deal with all the emotional stuff first. The LR does really help with that. Did you take any supplements like chromium to help? I am going in a couple of weeks to get my full labs. But, from previous labs (and before I really understood the significance), I know that I've had low vitamin D, low magnesium, high ANA, low (but also sometimes hug) steadily and high fsh. They always say my thyroid is normal but now I realize I was probably just doing a tsh. You're quite an inspiration so thanks!

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-12T21:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23478>

I didn't take any supplements at all (other than whole foods, like fermented cod liver oil, but nothing geared toward cravings). Getting D & Mg up are a must for everyone. I know I was very low in both before. That ANA means zero wheat; you know that, right? I didn't. It was a shocker to see my ANA.

Keep us posted and let us support you here! It's hard to do alone.

### Sem — 2012-05-12T22:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23481>

I did not know that. Mine was 320. The doctor said he wasn't too concerned since he ran a bunch of other tests for lupus and they supposedly came back negative. Of course he never mentioned wheat! Thankfully, I'm doing it on my own and have switched the whole family over to gluten free. They cried and screamed at first but were fine with it within a week. They even turn down cake at a party because "we don't eat that. It has gluten." yay! Now I have to get them off of other grains and the "gluten free" stuff.  thanks again for all your insight.

### Sem — 2012-05-12T22:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-and-carb-cravings.1404/post-23485>

Anti nuclear antibodies. They're an indicator of autoimmune disease.

# Thread: Sleep.....I took Ambien...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2012-05-11T19:06:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23196>

...I am sure taking a sleeping pill is not helping my reset, but when DH and I were traveling a few nights this week, I had to take Ambien to sleep because he snores so loud, whereas at home we have separate bedrooms for said reason. So, maybe I took 2 or so, all tolled...(I cut them in half). Now let me tell you, this makes it hard to get to sleep the night you stop taking them. I've been through this before. Last year this time I was going through extreme anxiety related to my mother's terminal cancer diagnosis, and I took Ambien to sleep for ...weeks? I kind of went through hell getting off of it.

Taking GABA helped. Then I weaned off that. Should I take GABA?

I told my husband, No one can go through that level of anxiety without some serious physical repercussions, which is probably why I have adrenal and thyroid issues. I am just guessing that I have those....but its a good guess.

The anxiety also caused me to lose weight rapidly and I lost about 30 lbs. I have gained those back and am now trying to lose them for real. Consciously.

### janagram — 2012-05-11T21:29:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23246>

All the last year stuff was before I heard of leptin reset....tho I did eat paleo, and very sparsely. I gained back the weight and then started looking for a strategy and found this. I am following the reset...for about 6 wks now, with a couple of pecadillos...

I think there should be a whole thread for sleep.

### Pamela — 2012-05-12T07:29:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23355>

I used to sleep all the way through the night until probably my early 40's and then gradually it has declined over the past 10 years. I never took sleeping pills but have used a host of other items. Currently I am taking my mag malate and L-Tryptophan (1000mg) before bed. This helps me sleep straight through for 6.5 hours, then I get up, pee, take 2 cal-mag caps and go back to sleep.

### janagram — 2012-05-12T10:36:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23361>

Awesome, Pamela. I'd call that a good night's sleep.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-12T15:14:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23367>

> **janagram;23278 wrote:**
>
> ...I am sure taking a sleeping pill is not helping my reset, but when DH and I were traveling a few nights this week, I had to take Ambien to sleep because he snores so loud, whereas at home we have separate bedrooms for said reason.

I would say this part is the result of bad planning. This is preventable. Sounds like your DH and you need to have a conversation about how to handle sleeping away from home. If he snores that loud I'd think he needs to be seen by a doctor. There's also mouth appliances that can help. And you need a pair of good ear plugs.

If all that fails, my sleep would be important enough to insist on two rooms even when sleeping away from home. Honestly, I hope I don't sound harsh (not meaning to) but I don't quite understand how you taking Ambien is a solution for your DH's snoring.

### janagram — 2012-05-13T08:40:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23621>

well, it's not. It's a p--- poor solution. But, sleep is a touchy thing with me. I get your point though, and should insist hubby gets some kind of treatment. Thanks for the reality check!

SLEEP is the next frontier!!

# Thread: Kid/picky family friendly Optimized recipes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kid-picky-family-friendly-optimized-recipes.1407/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BeingVenus — 2012-05-11T23:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kid-picky-family-friendly-optimized-recipes.1407/post-23259>

I thought it would be great to have a thread devoted to all these great recipes you guys are posting so we have it all aggregated. I'll start with this awesome looking "coconut oil disguised as dessert delivery system" recipe

[http://untilthethinladysings.blogspot.com/2012/05/lemon-coconut-energy-bombs.html](http://http://untilthethinladysings.blogspot.com/2012/05/lemon-coconut-energy-bombs.html)

Lemon Coconut Energy Bombs

Makes 9

1/4 cup coconut butter (see below)

1/4 cup virgin coconut oil, high quality

1 Tablespoon of organic lemon zest

1 Tablespoon of organic lemon juice

stevia powder or liquid to taste, I used 1/8 teaspoon NuNaturals powder

toasted coconut to garnish (see below)

mini cupcake wrappers or pan

Combine all ingredients except toasted coconut and mix well. Scoop into cupcake wrappers. Sprinkle toasted coconut on top. Place in freezer for 20 minutes, then place in refrigerator. Lasts a very long time.

How to make coconut butter: Grind unsweetened coconut flakes in a nut/coffee grinder or food processor for at least 20 minutes, or until the consistency of nut butter.

How to make toasted coconut: Preheat oven to Broil. On a cookie sheet, put a single layer of unsweetened coconut flakes and broil for 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until brown. Burns fast! Don't walk away!

[http://eatkamloops.org/christmas-butter-tart-squares/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/christmas-butter-tart-squares/)

[http://eatkamloops.org/birthday-cheesecake/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/birthday-cheesecake/)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-19T02:01:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kid-picky-family-friendly-optimized-recipes.1407/post-25348>

> **BeingVenus;23341 wrote:** I thought it would be great to have a thread devoted to all these great recipes you guys are posting so we have it all aggregated. I'll start with this awesome looking "coconut oil disguised as dessert delivery system" recipe

>

>
> Lemon Coconut Energy Bombs
>
> Makes 9
>
>
>
> 1/4 cup coconut butter (see below)
>
> 1/4 cup virgin coconut oil, high quality
>
> 1 Tablespoon of organic lemon zest
>
> 1 Tablespoon of organic lemon juice
>
> stevia powder or liquid to taste, I used 1/8 teaspoon NuNaturals powder
>
> toasted coconut to garnish (see below)
>
> mini cupcake wrappers or pan
>
>
>
> Combine all ingredients except toasted coconut and mix well. Scoop into cupcake wrappers. Sprinkle toasted coconut on top. Place in freezer for 20 minutes, then place in refrigerator. Lasts a very long time.
>
>
>
> How to make coconut butter: Grind unsweetened coconut flakes in a nut/coffee grinder or food processor for at least 20 minutes, or until the consistency of nut butter.
>
> How to make toasted coconut: Preheat oven to Broil. On a cookie sheet, put a single layer of unsweetened coconut flakes and broil for 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until brown. Burns fast! Don't walk away!

Thank you! This is a great recipe. I eliminated the roasted coconut and subbed rushed mac nuts and put on top. No stevia for me as the coconut butter (I used manna) was sweet enough. YUM!

# Thread: Endicronologist supports Paleo!!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endicronologist-supports-paleo.1409/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ineedanLR — 2012-05-12T00:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endicronologist-supports-paleo.1409/post-23271>

Yesterday, I saw an endicronologist. At the end of the visit, I asked him about Paleo.

As some of you might know, I am deeply disturbed by the American Diabetes Association and their continued support of 60 carbs per meal, with an additional 30 carbs for snacks (~210 carbs per day). There is so much evidence that lower carb limits help diabetics, but still the professional diabetes educators insist on recommending a diet per the ADA guidelines.

So, the endicronologist said lower carbs is better!!! When I asked him if a lower carb diet was going against medical advice, he said NO! He explained that somewhere on the ADA website, there is a disclaimer stating that lower carb diets have been proven to benefit diabetics....but they stop short of actually recommending lower carb intake.

With regard to Paleo, he supported it fully!!!! The elimination of foods known to cause inflammatory responses was his primary reason for supporting it. He also stated that clogging and overloading of the liver with fat interferes with the proper function of the liver and believes the body should burn excess fat in the liver instead of storing it forever. His explanation of the fat burning process sure sounded allot like Dr. J's explanation of detoxification!

The endicronologist was associated with the diabetes clinic of a regional hospital here in Maine.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T01:05:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endicronologist-supports-paleo.1409/post-23276>

# Thread: Nitrogen Excretion: three end products
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nitrogen-excretion-three-end-products.1410/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-12T01:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nitrogen-excretion-three-end-products.1410/post-23281>

[http://jeb.biologists.org/content/198/2/273.short?rss=1&amp%3bssource=mfr](http://http://jeb.biologists.org/content/198/2/273.short?rss=1&amp%3bssource=mfr)

**Nitrogen excretion: three end products, many physiological roles.

**There are diverse physiological functions of nitrogen end products in different animal groups, including excretion, acid-base regulation, osmoregulation and buoyancy.** Animals excrete a variety of nitrogen waste products, but ammonia, urea and uric acid predominate. A major factor in determining the mode of nitrogen excretion is the availability of water in the environment. Generally, aquatic animals excrete mostly ammonia, whereas terrestrial animals excrete either urea or uric acid. Ammonia, urea and uric acid are transported across cell membranes by different mechanisms corresponding to their different chemical properties in solution. Ammonia metabolism and excretion are linked to acid-base regulation in the kidney, but the role of urea and uric acid is less clear. Both invertebrates and vertebrates use nitrogen-containing organic compounds as intracellular osmolytes. In some marine invertebrates, NH4+ is sequestered in specific compartments to increase buoyancy.**


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-23283>

Can I make my own decaffeinated green tea? I'm tired of buying tasteless expensive decaf... but the caffeine can mess with me even though it's little. I was thinking of brewing a whole batch of tea leaves for a minute or so, dumping out the tea, and then drying the leaves for future use.

Would this work to get rid of most of the caffeine but still have a tasty cup of tea in the end? I already brew my leaves about three times.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-17T20:57:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24966>

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T21:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24969>

Haven't tried yet but am getting ready to as the next box of decaf... I could just steep water as well, lol.

The steeping/throwing out/steeping again doesn't work for me at all b/c my Indian Peaks Mountain Spring Water purchased in glass bottles is way to precious to throw out. So I'd have to have a different water boiler, etc. Too much work. I end up just drinking the caffeine...

### JC2K — 2012-05-17T22:06:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24985>

I believe anything you do to remove caffeine will reduce the flavor. For the commercial methods in the US, they use Ethyl Acetate or CO2. The CO2 method is supposed to keep the flavor better than Ethyl Acetate. You'll have to search for companies that advertise that method though. Usually they don't say.

Non commercially, the longer you hot brew tea, the more bitter components come out, so the tea doesn't taste as good. You don't want long hot brew times for green tea.

My recollection when looking at this a while back is that the majority of the caffeine in the tea comes out in the first 30 seconds of hot brewing. So you brew 30 seconds, dump off, then brew normally and it's supposed to be fine. You say you brew 3 times though, so I would think the caffeine would be gone (along with the flavor  ). You're still having problems with that?

The other thing I've seen is cold steeping for 10+ hours in cold water. The caffeine and bitter components don't come out because it's not hot water. Not sure on the taste though.

I'm a bit of a caffeine fiend, so I don't drink decaf.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T22:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24989>

Well, I drink them all  First second and third brew. First is AM after waking, so I figure the caffeine is sort of okay. But really, I'm fixing my adrenals and any caffeine can keep me up at night. Last brew is at night, so I'm happy the caffeine is gone.

I know that a bit of the flavor will go... nothing one can do about that. I'm trying to improve on the store bought version.

I'm going to try the 30secs and dump method. I think it will still be better than the stuff I've been buying. I like the caffeine, especially b/c it helps with my neuro symptoms, but I'm pretty sure, right now, it's not the smartest move. One of the reasons I haven't tried this yet is b/c the caffeine helps me function

### chocolate — 2012-05-12T01:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-inflammation.1412/post-23303>

Do you have lithium orotate? Or any vege extracted lithium?

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2010/05/100521191440.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2010/05/100521191440.htm)

ScienceDaily (May 21, 2010) — Though it has been prescribed for over 50 years to treat bipolar disorder, there are still many questions regarding exactly how lithium works. However, in a study appearing in this month's Journal of Lipid Research, researchers have provided solid evidence that lithium reduces brain inflammation by adjusting the metabolism of the health-protective omega-3-fatty acid called DHA.

They found that in agreement with some other studies, rats given a six-week lithium treatment had reduced levels of arachidonic acid and its products, which can contribute to inflammation.

In addition, they also demonstrated, for the first time, that lithium treatment increased levels of a metabolite called 17-OH-DHA in response to inflammation. 17-OH-DHA is formed from the omega-3 fatty acid DHA (docosahexaenoic acid) and is the precursor to a wide range of anti-inflammatory compounds known as docosanoids. **Other anti-inflammatory drugs, like aspirin, are known to also enhance docosanoids in their mode of action.**

_My kid has incredible brain inflammation, getting better every day, but as soon as she took lithium, she said she felt like she was first born. When I take the orotate, I get on a really natural mellow sleepy feeling. Like when you are a little kid. It is easy to control. If you get stiff knees back off. You've had enough._

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T05:08:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-inflammation.1412/post-23809>

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0161813X07000496](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0161813X07000496)

Manganese induces cell swelling in cultured astrocytes

Abstract

Manganese in excess is neurotoxic and causes a CNS disorder that resembles Parkinson's disease (manganism). Manganese highly accumulates in astrocytes, which renders these cells more vulnerable to its toxicity. Consistent with this vulnerability, manganese has been shown to cause histopathological changes in astrocytes (Alzheimer type II change), generates oxidative stress and bring about mitochondrial dysfunction, including the induction of the mitochondrial permeability transition (mPT) in astrocytes. In addition to manganism, increased brain levels of manganese have been found in hepatic encephalopathy, a changes are also observed. As low-grade brain edema, possibly secondary to astrocyte swelling, has been reported in hepatic encephalopathy, we hypothesized that manganese may contribute to such edema. We therefore exposed cultured astrocytes to manganese (Mn3+) acetate (25 and 50 μM) for different time periods and examined for changes in cell volume. **Manganese dose-dependently induced astrocyte swelling; such swelling was first observed at 12 h (28%), which further increased (54%) at later time points (24–48 h). Pretreatment of astrocyte cultures with antioxidants, including vitamin E, the spin trapping agent PBN, and the iron-chelating agent desferroximine, as well as the nitric oxide synthase inhibitor l-NAME, all significantly blocked (50–80%) astrocyte swelling caused by manganese, suggesting that oxidative/nitrosative stress is involved in the mechanism of such swelling**. Cyclosporin A, an inhibitor of mPT also blocked (90%) manganese-induced astrocyte swelling. The data indicate that manganese exposure results in astrocyte swelling and such swelling, at least in part, may be caused by oxidative stress and/or mPT. Astrocyte swelling by manganese may represent an important aspect of manganese neurotoxicity, and may be a factor in low-grade brain edema associated with chronic hepatic encephalopathy.

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T22:51:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-inflammation.1412/post-23994>

Quelsen there had to be a weather astro some event Saturday. I had to make myself get in a pool, for flips sakes. It felt like nothing...idk why I was avoiding it.

I have a neuro genesis tidbit ONE nights sleep deprivation, not more.

[http://www.biopsychiatry.com/neurogenesis-sleepdep.htm](http://http://www.biopsychiatry.com/neurogenesis-sleepdep.htm)

'One night' sleep deprivation stimulates hippocampal neurogenesis

ABSTRACT

Neurogenesis in the adult hippocampus can be up- or downregulated in response to a variety of physiological and pathological conditions. Among these, dysregulation of hippocampal neurogenesis has been recently implicated in the pathogenesis of depression. In addition, in animal models of depression, a variety of antidepressant treatments reverse that condition by increasing neurogenesis. **As one night sleep deprivation is known to improve mood in depressed patients for at least 1 day, we investigated **whether a comparable treatment may affect hippocampal neurogenesis in adult rats.** Accordingly, rats were sleep-deprived by gentle handling for 12h during their physiological period of rest, and were injected with bromodeoxyuridine 4h and 2h before the end of sleep deprivation. They were then perfused immediately thereafter, or after 15 days and 30 days. We found that 12h sleep deprivation significantly increased cell proliferation and the total number of surviving cells in the hippocampal dentate gyrus soon after sleep deprivation, as well as 15 days and 30 days later, in comparison to control rats allowed to sleep. No changes were instead found in the subventricular zone of the lateral ventricles, indicating that 12h sleep deprivation selectively triggers neurogenic signals to the hippocampus.** The present data include acute sleep deprivation among the conditions which upregulate hippocampal neurogenesis and raise the possibility that such response could be implicated in the beneficial effects elicited in depressed patients by one night sleep deprivation. Thus, the findings could contribute to the understanding of the intriguing relationship between depression and neurogenesis in the adult brain.

### quelsen — 2012-05-14T22:53:09+0300

It seems another Dr. is using the latest technology!!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T02:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23315>

its in my last blog.....

### MonteD — 2012-05-12T19:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23439>

I don't like cool sculpting. Its only approved for the love handles and it cost 1500 dollars per 1 hour session. And the guy in the video had a good 25 pounds to lose and was worrying about "love handles"....lol. With as much as he is going to spend on the doc (and subsequently gain back) he could have invested in a complete cryo setup at home.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T21:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23468>

But Monte what is very important here is that science is now aware that fat cells empty and go away with cold......right now they are using it for vanity.......I have been pounding the table for 7 yrs that this is the answer for health, obesity and diabetes.........The next leap is not that large from cool sculpting.......I saw that earlier than most......and it was into my CT 4 and CT 6 blogs. They are critical parts of the CT series.

So many people who view this cold issue from a narrow perspective call it hormetic.........It is not hormetic at all. It is curative for many patients with circadian mismatches. It maybe hormetic for a performance athlete who has a different goal.......but when I here a leader call cold a hormetic only thing........well that tells me they are pretty closed minded about how far reaching this science can be. I on the other hand have expanded this science to its outer boundaries in what I have been able to do and how I have demonstrated it to work in humans. How I am now using CT in neurosurgery is blowing this hormetic issue completely out of the window but only those with an open mind will see that because some "paleo leader" calls it hormesis only. When I have definitive proof that I can remove a tumor and do surgeries on people I normally could not do it on before it opens the question of what really is the boundary. A closed mind puts a frame or boundary around a concept based upon our current knowledge.

The great danger of success is thinking what made you successful will keep you successful........

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23537>

> The great danger of success is thinking what made you successful will keep you successful........

Jack, I appreciate your last sentence. We had a saying in the fighter pilot community--You are only as good as your next sortie. What people consider success today in the medical community (and I would submit, in many others) is actually total failure when you look at it from a higher altitude. The medical community is making money hand over fist, but the people are just getting sicker. This is somewhat analogous to what has happened to the financial community--the banksters get richer, and the rest of the people get poorer and suffer. Whatever happened to the ethic of real service to your fellows and your communities? When you view both the medical community and the financial community as exemplars, why aren't they viewing the results of their professions from a higher level and actually see the obvious--they are just making things worse even if they may firmly believe they are doing the right thing. That's what CW will do to you. What made you successful, may, in the end, be the seeds of your own demise. You are only as good as your next sortie!!

### AmyK — 2012-05-13T02:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23547>

Whatever happened to the ethic of real service to your fellows and your communities? When you view both the medical community and the financial community as exemplars, why aren't they viewing the results of their professions from a higher level and actually see the obvious--they are just making things worse even if they may firmly believe they are doing the right thing. That's what CW will do to you. What made you successful, may, in the end, be the seeds of your own demise. You are only as good as your next sortie!!

Love these thoughts, Randychaps! Many people in the medical community ARE in it to genuinely serve--not just for profit. Many DO recognize that people are not getting better, they just can't SEE an alternative to what they have been taught. The assertion of freedom of choice and lifestyle among patients is a powerful force as well... they may want to be well, but may want to continue destructive habits more, especially if they don't believe they will actually feel that much better. It's often difficult for practitioners to convey to patients just how much better their quality of life could be when those patients can't remember feeling or have never felt all that great. My feeling is... we need to help practitioners as well as patients see things from a higher altitude.

### QuantumRadical — 2012-05-13T02:36:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23551>

Many DO recognize that people are not getting better, they just can't SEE an alternative to what they have been taught. The assertion of freedom of choice and lifestyle among patients is a powerful force as well... they may want to be well, but may want to continue destructive habits more, especially if they don't believe they will actually feel that much better. It's often difficult for practitioners to convey to patients just how much better their quality of life could be when those patients can't remember feeling or have never felt all that great. My feeling is... we need to help practitioners as well as patients see things from a higher altitude.

AmyK. Totally agree. Some individual responsibility needs to be taken. But what action to take needs to be informed. Unfortunately, the message out there, for example, the food pyramid, that is supposedly authoritative for an individual to take informed action, is little more than a consensus of opinion heavily influenced by lobbying from the food industry and contributions to various political campaigns and candidates. It is certainly not a strictly scientifically based food pyramid. So, individuals are responsible for their own health, they expect sound guidance from the "authorities", and yet act on less than enlightened so-called conventional wisdom. No wonder they get sick and don't know what to do about it. At least Dr. Kruse attacks the problem from a scientific perspective, that is, an evolutionary biology perspective, which is, in my mind, intuitively a more sound approach than a politically and a make-money based approach.

The system is clearly broken. So, if it is broken, you need to stop doing what you are doing and find another path. Dr. Kruse is doing just that. It is working for me. That's good enough for me. I hope it works for everybody!

### QuantumRadical — 2012-05-13T02:54:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/article-on-yahoo-about-using-cold-to-zap-the-fat-cells-away.1413/post-23554>

> **AmyK;23629 wrote:** Love these thoughts, Randychaps! Many people in the medical community ARE in it to genuinely serve--not just for profit. Many DO recognize that people are not getting better, they just can't SEE an alternative to what they have been taught. The assertion of freedom of choice and lifestyle among patients is a powerful force as well... they may want to be well, but may want to continue destructive habits more, especially if they don't believe they will actually feel that much better. It's often difficult for practitioners to convey to patients just how much better their quality of life could be when those patients can't remember feeling or have never felt all that great. My feeling is... we need to help practitioners as well as patients see things from a higher altitude.

Also, I know many people involved in the medical profession, and, to a person, they are hardworking, and want to help patients get healthy and stay healthy. But, they too must have the best informed information to take the appropriate action. So, again, it gets down to what "science" is being espoused and is it based on real evidence or some biased statistical correlation that under any reasonable scrutiny is easily dissected and declared as voodoo science. It is a total mess right now. But the great people working in the system are somewhat handicapped by bureaucracy, policy, protocol, and the threat of lawsuit. Very tough problem that will probably take a decade or two to correct--AT LEAST. But I will continue to do my own research, my own N=1 experiments, and make my own conclusions about what works for me and what doesn't. So far, Jack's Blog just resonated with me back last August. It was so integrative! I had tried just the Paleo diet (and even gave Crossfit a go) before and always got hung up at some point and could never break through to where I wanted to be. Now, integrating all kinds of scientific data, Jack has essentially come up with a fairly straightforward and actionable path to health. Eat a Paleo Diet, embrace chronic exposure to cold (CT), get good sleep, and minimize your mismatches with your environment, technology, and your biologically mandated circadian rhythms. Simple solution, not necessarily easy to implement, but worth a shot. I took the shot, there was no danger (Top Gun ), and I have seen remarkable benefits to my own health. And, I have convinced a few others to take the leap, give it a try, and see if it is or isn't effective. So far, all seems to be progressing just as Jack has laid it out. Can't ask for much more. Beats "you have high cholesterol; here is your prescription for statins." Really? Really.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-13T04:07:28+0300

# Thread: Lipoic Acid antioxidant and wiked chelator... ummmmmm... fixes ED in rats, peeps anec
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipoic-acid-antioxidant-and-wiked-chelator-ummmmmm-fixes-ed-in-rats-peeps-anec.1414/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-12T03:25:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipoic-acid-antioxidant-and-wiked-chelator-ummmmmm-fixes-ed-in-rats-peeps-anec.1414/post-23323>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipoic\_acid](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipoic_acid)

Metal chelation

Owing to the presence of two thiol groups, dihydrolipoic acid is a chelating agent. **It chelates both intracellular and extracellular mercury in the brain and in the body. Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) has a half life of 3 hours. Due to this half life issue it can be dangerous to take chelators unless they are taken on a very strict schedule (every 3â€“4 hours even at night, a few days on, 10â€“14 days off) or they can cause more mercury to enter the brain, especially within 3 months of mercury filling removal.** Lipoic acid administration can significantly enhance biliary excretion of inorganic mercury in rat experiments, although it is not known if this is due to chelation by lipoic acid or some other mechanism.\[97\] Lipoic acid has the potential to cross the blood-brain barrier in humans, unlike DMSA and DMPS; its effectiveness, however, is heavily dependent on the dosage and frequency of application.\[98\]

### chocolate — 2012-05-24T05:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipoic-acid-antioxidant-and-wiked-chelator-ummmmmm-fixes-ed-in-rats-peeps-anec.1414/post-26955>

[http://www.elmhurst.edu/~chm/vchembook/623acetylCoAfate.html](http://http://www.elmhurst.edu/~chm/vchembook/623acetylCoAfate.html)

Metabolic Fates of Acetyl CoA:

If you reflect on both the content lipid metabolism and the previous carbohydrate metabolism, you can appreciate that there is a special central role for acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA acts both as a metabolic "receiving and shipping department" for all classes of biomolecules and as a major source of useful metabolic energy. The diagram on the left summarizes all metabolism and the central role of acetyl CoA. The diagram the next lower panel focuses on other functions as well.

The interactions of amino acids with acetyl CoA and the citric acid cycle will be studied in protein metabolism. Notice that acetyl CoA can react "reversibly" in the degradation or synthesis of lipids and amino acids. This is not the case with carbohydrate metabolism. In mammals, it is impossible to use acetyl CoA to make carbohydrates.

Synthesis of Cholesterol and other Steroids:

**Without going into detail, acetyl CoA forms the basis from which the fairly complicated steroids are synthesized. Some steroids of importance include cholesterol, bile salts, sex hormones, aldosterone, and cortisol.**

The major concern about cholesterol in the diet is muted somewhat by the knowledge that the liver can and does synthesize all of the cholesterol that the body needs. Excess cholesterol, whether from food or synthesized by the liver, ends up in the blood stream where it builds up on the artery walls. It has been determined that cholesterol levels can be controlled by lowering the amount of saturated fat and increasing the unsaturated fats. Unsaturated fats seem to speed the rate at which cholesterol breaks down in the blood. Controlling fats and cholesterol in the diet can significantly affect the levels of these substances in the blood.

# Thread: Salivary Cortisol Test - how to do?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-05-12T18:12:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23404>

I'm planning to do a salivary cortisol test for myself (4 samples per day) and want to be sure I don't mess it up. The kit had good instructions but didn't answer all my questions. I know many of you have experience with this and hope you can comment on this please!

1) Should I avoid exercise the day of the test? Will a strength session in the gym or a easy-to-moderate bike ride interfere with cortisol production and thereby skew results?

2) The instructions say to freeze each vial after filling and then mail it in the pre-paid bag when I'm done. Is it worth upgrading return shipping to overnight, especially now that temperatures are warming? I don't want to go through the time & expense only to find questionable results.

3) The instructions about taking/continuing hormones are confusing. It says to stop sublingual hormones 36 hours before testing but in another place says not to stop taking hormones. The instructions appear to apply to all their saliva-based tests including hormone testing which I'm not doing so perhaps that advice doesn't apply?

4) Anything else I should know before doing the test? I will have stopped doing CT for a week before I do the test.

### PrimalPam — 2012-05-12T20:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23452>

I'm about to do salivary cortisol testing myself, and I have the kit from Canary. It also says to not wear any lip products at all on the day of testing (not even lip balm). Reading your question about upgrading to overnight shipping made me realize that I'm going to go ahead and do this as you said, due to warmer temps.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T20:24:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23453>

it measures a 6 -8 month window so what you do a few weeks before it does not matter. This is why it is a better test than a serum level

Reading your question about upgrading to overnight shipping made me realize that I'm going to go ahead and do this as you said, due to warmer temps.

The no lip balm (I love my Lip Trip!) is going to be a bummer but oh well, a small sacrifice on the path to optimal.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-12T20:42:34+0300

> **Jack Kruse;23535 wrote:** it measures a 6 -8 month window so what you do a few weeks before it does not matter. This is why it is a better test than a serum level

Thanks! Very good to know.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-12T20:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-cortisol-test-how-to-do.1418/post-23459>

I have the ZRT test and it is a little confusing with the hormones. Personally, I assumed it was meant with regard towards hormone testing. Something to ask the company about.

I've had this test sitting around for months now. I attempted twice to take it and each time AN threw a fit. My brain still cannot take "can't eat" for 4 hours, apparently. Reason enough to send it into overdrive and think that I must eat everything now. So I aborted the tests. Very strange, since I can not eat just fine according to leptin rx most days... the difference is I \*could\* snack, I just don't want to.

# Thread: Blog-Can Dr. Jack's responses to comments go directly under the comment itself?

**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions

### PrimalPam — 2012-05-12T18:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23409>

Is there any way for Dr. Jack's responses to comments go directly under the comment to which he's referring? Rather than having to scroll back up to see which post he's referring to?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-12T18:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23420>

We've tried it but we've had lots of problem's If enough people ask the MM they will seek and destroy.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-12T19:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23426>

I agree, that would be awesome. MMs come through for us!

### Lyndra — 2012-05-13T18:31:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23684>

I'd like to be able to read comments two ways: by date and threaded (replies indented just below comment). It is awkward to keep scrolling up and down to read Dr. K's response and the original comment. But, I want to make sure I see them all, so being able to view them by date is important, too. Good luck finding a way to make this work and keep everybody happy!

### admin — 2012-05-14T19:29:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23934>

We had that implemented a couple months ago and we got complaints that they wanted comments on the older platform. I do agree it would be easier to read with his responses directly under the initial comment. As Dr. Kruse mentioned, if enough people request it we'll re-implement it.

### ealachan — 2012-05-14T20:48:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blog-can-dr-jacks-responses-to-comments-go-directly-under-the-comment-itself.1419/post-23952>

Count me as a vote for Dr. K's comment under the reader comment format. Scrolling up and down and all around gets really annoying.

# Thread: Finish strong!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 43
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justjoan060 — 2012-05-12T19:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23427>

I'm starting LR tomorrow, and here's SOME of my story.

I was never overweight until I was hit by a car on my bike in 2003, tore up my knee (terrible triad) which deteriorated over a 3yr period with two scopes until I finally had a total knee replacement in 2006 at Mayo (those surgeons rock!). I'm getting along great with the knee. Can do everything but squat (it doesn't want to bend quite that far). Prior to that I managed to keep at a decent weight although it was a constant challenge. I did all the same yo-yo's as everybody else.

I've been under a LOT of stress most of my adult life. I lost a a son when I was 34, divorce followed. Single mom for 17 yrs with a youngest son that turned to drinking and pot in high school, which led to arrest, OMVI's, jail, etc. that went on for years, still isn't completely under control but I've made it not my problem now. Just trying to give you a sense of my YEARS of high stress. Remarried ten years ago about the time I started menopause, turned out to be a jerk and that whole ten years was constant stress!! I'm now going through another divorce, which is a good thing, and CLEANING UP MY LIFE!!

After the accident in 2003 I gained 50 lbs over the next 3 yrs from being sedentary. Lost 40 two winters ago with hcg. About 3 months ago I started comfort eating and gained 15 pounds back. I'm right now close to 20 pounds over what I consider a healthy weight for me. I just finished another round of hcg to get that weight back off, and being on the website a lot led me to Jack Kruse and LR. (It's a God thing!) So....last several weeks I've been reading, reading, questioning, planning to do LR instead of hcg's P3.

I've read Taube's Why We Get Fat, and have started the Primal Blueprint. Bought The Paleo Solution last night. I have no problem with going against CW, so this is not a problem for me, and I'm completely fascinated by all of it! I have some heart disease (dad's side) and my mom died with dementia, so at 63 I'm looking to _live well and drop dead!_

I'm one of those "jumpy" people - like always stressed even when I think I'm not. This winter I did some Reflexology which was awesome! Definitely helped me feel more calm and loosen some muscles. Right now I'm seeing a Physical Therapist that does cranio sacral work, and that is helping my sleep and helping me feel calm also - in the face of going through a divorce and breaking up with my best friend (not my best friend after all). I work in a Physical Therapy clinic where we are trying to address all aspects of wellness, so I'm in a good place there. I believe it's time for a metamorphosis!

My goal is get healthy - hopefully that includes a healthy weight I can get to fairly soon. But more energy, better sleep, stronger bones and muscles, great attitude! It would be awesome if, in the next year or so, I become an example at my age of what this way of life can do for you. I'm taking before pictures

I haven't had all the labs done they talk about, but I know my cholesterol/trig numbers are awesome, and everything else I know about is good. Other than some mild atherosclerosis, I haven't been diagnosed with anything, so that's my base of knowledge!

So, tomorrow, on Mother's Day, I officially start LR, and will journal my progress here. I'm braced for ups and downs, but have high expectations!

Judge your successes by what you had to give up to get it! Dalai Lama

### Lanie — 2012-05-13T02:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23552>

Welcome, JustJoan, and good luck to you! Don't be afraid to ask questions - lots of very helpful people around here. You deserve to take care of you. Glad you're here to do it!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-13T05:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23591>

Thanks for the welcome, Lanie! I have asked Jack a couple of questions, and he's great about answering! I didn't really mean to start LR today, but it kind of turned out that way! I got up and had 4 eggs fried in CO and two Jimmy Dean sausage patties (I know, a little sugar in them), but I ended up at 42 g protien so not too bad, so I decided to take a stab at a first day. Had about a 6 oz ground beef patty fried in CO, steamed green beans then drizzled the left over oil in the pan over the beans. 8 oz ribeye and mixed salad for supper which turned out to be a little late. Time got away from me. I used a little EVOO with some Herbs de Provence and thyme in it for dressing. I'm drinking some water with lemon juice and Stevia in it this evening. Hope that's ok! Jack said if it's got calories it's a snack and this has no calories, so hope it's ok. If it's not, somebody let me know!!  Already seem to have more energy - dont know if it's in my head or not, but I'll take it! Happy Mother's Day to me!!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-14T01:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23771>

Was pleasantly surprised to get up to a 1.4 loss today! Feeling good - had a great Mother's Day! Beautiful day here in Iowa! I had an 8 oz ribeye (cooked two last night in CO) and a 4 egg omelet fried in CO for breakfast. The kids grilled chicken, made steamed green beans, mixed salad and red potatoes. So I had everything but the potatoes. Trying not to eat my carbs at suppertime so I had more veggies at lunch. Trying to drink a lot of water. Need to eat supper soon - better figure out what to have! cya

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-14T17:32:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23906>

GEEZ!!! I just wrote a longish, very profound  post to start the day - hit the wrong button and it all disappeared! Now I have to get to work, so I'll post later

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-14T21:35:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-23967>

Welcome! I saw your posts on Darleen's journal & had to tell you I love the quote you had in your signature, although I don't see it now. So either the brain fog & memory thing are worse than I thought...lol...or it's just decided to disappear! Either way...Welcome!!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-14T23:27:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24007>

Thanks for the welcome, paulalynn! I just put my signature on this morning, so it may not be on all posts. I'll see if it shows up on this one. I woke up ready to get up at 7:00 which is unusual for me. I have to take meds to sleep so far, and usually have to drag my butt out of bed at 8:00 - so that's a good sign! Make 4 eggs, a 3 oz Smithfield ham, was coming up short on protein so got out some chicken tenders I cooked last night and a little homemade quac; coffee. I was down .2 this morning, but need to get "some things moving", a constant problem since I quit smoking in 1992 (go figure)! I'm starting to feel sluggish because of it. Had a mixed salad with more chicken tenders for lunch. I've been making a dressing with EVOO, Herbs de Provence and thyme, until I find a recipe I like. I'm not much of a vinegar person!

I'm at work, so I'll keep this short. Maybe edit it tonight. Now to see if my signature appears!!!!!!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-15T19:12:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24265>

I'm up a pound this morning, but Ihad some pretty salty meat yesterday. Maybe I'll stay away from ham pretty much since i'm already eating bacon every morning. Wish I could afford a ribeye once a day! I had about 3- 3 oz pieces of pork I had in the freezer and some green beans for supper last night. Ate about 1/2 oz of Green and Blacks 85% chocolate cause I thought it might help me not crave it the rest of the evening. Counting carbs and an hanging around 25 pretty closely because I'd like to lose weight.

Dr. K gave me some advice about supplementing with K2 and D yesterday because I have some heart disease. Taking K with heart disease didn't make sense to me, but I finally found his previous blog on it, and now it makes more sense. I'm not having too much trouble not snacking between meals. Still have the mind game at bedtime, but I'm working on it!! Have a meeting to go to. Later....

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T20:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24301>

Joan ... take magnesium! I can't speak for others but evidently my Mg stores were flatlined because I have been taking 1200mg every night for months on end and just added neuromag in the morning. My sense of smell is finally beginning to revive (it's been near dead for decades) and I'm starting to feel bunches better. Really help with keeping you regular as well. Now if my hair would just start growing back in I'd be in 7th heaven!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-15T21:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24333>

Hi Darlene: I take mag but only 400 since that's what I saw him say somewhere. I take it a couple hours before bedtime to help me sleep. Maybe I should take two then and another one in the morning? I lost hair after doing hcg so mine is thinner than it used to be. Didn't even lose any going through menopause, but I did after 2nd round of hcg.

It's almost 2;00 pm and I wasn't hungry for lunch today, so I'm going to wait and have supper. I had more like 70 mg protein for BAB - need to plan better so I'm not just grabbing meat to get to my total! My mouth tastes like birds built a nest in it, and I seem to remember you talking about mucus? That too! Is that detoxing? I've only been doing this 4 days. I have to go check out your blog and see how you're doing! Good, I hope!

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T22:50:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24356>

YAY for you on the BAB! I can barely chow down on about 40g. I forced myself to do 50+ for a while but it's just too much.

I think you're in ketosis. Nasty breath is a sign of that and it's a good thing.

On the mag ... I'd suggest increasing your dosage gradually until you start noticing (and there's no nice way to put this) loose stools. Then back off just a tad. That's what I did.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-15T23:45:49+0300

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T20:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24637>

I've been up a pound yesterday and again today. I know Dr. K says women sometimes gain. I'm going to post somewhere what the norm is because there will come a time where I refuse to keep gaining. I know my carbs are below 25 g - yesterday probably more like 12 g because I had none at breakfast, didn't eat lunch and had about a cup of steamed broccoli and a little homemade quac with about a 7 oz piece of sirloin for supper. I also had cocoa crack (which for you non-hcger's is CO melted with sweetener and cocoa stirred in - frozen - then you break off pieces to eat). Maybe it intereferes with something although I see other people on here having it with their breakfast even to get in more CO. This morning I had the other 7 oz sirloin, 3 eggs in CO and 2 strips of bacon. I know I've been getting 50 g protein BAB easy! It's a little after noon and I brought a salad with some chicken strips for lunch, but really don't feel like eating. Almost a little queasy! I'll decide soon if I'm going to eat it or skip lunch.

I'm getting a little mind boggled over supplements. Between reading what the Dr recommends while on the reset, plus other supplements recommended for this and that, my counter is getting full! For the most part, I have no idea about which ones to take when, so I play it by ear. I take Mag a couple of hours before I go to bed to help sleep, and my krill oil says to take with the first meal of the day. Other than that, I wing it!! Haven't figured out where to get K2 yet which doc recommended since I have some heart disease. Trudging along.............

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T21:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24670>

Crap! Right now I'm discouraged! I've gained a lb a day the last two days and am trying to get some answers before it gets out of hand. So far, it's like a needle in a haystack. I've been told if you're gaining on the reset, your hormones are wacky, you have to get them checked first, etc., etc. When I started this, it said do a, b and c and it all made so much sense! But I didn't see "it'll work UNLESS your hormones are wacked out, or UNLESS your blood sugar is "X", or IF........ Sorry, I did so much planning and studying to do this reset, and now I'm discouraged! Both my OBGYN and family doctor here look at me like i've got two heads if I ask for certain labs - they feel they can't justify ordering them. I actually got told recently by my OB's PA that, since I'm post menopausal, "if we test you, we really don't know what to do about it anyway" in view of the big HRT studies of a few years ago. Geez! I might have to ask Dr. Kruse because I'm getting all sorts of varied answers (which I appreciate), but don't want to charge ahead trying to figure out how to get labs done, and which labs to get, if it'll just pass or something. GGRRRRR.............

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-17T00:53:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24722>

Back to the reset. I'm doing better than expected with the not snacking. It hasn't been a problem really. I then had about 7 oz sirloin, steamed broccoli and a little homemade quac for supper and had some cocoa crack for dessert.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-17T02:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-24743>

Joan I answered your questions over on my journal.

On the hormone thing, if you're serious about it then you should find a Wiley Protocol [practitioner](http://http://www.wileyprotocol.com/wp-directory/find-a-wp-provider.html).

The HRT study your current doc referenced? According to TS Wiley, when the results were made public they conveniently "forgot" to mention that EVERY SINGLE WOMAN was on SYNTHETIC hormones. They might as well have been taking rat poison.

Actually, even horse estrogen provides a little bit of protection but when you add in synthetic progesterone it's like pouring gasoline on a fire. You may need to educate your doc and his p.a.

On the grass fed, be sure and ask if it's grass fed or grass finished. They're two different things. Don't let 'em fool ya.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-05-18T03:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-25042>

I had the same problem with the bowels but I got some Naturally Calm Magnesium (citrate) powder and started with 2 teaspoons for a couple of days, then incresed to 3 teaspoons, then 4 teaspoons. Viola! 4 teaspoons the magic number for me. I also started sleeping like a log!!!

Good luck!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-18T21:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-25284>

Thanks Paula! I'll try that and hopefully it'll work as well for me in both areas!!

Here's what I've decided. Yesterday and today I still gained a pound each day, and definitely can't keep going at that rate. It occurred to me last night that maybe I'm sensitive to CO because any time I've tried to use it in the past or eat cocoa crack during hcg P2, I've thought I gained from it. So I posed the question on the Monster Thread whether some people report not going well with CO? Dr. K said some people don't tolerate it well, and that hcg people seem to have trouble with it! I've never liked coconut, and maybe I don't like it because my body doesn't like it? (out there, I know!) Anyway, I've been pouring it on all week, so maybe that's why I've been gaining so fast. I'm going to back off to just a modified high protein diet for now and use butter instead since Dr K doesn't like EVOO when you're LR. Tuesday I'm seeing the local doc that works with BHRT and see where I stand with the hormone thing and work on getting myself straightened out there. I don't like doing too many things at once because you can't tell what's causing what??!!

I'm hopefully going to find what allows me to maintain my weight even if I can't lose right now. Will post later!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-19T16:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-25483>

I was down .5 lb this morning, so I think I've narrowed it down to intolerance to CO being the culprit. Once I'm confident I'm not gaining at the rate of a pound a day!!.....I can quit weighing myself. Yesterday I ate, well, I don't know what! I still ate high protein fairly big breakfast, no lunch, some raw almonds and an oz of 85% chocolate in the afternoon, quite a bit of pork roast and some broccoli with butter for supper. Took my Golden for about a 2 mile walk around the lake, and retrieving in the water. Oh yes, he put on his big boy pants this year and jumps right off the end of the dock to swim out after the ball (was scared to do that as a puppy last year after he did a face plant in the water!) Stayed up a little later than usual watching The Big Chill (love that movie!) and ate probably 2 more oz 85% chocolate.

So......I didn't sleep as well and woke up with a headache and feeling toxic. I also had some Physical Therapy on my knee which flared it up because it hurts worse this morning. I've got some tendonitis or something going on. Did a lot of up and down a ladder and kneeling laying a new floor this winter redecorating two rooms after my husband moved out. I think I got something started then. My doctor at Mayo didn't think I have a meniscus tear.

4 eggs and 4 strips of bacon for breakfast plus coffee. Will try to get back to not snacking today. Also going out to try and find some K2 (Dr. recommended since I have some heart disease) and the mag that Dr. K recommends, or at least mag citrate to help keep things moving.

Am excited to see the BHRT doc this week. People who have seen her like her work. I'm anxious to finally see where my hormone levels are. I gave up on that going through menopause because nobody around here worked with it. But this got me on it again, and I found her! Puzzle pieces!!

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-20T21:23:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-25773>

4 eggs, 4 strips bacon and about 4 oz sirloin in butter. No lunch so far - not hungry. Feeling tired, no energy yesterday and today. Fell off the wagon with snacking yesterday. I was conflicted about whether to just eat more paleo until I've seen the BHRT doc this week and find out my labs. I wish now I would have decided to do the labs before I ever started the reset. Easier to tell what's reacting how with your body when you're only doing one thing at a time in my estimation! I finally found K2 which also has D3 that Dr. K told me to take due to heart disease. But again, I don't think I'm going to add anything until I get labs done.

Does anybody know where safflower oil falls in the grand scheme of fats since I'm not tolerating CO very well?

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-20T22:41:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-25812>

DAMN woman where'd you put all that breakfast?! LOL. I can do the eggs and the bacon but just barely.

You're probably tired because you aren't eating carbs. It WILL pass. Allow your body to adjust and it will eventually start burning fat. Eating coconut oil helps, but you already know that, right? My 2 cents ... DO NOT use safflower oil. If I understand it (and I may not have it right) vegetable oils are loaded with omega 6's. Except CO of course.

If the CO is causing you to bloat? Use less until you get used to it. You might try coconut butter (aka manna). Jack swears by Nutiva brand but my preference (guess I'm not THAT big a groupie after all, lol) is Artisana organic. It is YUMMMMMY. Pricey but yummy. Also the only coconut oil I will use is from Tropical Traditions. They're online. It's pricey but it's made by hand the old fashioned way (according to the website) and is so good I can eat it straight. It's not cheap. But a little goes a long way. I just got another gallon tub of it (which weighs about 10 pounds, I swear) so we're now good for several months.

I doubt this will help but after I did 18 months on that awful jenny diet and then 8-9 months on hcg all I did was gain. 20 on, 20 off, 20 back on with another 24 friends. I have given up worrying about it. That's the best advice I have. Let go. This is not a diet. This will take time for those of us with long-term serious issues. Every day that passes I become more convinced it will work and when the flab begins to go I think/hope it will go FAST. But it WILL take time. Acceptance of that little fact has come hard for me. But I finally have gotten there. Now I just need to really stick with the program.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-21T05:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finish-strong.1420/post-25887>

How long have you been doing the reset again, Darlene? I guess I could look back. I get it takes time, but I will not gain at the rate of a pound a day! I can't buy a new size wardrobe again! I refuse! I can see gaining some, but not that! I was back on the old hcg site reading a couple of threads and Colleen (Grammy) says to take away eggs and see how you do because they're inflammatory. I've never thought i had trouble with them, but I will try that. BUT, my food choices are then getting slimmer and slimmer which makes it harder to handle. No dairy, no eggs, no nuts, no carbs, no sugar. That leaves meat and veggies! I am eating my mixed salad with a little feta cheese and a few olives, with my protein of course; steamed broccoli or cauliflower; using butter or EVOO. I know Dr K doesn't like olive oil at this point, but he also said CO doesn't agree with some people and I think I'm one. I'm going to lay low until I see what my hormone panel looks like, and get adjusted to whatever she puts me on. I'm sure they're wacked out! I have no idea what they are. Nobody around here would ever order it for me when I was going through menopause.

That was in the middle of the big HRT scare, and they said "we don't know what to do, we just know what not to do". That was the answer I got! Geez!! Having high hopes for this doc!

But.....for me I don't think it's a good idea to do the reset full throttle, CT and go on hormones at the same time. I've also been weaning off my statin, so don't want to put my system through too much too fast! Does that make sense to you?

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-23T04:33:08+0300

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-24T18:14:21+0300

My funny story: I went to take my bath last night, ran the water the usual temp. Then as I sat down in it I decided, "hey, I'll just turn on cold water and slowly cool it down until it's cool, but not way uncomfortable and work my way into this CT thing. So that's what I did. When it got to where it felt cool, I just decided to lay back for a bit. I don't have a big enough tub either, wish I had my old one back. Now THAT was a tub!! Anyway, as I got out I left the water in the tub because I wanted to go get the thermometer (just out of curiosity) and see what the temp was. It was only 80 degrees!!!! I thought to myself "I do believe I have a ways to go with that!" I don't know how you people do it!

I'm still going to wait until I get started on whatever hormones the doc things I need, so if I get the zits I hear everybody talking about, I can tell if it's from hormones or what. ### justjoan060 — 2012-05-27T02:36:01+0300

Read some more of Primal Blueprint today. I'm like a sponge for information about paleo/primal and how everything works in the body v.s. CW diet and drugs. I registered on Fitday and got the app for my phone so I can keep track of what I'm eating better.

Breakfast: 3 eggs fried in butter; 4 strips bacon

Lunch: 5-6 oz sirloin leftover; salad with greens, cukes, red bell pepper, a few walnuts, tomato. I found this dressing from Bolthouse Farms "Olive Oil Vinaigrette" that I really like. I'm a huge range dressing person, and I had started making my own from sour cream, greek yogurt and spices that's great, but didn't know if I should be eating that right now. I think this isn't too bad; the ingredients are: reduced acid pineapple juice from concentrate, balsalmic vinegar, salt, garlic, EVOO, red bell pepper, zanthan gum, onion, flaxseed oil, spice. 6 carbs and 5 sugars in 2 T.

Trying to find a paleo recipe I found somewhere for a salad with chopped broccoli and cauliflower. I think maybe it was on Nora Gedgaudes' site. NOT EATING CHEESE, which I can't even believe! Cheese is right up there, or maybe past chocolate in my book, so I'm doing really good to deny myself that. I just can't even have it in the house!

Right now I'm trying out some paleo meals, a little less protein, but still not adding much for fruits until I get my labs back and see where I stand with them. Than I'll decide about my diet. I did a modified "steak day" from hcg yesterday and lost a pound. I'd love to get to where I was before I started the reset! Hope you all have a safe Memorial Day weekend!
